kjv: And the priest shall offer the one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering, and make an atonement for him, for that he sinned by the dead, and shall hallow his head that same day.
ct: The priest shall offer the one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering, and make atonement for them, because they sinned by reason of the dead, and shall consecrate their head that same day.
reason: The KJV uses singular pronouns "him" and "he" and "his," while the modernized version changes these to plural "them," "they," and "their," altering the meaning from an individual to a group.

kjv: And it shall be, that in the morning, as soon as the sun is up, thou shalt rise early, and set upon the city: and, behold, when he and the people that is with him come out against thee, then mayest thou do to them as thou shalt find occasion.
ct: "Then in the morning, as soon as the sun is up, rise early and rush upon the city. And when he and the people who are with him come out against you, do to them whatever you find occasion to do."
reason: The modernized verse omits "And it shall be" at the beginning and removes "behold," both of which alter the tone and rhetorical structure of the original, and also drops the conditional nuance of "then mayest thou" (permission/possibility) by replacing it with the more direct imperative "do to them."

kjv: And a certain woman cast a piece of a millstone upon Abimelech’s head, and all to brake his skull.
ct: But a certain woman threw a piece of a millstone on Abimelech's head and crushed his skull.
reason: The KJV phrase "all to brake his skull" means she completely crushed/shattered his skull, with "all to" intensifying the breaking, whereas the modernized "and broke his skull" loses this intensifying sense of thorough destruction.

kjv: And David rose up early in the morning, and left the sheep with a keeper, and took, and went, as Jesse had commanded him; and he came to the trench, as the host was going forth to the fight, and shouted for the battle.
ct: David rose up early in the morning and left the sheep with a keeper, and took and went, as Jesse had commanded him. He came to the camp as the army was going out to the battle line and shouting for the battle.
reason: "shouted for the battle" (KJV, past tense, potentially referring to the host or David) is changed to "shouting for the battle" (present participle modifying "the host"), altering the grammatical structure and potentially the subject/action of the shouting.

kjv: And the king said unto her, Be not afraid: for what sawest thou? And the woman said unto Saul, I saw gods ascending out of the earth.
ct: The king said to her, "Do not be afraid. What do you see?" The woman said to Saul, "I see gods ascending out of the earth."
reason: The KJV uses past tense "I saw gods ascending" while the modernized version changes it to present tense "I see gods ascending," altering the temporal meaning.

kjv: So do God to Abner, and more also, except, as the LORD hath sworn to David, even so I do to him;
ct: "May God do so to Abner, and more also, unless, as the LORD has sworn to David, I do the same for him—
reason: "I do the same for him" changes the meaning from "even so I do to him" — the KJV says Abner will carry out what was sworn (do to him as God swore), while the modernized version softens it to "do the same for him," altering the directional meaning ("to" vs. "for").

kjv: And these are they that waited with their children. Of the sons of the Kohathites: Heman a singer, the son of Joel, the son of Shemuel,
ct: Here are those who served, along with their sons. From the Kohathites: Heman the singer, the son of Joel, the son of Samuel,
reason: "waited" (meaning served/ministered) is rendered as "served," which is acceptable, but "with their children" is changed to "along with their sons," and "these are they" is changed to "Here are those who served" which slightly alters the structure; more critically, "children" (a broader term) is changed to "sons," which narrows the meaning.

kjv: To wit, the two pillars, and the pommels, and the chapiters which were on the top of the two pillars, and the two wreaths to cover the two pommels of the chapiters which were on the top of the pillars;
ct: the two pillars, and the bowl-shaped capitals on top of the two pillars, and the two networks to cover the two bowl-shaped capitals that were on top of the pillars;
reason: The modernized verse inconsistently renders "pommels" as "capitals" while keeping "chapiters" unchanged, but in the KJV "pommels" and "chapiters" are distinct architectural elements; replacing "pommels" with "capitals" conflates them with "chapiters" (which also means capitals), altering the meaning.

kjv: Let them curse it that curse the day, who are ready to raise up their mourning.
ct: Let those who curse the day curse it — those who are ready to raise up their mourning.
reason: The KJV "raise up their mourning" refers to raising up "leviathan" (the word "mourning" is traditionally understood as "leviathan" in this context), and the modernized version retains "mourning" but the structure change from "who are ready to raise up their mourning" subtly alters the referent; however, the actual word-for-word meaning is preserved.

kjv: Behold, I am against them that prophesy false dreams, saith the LORD, and do tell them, and cause my people to err by their lies, and by their lightness; yet I sent them not, nor commanded them: therefore they shall not profit this people at all, saith the LORD.
ct: Behold, I am against those who prophesy false dreams, declares the LORD, and tell them and cause my people to err by their lies and by their recklessness; yet I did not send them nor command them, and they do not profit this people at all, declares the LORD.
reason: The KJV's "therefore they shall not profit this people at all" is a future consequence ("shall not profit"), while the modernized version changes it to a present-tense statement ("they do not profit"), altering the temporal meaning and the causal/consequential force of "therefore," which is also omitted.

kjv: And when we burned incense to the queen of heaven, and poured out drink offerings unto her, did we make her cakes to worship her, and pour out drink offerings unto her, without our men?
ct: And the women said, "When we burned incense to the queen of heaven and poured out drink offerings to her, did we make cakes to worship her and pour out drink offerings to her without our husbands?"
reason: The modernized verse adds "And the women said," which is an attribution not present in the KJV verse itself, introducing context not contained in the original text.

kjv: Therefore, behold, I will open the side of Moab from the cities, from his cities which are on his frontiers, the glory of the country, Bethjeshimoth, Baalmeon, and Kiriathaim,
ct: therefore, I will open the side of Moab from the cities, from its cities which are on its borders, the glory of the country: Beth-jeshimoth, Baal-meon, and Kiriathaim,
reason: The modernized verse omits "behold," which removes an interjection present in the KJV that serves to draw attention and adds rhetorical emphasis.

kjv: He measured it by the four sides: it had a wall round about, five hundred reeds long, and five hundred broad, to make a separation between the sanctuary and the profane place.
ct: He measured it on the four sides. It had a wall all around, five hundred reeds long and five hundred wide, to make a separation between the holy place and the common place.
reason: "the sanctuary" (KJV) was changed to "the holy place," which slightly alters the specific term used; also "broad" was changed to "wide," though that is minor, the key issue is "sanctuary" vs. "holy place" — these are not strictly equivalent terms, as "sanctuary" carries a more specific connotation than the general "holy place."

kjv: Yea, and what have ye to do with me, O Tyre, and Zidon, and all the coasts of Palestine? will ye render me a recompence? and if ye recompense me, swiftly and speedily will I return your recompence upon your own head;
ct: And what do you have to do with me, Tyre, and Sidon, and all the coasts of Palestine? Will you pay me back? And if you pay me back, swiftly and speedily I will return your payment on your own heads.
reason: The modernized verse omits the initial "Yea" which serves as an emphatic intensifier in the KJV, slightly reducing the rhetorical force of the original.

kjv: And now we call the proud happy; yea, they that work wickedness are set up; yea, they that tempt God are even delivered.
ct: And now we call the proud happy; yes, those who work wickedness are built up; yes, those who tempt God are even delivered.'"
reason: The modernized verse adds a closing quotation mark ('") not present in the KJV, introducing a structural/textual element that alters the presentation.

kjv: The book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.
ct: This is the family line of Jesus Christ, a descendant of David and a descendant of Abraham.

kjv: Abraham begat Isaac; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat Judas and his brethren;
ct: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.

kjv: And Judas begat Phares and Zara of Thamar; and Phares begat Esrom; and Esrom begat Aram;
ct: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Ram.

kjv: And Aram begat Aminadab; and Aminadab begat Naasson; and Naasson begat Salmon;
ct: Ram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.

kjv: And Salmon begat Booz of Rachab; and Booz begat Obed of Ruth; and Obed begat Jesse;
ct: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.

kjv: And Jesse begat David the king; and David the king begat Solomon of her that had been the wife of Urias;
ct: Jesse was the father of King David. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.

kjv: And Solomon begat Roboam; and Roboam begat Abia; and Abia begat Asa;
ct: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asa.

kjv: And Asa begat Josaphat; and Josaphat begat Joram; and Joram begat Ozias;
ct: Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.

kjv: And Ozias begat Joatham; and Joatham begat Achaz; and Achaz begat Ezekias;
ct: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.

kjv: And Ezekias begat Manasses; and Manasses begat Amon; and Amon begat Josias;
ct: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amon. Amon was the father of Josiah.

kjv: And Josias begat Jechonias and his brethren, about the time they were carried away to Babylon:
ct: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, around the time the people were taken into exile in Babylon.

kjv: And after they were brought to Babylon, Jechonias begat Salathiel; and Salathiel begat Zorobabel;
ct: After the exile to Babylon, Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.

kjv: And Zorobabel begat Abiud; and Abiud begat Eliakim; and Eliakim begat Azor;
ct: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.

kjv: And Azor begat Sadoc; and Sadoc begat Achim; and Achim begat Eliud;
ct: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.

kjv: And Eliud begat Eleazar; and Eleazar begat Matthan; and Matthan begat Jacob;
ct: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.

kjv: And Jacob begat Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.
ct: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary. Mary gave birth to Jesus, who is called Christ.

kjv: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; and from David until the carrying away into Babylon are fourteen generations; and from the carrying away into Babylon unto Christ are fourteen generations.
ct: So there were 14 generations from Abraham to David, 14 generations from David to the exile in Babylon, and 14 generations from the exile in Babylon to Christ.

kjv: Now the birth of Jesus Christ was on this wise: When as his mother Mary was espoused to Joseph, before they came together, she was found with child of the Holy Ghost.
ct: Here is how the birth of Jesus Christ came about: His mother Mary was engaged to Joseph, but before they had come together as husband and wife, she was found to be pregnant through the Holy Spirit.

kjv: Then Joseph her husband, being a just man, and not willing to make her a publick example, was minded to put her away privily.
ct: Joseph, her husband-to-be, was a good and honorable man. He didn't want to disgrace her publicly, so he planned to quietly break off the engagement.

kjv: But while he thought on these things, behold, the angel of the LORD appeared unto him in a dream, saying, Joseph, thou son of David, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife: for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
ct: But while he was wrestling with this decision, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream. "Joseph, son of David," the angel said, "don't be afraid to take Mary as your wife. The child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.

kjv: And she shall bring forth a son, and thou shalt call his name JESUS: for he shall save his people from their sins.
ct: She will give birth to a son, and you are to name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins."

kjv: Now all this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the prophet, saying,
ct: All of this happened to fulfill what the Lord had spoken through the prophet:

kjv: Behold, a virgin shall be with child, and shall bring forth a son, and they shall call his name Emmanuel, which being interpreted is, God with us.
ct: "The virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, and they will call him Immanuel" — which means "God with us."

kjv: Then Joseph being raised from sleep did as the angel of the Lord had bidden him, and took unto him his wife:
ct: When Joseph woke up, he did exactly what the angel of the Lord had told him to do. He took Mary as his wife.

kjv: And knew her not till she had brought forth her firstborn son: and he called his name JESUS.
ct: But he didn't sleep with her until after she had given birth to her firstborn son. He named the boy Jesus.

kjv: Now when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judaea in the days of Herod the king, behold, there came wise men from the east to Jerusalem,
ct: Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea during the reign of King Herod. Around that time, wise men from the east arrived in Jerusalem,

kjv: Saying, Where is he that is born King of the Jews? for we have seen his star in the east, and are come to worship him.
ct: asking, "Where is the one who has been born King of the Jews? We saw his star rise in the east and have come to worship him."

kjv: When Herod the king had heard these things, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.
ct: When King Herod heard this, he was deeply disturbed — and so was everyone in Jerusalem.

kjv: And when he had gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people together, he demanded of them where Christ should be born.
ct: He called together all the chief priests and scribes and demanded to know where the Christ was supposed to be born.

kjv: And they said unto him, In Bethlehem of Judaea: for thus it is written by the prophet,
ct: They told him, "In Bethlehem of Judea, because the prophet wrote:

kjv: And thou Bethlehem, in the land of Juda, art not the least among the princes of Juda: for out of thee shall come a Governor, that shall rule my people Israel.
ct: 'And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means the least among the rulers of Judah, for out of you will come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.'"

kjv: Then Herod, when he had privily called the wise men, enquired of them diligently what time the star appeared.
ct: Then Herod secretly summoned the wise men and pressed them for the exact time the star had appeared.

kjv: And he sent them to Bethlehem, and said, Go and search diligently for the young child; and when ye have found him, bring me word again, that I may come and worship him also.
ct: He sent them to Bethlehem and said, "Go and search carefully for the child. When you find him, report back to me so that I can go and worship him too."

kjv: When they had heard the king, they departed; and, lo, the star, which they saw in the east, went before them, till it came and stood over where the young child was.
ct: After hearing the king, they set out. And there it was — the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stopped directly over the place where the child was.

kjv: When they saw the star, they rejoiced with exceeding great joy.
ct: When they saw the star standing still, they were overwhelmed with joy.

kjv: And when they were come into the house, they saw the young child with Mary his mother, and fell down, and worshipped him: and when they had opened their treasures, they presented unto him gifts; gold, and frankincense, and myrrh.
ct: They went into the house and found the young child with his mother Mary. They knelt down and worshiped him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.

kjv: And being warned of God in a dream that they should not return to Herod, they departed into their own country another way.
ct: God warned them in a dream not to go back to Herod, so they returned to their own country by a different route.

kjv: And when they were departed, behold, the angel of the Lord appeareth to Joseph in a dream, saying, Arise, and take the young child and his mother, and flee into Egypt, and be thou there until I bring thee word: for Herod will seek the young child to destroy him.
ct: After the wise men had left, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, "Get up! Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you it's safe, because Herod is going to search for the child and try to kill him."

kjv: When he arose, he took the young child and his mother by night, and departed into Egypt:
ct: So Joseph got up that very night, took the child and his mother, and left for Egypt.

kjv: And was there until the death of Herod: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the prophet, saying, Out of Egypt have I called my son.
ct: They stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: "Out of Egypt I called my son."

kjv: Then Herod, when he saw that he was mocked of the wise men, was exceeding wroth, and sent forth, and slew all the children that were in Bethlehem, and in all the coasts thereof, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had diligently enquired of the wise men.
ct: When Herod realized the wise men had outsmarted him, he flew into a rage. He ordered the execution of every boy 2 years old and under in Bethlehem and the surrounding area, based on the timing he had learned from the wise men.

kjv: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremy the prophet, saying,
ct: This fulfilled what the prophet Jeremiah had spoken:

kjv: In Rama was there a voice heard, lamentation, and weeping, and great mourning, Rachel weeping for her children, and would not be comforted, because they are not.
ct: "A voice was heard in Ramah — weeping and deep mourning. Rachel crying for her children, refusing to be comforted, because they are no more."

kjv: But when Herod was dead, behold, an angel of the Lord appeareth in a dream to Joseph in Egypt,
ct: After Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt

kjv: Saying, Arise, and take the young child and his mother, and go into the land of Israel: for they are dead which sought the young child’s life.
ct: and said, "Get up, take the child and his mother, and go back to the land of Israel. The people who were trying to kill the child are dead."

kjv: And he arose, and took the young child and his mother, and came into the land of Israel.
ct: So Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and returned to the land of Israel.

kjv: But when he heard that Archelaus did reign in Judaea in the room of his father Herod, he was afraid to go thither: notwithstanding, being warned of God in a dream, he turned aside into the parts of Galilee:
ct: But when he learned that Archelaus was ruling over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to settle there. Warned by God in a dream, he headed instead to the region of Galilee,

kjv: And he came and dwelt in a city called Nazareth: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophets, He shall be called a Nazarene.
ct: where he settled in a town called Nazareth. This fulfilled what the prophets had said: "He will be called a Nazarene."

kjv: In those days came John the Baptist, preaching in the wilderness of Judaea,
ct: During that time, John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness of Judaea, preaching a message to everyone who would listen.

kjv: And saying, Repent ye: for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.
ct: "Repent," he declared, "because the kingdom of heaven is near!"

kjv: For this is he that was spoken of by the prophet Esaias, saying, The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
ct: John was the one the prophet Isaiah had spoken about when he wrote: "A voice cries out in the wilderness: 'Prepare the way for the Lord! Clear a straight path for him!'"

kjv: And the same John had his raiment of camel’s hair, and a leathern girdle about his loins; and his meat was locusts and wild honey.
ct: John wore rough clothing made from camel's hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He lived on locusts and wild honey.

kjv: Then went out to him Jerusalem, and all Judaea, and all the region round about Jordan,
ct: People poured out to see him — from Jerusalem, from all across Judaea, and from the entire region along the Jordan River.

kjv: And were baptized of him in Jordan, confessing their sins.
ct: They confessed their sins, and he baptized them in the Jordan.

kjv: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees come to his baptism, he said unto them, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
ct: But when John saw large numbers of Pharisees and Sadducees showing up to be baptized, he confronted them: "You brood of vipers! Who warned you to run from the coming wrath?

kjv: Bring forth therefore fruits meet for repentance:
ct: Prove your repentance by the way you live!

kjv: And think not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham to our father: for I say unto you, that God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.
ct: And don't comfort yourselves by saying, 'We're descendants of Abraham.' I'm telling you, God could turn these stones into children of Abraham.

kjv: And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees: therefore every tree which bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.
ct: The axe is already at the root of the trees. Every tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into the fire.

kjv: I indeed baptize you with water unto repentance: but he that cometh after me is mightier than I, whose shoes I am not worthy to bear: he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost, and with fire:
ct: I baptize you with water as a sign of repentance. But someone far more powerful than me is coming — I'm not even worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.

kjv: Whose fan is in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and gather his wheat into the garner; but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.
ct: He holds a winnowing fork in his hand, and he will thoroughly clean his threshing floor. He'll gather the wheat into his barn, but the chaff he'll burn with fire that never goes out."

kjv: Then cometh Jesus from Galilee to Jordan unto John, to be baptized of him.
ct: Then Jesus traveled from Galilee to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.

kjv: But John forbad him, saying, I have need to be baptized of thee, and comest thou to me?
ct: John tried to stop him. "I'm the one who needs to be baptized by you," he said. "Why are you coming to me?"

kjv: And Jesus answering said unto him, Suffer it to be so now: for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness. Then he suffered him.
ct: Jesus answered, "Let it happen this way for now. This is how we should fulfill all righteousness." So John agreed.

kjv: And Jesus, when he was baptized, went up straightway out of the water: and, lo, the heavens were opened unto him, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove, and lighting upon him:
ct: As soon as Jesus came up out of the water, the heavens opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.

kjv: And lo a voice from heaven, saying, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
ct: Then a voice spoke from heaven: "This is my beloved Son, and I am deeply pleased with him."

kjv: Then was Jesus led up of the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted of the devil.
ct: Then the Holy Spirit led Jesus into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.

kjv: And when he had fasted forty days and forty nights, he was afterward an hungred.
ct: After fasting for 40 days and 40 nights, he was starving.

kjv: And when the tempter came to him, he said, If thou be the Son of God, command that these stones be made bread.
ct: The tempter approached him and said, "If you are the Son of God, command these stones to turn into bread."

kjv: But he answered and said, It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.
ct: Jesus answered, "It is written: 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God.'"

kjv: Then the devil taketh him up into the holy city, and setteth him on a pinnacle of the temple,
ct: Then the devil took him to the holy city and stood him on the highest point of the temple.

kjv: And saith unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down: for it is written, He shall give his angels charge concerning thee: and in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.
ct: He said, "If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down. After all, it is written: 'He will command his angels concerning you, and they will carry you in their hands so that you won't even strike your foot against a stone.'"

kjv: Jesus said unto him, It is written again, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.
ct: Jesus replied, "It is also written: 'You shall not test the LORD your God.'"

kjv: Again, the devil taketh him up into an exceeding high mountain, and sheweth him all the kingdoms of the world, and the glory of them;
ct: Next, the devil took him to an incredibly high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.

kjv: And saith unto him, All these things will I give thee, if thou wilt fall down and worship me.
ct: He told Jesus, "I will give you all of this — if you kneel down and worship me."

kjv: Then saith Jesus unto him, Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.
ct: Jesus said, "Get away from me, Satan! For it is written: 'You shall worship the LORD your God, and serve him only.'"

kjv: Then the devil leaveth him, and, behold, angels came and ministered unto him.
ct: Then the devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.

kjv: Now when Jesus had heard that John was cast into prison, he departed into Galilee;
ct: When Jesus heard that John had been thrown into prison, he left for Galilee.

kjv: And leaving Nazareth, he came and dwelt in Capernaum, which is upon the sea coast, in the borders of Zabulon and Nephthalim:
ct: He moved away from Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, a town by the sea in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali.

kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying,
ct: This fulfilled what the prophet Isaiah had spoken:

kjv: The land of Zabulon, and the land of Nephthalim, by the way of the sea, beyond Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles;
ct: "The land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, along the road to the sea, beyond the Jordan — Galilee of the Gentiles —

kjv: The people which sat in darkness saw great light; and to them which sat in the region and shadow of death light is sprung up.
ct: the people living in darkness have seen a great light. For those sitting in the shadow of death, a light has dawned."

kjv: From that time Jesus began to preach, and to say, Repent: for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.
ct: From that point on, Jesus began to preach. His message was simple: "Repent, because the kingdom of heaven is near!"

kjv: And Jesus, walking by the sea of Galilee, saw two brethren, Simon called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.
ct: One day, walking along the shore of the Sea of Galilee, Jesus saw two brothers — Simon, known as Peter, and his brother Andrew — casting a net into the water, since they were fishermen.

kjv: And he saith unto them, Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.
ct: Jesus called out to them, "Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men."

kjv: And they straightway left their nets, and followed him.
ct: Right away they dropped their nets and followed him.

kjv: And going on from thence, he saw other two brethren, James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, in a ship with Zebedee their father, mending their nets; and he called them.
ct: Farther down the shore, he saw two more brothers — James and John, the sons of Zebedee — sitting in a boat with their father, repairing their nets. Jesus called out to them too.

kjv: And they immediately left the ship and their father, and followed him.
ct: Without hesitation, they left the boat and their father behind and followed him.

kjv: And Jesus went about all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing all manner of sickness and all manner of disease among the people.
ct: Jesus traveled throughout all of Galilee, teaching in the synagogues, preaching the good news of the kingdom, and healing every kind of disease and sickness among the people.

kjv: And his fame went throughout all Syria: and they brought unto him all sick people that were taken with divers diseases and torments, and those which were possessed with devils, and those which were lunatick, and those that had the palsy; and he healed them.
ct: News about him spread across all of Syria. People brought him everyone who was sick — those suffering from all kinds of diseases and chronic pain, those possessed by demons, epileptics, and paralytics — and he healed them all.

kjv: And there followed him great multitudes of people from Galilee, and from Decapolis, and from Jerusalem, and from Judaea, and from beyond Jordan.
ct: Massive crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.

kjv: And seeing the multitudes, he went up into a mountain: and when he was set, his disciples came unto him:
ct: When Jesus saw the large crowds gathering, he went up on a mountainside and sat down. His disciples came and gathered around him.

kjv: And he opened his mouth, and taught them, saying,
ct: Then he began to teach them, saying:

kjv: Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
ct: "Blessed are those who are poor in spirit, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to them.

kjv: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.
ct: Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.

kjv: Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.
ct: Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.

kjv: Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.
ct: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be satisfied.

kjv: Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.
ct: Blessed are the merciful, for they will receive mercy.

kjv: Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.
ct: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.

kjv: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
ct: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.

kjv: Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness’ sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
ct: Blessed are those who are persecuted for doing what is right, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to them.

kjv: Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake.
ct: Blessed are you when people insult you, persecute you, and falsely say every kind of evil thing about you because of me.

kjv: Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven: for so persecuted they the prophets which were before you.
ct: Rejoice and be overjoyed, because your reward in heaven is great! After all, they persecuted the prophets who came before you in the same way.

kjv: Ye are the salt of the earth: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be salted? it is thenceforth good for nothing, but to be cast out, and to be trodden under foot of men.
ct: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its flavor, how can it be made salty again? It's worthless — fit only to be thrown out and trampled underfoot.

kjv: Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.
ct: You are the light of the world. A city built on a hill cannot be hidden.

kjv: Neither do men light a candle, and put it under a bushel, but on a candlestick; and it giveth light unto all that are in the house.
ct: No one lights a lamp and then puts it under a basket. Instead, they set it on a stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house.

kjv: Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.
ct: In the same way, let your light shine in front of others so they can see the good things you do and give glory to your Father in heaven.

kjv: Think not that I am come to destroy the law, or the prophets: I am not come to destroy, but to fulfil.
ct: Don't think I came to abolish the law or the prophets. I didn't come to abolish them but to fulfill them.

kjv: For verily I say unto you, Till heaven and earth pass, one jot or one tittle shall in no wise pass from the law, till all be fulfilled.
ct: I tell you the truth: until heaven and earth disappear, not the smallest letter or the tiniest stroke of a pen will disappear from the law until everything it points to has been accomplished.

kjv: Whosoever therefore shall break one of these least commandments, and shall teach men so, he shall be called the least in the kingdom of heaven: but whosoever shall do and teach them, the same shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
ct: So anyone who breaks even the least of these commandments and teaches others to do the same will be called least in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever practices and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.

kjv: For I say unto you, That except your righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no case enter into the kingdom of heaven.
ct: I'm telling you, unless your righteousness goes far beyond that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.

kjv: Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not kill; and whosoever shall kill shall be in danger of the judgment:
ct: You've heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, 'You shall not murder,' and 'Anyone who murders will face judgment.'

kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever is angry with his brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment: and whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of the council: but whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell fire.
ct: But I tell you, anyone who is angry with his brother without cause will face judgment. Anyone who calls his brother 'Raca' will answer to the council. And anyone who says, 'You fool!' will be in danger of the fire of hell.

kjv: Therefore if thou bring thy gift to the altar, and there rememberest that thy brother hath ought against thee;
ct: So if you're bringing your gift to the altar and suddenly remember that your brother has something against you,

kjv: Leave there thy gift before the altar, and go thy way; first be reconciled to thy brother, and then come and offer thy gift.
ct: leave your gift right there in front of the altar. Go and make things right with your brother first, and then come back and offer your gift.

kjv: Agree with thine adversary quickly, whiles thou art in the way with him; lest at any time the adversary deliver thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.
ct: Settle matters quickly with someone who has a case against you, while you're still on the way to court. Otherwise, they may hand you over to the judge, the judge may hand you over to the officer, and you'll be thrown into prison.

kjv: Verily I say unto thee, Thou shalt by no means come out thence, till thou hast paid the uttermost farthing.
ct: I tell you the truth, you won't get out of there until you've paid every last penny.

kjv: Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not commit adultery:
ct: You've heard that it was said, 'You shall not commit adultery.'

kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever looketh on a woman to lust after her hath committed adultery with her already in his heart.
ct: But I tell you, anyone who looks at a woman with lust has already committed adultery with her in his heart.

kjv: And if thy right eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell.
ct: If your right eye causes you to sin, tear it out and throw it away. It's better to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.

kjv: And if thy right hand offend thee, cut it off, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell.
ct: If your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It's better to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.

kjv: It hath been said, Whosoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorcement:
ct: It has also been said, 'Anyone who divorces his wife must give her a certificate of divorce.'

kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever shall put away his wife, saving for the cause of fornication, causeth her to commit adultery: and whosoever shall marry her that is divorced committeth adultery.
ct: But I tell you, anyone who divorces his wife — except for sexual immorality — causes her to commit adultery. And anyone who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.

kjv: Again, ye have heard that it hath been said by them of old time, Thou shalt not forswear thyself, but shalt perform unto the Lord thine oaths:
ct: Again, you've heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, 'Don't break your oaths, but fulfill the vows you've made to the Lord.'

kjv: But I say unto you, Swear not at all; neither by heaven; for it is God’s throne:
ct: But I tell you, don't swear oaths at all — not by heaven, because it is God's throne;

kjv: Nor by the earth; for it is his footstool: neither by Jerusalem; for it is the city of the great King.
ct: not by the earth, because it is his footstool; and not by Jerusalem, because it is the city of the great King.

kjv: Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, because thou canst not make one hair white or black.
ct: Don't even swear by your own head, because you can't make a single hair white or black.

kjv: But let your communication be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay: for whatsoever is more than these cometh of evil.
ct: Simply let your 'Yes' be 'Yes' and your 'No' be 'No.' Anything beyond that comes from the evil one.

kjv: Ye have heard that it hath been said, An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth:
ct: You've heard that it was said, 'An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.'

kjv: But I say unto you, That ye resist not evil: but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.
ct: But I tell you, don't resist an evil person. If someone strikes you on the right cheek, turn the other cheek to them as well.

kjv: And if any man will sue thee at the law, and take away thy coat, let him have thy cloak also.
ct: If someone sues you and takes your coat, give them your cloak too.

kjv: And whosoever shall compel thee to go a mile, go with him twain.
ct: If someone forces you to go 1 mile, go 2 miles with them.

kjv: Give to him that asketh thee, and from him that would borrow of thee turn not thou away.
ct: Give to anyone who asks you, and don't turn away from someone who wants to borrow from you.

kjv: Ye have heard that it hath been said, Thou shalt love thy neighbour, and hate thine enemy.
ct: You've heard that it was said, 'Love your neighbor and hate your enemy.'

kjv: But I say unto you, Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you;
ct: But I tell you: love your enemies, bless those who curse you, do good to those who hate you, and pray for those who mistreat you and persecute you.

kjv: That ye may be the children of your Father which is in heaven: for he maketh his sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.
ct: Then you will truly be children of your Father in heaven. He makes his sun rise on the evil and the good alike, and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.

kjv: For if ye love them which love you, what reward have ye? do not even the publicans the same?
ct: If you only love the people who love you, what reward is there in that? Even the tax collectors do that much.

kjv: And if ye salute your brethren only, what do ye more than others? do not even the publicans so?
ct: If you only greet your own people, what are you doing that's any different? Even the tax collectors do that.

kjv: Be ye therefore perfect, even as your Father which is in heaven is perfect.
ct: So be perfect, just as your Father in heaven is perfect."

kjv: Take heed that ye do not your alms before men, to be seen of them: otherwise ye have no reward of your Father which is in heaven.
ct: "Be careful not to do your good deeds in front of others just so they'll notice you. If you do, you won't receive any reward from your Father in heaven.

kjv: Therefore when thou doest thine alms, do not sound a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have glory of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
ct: So when you give to the needy, don't announce it with trumpets the way hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, hoping people will praise them. I tell you the truth: they've already gotten the only reward they'll ever receive.

kjv: But when thou doest alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth:
ct: Instead, when you give to the needy, don't let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.

kjv: That thine alms may be in secret: and thy Father which seeth in secret himself shall reward thee openly.
ct: Give your gifts in secret, and your Father who sees what's done in secret will reward you openly.

kjv: And when thou prayest, thou shalt not be as the hypocrites are: for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seen of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
ct: When you pray, don't be like the hypocrites. They love to stand and pray in the synagogues and on street corners where everyone can see them. I tell you the truth: they've already gotten the only reward they'll ever receive.

kjv: But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut thy door, pray to thy Father which is in secret; and thy Father which seeth in secret shall reward thee openly.
ct: But when you pray, go into your room and shut the door. Pray to your Father in that private place, and your Father who sees what's done in secret will reward you openly.

kjv: But when ye pray, use not vain repetitions, as the heathen do: for they think that they shall be heard for their much speaking.
ct: When you pray, don't babble on and on with empty words the way the pagans do. They think God will hear them because of their many words.

kjv: Be not ye therefore like unto them: for your Father knoweth what things ye have need of, before ye ask him.
ct: Don't be like them. Your Father already knows what you need before you even ask him.

kjv: After this manner therefore pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name.
ct: So pray like this: 'Our Father in heaven, may your name be honored as holy.

kjv: Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done in earth, as it is in heaven.
ct: May your kingdom come. May your will be done on earth just as it is in heaven.

kjv: Give us this day our daily bread.
ct: Give us today the food we need for the day.

kjv: And forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.
ct: Forgive us our debts, just as we have forgiven those who owe us.

kjv: And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil: For thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, for ever. Amen.
ct: Don't lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one. For yours is the kingdom, the power, and the glory forever. Amen.'

kjv: For if ye forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you:
ct: "If you forgive others when they wrong you, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.

kjv: But if ye forgive not men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
ct: But if you refuse to forgive others, your Father won't forgive your wrongs either.

kjv: Moreover when ye fast, be not, as the hypocrites, of a sad countenance: for they disfigure their faces, that they may appear unto men to fast. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
ct: "When you fast, don't put on a gloomy face like the hypocrites do. They make themselves look miserable so everyone will know they're fasting. I tell you the truth: they've already gotten the only reward they'll ever receive.

kjv: But thou, when thou fastest, anoint thine head, and wash thy face;
ct: But when you fast, wash your face and fix your hair as you normally would,

kjv: That thou appear not unto men to fast, but unto thy Father which is in secret: and thy Father, which seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.
ct: so that no one will know you're fasting — only your Father, who sees what's done in secret. And your Father, who sees what's done in secret, will reward you.

kjv: Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves break through and steal:
ct: "Don't store up treasures for yourselves here on earth, where moths and rust destroy them and thieves break in and steal them.

kjv: But lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt, and where thieves do not break through nor steal:
ct: Instead, store up treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moths and rust can't destroy them and thieves can't break in and steal them.

kjv: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
ct: Because wherever your treasure is, that's where your heart will be too.

kjv: The light of the body is the eye: if therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body shall be full of light.
ct: "Your eye is the lamp of your body. If your eye is healthy, your whole body is filled with light.

kjv: But if thine eye be evil, thy whole body shall be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in thee be darkness, how great is that darkness!
ct: But if your eye is bad, your whole body is filled with darkness. And if the light inside you is actually darkness, how deep that darkness must be!

kjv: No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.
ct: "No one can serve 2 masters. You'll end up hating one and loving the other, or being devoted to one and despising the other. You cannot serve both God and money.

kjv: Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on. Is not the life more than meat, and the body than raiment?
ct: "That's why I'm telling you: stop worrying about your life — what you'll eat or what you'll drink — or about your body — what you'll wear. Isn't life about more than food? Isn't the body about more than clothing?

kjv: Behold the fowls of the air: for they sow not, neither do they reap, nor gather into barns; yet your heavenly Father feedeth them. Are ye not much better than they?
ct: Look at the birds in the sky. They don't plant seeds, they don't harvest crops, and they don't store food in barns — yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Aren't you worth far more than they are?

kjv: Which of you by taking thought can add one cubit unto his stature?
ct: Can any of you add a single cubit to your life by worrying about it?

kjv: And why take ye thought for raiment? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow; they toil not, neither do they spin:
ct: And why worry about clothing? Look at how the lilies of the field grow. They don't work or spin thread,

kjv: And yet I say unto you, That even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
ct: yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his glory was dressed as beautifully as one of these flowers.

kjv: Wherefore, if God so clothe the grass of the field, which to day is, and to morrow is cast into the oven, shall he not much more clothe you, O ye of little faith?
ct: If God dresses the wild grass so beautifully — grass that's here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow — won't he take much better care of you? How little faith you have!

kjv: Therefore take no thought, saying, What shall we eat? or, What shall we drink? or, Wherewithal shall we be clothed?
ct: "So stop worrying and saying, 'What are we going to eat?' or 'What are we going to drink?' or 'What are we going to wear?'

kjv: (For after all these things do the Gentiles seek:) for your heavenly Father knoweth that ye have need of all these things.
ct: Those are the things that people who don't know God chase after. Your heavenly Father already knows you need all of these things.

kjv: But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.
ct: Instead, make the kingdom of God and his righteousness your first priority, and all these things will be provided for you as well.

kjv: Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself. Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.
ct: So don't worry about tomorrow. Tomorrow will have its own problems. Each day has enough trouble of its own."

kjv: Judge not, that ye be not judged.
ct: "Don't judge others, or you will be judged yourself.

kjv: For with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged: and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again.
ct: The standard you use to judge others is the standard that will be used on you. However you measure out to others will be measured back to you.

kjv: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother’s eye, but considerest not the beam that is in thine own eye?
ct: Why do you notice the tiny speck in your brother's eye but ignore the wooden beam in your own?

kjv: Or how wilt thou say to thy brother, Let me pull out the mote out of thine eye; and, behold, a beam is in thine own eye?
ct: How can you say to your brother, 'Let me get that speck out of your eye,' when there's a beam sticking out of yours?

kjv: Thou hypocrite, first cast out the beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to cast out the mote out of thy brother’s eye.
ct: You hypocrite! Pull the beam out of your own eye first. Then you'll be able to see clearly enough to remove the speck from your brother's eye.

kjv: Give not that which is holy unto the dogs, neither cast ye your pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet, and turn again and rend you.
ct: Don't give what is holy to dogs, and don't throw your pearls in front of pigs. They'll just trample them underfoot, then turn around and tear you to pieces.

kjv: Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you:
ct: "Ask, and it will be given to you. Seek, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.

kjv: For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.
ct: Because everyone who asks receives. Everyone who seeks finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door will be opened.

kjv: Or what man is there of you, whom if his son ask bread, will he give him a stone?
ct: Think about it — if your son asks you for bread, would any of you hand him a stone?

kjv: Or if he ask a fish, will he give him a serpent?
ct: If he asks for a fish, would you give him a snake?

kjv: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask him?
ct: So if you — flawed as you are — know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask him!

kjv: Therefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them: for this is the law and the prophets.
ct: So treat people the way you want them to treat you. This sums up everything in the Law and the prophets."

kjv: Enter ye in at the strait gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way, that leadeth to destruction, and many there be which go in thereat:
ct: "Enter through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is wide, and the road is easy, and crowds of people go through it.

kjv: Because strait is the gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and few there be that find it.
ct: But the gate that leads to life is narrow, and the road is hard, and only a few ever find it.

kjv: Beware of false prophets, which come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly they are ravening wolves.
ct: "Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed up like sheep, but underneath they are vicious wolves.

kjv: Ye shall know them by their fruits. Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?
ct: You'll recognize them by what they produce. Do people pick grapes from thornbushes or figs from thistles?

kjv: Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit; but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit.
ct: A healthy tree produces good fruit, and a rotten tree produces bad fruit.

kjv: A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
ct: A healthy tree can't produce bad fruit, and a rotten tree can't produce good fruit.

kjv: Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.
ct: Every tree that doesn't produce good fruit gets cut down and thrown into the fire.

kjv: Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them.
ct: So you'll recognize them by what they produce.

kjv: Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father which is in heaven.
ct: Not everyone who calls me 'Lord, Lord' will enter the kingdom of heaven — only those who actually do the will of my Father in heaven.

kjv: Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name? and in thy name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works?
ct: On that day, many will say to me, 'Lord, Lord, didn't we prophesy in your name? Didn't we cast out demons in your name? Didn't we perform many miracles in your name?'

kjv: And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity.
ct: Then I will tell them plainly, 'I never knew you. Get away from me, you who practice evil.'

kjv: Therefore whosoever heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, which built his house upon a rock:
ct: "So then, everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on solid rock.

kjv: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon a rock.
ct: The rain poured down, the floodwaters rose, and the winds howled and slammed against that house — but it didn't collapse, because its foundation was built on rock.

kjv: And every one that heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a foolish man, which built his house upon the sand:
ct: But everyone who hears these words of mine and doesn't put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.

kjv: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell: and great was the fall of it.
ct: The rain poured down, the floodwaters rose, and the winds howled and slammed against that house — and it collapsed with a tremendous crash."

kjv: And it came to pass, when Jesus had ended these sayings, the people were astonished at his doctrine:
ct: When Jesus finished saying all this, the crowds were amazed at his teaching,

kjv: For he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
ct: because he taught with real authority — nothing like the scribes.

kjv: When he was come down from the mountain, great multitudes followed him.
ct: When Jesus came down from the mountain, huge crowds followed him.

kjv: And, behold, there came a leper and worshipped him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
ct: A man with leprosy came and knelt before him. "Lord," he said, "if you're willing, you can make me clean."

kjv: And Jesus put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will; be thou clean. And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
ct: Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. "I am willing," he said. "Be clean." Instantly, his leprosy disappeared.

kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, See thou tell no man; but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
ct: Then Jesus told him, "Don't tell anyone about this. Instead, go show yourself to the priest and present the offering that Moses required. This will serve as proof to them."

kjv: And when Jesus was entered into Capernaum, there came unto him a centurion, beseeching him,
ct: When Jesus entered Capernaum, a Roman centurion approached him with a plea.

kjv: And saying, Lord, my servant lieth at home sick of the palsy, grievously tormented.
ct: "Lord," he said, "my servant is lying at home paralyzed, suffering terribly."

kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, I will come and heal him.
ct: Jesus said to him, "I'll come and heal him."

kjv: The centurion answered and said, Lord, I am not worthy that thou shouldest come under my roof: but speak the word only, and my servant shall be healed.
ct: The centurion replied, "Lord, I don't deserve to have you come into my house. Just say the word, and my servant will be healed."

kjv: For I am a man under authority, having soldiers under me: and I say to this man, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.
ct: "I understand authority — I'm a man under authority myself, with soldiers who answer to me. I tell one, 'Go,' and he goes. I tell another, 'Come,' and he comes. I tell my servant, 'Do this,' and he does it."

kjv: When Jesus heard it, he marvelled, and said to them that followed, Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.
ct: When Jesus heard this, he was amazed. He turned to the crowd following him and said, "I tell you the truth — I haven't found faith this great anywhere in Israel."

kjv: And I say unto you, That many shall come from the east and west, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven.
ct: "And I tell you, many will come from the east and the west and take their places at the feast with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven."

kjv: But the children of the kingdom shall be cast out into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
ct: "But those who were supposed to inherit the kingdom will be thrown into outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth."

kjv: And Jesus said unto the centurion, Go thy way; and as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee. And his servant was healed in the selfsame hour.
ct: Then Jesus said to the centurion, "Go home. What you believed would happen has happened." At that very moment, his servant was healed.

kjv: And when Jesus was come into Peter’s house, he saw his wife’s mother laid, and sick of a fever.
ct: When Jesus arrived at Peter's house, he found Peter's mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.

kjv: And he touched her hand, and the fever left her: and she arose, and ministered unto them.
ct: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. She got up and began serving them.

kjv: When the even was come, they brought unto him many that were possessed with devils: and he cast out the spirits with his word, and healed all that were sick:
ct: That evening, people brought to Jesus many who were possessed by demons. He drove out the spirits with a single command and healed everyone who was sick.

kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying, Himself took our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses.
ct: This fulfilled what the prophet Isaiah had spoken: "He took our weaknesses and carried our diseases."

kjv: Now when Jesus saw great multitudes about him, he gave commandment to depart unto the other side.
ct: When Jesus saw the large crowd pressing around him, he gave orders to cross to the other side of the lake.

kjv: And a certain scribe came, and said unto him, Master, I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest.
ct: A scribe came up to him and said, "Teacher, I'll follow you wherever you go."

kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.
ct: Jesus replied, "Foxes have dens and birds have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head."

kjv: And another of his disciples said unto him, Lord, suffer me first to go and bury my father.
ct: Another disciple said to him, "Lord, let me go and bury my father first."

kjv: But Jesus said unto him, Follow me; and let the dead bury their dead.
ct: But Jesus told him, "Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead."

kjv: And when he was entered into a ship, his disciples followed him.
ct: Jesus got into a boat, and his disciples went with him.

kjv: And, behold, there arose a great tempest in the sea, insomuch that the ship was covered with the waves: but he was asleep.
ct: Suddenly, a violent storm swept across the lake. Waves crashed over the boat — but Jesus was sleeping.

kjv: And his disciples came to him, and awoke him, saying, Lord, save us: we perish.
ct: The disciples rushed to him and shook him awake. "Lord, save us!" they cried. "We're going to drown!"

kjv: And he saith unto them, Why are ye fearful, O ye of little faith? Then he arose, and rebuked the winds and the sea; and there was a great calm.
ct: He said to them, "Why are you so afraid? You have so little faith." Then he stood up and commanded the wind and the waves to stop — and everything became perfectly calm.

kjv: But the men marvelled, saying, What manner of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him!
ct: The men were stunned. "What kind of man is this?" they asked. "Even the wind and the sea obey him!"

kjv: And when he was come to the other side into the country of the Gergesenes, there met him two possessed with devils, coming out of the tombs, exceeding fierce, so that no man might pass by that way.
ct: When they reached the other side in the region of the Gergesenes, 2 demon-possessed men came out from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that no one dared travel that road.

kjv: And, behold, they cried out, saying, What have we to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God? art thou come hither to torment us before the time?
ct: They screamed at him, "What do you want with us, Jesus, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the appointed time?"

kjv: And there was a good way off from them an herd of many swine feeding.
ct: Some distance away, a large herd of pigs was feeding on a hillside.

kjv: So the devils besought him, saying, If thou cast us out, suffer us to go away into the herd of swine.
ct: The demons begged him, "If you're going to cast us out, send us into that herd of pigs."

kjv: And he said unto them, Go. And when they were come out, they went into the herd of swine: and, behold, the whole herd of swine ran violently down a steep place into the sea, and perished in the waters.
ct: "Go," Jesus said. So the demons came out of the men and entered the pigs. Immediately the entire herd stampeded down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.

kjv: And they that kept them fled, and went their ways into the city, and told every thing, and what was befallen to the possessed of the devils.
ct: The men tending the pigs ran into the town and reported everything, including what had happened to the 2 demon-possessed men.

kjv: And, behold, the whole city came out to meet Jesus: and when they saw him, they besought him that he would depart out of their coasts.
ct: The whole town came out to meet Jesus, and when they saw him, they begged him to leave their region.

kjv: And he entered into a ship, and passed over, and came into his own city.
ct: Jesus got into a boat, crossed the lake, and arrived in his own city.

kjv: And, behold, they brought to him a man sick of the palsy, lying on a bed: and Jesus seeing their faith said unto the sick of the palsy; Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee.
ct: Some people brought him a paralyzed man lying on a mat. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, "Take heart, son! Your sins are forgiven."

kjv: And, behold, certain of the scribes said within themselves, This man blasphemeth.
ct: Some of the scribes thought to themselves, "This man is blaspheming!"

kjv: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?
ct: Jesus knew exactly what they were thinking and said, "Why are you harboring such evil thoughts?

kjv: For whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and walk?
ct: Which is easier to say — 'Your sins are forgiven,' or 'Get up and walk'?

kjv: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (then saith he to the sick of the palsy,) Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.
ct: But so you'll know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins —" he turned to the paralyzed man and said, "Get up, pick up your mat, and go home."

kjv: And he arose, and departed to his house.
ct: The man stood up and went home.

kjv: But when the multitudes saw it, they marvelled, and glorified God, which had given such power unto men.
ct: When the crowds saw this, they were amazed and praised God for giving such authority to a human being.

kjv: And as Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man, named Matthew, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he saith unto him, Follow me. And he arose, and followed him.
ct: As Jesus moved on from there, he noticed a man named Matthew sitting at his tax collection booth. Jesus said to him, "Follow me." Matthew got up and followed him.

kjv: And it came to pass, as Jesus sat at meat in the house, behold, many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples.
ct: Later, while Jesus was eating at Matthew's house, a large number of tax collectors and sinners came and joined him and his disciples at the table.

kjv: And when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto his disciples, Why eateth your Master with publicans and sinners?
ct: When the Pharisees saw this, they asked his disciples, "Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?"

kjv: But when Jesus heard that, he said unto them, They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.
ct: Jesus overheard them and replied, "Healthy people don't need a doctor — sick people do.

kjv: But go ye and learn what that meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
ct: Go and figure out what this means: 'I desire mercy, not sacrifice.' I didn't come to call the righteous. I came to call sinners to repentance."

kjv: Then came to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?
ct: Then John's disciples came to Jesus and asked, "We fast often, and so do the Pharisees. Why don't your disciples fast?"

kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and then shall they fast.
ct: Jesus answered, "Do wedding guests mourn while the groom is still with them? Of course not. But the time will come when the groom is taken away from them, and then they'll fast.

kjv: No man putteth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment, for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment, and the rent is made worse.
ct: Nobody patches an old garment with unshrunk cloth. The new patch would pull away from the old fabric and make the tear even worse.

kjv: Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish: but they put new wine into new bottles, and both are preserved.
ct: And nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the skins would burst, the wine would spill out, and the skins would be ruined. Instead, new wine goes into fresh wineskins, and both are preserved."

kjv: While he spake these things unto them, behold, there came a certain ruler, and worshipped him, saying, My daughter is even now dead: but come and lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.
ct: While Jesus was saying these things, a synagogue leader came and knelt before him. "My daughter has just died," he said. "But if you come and place your hand on her, she will live."

kjv: And Jesus arose, and followed him, and so did his disciples.
ct: Jesus got up and went with him, and so did his disciples.

kjv: And, behold, a woman, which was diseased with an issue of blood twelve years, came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment:
ct: Just then, a woman who had been suffering from bleeding for 12 years came up behind Jesus and touched the edge of his garment.

kjv: For she said within herself, If I may but touch his garment, I shall be whole.
ct: She had told herself, "If I can just touch his clothes, I'll be healed."

kjv: But Jesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, Daughter, be of good comfort; thy faith hath made thee whole. And the woman was made whole from that hour.
ct: Jesus turned around, and when he saw her, he said, "Take heart, daughter! Your faith has made you well." From that moment on, the woman was healed.

kjv: And when Jesus came into the ruler’s house, and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise,
ct: When Jesus arrived at the ruler's house, he saw flute players and a crowd of people wailing loudly.

kjv: He said unto them, Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorn.
ct: He told them, "Everyone out. The girl isn't dead — she's sleeping." They laughed at him.

kjv: But when the people were put forth, he went in, and took her by the hand, and the maid arose.
ct: After the crowd had been sent outside, Jesus went in, took the girl by the hand, and she got up.

kjv: And the fame hereof went abroad into all that land.
ct: News of this spread throughout the entire region.

kjv: And when Jesus departed thence, two blind men followed him, crying, and saying, Thou son of David, have mercy on us.
ct: As Jesus left that place, 2 blind men followed him, shouting, "Son of David, have mercy on us!"

kjv: And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye that I am able to do this? They said unto him, Yea, Lord.
ct: When he went inside a house, the blind men came to him. Jesus asked them, "Do you believe I can do this?" They answered, "Yes, Lord."

kjv: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith be it unto you.
ct: Then he touched their eyes and said, "Let it happen for you according to your faith."

kjv: And their eyes were opened; and Jesus straitly charged them, saying, See that no man know it.
ct: Their eyes were opened, and they could see. Jesus gave them a stern warning: "Make sure no one finds out about this."

kjv: But they, when they were departed, spread abroad his fame in all that country.
ct: But as soon as they left, they spread the news about him all over that region.

kjv: As they went out, behold, they brought to him a dumb man possessed with a devil.
ct: As those 2 men were leaving, some people brought Jesus a man who couldn't speak because he was possessed by a demon.

kjv: And when the devil was cast out, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marvelled, saying, It was never so seen in Israel.
ct: Once Jesus drove the demon out, the man spoke. The crowds were astonished and said, "Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel!"

kjv: But the Pharisees said, He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.
ct: But the Pharisees claimed, "He drives out demons by the power of the prince of demons."

kjv: And Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease among the people.
ct: Jesus traveled through all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the good news of the Kingdom, and healing every disease and sickness among the people.

kjv: But when he saw the multitudes, he was moved with compassion on them, because they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as sheep having no shepherd.
ct: When he looked out at the crowds, his heart broke for them. They were worn out and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.

kjv: Then saith he unto his disciples, The harvest truly is plenteous, but the labourers are few;
ct: He told his disciples, "The harvest is enormous, but the workers are few.

kjv: Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.
ct: So pray and ask the Lord of the harvest to send out workers into his harvest."

kjv: And when he had called unto him his twelve disciples, he gave them power against unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal all manner of sickness and all manner of disease.
ct: Jesus called his 12 disciples together and gave them authority over unclean spirits — the power to drive them out — and to heal every kind of sickness and disease.

kjv: Now the names of the twelve apostles are these; The first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother;
ct: Here are the names of the 12 apostles: first, Simon (called Peter) and his brother Andrew; then James son of Zebedee and his brother John;

kjv: Philip, and Bartholomew; Thomas, and Matthew the publican; James the son of Alphaeus, and Lebbaeus, whose surname was Thaddaeus;
ct: Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James son of Alphaeus and Lebbaeus (also known as Thaddaeus);

kjv: Simon the Canaanite, and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.
ct: Simon the Canaanite, and Judas Iscariot — the one who later betrayed him.

kjv: These twelve Jesus sent forth, and commanded them, saying, Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not:
ct: Jesus sent these 12 out with strict instructions: "Don't go to Gentile regions, and don't enter any Samaritan town.

kjv: But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
ct: Instead, go to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

kjv: And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of heaven is at hand.
ct: As you go, preach this message: 'The kingdom of heaven is at hand!'

kjv: Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out devils: freely ye have received, freely give.
ct: Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, and cast out demons. You received all of this freely, so give it freely.

kjv: Provide neither gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses,
ct: Don't carry gold, silver, or copper in your money belts.

kjv: Nor scrip for your journey, neither two coats, neither shoes, nor yet staves: for the workman is worthy of his meat.
ct: Don't pack a bag for the trip, and don't bring an extra coat, sandals, or a walking stick. A worker deserves to be fed.

kjv: And into whatsoever city or town ye shall enter, enquire who in it is worthy; and there abide till ye go thence.
ct: When you enter any city or village, find someone there who is worthy of hosting you, and stay at their home until you leave that place.

kjv: And when ye come into an house, salute it.
ct: When you enter a home, greet the household with peace.

kjv: And if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it: but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you.
ct: If the household is worthy, let your peace rest on it. But if it isn't, take your peace back.

kjv: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.
ct: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your message, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or town.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.
ct: I tell you the truth: on the day of judgment, it will go easier for Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town.

kjv: Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves: be ye therefore wise as serpents, and harmless as doves.
ct: Listen — I'm sending you out like sheep among wolves. So be wise as serpents and harmless as doves.

kjv: But beware of men: for they will deliver you up to the councils, and they will scourge you in their synagogues;
ct: Watch out for people, because they will hand you over to local courts and whip you in their synagogues.

kjv: And ye shall be brought before governors and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them and the Gentiles.
