ct: You'll be dragged before governors and kings because of me, as a testimony to them and to the Gentiles.

kjv: But when they deliver you up, take no thought how or what ye shall speak: for it shall be given you in that same hour what ye shall speak.
ct: But when they arrest you, don't worry about what to say or how to say it. In that moment, the right words will be given to you.

kjv: For it is not ye that speak, but the Spirit of your Father which speaketh in you.
ct: It won't be you speaking — it will be the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.

kjv: And the brother shall deliver up the brother to death, and the father the child: and the children shall rise up against their parents, and cause them to be put to death.
ct: Brothers will betray their own brothers to death, and fathers will turn on their children. Children will rebel against their parents and have them killed.

kjv: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake: but he that endureth to the end shall be saved.
ct: Everyone will hate you because of me. But whoever endures to the end will be saved.

kjv: But when they persecute you in this city, flee ye into another: for verily I say unto you, Ye shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of man be come.
ct: When they persecute you in one city, escape to the next. I tell you the truth: you won't finish going through all the cities of Israel before the Son of Man comes.

kjv: The disciple is not above his master, nor the servant above his lord.
ct: A disciple isn't above his teacher, and a servant isn't above his master.

kjv: It is enough for the disciple that he be as his master, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more shall they call them of his household?
ct: It's enough for a disciple to become like his teacher, and a servant like his master. If people have called the head of the house Beelzebub, how much worse will they treat the rest of his household!

kjv: Fear them not therefore: for there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; and hid, that shall not be known.
ct: So don't be afraid of them. Everything that's covered up will be uncovered, and every secret will be brought to light.

kjv: What I tell you in darkness, that speak ye in light: and what ye hear in the ear, that preach ye upon the housetops.
ct: What I tell you in the dark, speak openly in the daylight. What I whisper in your ear, shout from the rooftops.

kjv: And fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.
ct: Don't fear those who can kill the body but can't touch the soul. Instead, fear the one who can destroy both soul and body in hell.

kjv: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father.
ct: Aren't two sparrows sold for practically nothing? Yet not a single one of them falls to the ground outside your Father's will.

kjv: But the very hairs of your head are all numbered.
ct: Even the hairs on your head are all counted.

kjv: Fear ye not therefore, ye are of more value than many sparrows.
ct: So don't be afraid — you are worth far more than many sparrows.

kjv: Whosoever therefore shall confess me before men, him will I confess also before my Father which is in heaven.
ct: Everyone who acknowledges me before others, I will acknowledge before my Father in heaven.

kjv: But whosoever shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father which is in heaven.
ct: But whoever denies me before others, I will deny before my Father in heaven.

kjv: Think not that I am come to send peace on earth: I came not to send peace, but a sword.
ct: Don't assume I came to bring peace to the earth. I didn't come to bring peace — I came to bring a sword.

kjv: For I am come to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.
ct: I came to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.

kjv: And a man’s foes shall be they of his own household.
ct: A person's worst enemies will be the members of their own family.

kjv: He that loveth father or mother more than me is not worthy of me: and he that loveth son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.
ct: Anyone who loves their father or mother more than me isn't worthy of me. Anyone who loves their son or daughter more than me isn't worthy of me.

kjv: And he that taketh not his cross, and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.
ct: Anyone who won't take up their cross and follow me isn't worthy of me.

kjv: He that findeth his life shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it.
ct: Whoever clings to their life will lose it, but whoever gives up their life for my sake will find it.

kjv: He that receiveth you receiveth me, and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.
ct: Whoever welcomes you welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me.

kjv: He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet’s reward; and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man’s reward.
ct: Whoever welcomes a prophet because that person is a prophet will receive a prophet's reward. Whoever welcomes a righteous person because they are righteous will receive a righteous person's reward.

kjv: And whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the name of a disciple, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.
ct: And I tell you the truth: whoever gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones because they are my disciple will certainly not lose their reward."

kjv: And it came to pass, when Jesus had made an end of commanding his twelve disciples, he departed thence to teach and to preach in their cities.
ct: After Jesus finished giving instructions to his 12 disciples, he left that area to teach and preach in the surrounding cities.

kjv: Now when John had heard in the prison the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples,
ct: John the Baptist, who was in prison, heard about everything Christ was doing. So he sent 2 of his disciples

kjv: And said unto him, Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?
ct: to ask Jesus, "Are you the one we've been waiting for, or should we be looking for someone else?"

kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Go and shew John again those things which ye do hear and see:
ct: Jesus told them, "Go back and tell John what you're hearing and seeing for yourselves:

kjv: The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them.
ct: Blind people are receiving their sight. The lame are walking. Lepers are being healed. The deaf can hear. The dead are being raised to life. And the poor are hearing the good news.

kjv: And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.
ct: Blessed is anyone who doesn't lose faith in me."

kjv: And as they departed, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken with the wind?
ct: As John's disciples were leaving, Jesus turned to the crowds and spoke about John. "When you went out into the wilderness, what did you expect to see? A blade of grass swaying in the wind?

kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? behold, they that wear soft clothing are in kings’ houses.
ct: No? Then what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? People who wear fine clothes live in palaces.

kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? yea, I say unto you, and more than a prophet.
ct: So what did you really go out to see? A prophet? Yes — and I tell you, he is far more than a prophet.

kjv: For this is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
ct: This is the one Scripture talks about when it says, 'Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare the way for you.'

kjv: Verily I say unto you, Among them that are born of women there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist: notwithstanding he that is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
ct: I tell you the truth: no one ever born has been greater than John the Baptist. And yet even the least important person in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he is.

kjv: And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.
ct: From the time John the Baptist began preaching until now, the kingdom of heaven has been under violent assault, and forceful people try to seize it.

kjv: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.
ct: All the prophets and the Law pointed forward to what was coming — right up until John appeared.

kjv: And if ye will receive it, this is Elias, which was for to come.
ct: If you're willing to accept it, John is the Elijah who was prophesied to come.

kjv: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
ct: If you have ears to hear, then listen carefully!

kjv: But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows,
ct: "What can I compare this generation to? They're like children sitting in the marketplace, calling out to each other,

kjv: And saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned unto you, and ye have not lamented.
ct: 'We played music for you, and you didn't dance! We sang funeral songs, and you didn't mourn!'

kjv: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a devil.
ct: John came fasting and avoiding wine, and people said, 'He has a demon.'

kjv: The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners. But wisdom is justified of her children.
ct: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, 'Look at him — a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!' But wisdom proves itself right by what it produces."

kjv: Then began he to upbraid the cities wherein most of his mighty works were done, because they repented not:
ct: Then Jesus began to call out the cities where he had performed most of his miracles, because they still refused to repent.

kjv: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
ct: "How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible for you, Bethsaida! If the miracles I did in your streets had been done in Tyre and Sidon, those cities would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.

kjv: But I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the day of judgment, than for you.
ct: I tell you, on the day of judgment, Tyre and Sidon will be better off than you.

kjv: And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted unto heaven, shalt be brought down to hell: for if the mighty works, which have been done in thee, had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
ct: And you, Capernaum — do you think you'll be lifted up to heaven? You'll be thrown down to hell. If the miracles done in you had been done in Sodom, that city would still be standing today.

kjv: But I say unto you, That it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment, than for thee.
ct: I tell you, on the day of judgment, even the land of Sodom will be better off than you."

kjv: At that time Jesus answered and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.
ct: At that time, Jesus prayed, "I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and educated, and revealed them to those as trusting as little children.

kjv: Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in thy sight.
ct: Yes, Father, this is exactly what pleased you.

kjv: All things are delivered unto me of my Father: and no man knoweth the Son, but the Father; neither knoweth any man the Father, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son will reveal him.
ct: My Father has entrusted everything to me. No one truly knows the Son except the Father, and no one truly knows the Father except the Son — and anyone the Son chooses to reveal him to."

kjv: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
ct: "Come to me, all of you who are exhausted and weighed down with heavy burdens, and I will give you rest.

kjv: Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls.
ct: Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, because I am gentle and humble in heart. You will find rest for your souls.

kjv: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.
ct: My yoke is easy, and my burden is light."

kjv: At that time Jesus went on the sabbath day through the corn; and his disciples were an hungred, and began to pluck the ears of corn, and to eat.
ct: Around that time, Jesus was walking through the grain fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry, so they started picking heads of grain and eating them.

kjv: But when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto him, Behold, thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the sabbath day.
ct: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, "Look! Your disciples are doing something that's not allowed on the Sabbath."

kjv: But he said unto them, Have ye not read what David did, when he was an hungred, and they that were with him;
ct: Jesus replied, "Haven't you read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?

kjv: How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them which were with him, but only for the priests?
ct: He went into God's house and ate the sacred bread — which neither he nor his men were allowed to eat. Only the priests could eat it.

kjv: Or have ye not read in the law, how that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless?
ct: And haven't you read in the Law that the priests work in the temple every Sabbath, technically breaking the Sabbath, and yet they're considered blameless?

kjv: But I say unto you, That in this place is one greater than the temple.
ct: I'm telling you, someone greater than the temple is standing right here.

kjv: But if ye had known what this meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the guiltless.
ct: If you understood what this means — 'I desire mercy, not sacrifice' — you wouldn't have condemned people who've done nothing wrong.

kjv: For the Son of man is Lord even of the sabbath day.
ct: The Son of Man is Lord even over the Sabbath."

kjv: And when he was departed thence, he went into their synagogue:
ct: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.

kjv: And, behold, there was a man which had his hand withered. And they asked him, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath days? that they might accuse him.
ct: A man with a withered hand was there. Looking for a reason to accuse Jesus, they asked him, "Is it lawful to heal someone on the Sabbath?"

kjv: And he said unto them, What man shall there be among you, that shall have one sheep, and if it fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not lay hold on it, and lift it out?
ct: He answered them, "If any of you had a sheep that fell into a pit on the Sabbath, wouldn't you grab it and pull it out?

kjv: How much then is a man better than a sheep? Wherefore it is lawful to do well on the sabbath days.
ct: A person is worth far more than a sheep! So yes, it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath."

kjv: Then saith he to the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it forth; and it was restored whole, like as the other.
ct: Then he said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." The man stretched it out, and it was completely restored — as healthy as the other one.

kjv: Then the Pharisees went out, and held a council against him, how they might destroy him.
ct: The Pharisees walked out and began plotting how they could destroy Jesus.

kjv: But when Jesus knew it, he withdrew himself from thence: and great multitudes followed him, and he healed them all;
ct: When Jesus learned of this, he left that area. Large crowds followed him, and he healed everyone who was sick.

kjv: And charged them that they should not make him known:
ct: But he warned them not to tell others who he was.

kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying,
ct: This fulfilled what God spoke through the prophet Isaiah:

kjv: Behold my servant, whom I have chosen; my beloved, in whom my soul is well pleased: I will put my spirit upon him, and he shall shew judgment to the Gentiles.
ct: "Here is my servant, whom I have chosen — my beloved, in whom my soul is well pleased. I will put my Spirit on him, and he will proclaim justice to the nations.

kjv: He shall not strive, nor cry; neither shall any man hear his voice in the streets.
ct: He won't argue or shout. No one will hear his voice yelling in the streets.

kjv: A bruised reed shall he not break, and smoking flax shall he not quench, till he send forth judgment unto victory.
ct: He won't snap a bruised reed or snuff out a smoldering wick, until he brings justice to victory.

kjv: And in his name shall the Gentiles trust.
ct: And in his name, the nations will put their trust."

kjv: Then was brought unto him one possessed with a devil, blind, and dumb: and he healed him, insomuch that the blind and dumb both spake and saw.
ct: Then people brought Jesus a man who was demon-possessed, blind, and unable to speak. Jesus healed him, and the man could both talk and see.

kjv: And all the people were amazed, and said, Is not this the son of David?
ct: The whole crowd was stunned. "Could this really be the Son of David?" they asked.

kjv: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils, but by Beelzebub the prince of the devils.
ct: But when the Pharisees heard this, they said, "He only drives out demons by the power of Beelzebub, the prince of demons."

kjv: And Jesus knew their thoughts, and said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand:
ct: Jesus knew what they were thinking and said to them, "Any kingdom torn apart by civil war collapses. Any city or household divided against itself won't survive.

kjv: And if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself; how shall then his kingdom stand?
ct: If Satan is driving out Satan, he's fighting against himself. How could his kingdom possibly stand?

kjv: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast them out? therefore they shall be your judges.
ct: And if I drive out demons by Beelzebub, then by whose power do your own followers drive them out? They'll be the ones to judge you for saying that.

kjv: But if I cast out devils by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God is come unto you.
ct: But if I'm driving out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has arrived right at your doorstep.

kjv: Or else how can one enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man? and then he will spoil his house.
ct: Think about it — how can anyone break into a strong man's house and steal his possessions unless he first ties up the strong man? Only then can he ransack the house.

kjv: He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.
ct: Anyone who isn't with me is against me, and anyone who doesn't gather with me is scattering.

kjv: Wherefore I say unto you, All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men: but the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost shall not be forgiven unto men.
ct: So I'm telling you this: every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven, but blasphemy against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.

kjv: And whosoever speaketh a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this world, neither in the world to come.
ct: Anyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man can be forgiven. But anyone who speaks against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven — not in this age, and not in the age to come.

kjv: Either make the tree good, and his fruit good; or else make the tree corrupt, and his fruit corrupt: for the tree is known by his fruit.
ct: A tree is identified by its fruit. If the tree is good, its fruit will be good. If the tree is rotten, its fruit will be rotten.

kjv: O generation of vipers, how can ye, being evil, speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.
ct: You brood of vipers! How can evil people like you say anything good? The mouth speaks whatever fills the heart.

kjv: A good man out of the good treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.
ct: A good person draws good things from the good stored up inside them. An evil person draws evil things from the evil stored up inside them.

kjv: But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.
ct: I'm warning you: on the day of judgment, people will have to answer for every careless word they've spoken.

kjv: For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.
ct: Your own words will either prove you innocent or prove you guilty."

kjv: Then certain of the scribes and of the Pharisees answered, saying, Master, we would see a sign from thee.
ct: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to him, "Teacher, we want to see you perform a miraculous sign."

kjv: But he answered and said unto them, An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas:
ct: Jesus answered, "An evil and unfaithful generation demands a sign, but the only sign it will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.

kjv: For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale’s belly; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
ct: Just as Jonah spent 3 days and 3 nights in the belly of a great fish, the Son of Man will spend 3 days and 3 nights in the heart of the earth.

kjv: The men of Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.
ct: On judgment day, the people of Nineveh will stand up and condemn this generation, because they repented when Jonah preached to them — and someone far greater than Jonah is here now.

kjv: The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for she came from the uttermost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.
ct: On judgment day, the Queen of the South will rise up and condemn this generation, because she traveled from the ends of the earth to hear Solomon's wisdom — and someone far greater than Solomon is here now.

kjv: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none.
ct: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry, barren places looking for rest but finds none.

kjv: Then he saith, I will return into my house from whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished.
ct: Then it says, 'I'll go back to the house I left.' When it returns, it finds the place empty, swept clean, and neatly arranged.

kjv: Then goeth he, and taketh with himself seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this wicked generation.
ct: So it goes and brings along 7 other spirits even more wicked than itself. They all move in and settle there, and that person ends up worse off than before. That's exactly what will happen to this wicked generation."

kjv: While he yet talked to the people, behold, his mother and his brethren stood without, desiring to speak with him.
ct: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowd, his mother and brothers arrived and stood outside, wanting to talk to him.

kjv: Then one said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to speak with thee.
ct: Someone told him, "Your mother and brothers are standing outside, asking to speak with you."

kjv: But he answered and said unto him that told him, Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?
ct: Jesus replied to the one who told him, "Who is my mother? And who are my brothers?"

kjv: And he stretched forth his hand toward his disciples, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
ct: Then he gestured toward his disciples and said, "Here are my mother and my brothers!

kjv: For whosoever shall do the will of my Father which is in heaven, the same is my brother, and sister, and mother.
ct: Anyone who does the will of my Father in heaven — that person is my brother, my sister, and my mother."

kjv: The same day went Jesus out of the house, and sat by the sea side.
ct: That same day, Jesus left the house and went to sit by the lake.

kjv: And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, so that he went into a ship, and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.
ct: Such a huge crowd gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat down, while the entire crowd stood along the shore.

kjv: And he spake many things unto them in parables, saying, Behold, a sower went forth to sow;
ct: He taught them many things through parables. He said, "Listen — a farmer went out to plant his seeds.

kjv: And when he sowed, some seeds fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up:
ct: As he scattered them, some seeds fell along the path, and birds came and ate them up.

kjv: Some fell upon stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth:
ct: Some fell on rocky ground where the soil was thin. They sprouted quickly because the soil was so shallow.

kjv: And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.
ct: But when the sun came up, the young plants were scorched. Since they had no real roots, they shriveled and died.

kjv: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them:
ct: Other seeds fell among thorn bushes, and the thorns grew up and choked them out.

kjv: But other fell into good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.
ct: But some seeds fell on good soil and produced a harvest — some 100 times what was planted, some 60 times, and some 30 times.

kjv: Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
ct: If you have ears to hear, then listen carefully!"

kjv: And the disciples came, and said unto him, Why speakest thou unto them in parables?
ct: Later, the disciples came to him and asked, "Why do you teach the crowds using parables?"

kjv: He answered and said unto them, Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.
ct: He answered, "You've been given the ability to understand the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but they haven't.

kjv: For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.
ct: Whoever already has understanding will be given even more, until they have an abundance. But whoever lacks it will lose even the little they have.

kjv: Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.
ct: That's why I speak to them in parables — because even though they look, they don't really see, and even though they listen, they don't really hear or understand.

kjv: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive:
ct: In them, the prophecy of Isaiah is coming true: 'You will hear and hear but never understand. You will look and look but never truly see.

kjv: For this people’s heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.
ct: For this people's hearts have grown hard. Their ears can barely hear, and they've shut their eyes — so they won't see with their eyes, hear with their ears, or understand with their hearts, and turn back to me so I could heal them.'

kjv: But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.
ct: "But how blessed are your eyes, because they see! And your ears, because they hear!

kjv: For verily I say unto you, That many prophets and righteous men have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.
ct: I tell you the truth: many prophets and righteous people longed to see what you're seeing and never saw it, and to hear what you're hearing and never heard it.

kjv: Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower.
ct: "Now listen to what the parable of the farmer means.

kjv: When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, then cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side.
ct: When someone hears the message about the kingdom but doesn't understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was planted in their heart. This is the seed that fell along the path.

kjv: But he that received the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it;
ct: The seed that fell on rocky ground represents someone who hears the message and immediately receives it with joy.

kjv: Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.
ct: But it never takes deep root in them, so it only lasts a short time. The moment trouble or persecution comes because of the message, they quickly fall away.

kjv: He also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word; and the care of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful.
ct: The seed that fell among thorns represents someone who hears the message, but the worries of life and the seductive pull of wealth choke it out, and it never produces anything.

kjv: But he that received seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word, and understandeth it; which also beareth fruit, and bringeth forth, some an hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
ct: But the seed that fell on good soil represents someone who hears the message and truly understands it. That person produces a harvest — some 100 times, some 60 times, and some 30 times what was planted."

kjv: Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field:
ct: Jesus told them another parable: "The kingdom of heaven is like a man who planted good seed in his field.

kjv: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.
ct: But while everyone was sleeping, his enemy came and scattered weeds among the wheat, then slipped away.

kjv: But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.
ct: When the wheat sprouted and began forming grain, the weeds appeared too.

kjv: So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?
ct: The owner's servants came to him and said, 'Sir, didn't you plant good seed in your field? Where did all these weeds come from?'

kjv: He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?
ct: He told them, 'An enemy did this.' The servants asked, 'Do you want us to go pull them up?'

kjv: But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.
ct: "'No,' he said, 'because if you pull up the weeds, you might uproot the wheat along with them.

kjv: Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them: but gather the wheat into my barn.
ct: Let them both grow together until harvest time. Then I'll tell the harvesters: First, collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned. Then gather the wheat into my barn.'"

kjv: Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field:
ct: He told them yet another parable: "The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man planted in his field.

kjv: Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof.
ct: It's the smallest of all seeds, but when it grows, it becomes the largest of garden plants — it turns into a tree so big that birds come and nest in its branches."

kjv: Another parable spake he unto them; The kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.
ct: He shared another parable with them: "The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman mixed into 3 measures of flour until it worked all the way through the dough."

kjv: All these things spake Jesus unto the multitude in parables; and without a parable spake he not unto them:
ct: Jesus spoke all of these things to the crowds in parables. In fact, he didn't teach them anything without using a parable.

kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying, I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things which have been kept secret from the foundation of the world.
ct: This fulfilled what the prophet had spoken: "I will speak in parables. I will reveal things that have been hidden since the creation of the world."

kjv: Then Jesus sent the multitude away, and went into the house: and his disciples came unto him, saying, Declare unto us the parable of the tares of the field.
ct: Then Jesus sent the crowds away and went back into the house. His disciples came to him and said, "Explain the parable of the weeds in the field to us."

kjv: He answered and said unto them, He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man;
ct: He answered, "The one who plants the good seed is the Son of Man.

kjv: The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the tares are the children of the wicked one;
ct: The field is the world. The good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom, and the weeds are the people who belong to the evil one.

kjv: The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels.
ct: The enemy who planted the weeds is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age, and the harvesters are angels.

kjv: As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world.
ct: Just as the weeds are pulled up and burned in the fire, that's how it will be at the end of the age.

kjv: The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend, and them which do iniquity;
ct: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will remove from his kingdom everything that causes sin and everyone who does evil.

kjv: And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
ct: They will throw them into the blazing furnace, where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.

kjv: Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
ct: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Father's kingdom. If you have ears to hear, then listen!

kjv: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.
ct: "The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man found it, he hid it again, and then — overjoyed — he went and sold everything he owned and bought that field.

kjv: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a merchant man, seeking goodly pearls:
ct: "The kingdom of heaven is also like a merchant searching for fine pearls.

kjv: Who, when he had found one pearl of great price, went and sold all that he had, and bought it.
ct: When he discovered one pearl of incredible value, he went and sold everything he had and bought it.

kjv: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and gathered of every kind:
ct: "The kingdom of heaven is also like a large net thrown into the sea that caught all kinds of fish.

kjv: Which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.
ct: When it was full, the fishermen pulled it up onto the shore. They sat down and sorted the good fish into containers but threw the bad ones away.

kjv: So shall it be at the end of the world: the angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the just,
ct: That's how it will be at the end of the age. The angels will come and separate the wicked from the righteous,

kjv: And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
ct: and throw them into the blazing furnace, where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth."

kjv: Jesus saith unto them, Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him, Yea, Lord.
ct: Jesus asked them, "Do you understand all of this?" They answered, "Yes, Lord."

kjv: Then said he unto them, Therefore every scribe which is instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old.
ct: He said to them, "That's why every teacher of the law who has become a student of the kingdom of heaven is like a homeowner who brings both new and old treasures out of his storeroom."

kjv: And it came to pass, that when Jesus had finished these parables, he departed thence.
ct: When Jesus finished telling these parables, he left that area.

kjv: And when he was come into his own country, he taught them in their synagogue, insomuch that they were astonished, and said, Whence hath this man this wisdom, and these mighty works?
ct: He went to his hometown and began teaching in their synagogue. The people were amazed and said, "Where did this man get such wisdom and the ability to do these miracles?

kjv: Is not this the carpenter’s son? is not his mother called Mary? and his brethren, James, and Joses, and Simon, and Judas?
ct: Isn't he the carpenter's son? Isn't his mother named Mary? Aren't James, Joses, Simon, and Judas his brothers?

kjv: And his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence then hath this man all these things?
ct: And don't all his sisters live right here among us? So where did he get all of this?"

kjv: And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.
ct: They took offense at him. But Jesus told them, "A prophet is honored everywhere except in his own hometown and his own family."

kjv: And he did not many mighty works there because of their unbelief.
ct: He didn't do many miracles there because of their unbelief.

kjv: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard of the fame of Jesus,
ct: Around that time, Herod the tetrarch heard reports about Jesus.

kjv: And said unto his servants, This is John the Baptist; he is risen from the dead; and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.
ct: He told his servants, "This must be John the Baptist — he's risen from the dead! That's why these miraculous powers are at work in him."

kjv: For Herod had laid hold on John, and bound him, and put him in prison for Herodias’ sake, his brother Philip’s wife.
ct: Herod was the one who had arrested John, chained him up, and thrown him in prison. He did it because of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife.

kjv: For John said unto him, It is not lawful for thee to have her.
ct: John had confronted Herod directly, telling him, "It's not lawful for you to have her."

kjv: And when he would have put him to death, he feared the multitude, because they counted him as a prophet.
ct: Herod wanted to kill John, but he was afraid of the people, because they considered John a prophet.

kjv: But when Herod’s birthday was kept, the daughter of Herodias danced before them, and pleased Herod.
ct: On Herod's birthday, the daughter of Herodias danced for the guests and delighted Herod.

kjv: Whereupon he promised with an oath to give her whatsoever she would ask.
ct: He was so pleased that he swore an oath to give her whatever she asked for.

kjv: And she, being before instructed of her mother, said, Give me here John Baptist’s head in a charger.
ct: Her mother had already coached her on what to say, so the girl told him, "Give me the head of John the Baptist on a platter."

kjv: And the king was sorry: nevertheless for the oath’s sake, and them which sat with him at meat, he commanded it to be given her.
ct: The king was deeply troubled by this, but because of his oath — and because his dinner guests had heard him make it — he ordered it done.

kjv: And he sent, and beheaded John in the prison.
ct: He sent soldiers to the prison, and they beheaded John.

kjv: And his head was brought in a charger, and given to the damsel: and she brought it to her mother.
ct: His head was brought in on a platter and given to the girl, and she carried it to her mother.

kjv: And his disciples came, and took up the body, and buried it, and went and told Jesus.
ct: John's disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.

kjv: When Jesus heard of it, he departed thence by ship into a desert place apart: and when the people had heard thereof, they followed him on foot out of the cities.
ct: When Jesus heard the news, he got in a boat and withdrew to a remote place to be alone. But when the crowds found out, they followed him on foot from the surrounding towns.

kjv: And Jesus went forth, and saw a great multitude, and was moved with compassion toward them, and he healed their sick.
ct: Jesus stepped ashore and saw the massive crowd. His heart went out to them, and he healed those among them who were sick.

kjv: And when it was evening, his disciples came to him, saying, This is a desert place, and the time is now past; send the multitude away, that they may go into the villages, and buy themselves victuals.
ct: As evening approached, his disciples came to him and said, "This place is remote, and it's getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go to the villages and buy food for themselves."

kjv: But Jesus said unto them, They need not depart; give ye them to eat.
ct: But Jesus told them, "They don't need to leave. You give them something to eat."

kjv: And they say unto him, We have here but five loaves, and two fishes.
ct: They replied, "All we have here is 5 loaves of bread and 2 fish."

kjv: He said, Bring them hither to me.
ct: "Bring them to me," he said.

kjv: And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the grass, and took the five loaves, and the two fishes, and looking up to heaven, he blessed, and brake, and gave the loaves to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.
ct: He told the crowds to sit down on the grass. Then he took the 5 loaves and the 2 fish, looked up toward heaven, and gave thanks. He broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they distributed it to the crowds.

kjv: And they did all eat, and were filled: and they took up of the fragments that remained twelve baskets full.
ct: Everyone ate until they were full, and when they collected the leftovers, there were 12 baskets full of broken pieces.

kjv: And they that had eaten were about five thousand men, beside women and children.
ct: About 5,000 men had eaten — and that's not counting the women and children.

kjv: And straightway Jesus constrained his disciples to get into a ship, and to go before him unto the other side, while he sent the multitudes away.
ct: Right away, Jesus insisted that his disciples get into the boat and go ahead of him to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.

kjv: And when he had sent the multitudes away, he went up into a mountain apart to pray: and when the evening was come, he was there alone.
ct: After he had sent everyone away, he went up on a mountainside by himself to pray. When evening came, he was there alone.

kjv: But the ship was now in the midst of the sea, tossed with waves: for the wind was contrary.
ct: Meanwhile, the boat was already far out on the lake, battered by the waves because the wind was blowing against them.

kjv: And in the fourth watch of the night Jesus went unto them, walking on the sea.
ct: Sometime between 3 and 6 in the morning, Jesus came toward them, walking on the water.

kjv: And when the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, It is a spirit; and they cried out for fear.
ct: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified. "It's a ghost!" they screamed, crying out in fear.

kjv: But straightway Jesus spake unto them, saying, Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid.
ct: But Jesus immediately called out to them, "Take courage! It's me. Don't be afraid."

kjv: And Peter answered him and said, Lord, if it be thou, bid me come unto thee on the water.
ct: Peter spoke up: "Lord, if it's really you, tell me to come to you on the water."

kjv: And he said, Come. And when Peter was come down out of the ship, he walked on the water, to go to Jesus.
ct: "Come," Jesus said. So Peter climbed out of the boat and walked on the water toward Jesus.

kjv: But when he saw the wind boisterous, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried, saying, Lord, save me.
ct: But when he noticed how fierce the wind was, fear overtook him. He started to sink and cried out, "Lord, save me!"

kjv: And immediately Jesus stretched forth his hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?
ct: Instantly, Jesus reached out his hand and caught him. "You have so little faith," he said. "Why did you doubt?"

kjv: And when they were come into the ship, the wind ceased.
ct: As soon as they climbed into the boat, the wind died down.

kjv: Then they that were in the ship came and worshipped him, saying, Of a truth thou art the Son of God.
ct: The men in the boat fell down and worshiped him, saying, "You truly are the Son of God!"

kjv: And when they were gone over, they came into the land of Gennesaret.
ct: They crossed the lake and landed at Gennesaret.

kjv: And when the men of that place had knowledge of him, they sent out into all that country round about, and brought unto him all that were diseased;
ct: When the people there recognized Jesus, they sent word throughout the entire region, and everyone brought their sick to him.

kjv: And besought him that they might only touch the hem of his garment: and as many as touched were made perfectly whole.
ct: They begged him to let the sick just touch the edge of his robe — and everyone who touched it was completely healed.

kjv: And after six days Jesus taketh Peter, James, and John his brother, and bringeth them up into an high mountain apart,
ct: 6 days later, Jesus took Peter, James, and James's brother John and led them up a high mountain where they could be alone.

kjv: And was transfigured before them: and his face did shine as the sun, and his raiment was white as the light.
ct: There, his appearance was completely transformed right before their eyes. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light itself.

kjv: And, behold, there appeared unto them Moses and Elias talking with him.
ct: Suddenly, Moses and Elijah appeared and began talking with Jesus.

kjv: Then answered Peter, and said unto Jesus, Lord, it is good for us to be here: if thou wilt, let us make here three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.
ct: Peter spoke up and said to Jesus, "Lord, it's wonderful that we're here! If you'd like, I'll build 3 shelters — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah."

kjv: While he yet spake, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them: and behold a voice out of the cloud, which said, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear ye him.
ct: While Peter was still speaking, a bright cloud covered them, and a voice from the cloud said, "This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Listen to him."

kjv: And when the disciples heard it, they fell on their face, and were sore afraid.
ct: When the disciples heard this, they fell facedown on the ground, terrified.

kjv: And Jesus came and touched them, and said, Arise, and be not afraid.
ct: Jesus came over, touched them, and said, "Get up. Don't be afraid."

kjv: And when they had lifted up their eyes, they saw no man, save Jesus only.
ct: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus standing there alone.

kjv: And as they came down from the mountain, Jesus charged them, saying, Tell the vision to no man, until the Son of man be risen again from the dead.
ct: As they made their way down the mountain, Jesus gave them strict orders: "Don't tell anyone what you saw until the Son of Man has risen from the dead."

kjv: And his disciples asked him, saying, Why then say the scribes that Elias must first come?
ct: His disciples asked him, "Then why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"

kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Elias truly shall first come, and restore all things.
ct: Jesus replied, "Elijah does come first, and he will restore all things."

kjv: But I say unto you, That Elias is come already, and they knew him not, but have done unto him whatsoever they listed. Likewise shall also the Son of man suffer of them.
ct: "But I'm telling you, Elijah has already come. People didn't recognize him and did whatever they wanted to him. In the same way, the Son of Man is going to suffer at their hands."

kjv: Then the disciples understood that he spake unto them of John the Baptist.
ct: Then the disciples realized he was talking about John the Baptist.

kjv: And when they were come to the multitude, there came to him a certain man, kneeling down to him, and saying,
ct: When they reached the crowd, a man came and knelt before Jesus.

kjv: Lord, have mercy on my son: for he is lunatick, and sore vexed: for ofttimes he falleth into the fire, and oft into the water.
ct: "Lord, have mercy on my son," he said. "He has seizures and suffers terribly. He keeps falling into the fire or into the water."

kjv: And I brought him to thy disciples, and they could not cure him.
ct: "I brought him to your disciples, but they couldn't heal him."

kjv: Then Jesus answered and said, O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him hither to me.
ct: Jesus answered, "What a faithless and twisted generation! How long must I stay with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring the boy to me."

kjv: And Jesus rebuked the devil; and he departed out of him: and the child was cured from that very hour.
ct: Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of the boy. From that moment on, the child was completely healed.

kjv: Then came the disciples to Jesus apart, and said, Why could not we cast him out?
ct: Afterward, the disciples came to Jesus privately and asked, "Why couldn't we drive it out?"

kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Because of your unbelief: for verily I say unto you, If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove; and nothing shall be impossible unto you.
ct: He told them, "Because of your unbelief. I tell you the truth — if you have faith even as small as a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it would move. Nothing would be impossible for you."

kjv: Howbeit this kind goeth not out but by prayer and fasting.
ct: "But this kind of demon only comes out through prayer and fasting."

kjv: And while they abode in Galilee, Jesus said unto them, The Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of men:
ct: While they were gathered together in Galilee, Jesus told them, "The Son of Man is about to be handed over to men."

kjv: And they shall kill him, and the third day he shall be raised again. And they were exceeding sorry.
ct: "They will kill him, and on the 3rd day he will be raised back to life." The disciples were overwhelmed with grief.

kjv: And when they were come to Capernaum, they that received tribute money came to Peter, and said, Doth not your master pay tribute?
ct: When they arrived in Capernaum, the collectors of the temple tax approached Peter and asked, "Doesn't your teacher pay the temple tax?"

kjv: He saith, Yes. And when he was come into the house, Jesus prevented him, saying, What thinkest thou, Simon? of whom do the kings of the earth take custom or tribute? of their own children, or of strangers?
ct: "Yes, he does," Peter answered. When Peter came into the house, Jesus spoke first: "What do you think, Simon? Who do the kings of the earth collect taxes and duties from — their own children or from others?"

kjv: Peter saith unto him, Of strangers. Jesus saith unto him, Then are the children free.
ct: "From others," Peter replied. Jesus said, "Then the children are exempt."

kjv: Notwithstanding, lest we should offend them, go thou to the sea, and cast an hook, and take up the fish that first cometh up; and when thou hast opened his mouth, thou shalt find a piece of money: that take, and give unto them for me and thee.
ct: "But so we don't cause an offense, go to the lake and cast a hook. Open the mouth of the first fish you catch, and you'll find a coin inside. Take it and pay the tax for both of us."

kjv: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said unto his disciples,
ct: When Jesus had finished saying all these things, he told his disciples,

kjv: Ye know that after two days is the feast of the passover, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.
ct: "You know that the Passover is 2 days away, and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified."

kjv: Then assembled together the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders of the people, unto the palace of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas,
ct: Around that same time, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders of the people gathered at the palace of the high priest, a man named Caiaphas.

kjv: And consulted that they might take Jesus by subtilty, and kill him.
ct: They plotted together to arrest Jesus through some kind of trick and have him killed.

kjv: But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar among the people.
ct: But they agreed, "Not during the feast — the people might riot."

kjv: Now when Jesus was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,
ct: While Jesus was in Bethany, staying at the home of Simon the leper,

kjv: There came unto him a woman having an alabaster box of very precious ointment, and poured it on his head, as he sat at meat.
ct: a woman came to him carrying an alabaster jar filled with extremely expensive ointment. As he sat at the table, she poured it over his head.

kjv: But when his disciples saw it, they had indignation, saying, To what purpose is this waste?
ct: When the disciples saw this, they were angry. "What a waste!" they said.

kjv: For this ointment might have been sold for much, and given to the poor.
ct: "That ointment could have been sold for a fortune and the money given to the poor."

kjv: When Jesus understood it, he said unto them, Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me.
ct: Jesus knew what they were saying and told them, "Why are you giving this woman a hard time? She has done something beautiful for me.

kjv: For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have not always.
ct: The poor will always be among you, but you won't always have me.

kjv: For in that she hath poured this ointment on my body, she did it for my burial.
ct: By pouring this ointment on my body, she has prepared me for burial.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memorial of her.
ct: I tell you the truth: wherever this gospel is preached throughout the whole world, what this woman has done will be told in her memory."

kjv: Then one of the twelve, called Judas Iscariot, went unto the chief priests,
ct: Then one of the 12 disciples, Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.

kjv: And said unto them, What will ye give me, and I will deliver him unto you? And they covenanted with him for thirty pieces of silver.
ct: He asked them, "What will you pay me to hand him over to you?" They counted out 30 pieces of silver for him.

kjv: And from that time he sought opportunity to betray him.
ct: From that moment on, Judas looked for the right opportunity to betray Jesus.

kjv: Now the first day of the feast of unleavened bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the passover?
ct: On the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and asked, "Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal for you?"

kjv: And he said, Go into the city to such a man, and say unto him, The Master saith, My time is at hand; I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples.
ct: He said, "Go into the city to a certain man and tell him, 'The Teacher says: My time has come. I will celebrate the Passover at your house with my disciples.'"

kjv: And the disciples did as Jesus had appointed them; and they made ready the passover.
ct: The disciples did as Jesus instructed and prepared the Passover meal.

kjv: Now when the even was come, he sat down with the twelve.
ct: When evening came, Jesus sat down at the table with the 12 disciples.

kjv: And as they did eat, he said, Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
ct: While they were eating, he said, "I tell you the truth: one of you is going to betray me."

kjv: And they were exceeding sorrowful, and began every one of them to say unto him, Lord, is it I?
ct: They were deeply upset, and one after another they asked him, "Lord, it isn't me, is it?"

kjv: And he answered and said, He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, the same shall betray me.
ct: He answered, "The one who dipped his hand into the bowl with me — he's the one who will betray me.

kjv: The Son of man goeth as it is written of him: but woe unto that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! it had been good for that man if he had not been born.
ct: The Son of Man will go just as the Scriptures say he will. But how terrible it will be for the man who betrays the Son of Man! It would have been better for that man if he had never been born."

kjv: Then Judas, which betrayed him, answered and said, Master, is it I? He said unto him, Thou hast said.
ct: Then Judas, the one who would betray him, asked, "Rabbi, it isn't me, is it?" Jesus replied, "You said it yourself."

kjv: And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, and blessed it, and brake it, and gave it to the disciples, and said, Take, eat; this is my body.
ct: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, gave thanks, broke it, and handed it to his disciples. "Take this and eat it," he said. "This is my body."

kjv: And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink ye all of it;
ct: Then he took a cup, gave thanks, and passed it to them. "All of you, drink from it," he said.

kjv: For this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many for the remission of sins.
ct: "This is my blood of the new covenant, poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins.

kjv: But I say unto you, I will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father’s kingdom.
ct: I'm telling you, I will not drink this fruit of the vine again until the day I drink it new with you in my Father's kingdom."

kjv: And when they had sung an hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.
ct: After singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

kjv: Then saith Jesus unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad.
ct: Then Jesus told them, "Tonight, every one of you will fall away because of me. For the Scriptures say, 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.'

kjv: But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.
ct: But after I have been raised from the dead, I will go ahead of you to Galilee."

kjv: Peter answered and said unto him, Though all men shall be offended because of thee, yet will I never be offended.
ct: Peter spoke up and said, "Even if everyone else falls away because of you, I never will."

kjv: Jesus said unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.
ct: Jesus said to him, "I tell you the truth: tonight, before the rooster crows, you will deny me 3 times."

kjv: Peter said unto him, Though I should die with thee, yet will I not deny thee. Likewise also said all the disciples.
ct: Peter declared, "Even if I have to die with you, I will never deny you." All the other disciples said the same thing.

kjv: Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called Gethsemane, and saith unto the disciples, Sit ye here, while I go and pray yonder.
ct: Then Jesus went with them to a place called Gethsemane. He told his disciples, "Sit here while I go over there to pray."

kjv: And he took with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and began to be sorrowful and very heavy.
ct: He took Peter and the 2 sons of Zebedee with him, and deep sorrow and anguish came over him.

kjv: Then saith he unto them, My soul is exceeding sorrowful, even unto death: tarry ye here, and watch with me.
ct: He said to them, "My soul is crushed with grief — to the point of death. Stay here and keep watch with me."

kjv: And he went a little farther, and fell on his face, and prayed, saying, O my Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me: nevertheless not as I will, but as thou wilt.
ct: Going a little farther, he fell face down and prayed, "My Father, if it's possible, let this cup pass from me. Yet not what I want, but what you want."

kjv: And he cometh unto the disciples, and findeth them asleep, and saith unto Peter, What, could ye not watch with me one hour?
ct: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, "Couldn't you stay awake with me for just 1 hour?

kjv: Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation: the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.
ct: Stay awake and pray so that you don't fall into temptation. The spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak."

kjv: He went away again the second time, and prayed, saying, O my Father, if this cup may not pass away from me, except I drink it, thy will be done.
ct: He went away a second time and prayed, "My Father, if this cup cannot pass unless I drink it, your will be done."

kjv: And he came and found them asleep again: for their eyes were heavy.
ct: He came back and found them sleeping again — their eyes were too heavy to stay open.

kjv: And he left them, and went away again, and prayed the third time, saying the same words.
ct: So he left them once more, went away, and prayed a third time, saying the same words.

kjv: Then cometh he to his disciples, and saith unto them, Sleep on now, and take your rest: behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
ct: Then he returned to his disciples and said, "Are you still sleeping and resting? Look — the hour has come, and the Son of Man is being betrayed into the hands of sinners.

kjv: Rise, let us be going: behold, he is at hand that doth betray me.
ct: Get up, let's go. My betrayer is here."

kjv: And while he yet spake, lo, Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and elders of the people.
ct: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas — one of the 12 — arrived with a large mob carrying swords and clubs, sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.

kjv: Now he that betrayed him gave them a sign, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is he: hold him fast.
ct: Judas had arranged a signal with them: "The one I kiss is the man you want. Grab him."

kjv: And forthwith he came to Jesus, and said, Hail, master; and kissed him.
ct: He went straight to Jesus and said, "Greetings, Rabbi!" and kissed him.

kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Friend, wherefore art thou come? Then came they, and laid hands on Jesus, and took him.
ct: Jesus said to him, "Friend, do what you came to do." Then the men stepped forward, seized Jesus, and arrested him.

kjv: And, behold, one of them which were with Jesus stretched out his hand, and drew his sword, and struck a servant of the high priest’s, and smote off his ear.
ct: Suddenly, one of those with Jesus reached for his sword, drew it, and struck the high priest's servant, cutting off his ear.

kjv: Then said Jesus unto him, Put up again thy sword into his place: for all they that take the sword shall perish with the sword.
ct: Jesus told him, "Put your sword back where it belongs. Everyone who lives by the sword will die by the sword.

kjv: Thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my Father, and he shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels?
ct: Don't you realize I could ask my Father right now, and he would instantly send more than 12 legions of angels?

kjv: But how then shall the scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be?
ct: But then how would the Scriptures be fulfilled? They say it must happen this way."

kjv: In that same hour said Jesus to the multitudes, Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me? I sat daily with you teaching in the temple, and ye laid no hold on me.
ct: Then Jesus spoke to the crowd: "Am I some kind of criminal that you had to come after me with swords and clubs? I sat teaching in the temple day after day, and you never arrested me.

kjv: But all this was done, that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples forsook him, and fled.
ct: But all of this is happening so that the writings of the prophets would be fulfilled." At that point, every one of his disciples abandoned him and ran.

kjv: And they that had laid hold on Jesus led him away to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were assembled.
ct: The men who arrested Jesus brought him to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and elders had already assembled.

kjv: But Peter followed him afar off unto the high priest’s palace, and went in, and sat with the servants, to see the end.
ct: Peter followed at a distance all the way to the high priest's courtyard. He went inside and sat down with the guards to see what would happen.

kjv: Now the chief priests, and elders, and all the council, sought false witness against Jesus, to put him to death;
ct: The chief priests, the elders, and the entire council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so they could sentence him to death.

kjv: But found none: yea, though many false witnesses came, yet found they none. At the last came two false witnesses,
ct: They couldn't find any, even though many false witnesses came forward. Finally, 2 men stepped up

kjv: And said, This fellow said, I am able to destroy the temple of God, and to build it in three days.
ct: and said, "This man claimed, 'I can destroy the temple of God and rebuild it in 3 days.'"

kjv: And the high priest arose, and said unto him, Answerest thou nothing? what is it which these witness against thee?
ct: The high priest stood up and demanded, "Aren't you going to answer? What do you have to say about these accusations?"

kjv: But Jesus held his peace. And the high priest answered and said unto him, I adjure thee by the living God, that thou tell us whether thou be the Christ, the Son of God.
ct: But Jesus remained silent. The high priest said to him, "I command you under oath by the living God — tell us if you are the Christ, the Son of God."

kjv: Jesus saith unto him, Thou hast said: nevertheless I say unto you, Hereafter shall ye see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.
ct: Jesus replied, "You have said so. But I tell you this: from now on you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven."

kjv: Then the high priest rent his clothes, saying, He hath spoken blasphemy; what further need have we of witnesses? behold, now ye have heard his blasphemy.
ct: At that, the high priest tore his robes and shouted, "Blasphemy! Why do we need any more witnesses? You've all just heard him blaspheme!

kjv: What think ye? They answered and said, He is guilty of death.
ct: What's your verdict?" They answered, "He deserves to die!"

kjv: Then did they spit in his face, and buffeted him; and others smote him with the palms of their hands,
ct: Then they spit in his face and punched him. Others slapped him,

kjv: Saying, Prophesy unto us, thou Christ, Who is he that smote thee?
ct: taunting, "Prophesy for us, Christ! Who just hit you?"

kjv: Now Peter sat without in the palace: and a damsel came unto him, saying, Thou also wast with Jesus of Galilee.
ct: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant girl came up to him and said, "You were with Jesus the Galilean too!"

kjv: But he denied before them all, saying, I know not what thou sayest.
ct: But he denied it in front of everyone. "I have no idea what you're talking about," he said.

kjv: And when he was gone out into the porch, another maid saw him, and said unto them that were there, This fellow was also with Jesus of Nazareth.
ct: When he moved out to the gateway, another servant girl noticed him and told the people standing there, "This man was with Jesus of Nazareth."

kjv: And again he denied with an oath, I do not know the man.
ct: Again he denied it, this time with an oath: "I don't know the man!"

kjv: And after a while came unto him they that stood by, and said to Peter, Surely thou also art one of them; for thy speech bewrayeth thee.
ct: A little later, some bystanders came over to Peter and said, "You're definitely one of them — your accent gives you away."

kjv: Then began he to curse and to swear, saying, I know not the man. And immediately the cock crew.
ct: Then Peter began to curse and swear, "I don't know the man!" Immediately, the rooster crowed.

kjv: And Peter remembered the word of Jesus, which said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice. And he went out, and wept bitterly.
ct: Peter remembered what Jesus had told him: "Before the rooster crows, you will deny me 3 times." He went outside and wept bitterly.

kjv: And in those days, when the number of the disciples was multiplied, there arose a murmuring of the Grecians against the Hebrews, because their widows were neglected in the daily ministration.
ct: During this time, the number of disciples was growing rapidly. But a complaint arose from the Greek-speaking Jewish believers against the Hebrew-speaking ones, because the Greek-speaking widows were being overlooked when food was distributed each day.

kjv: Then the twelve called the multitude of the disciples unto them, and said, It is not reason that we should leave the word of God, and serve tables.
ct: So the 12 apostles called all the disciples together and said, "It wouldn't be right for us to neglect the word of God in order to manage food distribution.

kjv: Wherefore, brethren, look ye out among you seven men of honest report, full of the Holy Ghost and wisdom, whom we may appoint over this business.
ct: So, brothers, choose 7 men from among you who are well respected and full of the Holy Spirit and wisdom. We'll put them in charge of this responsibility.

kjv: But we will give ourselves continually to prayer, and to the ministry of the word.
ct: That way, we can devote ourselves fully to prayer and to teaching the word."

kjv: And the saying pleased the whole multitude: and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Ghost, and Philip, and Prochorus, and Nicanor, and Timon, and Parmenas, and Nicolas a proselyte of Antioch:
ct: Everyone was pleased with this idea. They chose Stephen, a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit, along with Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolas — a convert to Judaism from Antioch.

kjv: Whom they set before the apostles: and when they had prayed, they laid their hands on them.
ct: They brought these men before the apostles, who prayed over them and laid their hands on them.

kjv: And the word of God increased; and the number of the disciples multiplied in Jerusalem greatly; and a great company of the priests were obedient to the faith.
ct: The word of God kept spreading, and the number of disciples in Jerusalem grew dramatically. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the message.

kjv: And Stephen, full of faith and power, did great wonders and miracles among the people.
ct: Stephen, full of faith and power, performed incredible wonders and miraculous signs among the people.

kjv: Then there arose certain of the synagogue, which is called the synagogue of the Libertines, and Cyrenians, and Alexandrians, and of them of Cilicia and of Asia, disputing with Stephen.
ct: But some men from the synagogue known as the Freedmen — including Jews from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia — stood up and began arguing with Stephen.

kjv: And they were not able to resist the wisdom and the spirit by which he spake.
ct: They couldn't hold their own against the wisdom and the Spirit that guided his words.

kjv: Then they suborned men, which said, We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses, and against God.
ct: So they secretly paid men to claim, "We've heard him speak blasphemous things against Moses and against God."

kjv: And they stirred up the people, and the elders, and the scribes, and came upon him, and caught him, and brought him to the council,
ct: This stirred up the people, the elders, and the scribes. They rushed at Stephen, seized him, and dragged him before the council.

kjv: And set up false witnesses, which said, This man ceaseth not to speak blasphemous words against this holy place, and the law:
ct: They brought in false witnesses who testified, "This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the law.

kjv: For we have heard him say, that this Jesus of Nazareth shall destroy this place, and shall change the customs which Moses delivered us.
ct: We've heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs that Moses handed down to us."

kjv: And all that sat in the council, looking stedfastly on him, saw his face as it had been the face of an angel.
ct: Everyone sitting in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.

kjv: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I have given order to the churches of Galatia, even so do ye.
ct: Now, about the collection for God's people: follow the same instructions I gave to the churches in Galatia.

kjv: Upon the first day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as God hath prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come.
ct: On the first day of every week, each of you should set aside some money in proportion to how God has blessed you. That way, no one will need to scramble to collect funds when I arrive.

kjv: And when I come, whomsoever ye shall approve by your letters, them will I send to bring your liberality unto Jerusalem.
ct: When I get there, I'll send the people you approve — along with letters of introduction — to deliver your generous gift to Jerusalem.

kjv: And if it be meet that I go also, they shall go with me.
ct: If it seems right for me to go as well, they can travel with me.

kjv: Now I will come unto you, when I shall pass through Macedonia: for I do pass through Macedonia.
ct: I plan to visit you after I pass through Macedonia, since that's the route I'm taking.

kjv: And it may be that I will abide, yea, and winter with you, that ye may bring me on my journey whithersoever I go.
ct: I may end up staying with you for a while — possibly even through the winter — so that you can help send me on my way to wherever I go next.

kjv: For I will not see you now by the way; but I trust to tarry a while with you, if the Lord permit.
ct: I don't want to see you just in passing. I'm hoping to spend real time with you, if the Lord allows it.

kjv: But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.
ct: For now, I'll stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,

kjv: For a great door and effectual is opened unto me, and there are many adversaries.
ct: because a great and promising door of opportunity has opened for me here — though there are also many who oppose me.

kjv: Now if Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without fear: for he worketh the work of the Lord, as I also do.
ct: If Timothy comes to you, make sure he feels welcome and at ease, because he's doing the Lord's work just as I am.

kjv: Let no man therefore despise him: but conduct him forth in peace, that he may come unto me: for I look for him with the brethren.
ct: No one should look down on him. Send him off in peace so he can come back to me, because I'm expecting him along with the other brothers.

kjv: As touching our brother Apollos, I greatly desired him to come unto you with the brethren: but his will was not at all to come at this time; but he will come when he shall have convenient time.
ct: As for our brother Apollos, I strongly urged him to visit you with the other brothers, but he really didn't want to come right now. He'll come when the time is right.

kjv: Watch ye, stand fast in the faith, quit you like men, be strong.
ct: Stay alert! Stand firm in the faith! Be courageous! Be strong!

kjv: Let all your things be done with charity.
ct: Let everything you do be done with love.

kjv: I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and that they have addicted themselves to the ministry of the saints,)
ct: Brothers, you know that the household of Stephanas were the first believers in Achaia, and they've devoted themselves to serving God's people. I urge you —
