kjv: That ye submit yourselves unto such, and to every one that helpeth with us, and laboureth.
ct: follow the lead of people like them, and of everyone who works hard and serves alongside us.

kjv: I am glad of the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus: for that which was lacking on your part they have supplied.
ct: I'm so glad that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus came to me, because they made up for what was missing while you couldn't be here.

kjv: For they have refreshed my spirit and yours: therefore acknowledge ye them that are such.
ct: They refreshed my spirit and yours. Give people like them the recognition they deserve.

kjv: The churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.
ct: The churches in the province of Asia send you their greetings. Aquila and Priscilla send you warm greetings in the Lord, along with the church that meets in their house.

kjv: All the brethren greet you. Greet ye one another with an holy kiss.
ct: All the brothers and sisters here greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.

kjv: The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand.
ct: I, Paul, am writing this greeting with my own hand.

kjv: If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Maranatha.
ct: If anyone does not love the Lord Jesus Christ, let that person be cursed. Come, Lord!

kjv: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
ct: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

kjv: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.
ct: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.

kjv: Do we begin again to commend ourselves? or need we, as some others, epistles of commendation to you, or letters of commendation from you?
ct: Are we starting to promote ourselves again? Do we need letters of recommendation to you or from you, like some people do?

kjv: Ye are our epistle written in our hearts, known and read of all men:
ct: You yourselves are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.

kjv: Forasmuch as ye are manifestly declared to be the epistle of Christ ministered by us, written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in tables of stone, but in fleshy tables of the heart.
ct: It's clear that you are a letter from Christ, delivered through our ministry — written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on the living tablets of human hearts.

kjv: And such trust have we through Christ to God-ward:
ct: This is the kind of confidence we have before God through Christ.

kjv: Not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think any thing as of ourselves; but our sufficiency is of God;
ct: It's not that we're capable of doing any of this on our own — our ability comes from God.

kjv: Who also hath made us able ministers of the new testament; not of the letter, but of the spirit: for the letter killeth, but the spirit giveth life.
ct: He's the one who made us capable servants of a new covenant — not based on the written law, but on the Spirit. The written law brings death, but the Spirit gives life.

kjv: But if the ministration of death, written and engraven in stones, was glorious, so that the children of Israel could not stedfastly behold the face of Moses for the glory of his countenance; which glory was to be done away:
ct: Now, the ministry that brought death — its laws carved into stone — came with such glory that the people of Israel couldn't even look at Moses' face because it was shining so brightly, even though that glory was already fading.

kjv: How shall not the ministration of the spirit be rather glorious?
ct: So how much more glorious is the ministry of the Spirit?

kjv: For if the ministration of condemnation be glory, much more doth the ministration of righteousness exceed in glory.
ct: If the ministry that brought condemnation had glory, the ministry that brings righteousness overflows with even greater glory.

kjv: For even that which was made glorious had no glory in this respect, by reason of the glory that excelleth.
ct: In fact, what once seemed glorious has lost its glory entirely when compared to the glory that far surpasses it.

kjv: For if that which is done away was glorious, much more that which remaineth is glorious.
ct: If what was fading away came with glory, what remains forever carries an even greater glory.

kjv: Seeing then that we have such hope, we use great plainness of speech:
ct: Since we have this kind of hope, we speak with complete boldness.

kjv: And not as Moses, which put a vail over his face, that the children of Israel could not stedfastly look to the end of that which is abolished:
ct: We aren't like Moses, who put a veil over his face so the people of Israel couldn't watch the glory fade away to nothing.

kjv: But their minds were blinded: for until this day remaineth the same vail untaken away in the reading of the old testament; which vail is done away in Christ.
ct: But their minds were blinded. To this very day, when the old covenant is read aloud, that same veil remains — and it's only removed through Christ.

kjv: But even unto this day, when Moses is read, the vail is upon their heart.
ct: Even now, whenever the writings of Moses are read, a veil covers their hearts.

kjv: Nevertheless when it shall turn to the Lord, the vail shall be taken away.
ct: But whenever someone turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.

kjv: Now the Lord is that Spirit: and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.
ct: Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.

kjv: But we all, with open face beholding as in a glass the glory of the Lord, are changed into the same image from glory to glory, even as by the Spirit of the LORD.
ct: All of us, with unveiled faces, see the glory of the Lord as if reflected in a mirror — and we are being transformed into that same image, from one degree of glory to the next, by the Lord, who is the Spirit.

kjv: Paul and Timotheus, the servants of Jesus Christ, to all the saints in Christ Jesus which are at Philippi, with the bishops and deacons:
ct: From Paul and Timothy, servants of Jesus Christ — to all of God's people in Christ Jesus at Philippi, along with your bishops and deacons:

kjv: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
ct: May grace and peace be yours from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

kjv: I thank my God upon every remembrance of you,
ct: Every time I think of you, I thank my God.

kjv: Always in every prayer of mine for you all making request with joy,
ct: Whenever I pray for any of you, I do it with joy,

kjv: For your fellowship in the gospel from the first day until now;
ct: because you've been my partners in spreading the gospel from the very first day until now.

kjv: Being confident of this very thing, that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it until the day of Jesus Christ:
ct: I'm absolutely confident that God, who started this good work in you, will carry it through to completion by the day of Jesus Christ.

kjv: Even as it is meet for me to think this of you all, because I have you in my heart; inasmuch as both in my bonds, and in the defence and confirmation of the gospel, ye all are partakers of my grace.
ct: It's only right for me to feel this way about all of you, because you hold a special place in my heart. Whether I'm in chains or out defending and confirming the gospel, you've shared in God's grace right alongside me.

kjv: For God is my record, how greatly I long after you all in the bowels of Jesus Christ.
ct: God is my witness — I long for every one of you with the deep affection of Christ Jesus himself.

kjv: And this I pray, that your love may abound yet more and more in knowledge and in all judgment;
ct: Here's what I pray: that your love will keep growing and growing, hand in hand with knowledge and sharp discernment,

kjv: That ye may approve things that are excellent; that ye may be sincere and without offence till the day of Christ;
ct: so that you can recognize what truly matters and live with pure hearts, free from blame, until the day of Christ.

kjv: Being filled with the fruits of righteousness, which are by Jesus Christ, unto the glory and praise of God.
ct: May your lives overflow with the fruit of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ, bringing glory and praise to God.

kjv: But I would ye should understand, brethren, that the things which happened unto me have fallen out rather unto the furtherance of the gospel;
ct: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that everything that's happened to me has actually helped the gospel move forward.

kjv: So that my bonds in Christ are manifest in all the palace, and in all other places;
ct: The entire palace guard — and everyone else around — now knows that I'm in chains because of Christ.

kjv: And many of the brethren in the Lord, waxing confident by my bonds, are much more bold to speak the word without fear.
ct: On top of that, most of the believers here have grown bolder because of my imprisonment. They speak the word of God with more courage and without fear.

kjv: Some indeed preach Christ even of envy and strife; and some also of good will:
ct: It's true that some preach Christ out of jealousy and rivalry, but others do it with genuine goodwill.

kjv: The one preach Christ of contention, not sincerely, supposing to add affliction to my bonds:
ct: Those driven by selfish ambition preach Christ with impure motives, thinking they can pile more trouble on top of my chains.

kjv: But the other of love, knowing that I am set for the defence of the gospel.
ct: But the others preach out of love, knowing that God placed me here to defend the gospel.

kjv: What then? notwithstanding, every way, whether in pretence, or in truth, Christ is preached; and I therein do rejoice, yea, and will rejoice.
ct: So what does it matter? Either way — whether their motives are fake or sincere — Christ is being proclaimed. That makes me glad, and I'll keep on being glad.

kjv: For I know that this shall turn to my salvation through your prayer, and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,
ct: I know that through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ, all of this will lead to my salvation.

kjv: According to my earnest expectation and my hope, that in nothing I shall be ashamed, but that with all boldness, as always, so now also Christ shall be magnified in my body, whether it be by life, or by death.
ct: My deep hope and expectation is that I won't be put to shame in any way. Instead, with complete boldness — just as always — Christ will be honored through my body, whether I live or whether I die.

kjv: For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.
ct: For me, living means Christ, and dying is gain.

kjv: But if I live in the flesh, this is the fruit of my labour: yet what I shall choose I wot not.
ct: But if I go on living in this body, that means fruitful work for me. Honestly, I don't know which I would choose.

kjv: For I am in a strait betwixt two, having a desire to depart, and to be with Christ; which is far better:
ct: I'm torn between the two. Part of me longs to leave this life and be with Christ, which would be far better.

kjv: Nevertheless to abide in the flesh is more needful for you.
ct: But staying alive in this body is more necessary for your sake.

kjv: And having this confidence, I know that I shall abide and continue with you all for your furtherance and joy of faith;
ct: Since I'm convinced of this, I know I'll remain and continue with all of you, helping you grow and find joy in your faith.

kjv: That your rejoicing may be more abundant in Jesus Christ for me by my coming to you again.
ct: That way, when I come to you again, you'll have even more reason to rejoice in Christ Jesus because of me.

kjv: Only let your conversation be as it becometh the gospel of Christ: that whether I come and see you, or else be absent, I may hear of your affairs, that ye stand fast in one spirit, with one mind striving together for the faith of the gospel;
ct: Above all, live in a way that's worthy of the gospel of Christ. Then, whether I come and see you in person or only hear about you from a distance, I'll know that you're standing firm together in one spirit, fighting side by side for the faith of the gospel.

kjv: And in nothing terrified by your adversaries: which is to them an evident token of perdition, but to you of salvation, and that of God.
ct: Don't be intimidated in any way by those who oppose you. Your courage is a clear sign to them that they're headed for destruction — but for you, it's a sign of salvation, and that comes from God.

kjv: For unto you it is given in the behalf of Christ, not only to believe on him, but also to suffer for his sake;
ct: God has given you the privilege not only of believing in Christ, but also of suffering for his sake.

kjv: Having the same conflict which ye saw in me, and now hear to be in me.
ct: You're going through the same struggle you once watched me face — and that you hear I'm still facing now.

kjv: Thou therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
ct: So, my son, draw your strength from the grace that comes through Christ Jesus.

kjv: And the things that thou hast heard of me among many witnesses, the same commit thou to faithful men, who shall be able to teach others also.
ct: Take everything you've heard me teach in front of many witnesses and pass it on to trustworthy people who are able to teach others as well.

kjv: Thou therefore endure hardness, as a good soldier of Jesus Christ.
ct: Be willing to endure hardship like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.

kjv: No man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of this life; that he may please him who hath chosen him to be a soldier.
ct: A soldier on active duty doesn't get tangled up in civilian affairs — his goal is to please the one who enlisted him.

kjv: And if a man also strive for masteries, yet is he not crowned, except he strive lawfully.
ct: In the same way, an athlete doesn't win the prize unless he competes by the rules.

kjv: The husbandman that laboureth must be first partaker of the fruits.
ct: The farmer who does the hard work should be the first to enjoy the harvest.

kjv: Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.
ct: Think carefully about what I'm saying, and may the Lord give you understanding in all of this.

kjv: Remember that Jesus Christ of the seed of David was raised from the dead according to my gospel:
ct: Keep your focus on Jesus Christ — a descendant of David, raised from the dead. This is the heart of my gospel.

kjv: Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, even unto bonds; but the word of God is not bound.
ct: Because of this gospel, I'm suffering to the point of being chained up like a criminal. But God's word can't be chained.

kjv: Therefore I endure all things for the elect’s sakes, that they may also obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
ct: That's why I endure everything for the sake of God's chosen people, so that they too can receive the salvation that comes through Christ Jesus, along with eternal glory.

kjv: It is a faithful saying: For if we be dead with him, we shall also live with him:
ct: Here is a saying you can trust: If we died with him, we will also live with him.

kjv: If we suffer, we shall also reign with him: if we deny him, he also will deny us:
ct: If we endure, we will also reign with him. If we deny him, he will also deny us.

kjv: If we believe not, yet he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself.
ct: Even if we are faithless, he remains faithful — because he cannot deny who he is.

kjv: Of these things put them in remembrance, charging them before the Lord that they strive not about words to no profit, but to the subverting of the hearers.
ct: Keep reminding people of these things. Warn them before the Lord not to argue over words. It does no good and only ruins those who listen.

kjv: Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.
ct: Work hard to present yourself to God as someone he approves of — a worker who has no reason to be ashamed, handling the word of truth with skill and accuracy.

kjv: But shun profane and vain babblings: for they will increase unto more ungodliness.
ct: Stay away from godless, empty talk, because it only leads people deeper into ungodliness.

kjv: And their word will eat as doth a canker: of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus;
ct: That kind of teaching spreads like gangrene. Hymenaeus and Philetus are perfect examples.

kjv: Who concerning the truth have erred, saying that the resurrection is past already; and overthrow the faith of some.
ct: They've wandered away from the truth by claiming the resurrection has already happened, and they're destroying the faith of some believers.

kjv: Nevertheless the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his. And, Let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity.
ct: But God's foundation stands firm, and it carries this seal: "The Lord knows those who are his," and "Everyone who claims the name of Christ must turn away from sin."

kjv: But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of earth; and some to honour, and some to dishonour.
ct: In a large house, you don't just find dishes made of gold and silver. There are also ones made of wood and clay. Some are kept for special occasions, and others are used for ordinary purposes.

kjv: If a man therefore purge himself from these, he shall be a vessel unto honour, sanctified, and meet for the master’s use, and prepared unto every good work.
ct: If someone cleanses himself from dishonorable things, he becomes like a dish set apart for special use — made holy, useful to the master, and ready for every good work.

kjv: Flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness, faith, charity, peace, with them that call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
ct: Run from the desires that tempt young people. Instead, pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace alongside everyone who calls on the Lord with a pure heart.

kjv: But foolish and unlearned questions avoid, knowing that they do gender strifes.
ct: Don't get drawn into foolish, ignorant debates. You know they only produce conflict.

kjv: And the servant of the Lord must not strive; but be gentle unto all men, apt to teach, patient,
ct: The Lord's servant must not quarrel. Instead, he should be gentle with everyone, able to teach, and patient,

kjv: In meekness instructing those that oppose themselves; if God peradventure will give them repentance to the acknowledging of the truth;
ct: correcting opponents with gentleness. Perhaps God will give them repentance that leads to a full knowledge of the truth,

kjv: And that they may recover themselves out of the snare of the devil, who are taken captive by him at his will.
ct: and they'll come to their senses and escape the devil's trap, where he's been holding them captive to do his will.

kjv: Then came to Jesus scribes and Pharisees, which were of Jerusalem, saying,
ct: A group of Pharisees and scribes came from Jerusalem to confront Jesus.

kjv: Why do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the elders? for they wash not their hands when they eat bread.
ct: "Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders?" they asked. "They don't even wash their hands before they eat."

kjv: But he answered and said unto them, Why do ye also transgress the commandment of God by your tradition?
ct: Jesus fired back, "And why do you break God's commandment for the sake of your tradition?

kjv: For God commanded, saying, Honour thy father and mother: and, He that curseth father or mother, let him die the death.
ct: God commanded, 'Honor your father and mother,' and 'Anyone who curses their father or mother must be put to death.'

kjv: But ye say, Whosoever shall say to his father or his mother, It is a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me;
ct: But you teach that someone can say to their father or mother, 'Whatever money I might have used to help you has been dedicated to God' —

kjv: And honour not his father or his mother, he shall be free. Thus have ye made the commandment of God of none effect by your tradition.
ct: and then they don't have to honor their parents at all. You've completely gutted God's commandment with your tradition.

kjv: Ye hypocrites, well did Esaias prophesy of you, saying,
ct: You hypocrites! Isaiah was talking about you when he prophesied,

kjv: This people draweth nigh unto me with their mouth, and honoureth me with their lips; but their heart is far from me.
ct: 'These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are nowhere near me.

kjv: But in vain they do worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.
ct: Their worship is worthless because they teach human rules as if they were God's commands.'"

kjv: And he called the multitude, and said unto them, Hear, and understand:
ct: Then Jesus called the crowd over and said, "Listen carefully and understand this:

kjv: Not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man; but that which cometh out of the mouth, this defileth a man.
ct: It's not what goes into your mouth that makes you unclean. It's what comes out of your mouth — that's what makes you unclean."

kjv: Then came his disciples, and said unto him, Knowest thou that the Pharisees were offended, after they heard this saying?
ct: His disciples came to him and said, "Do you realize the Pharisees were offended when they heard that?"

kjv: But he answered and said, Every plant, which my heavenly Father hath not planted, shall be rooted up.
ct: Jesus replied, "Every plant that my heavenly Father didn't plant will be pulled up by the roots.

kjv: Let them alone: they be blind leaders of the blind. And if the blind lead the blind, both shall fall into the ditch.
ct: Ignore them. They're blind guides leading blind people. And when the blind lead the blind, both end up falling into a pit."

kjv: Then answered Peter and said unto him, Declare unto us this parable.
ct: Peter spoke up and said, "Explain this parable to us."

kjv: And Jesus said, Are ye also yet without understanding?
ct: Jesus said, "Are you still not getting it?

kjv: Do not ye yet understand, that whatsoever entereth in at the mouth goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the draught?
ct: Don't you understand that whatever goes into your mouth passes through your stomach and leaves your body?

kjv: But those things which proceed out of the mouth come forth from the heart; and they defile the man.
ct: But the things that come out of your mouth come from the heart — and those are what make a person unclean.

kjv: For out of the heart proceed evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, blasphemies:
ct: Because out of the heart come evil thoughts, murder, adultery, sexual sin, theft, lying, and slander.

kjv: These are the things which defile a man: but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man.
ct: These are the things that make a person unclean. Eating with unwashed hands doesn't make anyone unclean."

kjv: Then Jesus went thence, and departed into the coasts of Tyre and Sidon.
ct: Jesus left that area and traveled to the region of Tyre and Sidon.

kjv: And, behold, a woman of Canaan came out of the same coasts, and cried unto him, saying, Have mercy on me, O Lord, thou son of David; my daughter is grievously vexed with a devil.
ct: A Canaanite woman from that region came to him, crying out, "Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David! My daughter is tormented by a demon!"

kjv: But he answered her not a word. And his disciples came and besought him, saying, Send her away; for she crieth after us.
ct: But Jesus didn't say a word to her. His disciples came and urged him, "Send her away — she keeps shouting after us."

kjv: But he answered and said, I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
ct: Jesus answered, "I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel."

kjv: Then came she and worshipped him, saying, Lord, help me.
ct: But the woman came and knelt before him. "Lord, help me," she pleaded.

kjv: But he answered and said, It is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it to dogs.
ct: He replied, "It isn't right to take the children's bread and toss it to the dogs."

kjv: And she said, Truth, Lord: yet the dogs eat of the crumbs which fall from their masters’ table.
ct: "That's true, Lord," she said, "but even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters' table."

kjv: Then Jesus answered and said unto her, O woman, great is thy faith: be it unto thee even as thou wilt. And her daughter was made whole from that very hour.
ct: Then Jesus said to her, "Woman, your faith is remarkable! What you've asked for is yours." And from that very moment, her daughter was healed.

kjv: And Jesus departed from thence, and came nigh unto the sea of Galilee; and went up into a mountain, and sat down there.
ct: Jesus left there and went along the Sea of Galilee. He climbed a hillside and sat down.

kjv: And great multitudes came unto him, having with them those that were lame, blind, dumb, maimed, and many others, and cast them down at Jesus’ feet; and he healed them:
ct: Huge crowds came to him, bringing people who were lame, blind, mute, crippled, and many others. They laid them at Jesus' feet, and he healed them all.

kjv: Insomuch that the multitude wondered, when they saw the dumb to speak, the maimed to be whole, the lame to walk, and the blind to see: and they glorified the God of Israel.
ct: The crowd was amazed as they watched mute people speaking, crippled people made whole, lame people walking, and blind people seeing. They praised the God of Israel.

kjv: Then Jesus called his disciples unto him, and said, I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat: and I will not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.
ct: Jesus called his disciples together and said, "My heart goes out to these people. They've been with me for 3 days now and have nothing left to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry — they might collapse on the way home."

kjv: And his disciples say unto him, Whence should we have so much bread in the wilderness, as to fill so great a multitude?
ct: His disciples said, "Where could we possibly find enough bread out here in this remote place to feed such a huge crowd?"

kjv: And Jesus saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven, and a few little fishes.
ct: Jesus asked them, "How many loaves do you have?" They answered, "7, and a few small fish."

kjv: And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground.
ct: He told the crowd to sit down on the ground.

kjv: And he took the seven loaves and the fishes, and gave thanks, and brake them, and gave to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.
ct: Then he took the 7 loaves and the fish, gave thanks to God, broke them into pieces, and handed them to his disciples, who distributed them to the crowd.

kjv: And they did all eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets full.
ct: Everyone ate until they were full, and when they collected the leftovers, they filled 7 baskets.

kjv: And they that did eat were four thousand men, beside women and children.
ct: The number of men who ate was 4,000 — not counting the women and children.

kjv: And he sent away the multitude, and took ship, and came into the coasts of Magdala.
ct: After sending the crowds on their way, Jesus got into a boat and crossed over to the region of Magdala.

kjv: The Pharisees also with the Sadducees came, and tempting desired him that he would shew them a sign from heaven.
ct: The Pharisees and Sadducees approached Jesus together, trying to test him. They demanded that he show them a miraculous sign from heaven.

kjv: He answered and said unto them, When it is evening, ye say, It will be fair weather: for the sky is red.
ct: He replied, "In the evening you say, 'Tomorrow will be nice weather, because the sky is red.'

kjv: And in the morning, It will be foul weather to day: for the sky is red and lowring. O ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky; but can ye not discern the signs of the times?
ct: And in the morning you say, 'It's going to storm today, because the sky is red and overcast.' You hypocrites! You know how to read the sky, but you can't read the signs of the times?

kjv: A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas. And he left them, and departed.
ct: A wicked and unfaithful generation keeps demanding a sign, but the only sign it will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah." Then he turned away from them and left.

kjv: And when his disciples were come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.
ct: When the disciples crossed to the other side of the lake, they realized they had forgotten to bring bread.

kjv: Then Jesus said unto them, Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.
ct: Jesus told them, "Watch out — beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees."

kjv: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have taken no bread.
ct: They started discussing this among themselves, saying, "He must be saying this because we didn't bring any bread."

kjv: Which when Jesus perceived, he said unto them, O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread?
ct: Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, "You have so little faith! Why are you talking among yourselves about not having bread?

kjv: Do ye not yet understand, neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?
ct: Don't you understand yet? Don't you remember the 5 loaves that fed 5,000 people, and how many baskets of leftovers you collected?

kjv: Neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?
ct: Or the 7 loaves that fed 4,000, and how many baskets you collected then?

kjv: How is it that ye do not understand that I spake it not to you concerning bread, that ye should beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees?
ct: How can you not see that I wasn't talking about actual bread? I said beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees."

kjv: Then understood they how that he bade them not beware of the leaven of bread, but of the doctrine of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.
ct: Then they finally understood — he wasn't warning them about yeast in bread. He was warning them about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

kjv: When Jesus came into the coasts of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?
ct: When Jesus arrived in the region of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, "Who do people say the Son of Man is?"

kjv: And they said, Some say that thou art John the Baptist: some, Elias; and others, Jeremias, or one of the prophets.
ct: They answered, "Some say John the Baptist. Others say Elijah. Still others say Jeremiah or one of the prophets."

kjv: He saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am?
ct: "But what about you?" he asked. "Who do you say I am?"

kjv: And Simon Peter answered and said, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.
ct: Simon Peter spoke up: "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God."

kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto him, Blessed art thou, Simon Barjona: for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.
ct: Jesus replied, "You are blessed, Simon son of Jonah, because no human being revealed this to you. My Father in heaven revealed it to you.

kjv: And I say also unto thee, That thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it.
ct: And I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of hell will not overpower it.

kjv: And I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.
ct: I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven."

kjv: Then charged he his disciples that they should tell no man that he was Jesus the Christ.
ct: Then he strictly ordered his disciples not to tell anyone that he was the Christ.

kjv: From that time forth began Jesus to shew unto his disciples, how that he must go unto Jerusalem, and suffer many things of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised again the third day.
ct: From that point on, Jesus began explaining to his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem, where he would suffer terribly at the hands of the elders, chief priests, and scribes. He told them he would be killed and then raised to life on the 3rd day.

kjv: Then Peter took him, and began to rebuke him, saying, Be it far from thee, Lord: this shall not be unto thee.
ct: Peter pulled him aside and began to scold him. "God forbid, Lord! This will never happen to you!"

kjv: But he turned, and said unto Peter, Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art an offence unto me: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.
ct: But Jesus turned and said to Peter, "Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me. You're not thinking about things the way God does — you're thinking like a human being."

kjv: Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
ct: Then Jesus told his disciples, "If anyone wants to follow me, he must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.

kjv: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it.
ct: Because whoever tries to hold on to his life will lose it, but whoever gives up his life for my sake will find it.

kjv: For what is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?
ct: What good is it if someone gains the whole world but loses his own soul? What could anyone offer in exchange for his soul?

kjv: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of his Father with his angels; and then he shall reward every man according to his works.
ct: The Son of Man will come in his Father's glory with his angels, and then he will repay each person according to what they have done.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, There be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the Son of man coming in his kingdom.
ct: I tell you the truth: some of you standing here right now will not taste death before you see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom."

kjv: At the same time came the disciples unto Jesus, saying, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?
ct: Around that time, the disciples came to Jesus and asked, "Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?"

kjv: And Jesus called a little child unto him, and set him in the midst of them,
ct: Jesus called a little child over and had him stand right in the middle of the group.

kjv: And said, Verily I say unto you, Except ye be converted, and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.
ct: Then he said, "I tell you the truth: unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.

kjv: Whosoever therefore shall humble himself as this little child, the same is greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
ct: Whoever humbles himself like this child — that person is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.

kjv: And whoso shall receive one such little child in my name receiveth me.
ct: And whoever welcomes a child like this in my name is welcoming me.

kjv: But whoso shall offend one of these little ones which believe in me, it were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and that he were drowned in the depth of the sea.
ct: But if anyone causes one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for that person to have a huge millstone hung around his neck and be drowned in the depths of the sea.

kjv: Woe unto the world because of offences! for it must needs be that offences come; but woe to that man by whom the offence cometh!
ct: How terrible for the world because of the things that cause people to stumble! Those things are bound to happen, but how terrible for the person who brings them about!

kjv: Wherefore if thy hand or thy foot offend thee, cut them off, and cast them from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life halt or maimed, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into everlasting fire.
ct: If your hand or your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off and throw it away. It's better to enter life crippled or maimed than to have both hands and both feet and be thrown into everlasting fire.

kjv: And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire.
ct: If your eye causes you to stumble, tear it out and throw it away. It's better to enter life with one eye than to have both eyes and be thrown into the fire of hell.

kjv: Take heed that ye despise not one of these little ones; for I say unto you, That in heaven their angels do always behold the face of my Father which is in heaven.
ct: Be careful not to look down on any of these little ones. I'm telling you, their angels in heaven are always in the presence of my Father in heaven.

kjv: For the Son of man is come to save that which was lost.
ct: The Son of Man came to save those who are lost.

kjv: How think ye? if a man have an hundred sheep, and one of them be gone astray, doth he not leave the ninety and nine, and goeth into the mountains, and seeketh that which is gone astray?
ct: What do you think? If a man owns 100 sheep and one of them wanders off, won't he leave the 99 on the hills and go searching for the one that strayed?

kjv: And if so be that he find it, verily I say unto you, he rejoiceth more of that sheep, than of the ninety and nine which went not astray.
ct: I tell you the truth: if he finds it, he's happier about that one sheep than about the 99 that never wandered off.

kjv: Even so it is not the will of your Father which is in heaven, that one of these little ones should perish.
ct: In the same way, your Father in heaven doesn't want even one of these little ones to be lost.

kjv: Moreover if thy brother shall trespass against thee, go and tell him his fault between thee and him alone: if he shall hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother.
ct: If your brother sins against you, go and talk to him privately, just the two of you. If he listens, you've won your brother back.

kjv: But if he will not hear thee, then take with thee one or two more, that in the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established.
ct: But if he won't listen, bring 1 or 2 others along, so that every accusation can be confirmed by 2 or 3 witnesses.

kjv: And if he shall neglect to hear them, tell it unto the church: but if he neglect to hear the church, let him be unto thee as an heathen man and a publican.
ct: If he refuses to listen to them, bring the matter before the church. And if he refuses to listen even to the church, treat him as you would a Gentile or a tax collector.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever ye shall loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.
ct: I tell you the truth: whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you release on earth will be released in heaven.

kjv: Again I say unto you, That if two of you shall agree on earth as touching any thing that they shall ask, it shall be done for them of my Father which is in heaven.
ct: I also tell you this: if 2 of you on earth agree together about anything you pray for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.

kjv: For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.
ct: Because wherever 2 or 3 gather together in my name, I am right there with them."

kjv: Then came Peter to him, and said, Lord, how oft shall my brother sin against me, and I forgive him? till seven times?
ct: Then Peter came to him and asked, "Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother when he sins against me? Up to 7 times?"

kjv: Jesus saith unto him, I say not unto thee, Until seven times: but, Until seventy times seven.
ct: Jesus answered, "Not 7 times — but 70 times 7.

kjv: Therefore is the kingdom of heaven likened unto a certain king, which would take account of his servants.
ct: That's why the kingdom of heaven is like a king who decided to settle accounts with his servants.

kjv: And when he had begun to reckon, one was brought unto him, which owed him ten thousand talents.
ct: As he began going through the records, a man was brought before him who owed him 10,000 talents.

kjv: But forasmuch as he had not to pay, his lord commanded him to be sold, and his wife, and children, and all that he had, and payment to be made.
ct: Since the man couldn't pay, the king ordered that he, his wife, his children, and everything he owned be sold to cover the debt.

kjv: The servant therefore fell down, and worshipped him, saying, Lord, have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.
ct: The servant dropped to his knees and begged, 'Please, be patient with me! I'll pay back every bit of it!'

kjv: Then the lord of that servant was moved with compassion, and loosed him, and forgave him the debt.
ct: The king's heart was filled with compassion. He released the man and forgave the entire debt.

kjv: But the same servant went out, and found one of his fellowservants, which owed him an hundred pence: and he laid hands on him, and took him by the throat, saying, Pay me that thou owest.
ct: But that same servant went out and found a fellow servant who owed him 100 denarii. He grabbed him by the throat and demanded, 'Pay me what you owe!'

kjv: And his fellowservant fell down at his feet, and besought him, saying, Have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.
ct: His fellow servant fell to his knees and begged, 'Please, be patient with me! I'll pay you back!'

kjv: And he would not: but went and cast him into prison, till he should pay the debt.
ct: But he refused. Instead, he had the man thrown into prison until the debt was paid in full.

kjv: So when his fellowservants saw what was done, they were very sorry, and came and told unto their lord all that was done.
ct: When the other servants saw what happened, they were deeply upset. They went and reported the whole thing to the king.

kjv: Then his lord, after that he had called him, said unto him, O thou wicked servant, I forgave thee all that debt, because thou desiredst me:
ct: The king summoned the servant and said, 'You wicked servant! I forgave your entire debt because you begged me to.

kjv: Shouldest not thou also have had compassion on thy fellowservant, even as I had pity on thee?
ct: Shouldn't you have shown mercy to your fellow servant, just as I showed mercy to you?'

kjv: And his lord was wroth, and delivered him to the tormentors, till he should pay all that was due unto him.
ct: In his anger, the king handed him over to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.

kjv: So likewise shall my heavenly Father do also unto you, if ye from your hearts forgive not every one his brother their trespasses.
ct: My heavenly Father will treat each of you the same way if you don't forgive your brother from your heart."

kjv: And it came to pass, that when Jesus had finished these sayings, he departed from Galilee, and came into the coasts of Judaea beyond Jordan;
ct: When Jesus finished teaching these things, he left Galilee and traveled to the region of Judea on the other side of the Jordan River.

kjv: And great multitudes followed him; and he healed them there.
ct: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them there.

kjv: The Pharisees also came unto him, tempting him, and saying unto him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife for every cause?
ct: Some Pharisees came to him, trying to trap him. They asked, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any reason at all?"

kjv: And he answered and said unto them, Have ye not read, that he which made them at the beginning made them male and female,
ct: Jesus answered, "Haven't you read that the one who created them in the beginning made them male and female,

kjv: And said, For this cause shall a man leave father and mother, and shall cleave to his wife: and they twain shall be one flesh?
ct: and said, 'For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united with his wife, and the two will become one flesh'?

kjv: Wherefore they are no more twain, but one flesh. What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.
ct: So they are no longer two people, but one flesh. Therefore, what God has joined together, no one should tear apart."

kjv: They say unto him, Why did Moses then command to give a writing of divorcement, and to put her away?
ct: They pressed him, "Then why did Moses command that a man give his wife a certificate of divorce and send her away?"

kjv: He saith unto them, Moses because of the hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your wives: but from the beginning it was not so.
ct: Jesus told them, "Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts were hard. But that was never God's original design.

kjv: And I say unto you, Whosoever shall put away his wife, except it be for fornication, and shall marry another, committeth adultery: and whoso marrieth her which is put away doth commit adultery.
ct: I'm telling you: whoever divorces his wife — unless it's because of sexual immorality — and marries another woman commits adultery. And anyone who marries a divorced woman commits adultery."

kjv: His disciples say unto him, If the case of the man be so with his wife, it is not good to marry.
ct: His disciples said to him, "If that's how it is between a husband and wife, it's better not to marry at all."

kjv: But he said unto them, All men cannot receive this saying, save they to whom it is given.
ct: Jesus replied, "Not everyone can accept this teaching — only those to whom it has been given.

kjv: For there are some eunuchs, which were so born from their mother’s womb: and there are some eunuchs, which were made eunuchs of men: and there be eunuchs, which have made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heaven’s sake. He that is able to receive it, let him receive it.
ct: Some people are born unable to marry. Others have been made that way by other people. And some choose not to marry for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. Whoever can accept this, let them accept it."

kjv: Then were there brought unto him little children, that he should put his hands on them, and pray: and the disciples rebuked them.
ct: Then people brought little children to Jesus so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. But the disciples scolded them for it.

kjv: But Jesus said, Suffer little children, and forbid them not, to come unto me: for of such is the kingdom of heaven.
ct: Jesus said, "Let the little children come to me. Don't stop them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these."

kjv: And he laid his hands on them, and departed thence.
ct: He placed his hands on the children, and then he left that place.

kjv: And, behold, one came and said unto him, Good Master, what good thing shall I do, that I may have eternal life?
ct: A man came up to Jesus and asked, "Good Teacher, what good thing must I do to receive eternal life?"

kjv: And he said unto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is, God: but if thou wilt enter into life, keep the commandments.
ct: Jesus said to him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone. But if you want to enter into life, obey the commandments."

kjv: He saith unto him, Which? Jesus said, Thou shalt do no murder, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness,
ct: "Which ones?" the man asked. Jesus said, "'Do not murder.' 'Do not commit adultery.' 'Do not steal.' 'Do not give false testimony.'

kjv: Honour thy father and thy mother: and, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
ct: 'Honor your father and mother.' And, 'Love your neighbor as yourself.'"

kjv: The young man saith unto him, All these things have I kept from my youth up: what lack I yet?
ct: The young man said, "I've kept all of these since I was a boy. What am I still missing?"

kjv: Jesus said unto him, If thou wilt be perfect, go and sell that thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come and follow me.
ct: Jesus told him, "If you want to be perfect, go and sell everything you own. Give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come and follow me."

kjv: But when the young man heard that saying, he went away sorrowful: for he had great possessions.
ct: When the young man heard this, he walked away deeply sad — because he was very wealthy.

kjv: Then said Jesus unto his disciples, Verily I say unto you, That a rich man shall hardly enter into the kingdom of heaven.
ct: Then Jesus said to his disciples, "I tell you the truth: it is extremely hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.

kjv: And again I say unto you, It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
ct: Let me say it again — it's easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter God's kingdom."

kjv: When his disciples heard it, they were exceedingly amazed, saying, Who then can be saved?
ct: When the disciples heard this, they were completely stunned. "Then who can possibly be saved?" they asked.

kjv: But Jesus beheld them, and said unto them, With men this is impossible; but with God all things are possible.
ct: Jesus looked at them and said, "For people, this is impossible. But with God, all things are possible."

kjv: Then answered Peter and said unto him, Behold, we have forsaken all, and followed thee; what shall we have therefore?
ct: Peter spoke up and said, "Look, we've given up everything to follow you. What will we get in return?"

kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of his glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
ct: Jesus told them, "I tell you the truth: when the world is made new and the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on 12 thrones, judging the 12 tribes of Israel.

kjv: And every one that hath forsaken houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my name’s sake, shall receive an hundredfold, and shall inherit everlasting life.
ct: Everyone who has given up homes, brothers, sisters, father, mother, wife, children, or land for my sake will receive 100 times as much and will inherit eternal life.

kjv: But many that are first shall be last; and the last shall be first.
ct: But many who are first will end up last, and many who are last will end up first."

kjv: For the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which went out early in the morning to hire labourers into his vineyard.
ct: "The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.

kjv: And when he had agreed with the labourers for a penny a day, he sent them into his vineyard.
ct: He agreed to pay them a denarius for the day and sent them off to work.

kjv: And he went out about the third hour, and saw others standing idle in the marketplace,
ct: Around 9 in the morning, he went out again and saw other people standing around in the marketplace with nothing to do.

kjv: And said unto them; Go ye also into the vineyard, and whatsoever is right I will give you. And they went their way.
ct: He told them, 'Go work in my vineyard too, and I'll pay you whatever is fair.' So they went.

kjv: Again he went out about the sixth and ninth hour, and did likewise.
ct: He went out again around noon and again around 3 in the afternoon, doing the same thing each time.

kjv: And about the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle, and saith unto them, Why stand ye here all the day idle?
ct: Then around 5 in the afternoon, he went out and found still more people standing around. He asked them, 'Why have you been standing here all day doing nothing?'

kjv: They say unto him, Because no man hath hired us. He saith unto them, Go ye also into the vineyard; and whatsoever is right, that shall ye receive.
ct: They answered, 'Because no one has hired us.' He told them, 'Go work in my vineyard too, and you'll receive whatever is fair.'

kjv: So when even was come, the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward, Call the labourers, and give them their hire, beginning from the last unto the first.
ct: When evening came, the vineyard owner told his manager, 'Call the workers and pay them their wages — but start with the ones who were hired last and end with the ones hired first.'

kjv: And when they came that were hired about the eleventh hour, they received every man a penny.
ct: The workers hired at 5 in the afternoon came forward, and each one received a full denarius.

kjv: But when the first came, they supposed that they should have received more; and they likewise received every man a penny.
ct: So when the workers who had been hired first came forward, they expected to receive more. But each of them also received just one denarius.

kjv: And when they had received it, they murmured against the goodman of the house,
ct: When they got their pay, they complained to the landowner.

kjv: Saying, These last have wrought but one hour, and thou hast made them equal unto us, which have borne the burden and heat of the day.
ct: They said, 'These latecomers only worked one hour, and you paid them the same as us — and we've been working all day long in the scorching heat!'

kjv: But he answered one of them, and said, Friend, I do thee no wrong: didst not thou agree with me for a penny?
ct: But the landowner replied to one of them, 'Friend, I haven't cheated you. Didn't you agree to work for a denarius?

kjv: Take that thine is, and go thy way: I will give unto this last, even as unto thee.
ct: Take your pay and go. I've chosen to give these last workers the same amount I gave you.

kjv: Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own? Is thine eye evil, because I am good?
ct: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Or are you jealous because I'm generous?'

kjv: So the last shall be first, and the first last: for many be called, but few chosen.
ct: In the same way, the last will be first, and the first will be last. Many are called, but few are chosen."

kjv: And Jesus going up to Jerusalem took the twelve disciples apart in the way, and said unto them,
ct: As Jesus was heading up to Jerusalem, he pulled the 12 disciples aside and spoke to them privately along the way.

kjv: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be betrayed unto the chief priests and unto the scribes, and they shall condemn him to death,
ct: "Listen," he said, "we're going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes. They will condemn him to death

kjv: And shall deliver him to the Gentiles to mock, and to scourge, and to crucify him: and the third day he shall rise again.
ct: and turn him over to the Gentiles, who will mock him, whip him, and crucify him. But on the 3rd day, he will rise again."

kjv: Then came to him the mother of Zebedee’s children with her sons, worshipping him, and desiring a certain thing of him.
ct: Then the mother of James and John — the sons of Zebedee — came to Jesus with her sons. She knelt before him and asked him for a favor.

kjv: And he said unto her, What wilt thou? She saith unto him, Grant that these my two sons may sit, the one on thy right hand, and the other on the left, in thy kingdom.
ct: "What do you want?" Jesus asked her. She said, "Promise me that in your kingdom, my 2 sons will sit in the places of highest honor — one at your right hand and one at your left."

kjv: But Jesus answered and said, Ye know not what ye ask. Are ye able to drink of the cup that I shall drink of, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with? They say unto him, We are able.
ct: Jesus answered, "You don't understand what you're asking for. Can you drink the cup of suffering that I'm about to drink?" "We can," they replied.

kjv: And he saith unto them, Ye shall drink indeed of my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with: but to sit on my right hand, and on my left, is not mine to give, but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared of my Father.
ct: Jesus told them, "You will indeed drink from my cup. But the seats at my right and left aren't mine to give away. Those places belong to the ones my Father has already prepared them for."

kjv: And when the ten heard it, they were moved with indignation against the two brethren.
ct: When the other 10 disciples heard about this, they were furious with the 2 brothers.

kjv: But Jesus called them unto him, and said, Ye know that the princes of the Gentiles exercise dominion over them, and they that are great exercise authority upon them.
ct: So Jesus called them all together and said, "You know how it works in the world — rulers dominate their people, and those in power lord it over everyone beneath them.

kjv: But it shall not be so among you: but whosoever will be great among you, let him be your minister;
ct: But it must not be that way among you. Instead, whoever wants to be great among you must be your servant.

kjv: And whosoever will be chief among you, let him be your servant:
ct: Whoever wants to be first among you must be your slave.

kjv: Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.
ct: That's exactly why the Son of Man came — not to be served, but to serve others, and to give his life as a ransom for many."

kjv: And as they departed from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.
ct: As they were leaving Jericho, a huge crowd followed Jesus.

kjv: And, behold, two blind men sitting by the way side, when they heard that Jesus passed by, cried out, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, thou son of David.
ct: 2 blind men were sitting beside the road, and when they heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, "Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!"

kjv: And the multitude rebuked them, because they should hold their peace: but they cried the more, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, thou son of David.
ct: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they only shouted louder: "Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!"

kjv: And Jesus stood still, and called them, and said, What will ye that I shall do unto you?
ct: Jesus stopped and called out to them. "What do you want me to do for you?" he asked.

kjv: They say unto him, Lord, that our eyes may be opened.
ct: "Lord," they said, "we want to see."

kjv: So Jesus had compassion on them, and touched their eyes: and immediately their eyes received sight, and they followed him.
ct: Filled with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes. Instantly they could see, and they followed him.

kjv: And when they drew nigh unto Jerusalem, and were come to Bethphage, unto the mount of Olives, then sent Jesus two disciples,
ct: As they approached Jerusalem and reached Bethphage near the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent 2 disciples ahead.

kjv: Saying unto them, Go into the village over against you, and straightway ye shall find an ass tied, and a colt with her: loose them, and bring them unto me.
ct: He told them, "Go into the village just ahead of you. Right away you'll find a donkey tied up with her colt beside her. Untie them and bring them to me.

kjv: And if any man say ought unto you, ye shall say, The Lord hath need of them; and straightway he will send them.
ct: If anyone questions you, just say, 'The Lord needs them,' and they'll let you take them immediately."

kjv: All this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying,
ct: This happened to fulfill what the prophet had spoken:

kjv: Tell ye the daughter of Sion, Behold, thy King cometh unto thee, meek, and sitting upon an ass, and a colt the foal of an ass.
ct: "Tell the daughter of Zion: Look, your King is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey — on a colt, the foal of a donkey."

kjv: And the disciples went, and did as Jesus commanded them,
ct: The disciples went and did exactly what Jesus told them to do.

kjv: And brought the ass, and the colt, and put on them their clothes, and they set him thereon.
ct: They brought the donkey and the colt, draped their cloaks over them, and Jesus sat on them.

kjv: And a very great multitude spread their garments in the way; others cut down branches from the trees, and strawed them in the way.
ct: A massive crowd spread their cloaks on the road, while others cut branches from the trees and laid them along the path.

kjv: And the multitudes that went before, and that followed, cried, saying, Hosanna to the son of David: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord; Hosanna in the highest.
ct: The crowds walking ahead of him and those following behind kept shouting, "Hosanna to the son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!"

kjv: And when he was come into Jerusalem, all the city was moved, saying, Who is this?
ct: When Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was buzzing with excitement. "Who is this?" people asked.

kjv: And the multitude said, This is Jesus the prophet of Nazareth of Galilee.
ct: The crowds answered, "This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee."

kjv: And Jesus went into the temple of God, and cast out all them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves,
ct: Jesus went into the temple of God and drove out everyone who was buying and selling there. He flipped over the money changers' tables and knocked over the seats of those selling doves.

kjv: And said unto them, It is written, My house shall be called the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.
ct: He told them, "Scripture says, 'My house shall be called a house of prayer,' but you've turned it into a den of thieves!"

kjv: And the blind and the lame came to him in the temple; and he healed them.
ct: Blind and lame people came to him in the temple, and he healed them.

kjv: And when the chief priests and scribes saw the wonderful things that he did, and the children crying in the temple, and saying, Hosanna to the son of David; they were sore displeased,
ct: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the amazing things he was doing — and heard children shouting in the temple, "Hosanna to the son of David!" — they were furious.

kjv: And said unto him, Hearest thou what these say? And Jesus saith unto them, Yea; have ye never read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise?
ct: "Do you hear what these children are saying?" they demanded. Jesus replied, "Yes. Haven't you ever read, 'Out of the mouth of babes and nursing babies you have perfected praise'?"

kjv: And he left them, and went out of the city into Bethany; and he lodged there.
ct: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany, where he spent the night.

kjv: Now in the morning as he returned into the city, he hungered.
ct: Early the next morning, as he was heading back to the city, he was hungry.

kjv: And when he saw a fig tree in the way, he came to it, and found nothing thereon, but leaves only, and said unto it, Let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever. And presently the fig tree withered away.
ct: He spotted a fig tree by the road and went over to it, but found nothing on it except leaves. He said to it, "May you never bear fruit again!" Instantly the fig tree withered.

kjv: And when the disciples saw it, they marvelled, saying, How soon is the fig tree withered away!
ct: The disciples were stunned. "How did the fig tree wither so fast?" they asked.

kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Verily I say unto you, If ye have faith, and doubt not, ye shall not only do this which is done to the fig tree, but also if ye shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; it shall be done.
ct: Jesus answered, "I tell you the truth — if you have faith and don't doubt, you'll not only do what was done to this fig tree, but you could even say to this mountain, 'Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,' and it would happen.

kjv: And all things, whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive.
ct: Whatever you ask for in prayer, if you believe, you will receive it."

kjv: And when he was come into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came unto him as he was teaching, and said, By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority?
ct: When Jesus entered the temple and began teaching, the chief priests and elders of the people approached him. "By what authority are you doing these things?" they demanded. "Who gave you this authority?"

kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, I also will ask you one thing, which if ye tell me, I in like wise will tell you by what authority I do these things.
ct: Jesus replied, "I'll ask you one question first. If you answer me, I'll tell you by what authority I do these things.

kjv: The baptism of John, whence was it? from heaven, or of men? And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say unto us, Why did ye not then believe him?
ct: John's baptism — where did it come from? Was it from heaven, or was it a human invention?" They debated among themselves: "If we say, 'From heaven,' he'll ask us, 'Then why didn't you believe him?'

kjv: But if we shall say, Of men; we fear the people; for all hold John as a prophet.
ct: But if we say, 'From men,' we have the crowd to worry about — everyone considers John a prophet."

kjv: And they answered Jesus, and said, We cannot tell. And he said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
ct: So they answered Jesus, "We don't know." He replied, "Then I'm not going to tell you by what authority I do these things either.

kjv: But what think ye? A certain man had two sons; and he came to the first, and said, Son, go work to day in my vineyard.
ct: But let me ask you this: A man had 2 sons. He went to the first and said, 'Son, go work in my vineyard today.'

kjv: He answered and said, I will not: but afterward he repented, and went.
ct: The son answered, 'No, I won't.' But later he changed his mind and went.

kjv: And he came to the second, and said likewise. And he answered and said, I go, sir: and went not.
ct: Then the father went to the second son and said the same thing. He answered, 'Sure, sir, I'll go' — but he never went.

kjv: Whether of them twain did the will of his father? They say unto him, The first. Jesus saith unto them, Verily I say unto you, That the publicans and the harlots go into the kingdom of God before you.
ct: Which of the 2 did what his father wanted?" "The first one," they answered. Jesus said to them, "I tell you the truth — tax collectors and prostitutes are entering God's Kingdom ahead of you.

kjv: For John came unto you in the way of righteousness, and ye believed him not: but the publicans and the harlots believed him: and ye, when ye had seen it, repented not afterward, that ye might believe him.
ct: John came to you living a righteous life, and you didn't believe him. But the tax collectors and prostitutes did. Even after you saw that, you still refused to repent and believe him.

kjv: Hear another parable: There was a certain householder, which planted a vineyard, and hedged it round about, and digged a winepress in it, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country:
ct: Listen to another parable: A landowner planted a vineyard, put a wall around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a watchtower. Then he leased it to some farmers and left for a distant country.

kjv: And when the time of the fruit drew near, he sent his servants to the husbandmen, that they might receive the fruits of it.
ct: When harvest time came, he sent his servants to the farmers to collect his share of the fruit.

kjv: And the husbandmen took his servants, and beat one, and killed another, and stoned another.
ct: But the farmers grabbed his servants — they beat one, killed another, and stoned a third.

kjv: Again, he sent other servants more than the first: and they did unto them likewise.
ct: So the owner sent another group of servants, even more than the first time, and the farmers treated them the same way.

kjv: But last of all he sent unto them his son, saying, They will reverence my son.
ct: Finally, he sent his own son, thinking, 'Surely they'll respect my son.'

kjv: But when the husbandmen saw the son, they said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and let us seize on his inheritance.
ct: But when the farmers saw the son coming, they said to each other, 'This is the heir! Let's kill him and take his inheritance for ourselves.'

kjv: And they caught him, and cast him out of the vineyard, and slew him.
ct: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.

kjv: When the lord therefore of the vineyard cometh, what will he do unto those husbandmen?
ct: Now, when the owner of the vineyard comes back, what do you think he'll do to those farmers?"

kjv: They say unto him, He will miserably destroy those wicked men, and will let out his vineyard unto other husbandmen, which shall render him the fruits in their seasons.
ct: They answered, "He'll bring those wretches to a miserable end and lease the vineyard to other farmers who will give him his share of the harvest when it's due."

kjv: Jesus saith unto them, Did ye never read in the scriptures, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner: this is the Lord’s doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?
ct: Jesus said to them, "Have you never read in the Scriptures: 'The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. This was the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes'?

kjv: Therefore say I unto you, The kingdom of God shall be taken from you, and given to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof.
ct: That's why I'm telling you: God's Kingdom will be taken away from you and given to a people who will produce its fruit.

kjv: And whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken: but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.
ct: Anyone who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, and anyone it falls on will be crushed to powder."

kjv: And when the chief priests and Pharisees had heard his parables, they perceived that he spake of them.
ct: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they realized he was talking about them.

kjv: But when they sought to lay hands on him, they feared the multitude, because they took him for a prophet.
ct: They wanted to arrest him, but they were afraid of the crowds, who regarded Jesus as a prophet.

kjv: And Jesus answered and spake unto them again by parables, and said,
ct: Jesus responded by telling them another parable.

kjv: The kingdom of heaven is like unto a certain king, which made a marriage for his son,
ct: "The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding banquet for his son.

kjv: And sent forth his servants to call them that were bidden to the wedding: and they would not come.
ct: He sent his servants to tell the invited guests that the feast was ready, but they refused to come.

kjv: Again, he sent forth other servants, saying, Tell them which are bidden, Behold, I have prepared my dinner: my oxen and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready: come unto the marriage.
ct: So he sent more servants with this message: 'Tell the guests, "Look, the banquet is prepared! My oxen and fattened cattle have been slaughtered, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding feast!"'

kjv: But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his farm, another to his merchandise:
ct: But the guests didn't care. They shrugged it off and went about their business — one headed to his farm, another to his shop.

kjv: And the remnant took his servants, and entreated them spitefully, and slew them.
ct: The rest seized his servants, beat them, and killed them.

kjv: But when the king heard thereof, he was wroth: and he sent forth his armies, and destroyed those murderers, and burned up their city.
ct: The king was furious. He sent his armies to destroy those murderers and burn their city to the ground.

kjv: Then saith he to his servants, The wedding is ready, but they which were bidden were not worthy.
ct: Then he told his servants, 'The wedding banquet is ready, but the people I invited didn't deserve to come.

kjv: Go ye therefore into the highways, and as many as ye shall find, bid to the marriage.
ct: So go out to the main roads and invite everyone you can find to the feast.'

kjv: So those servants went out into the highways, and gathered together all as many as they found, both bad and good: and the wedding was furnished with guests.
ct: The servants went out into the streets and gathered everyone they could find — both bad and good — until the wedding hall was packed with guests.

kjv: And when the king came in to see the guests, he saw there a man which had not on a wedding garment:
ct: But when the king came in to greet the guests, he noticed a man who wasn't wearing wedding clothes.

kjv: And he saith unto him, Friend, how camest thou in hither not having a wedding garment? And he was speechless.
ct: He said to him, 'Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes?' The man had nothing to say.

kjv: Then said the king to the servants, Bind him hand and foot, and take him away, and cast him into outer darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
ct: Then the king told his servants, 'Tie him hand and foot and throw him outside into the darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.'

kjv: For many are called, but few are chosen.
ct: For many are called, but few are chosen."

kjv: Then went the Pharisees, and took counsel how they might entangle him in his talk.
ct: After this, the Pharisees got together and plotted how to trap Jesus with his own words.

kjv: And they sent out unto him their disciples with the Herodians, saying, Master, we know that thou art true, and teachest the way of God in truth, neither carest thou for any man: for thou regardest not the person of men.
ct: They sent their disciples to him along with supporters of Herod. "Teacher," they said, "we know you're an honest man who teaches God's way truthfully. You don't play favorites or care about anyone's status.

kjv: Tell us therefore, What thinkest thou? Is it lawful to give tribute unto Caesar, or not?
ct: So tell us what you think: Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?"

kjv: But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said, Why tempt ye me, ye hypocrites?
ct: But Jesus saw right through their scheme. "Why are you trying to trap me, you hypocrites?" he said.

kjv: Shew me the tribute money. And they brought unto him a penny.
ct: "Show me the coin used to pay the tax." They handed him a denarius.

kjv: And he saith unto them, Whose is this image and superscription?
ct: "Whose image and inscription is on this?" he asked them.

kjv: They say unto him, Caesar’s. Then saith he unto them, Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar’s; and unto God the things that are God’s.
ct: "Caesar's," they answered. Then Jesus said, "So give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar, and give to God what belongs to God."

kjv: When they had heard these words, they marvelled, and left him, and went their way.
ct: His answer stunned them. They had nothing to say, so they walked away.

kjv: The same day came to him the Sadducees, which say that there is no resurrection, and asked him,
ct: That same day, some Sadducees — who don't believe in the resurrection — came to Jesus with a question.

kjv: Saying, Master, Moses said, If a man die, having no children, his brother shall marry his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
ct: "Teacher," they said, "Moses taught that if a man dies without having children, his brother must marry the widow and have children on his behalf.

kjv: Now there were with us seven brethren: and the first, when he had married a wife, deceased, and, having no issue, left his wife unto his brother:
ct: Now, there were 7 brothers among us. The first one married and died without children, so his wife passed to his brother.

kjv: Likewise the second also, and the third, unto the seventh.
ct: The same thing happened with the second brother, then the third, and so on through all 7.

kjv: And last of all the woman died also.
ct: Finally, the woman died too.

kjv: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife shall she be of the seven? for they all had her.
ct: So here's our question: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? After all, she was married to all 7 of them."

kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Ye do err, not knowing the scriptures, nor the power of God.
ct: Jesus replied, "You're completely wrong, because you don't understand the Scriptures or the power of God.

kjv: For in the resurrection they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are as the angels of God in heaven.
ct: In the resurrection, people won't marry or be given in marriage. They'll be like the angels of God in heaven.

kjv: But as touching the resurrection of the dead, have ye not read that which was spoken unto you by God, saying,
ct: And as for whether the dead are raised — haven't you read what God himself said to you:

kjv: I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.
ct: 'I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'? He's not the God of the dead — he's the God of the living."

kjv: And when the multitude heard this, they were astonished at his doctrine.
ct: When the crowd heard this, they were amazed at his teaching.

kjv: But when the Pharisees had heard that he had put the Sadducees to silence, they were gathered together.
ct: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together.

kjv: Then one of them, which was a lawyer, asked him a question, tempting him, and saying,
ct: One of them, an expert in the law, tested him with a question.

kjv: Master, which is the great commandment in the law?
ct: "Teacher, which is the greatest commandment in the law?"

kjv: Jesus said unto him, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind.
