ct: Jesus answered, "'Love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.'

kjv: This is the first and great commandment.
ct: This is the first and greatest commandment.

kjv: And the second is like unto it, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
ct: The second is just as important: 'Love your neighbor as yourself.'

kjv: On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets.
ct: Everything in the law and the prophets hangs on these 2 commandments."

kjv: While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them,
ct: While the Pharisees were still gathered, Jesus asked them a question of his own.

kjv: Saying, What think ye of Christ? whose son is he? They say unto him, The son of David.
ct: "What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he?" They answered, "He is the son of David."

kjv: He saith unto them, How then doth David in spirit call him Lord, saying,
ct: Jesus said, "Then how is it that David, speaking by the Spirit, calls him Lord? David said,

kjv: The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool?
ct: 'The LORD said to my Lord, sit at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet.'

kjv: If David then call him Lord, how is he his son?
ct: If David calls him Lord, how can he be David's son?"

kjv: And no man was able to answer him a word, neither durst any man from that day forth ask him any more questions.
ct: No one could answer him. And from that day on, nobody dared ask him any more questions.

kjv: Then spake Jesus to the multitude, and to his disciples,
ct: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and to his disciples.

kjv: Saying, The scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses’ seat:
ct: He said, "The scribes and the Pharisees have the authority to teach the Law of Moses.

kjv: All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do; but do not ye after their works: for they say, and do not.
ct: So obey and follow whatever they tell you. But don't follow their example, because they don't practice what they preach.

kjv: For they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne, and lay them on men’s shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers.
ct: They pile heavy, crushing burdens on people's shoulders, but they won't lift a finger to help carry the load.

kjv: But all their works they do for to be seen of men: they make broad their phylacteries, and enlarge the borders of their garments,
ct: Everything they do is for show. They wear extra-wide phylacteries and make the tassels on their garments extra long.

kjv: And love the uppermost rooms at feasts, and the chief seats in the synagogues,
ct: They love the seats of honor at banquets and the best seats in the synagogues.

kjv: And greetings in the markets, and to be called of men, Rabbi, Rabbi.
ct: They love being greeted with respect in the marketplaces and having people call them 'Rabbi, Rabbi.'

kjv: But be not ye called Rabbi: for one is your Master, even Christ; and all ye are brethren.
ct: But don't let anyone call you 'Rabbi,' because you have one Teacher — Christ — and you are all brothers and sisters.

kjv: And call no man your father upon the earth: for one is your Father, which is in heaven.
ct: Don't call anyone on earth your father, because you have one Father, and he is in heaven.

kjv: Neither be ye called masters: for one is your Master, even Christ.
ct: Don't let anyone call you 'master' either, because you have one Master — Christ.

kjv: But he that is greatest among you shall be your servant.
ct: The greatest among you must be the one who serves the others.

kjv: And whosoever shall exalt himself shall be abased; and he that shall humble himself shall be exalted.
ct: Whoever lifts himself up will be brought low, and whoever humbles himself will be lifted up.

kjv: But woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye shut up the kingdom of heaven against men: for ye neither go in yourselves, neither suffer ye them that are entering to go in.
ct: How terrible it will be for you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You slam the door of the kingdom of heaven in people's faces. You won't go in yourselves, and you won't let anyone else in either.

kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye devour widows’ houses, and for a pretence make long prayer: therefore ye shall receive the greater damnation.
ct: How terrible it will be for you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You devour widows' homes and then cover it up with long, showy prayers. Because of this, your punishment will be even worse.

kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye compass sea and land to make one proselyte, and when he is made, ye make him twofold more the child of hell than yourselves.
ct: How terrible it will be for you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You cross land and sea to win a single convert, and when you do, you turn that person into twice the child of hell that you are.

kjv: Woe unto you, ye blind guides, which say, Whosoever shall swear by the temple, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gold of the temple, he is a debtor!
ct: How terrible it will be for you, blind guides! You say, 'If someone swears by the temple, it means nothing. But if someone swears by the gold of the temple, that oath is binding.'

kjv: Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gold, or the temple that sanctifieth the gold?
ct: You blind fools! Which is greater — the gold, or the temple that makes the gold sacred?

kjv: And, Whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is nothing; but whosoever sweareth by the gift that is upon it, he is guilty.
ct: You also say, 'If someone swears by the altar, it means nothing. But if someone swears by the gift on the altar, that oath is binding.'

kjv: Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifieth the gift?
ct: You blind fools! Which is greater — the gift, or the altar that makes the gift sacred?

kjv: Whoso therefore shall swear by the altar, sweareth by it, and by all things thereon.
ct: So when someone swears by the altar, they're swearing by it and by everything on it.

kjv: And whoso shall swear by the temple, sweareth by it, and by him that dwelleth therein.
ct: When someone swears by the temple, they're swearing by it and by God who lives in it.

kjv: And he that shall swear by heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon.
ct: And when someone swears by heaven, they're swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on it.

kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye pay tithe of mint and anise and cummin, and have omitted the weightier matters of the law, judgment, mercy, and faith: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.
ct: How terrible it will be for you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You carefully tithe your mint, dill, and cumin, but you've neglected the things that matter most in the Law — justice, mercy, and faith. You should have practiced these without neglecting the others.

kjv: Ye blind guides, which strain at a gnat, and swallow a camel.
ct: You blind guides! You strain out a gnat but swallow a camel.

kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye make clean the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full of extortion and excess.
ct: How terrible it will be for you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You polish the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they're full of greed and self-indulgence.

kjv: Thou blind Pharisee, cleanse first that which is within the cup and platter, that the outside of them may be clean also.
ct: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup and dish, and then the outside will be clean too.

kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are like unto whited sepulchres, which indeed appear beautiful outward, but are within full of dead men’s bones, and of all uncleanness.
ct: How terrible it will be for you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You're like whitewashed tombs — beautiful on the outside, but inside you're full of dead men's bones and everything unclean.

kjv: Even so ye also outwardly appear righteous unto men, but within ye are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.
ct: That's exactly what you're like. On the outside you look righteous to everyone, but on the inside you're full of hypocrisy and wickedness.

kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye build the tombs of the prophets, and garnish the sepulchres of the righteous,
ct: How terrible it will be for you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of the righteous,

kjv: And say, If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.
ct: and you say, 'If we had lived back in our ancestors' day, we never would have joined them in killing the prophets.'

kjv: Wherefore ye be witnesses unto yourselves, that ye are the children of them which killed the prophets.
ct: By saying that, you're admitting that you are the descendants of those who murdered the prophets.

kjv: Fill ye up then the measure of your fathers.
ct: Go ahead then — finish what your ancestors started.

kjv: Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers, how can ye escape the damnation of hell?
ct: You snakes! You brood of vipers! How will you escape being condemned to hell?

kjv: Wherefore, behold, I send unto you prophets, and wise men, and scribes: and some of them ye shall kill and crucify; and some of them shall ye scourge in your synagogues, and persecute them from city to city:
ct: Listen — I am sending you prophets, wise men, and scribes. Some of them you will kill and crucify. Others you will whip in your synagogues and chase from city to city.

kjv: That upon you may come all the righteous blood shed upon the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel unto the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias, whom ye slew between the temple and the altar.
ct: As a result, you will be held responsible for all the righteous blood ever shed on the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias, whom you murdered between the temple and the altar.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, All these things shall come upon this generation.
ct: I tell you the truth: all of this will fall on this generation.

kjv: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets, and stonest them which are sent unto thee, how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, and ye would not!
ct: "Jerusalem, Jerusalem — you who kill the prophets and stone those sent to you! How many times I wanted to gather your children together, the way a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you refused!

kjv: Behold, your house is left unto you desolate.
ct: Look — your house is left to you abandoned and empty.

kjv: For I say unto you, Ye shall not see me henceforth, till ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.
ct: I tell you this: you will not see me again until you say, 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.'"

kjv: And Jesus went out, and departed from the temple: and his disciples came to him for to shew him the buildings of the temple.
ct: Jesus left the temple and was walking away when his disciples came up to him, pointing out the temple buildings.

kjv: And Jesus said unto them, See ye not all these things? verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.
ct: Jesus replied, "You see all of this, don't you? I tell you the truth — not one stone here will be left standing on another. Every one of them will be torn down."

kjv: And as he sat upon the mount of Olives, the disciples came unto him privately, saying, Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign of thy coming, and of the end of the world?
ct: Later, as he sat on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately. "Tell us," they said, "when will this happen? And what will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age?"

kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Take heed that no man deceive you.
ct: Jesus answered, "Be careful that no one deceives you.

kjv: For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.
ct: Many will come using my name, claiming, 'I am the Christ,' and they will deceive many people.

kjv: And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
ct: You'll hear about wars and rumors of wars, but don't be alarmed. These things must happen, but the end hasn't come yet.

kjv: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places.
ct: Nation will go to war against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be famines, plagues, and earthquakes in various places.

kjv: All these are the beginning of sorrows.
ct: All of these are just the beginning of birth pains.

kjv: Then shall they deliver you up to be afflicted, and shall kill you: and ye shall be hated of all nations for my name’s sake.
ct: Then they will hand you over to be persecuted and put to death. Every nation will hate you because of me.

kjv: And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.
ct: At that time, many will turn away from the faith. They'll betray each other and hate each other.

kjv: And many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many.
ct: Many false prophets will appear and deceive large numbers of people.

kjv: And because iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.
ct: Because sin will be everywhere, the love of most people will grow cold.

kjv: But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.
ct: But whoever endures to the end will be saved.

kjv: And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come.
ct: This good news of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the whole world as a testimony to every nation — and then the end will come.

kjv: When ye therefore shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place, (whoso readeth, let him understand:)
ct: So when you see the abomination of desolation that the prophet Daniel spoke about, standing in the holy place — let the reader understand —

kjv: Then let them which be in Judaea flee into the mountains:
ct: then those in Judea should flee to the mountains.

kjv: Let him which is on the housetop not come down to take any thing out of his house:
ct: If you're on the roof of your house, don't go back inside to grab your belongings.

kjv: Neither let him which is in the field return back to take his clothes.
ct: If you're out in the field, don't go back for your coat.

kjv: And woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!
ct: How terrible it will be for pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days!

kjv: But pray ye that your flight be not in the winter, neither on the sabbath day:
ct: Pray that your escape doesn't happen in winter or on the Sabbath,

kjv: For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time, no, nor ever shall be.
ct: because there will be a great tribulation unlike anything the world has ever seen from the beginning until now — and nothing like it will ever happen again.

kjv: And except those days should be shortened, there should no flesh be saved: but for the elect’s sake those days shall be shortened.
ct: If those days weren't cut short, no one would survive. But for the sake of God's chosen ones, those days will be cut short.

kjv: Then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or there; believe it not.
ct: If anyone tells you then, 'Look, here is the Christ!' or 'He's over there!' — don't believe it.

kjv: For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall shew great signs and wonders; insomuch that, if it were possible, they shall deceive the very elect.
ct: False christs and false prophets will appear and perform spectacular signs and wonders, so convincing that they would deceive even God's chosen ones if that were possible.

kjv: Behold, I have told you before.
ct: Remember, I've warned you ahead of time.

kjv: Wherefore if they shall say unto you, Behold, he is in the desert; go not forth: behold, he is in the secret chambers; believe it not.
ct: So if someone says, 'He's out in the wilderness!' — don't go. Or if they say, 'He's hiding in a secret room!' — don't believe it.

kjv: For as the lightning cometh out of the east, and shineth even unto the west; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.
ct: The coming of the Son of Man will be like lightning that flashes across the sky from east to west — visible to everyone.

kjv: For wheresoever the carcase is, there will the eagles be gathered together.
ct: Wherever there's a carcass, the vultures will gather.

kjv: Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken:
ct: Immediately after the tribulation of those days, the sun will go dark, the moon will stop giving its light, the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

kjv: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
ct: Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky. Every tribe on earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.

kjv: And he shall send his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.
ct: He will send out his angels with a loud trumpet blast, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four winds — from one end of the heavens to the other.

kjv: Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh:
ct: Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: When its branches become tender and its leaves start to sprout, you know summer is near.

kjv: So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.
ct: In the same way, when you see all these things happening, you'll know that the time is near — right at the door.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass, till all these things be fulfilled.
ct: I tell you the truth, this generation will not pass away until all these things have taken place.

kjv: Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away.
ct: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.

kjv: But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven, but my Father only.
ct: No one knows the day or the hour — not the angels in heaven, not anyone — only my Father knows.

kjv: But as the days of Noe were, so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.
ct: The coming of the Son of Man will be just like the days of Noah.

kjv: For as in the days that were before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark,
ct: In the days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, getting married and giving their children in marriage, right up until the day Noah entered the ark.

kjv: And knew not until the flood came, and took them all away; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.
ct: They had no idea what was coming until the flood swept them all away. That's exactly how the coming of the Son of Man will be.

kjv: Then shall two be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
ct: Two men will be working in a field — one will be taken and the other left behind.

kjv: Two women shall be grinding at the mill; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
ct: Two women will be grinding grain at the mill — one will be taken and the other left behind.

kjv: Watch therefore: for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.
ct: So stay alert, because you don't know what day your Lord is coming.

kjv: But know this, that if the goodman of the house had known in what watch the thief would come, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be broken up.
ct: Understand this: if a homeowner had known what time of night a thief was coming, he would have stayed awake and not let his house be broken into.

kjv: Therefore be ye also ready: for in such an hour as ye think not the Son of man cometh.
ct: So you must be ready too, because the Son of Man will come at an hour you don't expect.

kjv: Who then is a faithful and wise servant, whom his lord hath made ruler over his household, to give them meat in due season?
ct: Who then is the faithful and wise servant that the master has put in charge of his household, to give the others their food at the right time?

kjv: Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.
ct: That servant is blessed if his master arrives and finds him doing his job.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, That he shall make him ruler over all his goods.
ct: I tell you the truth, the master will put that servant in charge of everything he owns.

kjv: But and if that evil servant shall say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming;
ct: But suppose that servant is wicked and thinks to himself, 'My master won't be back for a long time.'

kjv: And shall begin to smite his fellowservants, and to eat and drink with the drunken;
ct: Then he starts beating the other servants and eating and drinking with drunkards.

kjv: The lord of that servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for him, and in an hour that he is not aware of,
ct: That servant's master will show up on a day he doesn't expect, at a time he doesn't know.

kjv: And shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
ct: The master will cut him to pieces and throw him in with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth."

kjv: Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom.
ct: "At that time, the kingdom of heaven will be like 10 young women who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.

kjv: And five of them were wise, and five were foolish.
ct: 5 of them were foolish, and 5 were wise.

kjv: They that were foolish took their lamps, and took no oil with them:
ct: The foolish ones brought their lamps but didn't bring any extra oil.

kjv: But the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.
ct: The wise ones, however, brought jars of oil along with their lamps.

kjv: While the bridegroom tarried, they all slumbered and slept.
ct: The bridegroom took a long time to arrive, and all 10 of them grew drowsy and fell asleep.

kjv: And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him.
ct: Then at midnight, someone shouted, "Look! The bridegroom is coming! Come out to meet him!"

kjv: Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps.
ct: All 10 women woke up and trimmed their lamps.

kjv: And the foolish said unto the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps are gone out.
ct: The foolish ones said to the wise, "Give us some of your oil — our lamps are going out!"

kjv: But the wise answered, saying, Not so; lest there be not enough for us and you: but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.
ct: But the wise ones replied, "There won't be enough for all of us if we share. Go to the merchants and buy some for yourselves."

kjv: And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage: and the door was shut.
ct: While they were gone to buy oil, the bridegroom arrived. The women who were ready went in with him to the wedding feast, and the door was shut behind them.

kjv: Afterward came also the other virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us.
ct: Later, the other women came back and called out, "Lord, Lord, open the door for us!"

kjv: But he answered and said, Verily I say unto you, I know you not.
ct: But he answered, "I tell you the truth — I don't know you."

kjv: Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh.
ct: So stay alert, because you don't know the day or the hour when the Son of Man will come.

kjv: For the kingdom of heaven is as a man travelling into a far country, who called his own servants, and delivered unto them his goods.
ct: "Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a man about to leave on a long journey. He called his servants together and put them in charge of his wealth.

kjv: And unto one he gave five talents, to another two, and to another one; to every man according to his several ability; and straightway took his journey.
ct: He gave 5 talents to one servant, 2 talents to another, and 1 talent to a third — each according to his ability. Then he left on his journey.

kjv: Then he that had received the five talents went and traded with the same, and made them other five talents.
ct: The servant who received 5 talents immediately went to work investing the money, and he earned 5 more.

kjv: And likewise he that had received two, he also gained other two.
ct: The one with 2 talents did the same and earned 2 more.

kjv: But he that had received one went and digged in the earth, and hid his lord’s money.
ct: But the servant who received 1 talent went off, dug a hole in the ground, and buried his master's money.

kjv: After a long time the lord of those servants cometh, and reckoneth with them.
ct: After a long time, the master returned and settled accounts with them.

kjv: And so he that had received five talents came and brought other five talents, saying, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents: behold, I have gained beside them five talents more.
ct: The servant who had received 5 talents came forward and presented 5 more, saying, "Master, you entrusted me with 5 talents. Look — I've earned 5 more."

kjv: His lord said unto him, Well done, thou good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.
ct: His master said, "Well done, good and faithful servant! You've been faithful with a small amount, so I'll put you in charge of much more. Come and share in your master's joy!"

kjv: He also that had received two talents came and said, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me two talents: behold, I have gained two other talents beside them.
ct: The servant with 2 talents also came forward and said, "Master, you entrusted me with 2 talents. Look — I've earned 2 more."

kjv: His lord said unto him, Well done, good and faithful servant; thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.
ct: His master said, "Well done, good and faithful servant! You've been faithful with a small amount, so I'll put you in charge of much more. Come and share in your master's joy!"

kjv: Then he which had received the one talent came and said, Lord, I knew thee that thou art an hard man, reaping where thou hast not sown, and gathering where thou hast not strawed:
ct: Then the servant who had received 1 talent came forward and said, "Master, I knew you were a hard man. You harvest where you didn't plant and gather where you didn't scatter seed.

kjv: And I was afraid, and went and hid thy talent in the earth: lo, there thou hast that is thine.
ct: I was afraid, so I went and buried your talent in the ground. Here — you have what belongs to you."

kjv: His lord answered and said unto him, Thou wicked and slothful servant, thou knewest that I reap where I sowed not, and gather where I have not strawed:
ct: His master replied, "You wicked, lazy servant! So you knew that I harvest where I didn't plant and gather where I didn't scatter?

kjv: Thou oughtest therefore to have put my money to the exchangers, and then at my coming I should have received mine own with usury.
ct: Then you should have at least deposited my money with the bankers so I would have gotten it back with interest when I returned.

kjv: Take therefore the talent from him, and give it unto him which hath ten talents.
ct: Take the talent away from him and give it to the one who has 10.

kjv: For unto every one that hath shall be given, and he shall have abundance: but from him that hath not shall be taken away even that which he hath.
ct: Everyone who has will be given more, and they will have an abundance. But the one who has nothing — even what they have will be taken away.

kjv: And cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
ct: Now throw this worthless servant into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth."

kjv: When the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then shall he sit upon the throne of his glory:
ct: "When the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, he will sit on his glorious throne.

kjv: And before him shall be gathered all nations: and he shall separate them one from another, as a shepherd divideth his sheep from the goats:
ct: Every nation will be gathered before him, and he will separate the people from one another the way a shepherd separates sheep from goats.

kjv: And he shall set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.
ct: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.

kjv: Then shall the King say unto them on his right hand, Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world:
ct: Then the King will say to those on his right, "Come, you who are blessed by my Father. Inherit the kingdom that has been prepared for you since the creation of the world.

kjv: For I was an hungred, and ye gave me meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me drink: I was a stranger, and ye took me in:
ct: I was hungry, and you gave me food. I was thirsty, and you gave me something to drink. I was a stranger, and you welcomed me in.

kjv: Naked, and ye clothed me: I was sick, and ye visited me: I was in prison, and ye came unto me.
ct: I was naked, and you gave me clothes. I was sick, and you looked after me. I was in prison, and you came to visit me."

kjv: Then shall the righteous answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, and fed thee? or thirsty, and gave thee drink?
ct: The righteous will answer him, "Lord, when did we ever see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you something to drink?

kjv: When saw we thee a stranger, and took thee in? or naked, and clothed thee?
ct: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you in, or naked and give you clothes?

kjv: Or when saw we thee sick, or in prison, and came unto thee?
ct: When did we see you sick or in prison and come to visit you?"

kjv: And the King shall answer and say unto them, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done it unto me.
ct: The King will answer them, "I tell you the truth — whatever you did for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you did for me."

kjv: Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels:
ct: Then he will say to those on his left, "Get away from me, you who are cursed, into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels.

kjv: For I was an hungred, and ye gave me no meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me no drink:
ct: I was hungry, and you gave me nothing to eat. I was thirsty, and you gave me nothing to drink.

kjv: I was a stranger, and ye took me not in: naked, and ye clothed me not: sick, and in prison, and ye visited me not.
ct: I was a stranger, and you didn't welcome me. I was naked, and you didn't clothe me. I was sick and in prison, and you never came to see me."

kjv: Then shall they also answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, or athirst, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and did not minister unto thee?
ct: They will answer, "Lord, when did we ever see you hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and not help you?"

kjv: Then shall he answer them, saying, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye did it not to one of the least of these, ye did it not to me.
ct: He will reply, "I tell you the truth — whatever you failed to do for one of the least of these, you failed to do for me."

kjv: And these shall go away into everlasting punishment: but the righteous into life eternal.
ct: Then they will go away into everlasting punishment, but the righteous will enter eternal life."

kjv: When the morning was come, all the chief priests and elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death:
ct: At dawn, all the chief priests and elders of the people met together and made plans to have Jesus put to death.

kjv: And when they had bound him, they led him away, and delivered him to Pontius Pilate the governor.
ct: They bound him, led him away, and handed him over to Pontius Pilate, the governor.

kjv: Then Judas, which had betrayed him, when he saw that he was condemned, repented himself, and brought again the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,
ct: When Judas, the one who had betrayed him, saw that Jesus had been condemned, he was overcome with remorse. He took the 30 pieces of silver back to the chief priests and elders.

kjv: Saying, I have sinned in that I have betrayed the innocent blood. And they said, What is that to us? see thou to that.
ct: "I have sinned," he said. "I betrayed an innocent man." But they replied, "Why should we care? That's your problem."

kjv: And he cast down the pieces of silver in the temple, and departed, and went and hanged himself.
ct: Judas threw the silver coins on the floor of the temple, walked out, and went and hanged himself.

kjv: And the chief priests took the silver pieces, and said, It is not lawful for to put them into the treasury, because it is the price of blood.
ct: The chief priests picked up the silver and said, "We can't put this in the temple treasury — it's blood money."

kjv: And they took counsel, and bought with them the potter’s field, to bury strangers in.
ct: So after discussing it, they used the money to buy the potter's field as a burial place for foreigners.

kjv: Wherefore that field was called, The field of blood, unto this day.
ct: That's why it's been called "The Field of Blood" ever since.

kjv: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremy the prophet, saying, And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of him that was valued, whom they of the children of Israel did value;
ct: This fulfilled what the prophet Jeremiah had spoken: "They took the 30 pieces of silver — the price set on him by the people of Israel —

kjv: And gave them for the potter’s field, as the Lord appointed me.
ct: and used them to buy the potter's field, just as the Lord directed me."

kjv: And Jesus stood before the governor: and the governor asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And Jesus said unto him, Thou sayest.
ct: Jesus stood before the governor, and Pilate asked him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" Jesus answered, "You have said so."

kjv: And when he was accused of the chief priests and elders, he answered nothing.
ct: When the chief priests and elders brought their accusations against him, Jesus said nothing.

kjv: Then said Pilate unto him, Hearest thou not how many things they witness against thee?
ct: Pilate asked him, "Don't you hear all these charges they're making against you?"

kjv: And he answered him to never a word; insomuch that the governor marvelled greatly.
ct: But Jesus didn't answer — not a single word. The governor was amazed.

kjv: Now at that feast the governor was wont to release unto the people a prisoner, whom they would.
ct: Now it was the governor's custom during the festival to release one prisoner — whichever one the crowd chose.

kjv: And they had then a notable prisoner, called Barabbas.
ct: At that time, they had a notorious prisoner named Barabbas.

kjv: Therefore when they were gathered together, Pilate said unto them, Whom will ye that I release unto you? Barabbas, or Jesus which is called Christ?
ct: So when the crowd had gathered, Pilate asked them, "Who do you want me to release — Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?"

kjv: For he knew that for envy they had delivered him.
ct: He knew perfectly well that they had handed Jesus over out of jealousy.

kjv: When he was set down on the judgment seat, his wife sent unto him, saying, Have thou nothing to do with that just man: for I have suffered many things this day in a dream because of him.
ct: While Pilate was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent him a message: "Don't have anything to do with that righteous man. I had a terrible dream about him today."

kjv: But the chief priests and elders persuaded the multitude that they should ask Barabbas, and destroy Jesus.
ct: But the chief priests and elders worked the crowd, convincing them to ask for Barabbas and to demand that Jesus be killed.

kjv: The governor answered and said unto them, Whether of the twain will ye that I release unto you? They said, Barabbas.
ct: The governor asked again, "Which of the two do you want me to release?" They shouted, "Barabbas!"

kjv: Pilate saith unto them, What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ? They all say unto him, Let him be crucified.
ct: Pilate asked, "Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ?" They all shouted, "Crucify him!"

kjv: And the governor said, Why, what evil hath he done? But they cried out the more, saying, Let him be crucified.
ct: "Why?" the governor asked. "What crime has he committed?" But they just screamed louder, "Crucify him!"

kjv: When Pilate saw that he could prevail nothing, but that rather a tumult was made, he took water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, I am innocent of the blood of this just person: see ye to it.
ct: When Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was about to break out, he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. "I am innocent of this righteous man's blood," he said. "This is on you."

kjv: Then answered all the people, and said, His blood be on us, and on our children.
ct: The whole crowd answered, "Let his blood be on us and on our children!"

kjv: Then released he Barabbas unto them: and when he had scourged Jesus, he delivered him to be crucified.
ct: So Pilate released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus flogged and then handed him over to be crucified.

kjv: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the common hall, and gathered unto him the whole band of soldiers.
ct: The governor's soldiers took Jesus into the Praetorium and assembled the entire garrison around him.

kjv: And they stripped him, and put on him a scarlet robe.
ct: They stripped him and draped a scarlet robe over him.

kjv: And when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon his head, and a reed in his right hand: and they bowed the knee before him, and mocked him, saying, Hail, King of the Jews!
ct: They twisted together a crown of thorns and shoved it onto his head, then placed a reed in his right hand. They knelt in front of him and mocked him, saying, "Hail, King of the Jews!"

kjv: And they spit upon him, and took the reed, and smote him on the head.
ct: They spit on him, grabbed the reed, and struck him on the head with it.

kjv: And after that they had mocked him, they took the robe off from him, and put his own raiment on him, and led him away to crucify him.
ct: After they finished mocking him, they pulled off the robe, put his own clothes back on him, and led him away to be crucified.

kjv: And as they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name: him they compelled to bear his cross.
ct: On the way out, they grabbed a man from Cyrene named Simon and forced him to carry Jesus' cross.

kjv: And when they were come unto a place called Golgotha, that is to say, a place of a skull,
ct: They arrived at a place called Golgotha, which means "The Place of the Skull."

kjv: They gave him vinegar to drink mingled with gall: and when he had tasted thereof, he would not drink.
ct: There they offered him wine mixed with gall to drink, but after tasting it, he refused it.

kjv: And they crucified him, and parted his garments, casting lots: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, They parted my garments among them, and upon my vesture did they cast lots.
ct: They crucified him and divided up his clothes by casting lots, fulfilling what the prophet had said: "They divided my garments among them and cast lots for my clothing."

kjv: And sitting down they watched him there;
ct: Then they sat down and kept watch over him.

kjv: And set up over his head his accusation written, THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
ct: Above his head they posted the charge against him: THIS IS JESUS, THE KING OF THE JEWS.

kjv: Then were there two thieves crucified with him, one on the right hand, and another on the left.
ct: Two thieves were crucified alongside him, one on his right and one on his left.

kjv: And they that passed by reviled him, wagging their heads,
ct: People passing by hurled insults at him, shaking their heads

kjv: And saying, Thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days, save thyself. If thou be the Son of God, come down from the cross.
ct: and saying, "You who were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in 3 days — save yourself! If you're the Son of God, come down from the cross!"

kjv: Likewise also the chief priests mocking him, with the scribes and elders, said,
ct: The chief priests, along with the scribes and elders, mocked him the same way.

kjv: He saved others; himself he cannot save. If he be the King of Israel, let him now come down from the cross, and we will believe him.
ct: "He saved others," they said, "but he can't save himself! If he's the King of Israel, let him come down from the cross right now, and we'll believe in him."

kjv: He trusted in God; let him deliver him now, if he will have him: for he said, I am the Son of God.
ct: "He trusts in God — let God rescue him now, if God even wants him! After all, he claimed, 'I am the Son of God.'"

kjv: The thieves also, which were crucified with him, cast the same in his teeth.
ct: Even the thieves crucified beside him threw the same insults at him.

kjv: Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land unto the ninth hour.
ct: From noon until 3 in the afternoon, darkness covered the entire land.

kjv: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani? that is to say, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
ct: Around 3 in the afternoon, Jesus cried out in a loud voice, "Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?" — which means, "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"

kjv: Some of them that stood there, when they heard that, said, This man calleth for Elias.
ct: Some of the people standing nearby heard this and said, "He's calling for Elijah."

kjv: And straightway one of them ran, and took a spunge, and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink.
ct: Immediately one of them ran and grabbed a sponge, soaked it in sour wine, stuck it on a reed, and held it up for him to drink.

kjv: The rest said, Let be, let us see whether Elias will come to save him.
ct: But the others said, "Leave him alone. Let's see if Elijah comes to save him."

kjv: Jesus, when he had cried again with a loud voice, yielded up the ghost.
ct: Jesus cried out once more in a loud voice and breathed his last.

kjv: And, behold, the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom; and the earth did quake, and the rocks rent;
ct: At that moment, the curtain of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook and rocks split apart.

kjv: And the graves were opened; and many bodies of the saints which slept arose,
ct: Tombs broke open, and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised to life.

kjv: And came out of the graves after his resurrection, and went into the holy city, and appeared unto many.
ct: After Jesus' resurrection, they came out of the tombs, went into the holy city, and appeared to many people.

kjv: Now when the centurion, and they that were with him, watching Jesus, saw the earthquake, and those things that were done, they feared greatly, saying, Truly this was the Son of God.
ct: When the centurion and the soldiers guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and everything that happened, they were terrified and said, "This truly was the Son of God."

kjv: And many women were there beholding afar off, which followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering unto him:
ct: Many women who had followed Jesus from Galilee and cared for his needs were watching from a distance.

kjv: Among which was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee’s children.
ct: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee's sons.

kjv: When the even was come, there came a rich man of Arimathaea, named Joseph, who also himself was Jesus’ disciple:
ct: That evening, a wealthy man from Arimathaea named Joseph arrived. He was a disciple of Jesus.

kjv: He went to Pilate, and begged the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be delivered.
ct: He went to Pilate and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate ordered that it be given to him.

kjv: And when Joseph had taken the body, he wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
ct: Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.

kjv: And laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock: and he rolled a great stone to the door of the sepulchre, and departed.
ct: He placed it in his own new tomb, which had been carved out of rock. Then he rolled a large stone across the entrance and left.

kjv: And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.
ct: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting there across from the tomb.

kjv: Now the next day, that followed the day of the preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees came together unto Pilate,
ct: The next day — the day after Preparation Day — the chief priests and Pharisees went together to Pilate.

kjv: Saying, Sir, we remember that that deceiver said, while he was yet alive, After three days I will rise again.
ct: "Sir," they said, "we remember that while he was still alive, that deceiver claimed, 'After 3 days I will rise again.'

kjv: Command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day, lest his disciples come by night, and steal him away, and say unto the people, He is risen from the dead: so the last error shall be worse than the first.
ct: So give the order to secure the tomb until the 3rd day. Otherwise, his disciples might come at night, steal the body, and tell the people, 'He has risen from the dead.' That deception would be even worse than the first."

kjv: Pilate said unto them, Ye have a watch: go your way, make it as sure as ye can.
ct: Pilate told them, "You have a guard. Go and make the tomb as secure as you can."

kjv: So they went, and made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone, and setting a watch.
ct: So they went, sealed the stone, and posted the guard to secure the tomb.

kjv: In the end of the sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.
ct: After the Sabbath ended, as Sunday morning began to dawn, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to visit the tomb.

kjv: And, behold, there was a great earthquake: for the angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door, and sat upon it.
ct: Suddenly there was a violent earthquake, because an angel of the Lord came down from heaven, rolled the stone away from the entrance, and sat on it.

kjv: His countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snow:
ct: His face blazed like lightning, and his clothes were as white as snow.

kjv: And for fear of him the keepers did shake, and became as dead men.
ct: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook uncontrollably and collapsed like dead men.

kjv: And the angel answered and said unto the women, Fear not ye: for I know that ye seek Jesus, which was crucified.
ct: The angel spoke to the women: "Don't be afraid. I know you're looking for Jesus, who was crucified."

kjv: He is not here: for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.
ct: "He isn't here — he has risen, just as he said he would! Come, see the place where the Lord was lying."

kjv: And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and, behold, he goeth before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him: lo, I have told you.
ct: "Now go quickly and tell his disciples: 'He has risen from the dead and is going ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there.' Remember, I've told you this."

kjv: And they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy; and did run to bring his disciples word.
ct: The women hurried away from the tomb, trembling with fear yet overwhelmed with joy, and ran to tell his disciples.

kjv: And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, All hail. And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.
ct: As they were on their way, Jesus himself met them and said, "Rejoice!" They rushed to him, grabbed hold of his feet, and worshiped him.

kjv: Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.
ct: Jesus told them, "Don't be afraid. Go tell my brothers to head to Galilee — they will see me there."

kjv: Now when they were going, behold, some of the watch came into the city, and shewed unto the chief priests all the things that were done.
ct: While the women were on their way, some of the guards went into the city and reported everything that had happened to the chief priests.

kjv: And when they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave large money unto the soldiers,
ct: The chief priests met with the elders and came up with a plan. They paid the soldiers a large sum of money

kjv: Saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.
ct: and told them, "Say that his disciples came during the night and stole the body while you were sleeping."

kjv: And if this come to the governor’s ears, we will persuade him, and secure you.
ct: "If the governor hears about this, we'll handle him and make sure you stay out of trouble."

kjv: So they took the money, and did as they were taught: and this saying is commonly reported among the Jews until this day.
ct: So the soldiers took the money and did what they were told. This story has been widely circulated among the Jewish people to this day.

kjv: Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountain where Jesus had appointed them.
ct: The 11 remaining disciples traveled to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had told them to go.

kjv: And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.
ct: When they saw him, they worshiped him — though some still had doubts.

kjv: And Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.
ct: Jesus came closer and said to them, "All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me."

kjv: Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:
ct: "So go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,"

kjv: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world. Amen.
ct: "and teaching them to follow everything I have commanded you. And know this: I am with you always, to the very end of the age." Amen.

kjv: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God;
ct: This is where the good news about Jesus Christ, the Son of God, begins.

kjv: As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
ct: As the prophets wrote long ago: "Look — I am sending my messenger ahead of you to prepare your way.

kjv: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
ct: A voice is calling out in the wilderness: 'Get the road ready for the Lord! Straighten out his paths!'"

kjv: John did baptize in the wilderness, and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
ct: John appeared in the wilderness, baptizing people and preaching that they should repent and be baptized for the forgiveness of their sins.

kjv: And there went out unto him all the land of Judaea, and they of Jerusalem, and were all baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.
ct: People from all across Judea and Jerusalem came out to see him. They confessed their sins and were baptized by him in the Jordan River.

kjv: And John was clothed with camel’s hair, and with a girdle of a skin about his loins; and he did eat locusts and wild honey;
ct: John wore clothes made of camel's hair with a leather belt around his waist, and he lived on locusts and wild honey.

kjv: And preached, saying, There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and unloose.
ct: He preached to the crowds, saying, "Someone far more powerful than me is coming after me. I'm not even worthy to kneel down and untie his sandals.

kjv: I indeed have baptized you with water: but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
ct: I've baptized you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit."

kjv: And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of John in Jordan.
ct: During that time, Jesus traveled from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.

kjv: And straightway coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon him:
ct: The moment he came up out of the water, he saw the heavens tear open and the Spirit coming down on him like a dove.

kjv: And there came a voice from heaven, saying, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
ct: Then a voice spoke from heaven: "You are my beloved Son, and I am deeply pleased with you."

kjv: And immediately the Spirit driveth him into the wilderness.
ct: Right away, the Spirit drove him out into the wilderness.

kjv: And he was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted of Satan; and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered unto him.
ct: He stayed in the wilderness for 40 days, being tempted by Satan. Wild animals surrounded him, and angels took care of him.

kjv: Now after that John was put in prison, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,
ct: After John was thrown in prison, Jesus went to Galilee and began preaching the good news of God's kingdom.

kjv: And saying, The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.
ct: He said, "The time has come! God's kingdom is near. Repent and believe the good news."

kjv: Now as he walked by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.
ct: As Jesus walked along the shore of the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew throwing a net into the water — they were fishermen.

kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.
ct: Jesus called out to them, "Come, follow me, and I'll make you fishers of men."

kjv: And straightway they forsook their nets, and followed him.
ct: They dropped their nets right then and followed him.

kjv: And when he had gone a little farther thence, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.
ct: A little farther down the shore, he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee, sitting in their boat and mending their nets.

kjv: And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, and went after him.
ct: He called them immediately. They left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers and went with Jesus.

kjv: And they went into Capernaum; and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue, and taught.
ct: They went to Capernaum, and on the Sabbath Jesus went straight into the synagogue and began teaching.

kjv: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for he taught them as one that had authority, and not as the scribes.
ct: The people were stunned by his teaching, because he spoke with real authority — nothing like the scribes.

kjv: And there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
ct: Just then, a man in the synagogue who was possessed by an unclean spirit screamed out,

kjv: Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Holy One of God.
ct: "What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know exactly who you are — the Holy One of God!"

kjv: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.
ct: Jesus commanded the spirit sharply, "Be quiet and come out of him!"

kjv: And when the unclean spirit had torn him, and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him.
ct: The unclean spirit threw the man into convulsions, let out a loud shriek, and came out of him.

kjv: And they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, What thing is this? what new doctrine is this? for with authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits, and they do obey him.
ct: Everyone was amazed. They turned to each other and said, "What is this? Some kind of new teaching? He gives orders to unclean spirits with authority, and they actually obey him!"

kjv: And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about Galilee.
ct: News about Jesus spread instantly across the entire region of Galilee.

kjv: And forthwith, when they were come out of the synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
ct: As soon as they left the synagogue, they went to the home of Simon and Andrew, along with James and John.

kjv: But Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick of a fever, and anon they tell him of her.
ct: Simon's mother-in-law was lying in bed with a fever, and they told Jesus about her right away.

kjv: And he came and took her by the hand, and lifted her up; and immediately the fever left her, and she ministered unto them.
ct: He went to her, took her hand, and helped her up. The fever left her instantly, and she began serving them.

kjv: And at even, when the sun did set, they brought unto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with devils.
ct: That evening after sunset, people brought to Jesus everyone who was sick or possessed by demons.

kjv: And all the city was gathered together at the door.
ct: The whole town crowded around the door.

kjv: And he healed many that were sick of divers diseases, and cast out many devils; and suffered not the devils to speak, because they knew him.
ct: He healed many people suffering from all kinds of diseases and drove out many demons. He wouldn't let the demons speak, because they knew who he was.

kjv: And in the morning, rising up a great while before day, he went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.
ct: Long before dawn the next morning, Jesus got up, slipped away to a deserted place, and prayed.

kjv: And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.
ct: Simon and the others went searching for him.

kjv: And when they had found him, they said unto him, All men seek for thee.
ct: When they found him, they said, "Everyone is looking for you!"

kjv: And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.
ct: Jesus replied, "Let's move on to the nearby towns so I can preach there too. That's why I came."

kjv: And he preached in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out devils.
ct: So he traveled throughout all of Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and driving out demons.

kjv: And there came a leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling down to him, and saying unto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
ct: A man with leprosy came to him, dropped to his knees, and begged, "If you're willing, you can make me clean."

kjv: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.
ct: Filled with compassion, Jesus reached out his hand and touched him. "I am willing," he said. "Be clean."

kjv: And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.
ct: The instant Jesus spoke, the leprosy vanished and the man was completely healed.

kjv: And he straitly charged him, and forthwith sent him away;
ct: Jesus gave him a stern warning and sent him away at once.

kjv: And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
ct: He told him, "Don't say a word about this to anyone. Go straight to the priest, show yourself to him, and make the offering that Moses required for your cleansing — so it serves as proof to everyone."

kjv: But he went out, and began to publish it much, and to blaze abroad the matter, insomuch that Jesus could no more openly enter into the city, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from every quarter.
ct: But the man went out and told everyone he could, spreading the news so widely that Jesus could no longer enter any town openly. He had to stay out in remote areas — and still people came to him from everywhere.

kjv: And again he entered into Capernaum after some days; and it was noised that he was in the house.
ct: After a few days, Jesus returned to Capernaum, and word quickly spread that he was home.

kjv: And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no room to receive them, no, not so much as about the door: and he preached the word unto them.
ct: So many people crowded into the house that there wasn't even standing room left near the door. Jesus was teaching them God's word.

kjv: And they come unto him, bringing one sick of the palsy, which was borne of four.
ct: 4 men arrived carrying a paralyzed man on a mat, trying to bring him to Jesus.

kjv: And when they could not come nigh unto him for the press, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken it up, they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.
ct: But they couldn't get anywhere near Jesus because of the crowd. So they climbed up on the roof, tore open a hole above where Jesus was standing, and lowered the paralyzed man down on his mat.

kjv: When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins be forgiven thee.
ct: When Jesus saw how much faith they had, he said to the paralyzed man, "Son, your sins are forgiven."

kjv: But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
ct: Some of the religious scholars were sitting nearby, and they began questioning this in their minds:

kjv: Why doth this man thus speak blasphemies? who can forgive sins but God only?
ct: "How dare he say something like that? That's blasphemy! Only God can forgive sins."

kjv: And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
ct: Jesus immediately knew in his spirit what they were thinking. He said to them, "Why are you questioning this in your hearts?

kjv: Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?
ct: Which is easier — to tell this paralyzed man, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up, pick up your mat, and walk'?

kjv: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)
ct: But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins." Then he turned to the paralyzed man and said,

kjv: I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.
ct: "Get up, pick up your mat, and go home."

kjv: And immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.
ct: The man stood up immediately, grabbed his mat, and walked out in front of everyone. The whole crowd was stunned. They praised God, saying, "We've never seen anything like this!"

kjv: And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.
ct: Jesus went out again along the shore of the lake. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.

kjv: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
ct: As he walked along, he noticed Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at his tax collection booth. Jesus said to him, "Follow me." Levi got up and followed him.

kjv: And it came to pass, that, as Jesus sat at meat in his house, many publicans and sinners sat also together with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.
ct: Later, Jesus was having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples, because a large number of them had begun following him.

kjv: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners, they said unto his disciples, How is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?
ct: When the scribes and Pharisees saw Jesus eating with tax collectors and sinners, they asked his disciples, "Why does he eat and drink with people like that?"

kjv: When Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
ct: Jesus overheard them and said, "Healthy people don't need a doctor — sick people do. I didn't come to call the righteous. I came to call sinners to repentance."

kjv: And the disciples of John and of the Pharisees used to fast: and they come and say unto him, Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?
ct: The disciples of John and the Pharisees were observing a time of fasting. Some people came to Jesus and asked, "John's disciples fast, and the Pharisees' disciples fast — why don't your disciples fast?"

kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
ct: Jesus answered, "Do wedding guests fast while the groom is still celebrating with them? Of course not! As long as the groom is with them, they can't fast.

kjv: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
ct: But the time will come when the groom is taken away from them, and then they will fast."

kjv: No man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.
ct: "Nobody patches an old garment with a piece of unshrunk cloth. If you do, the new patch will shrink and pull away from the old fabric, making the tear even worse.

kjv: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.
ct: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins either. The new wine would burst the old skins, and you'd lose both the wine and the skins. New wine needs fresh wineskins."

kjv: And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of corn.
ct: One Sabbath, Jesus was walking through the grain fields, and his disciples began picking heads of grain as they went.

kjv: And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
ct: The Pharisees confronted him: "Look! Why are your disciples doing what's not allowed on the Sabbath?"

kjv: And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
ct: Jesus replied, "Haven't you ever read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need?

kjv: How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?
ct: He went into God's house during the time of Abiathar the high priest and ate the sacred bread that only priests were allowed to eat — and he shared it with his men."

kjv: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
ct: Then he told them, "The Sabbath was made to serve people, not people to serve the Sabbath.

kjv: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
ct: So the Son of Man is Lord even over the Sabbath."

kjv: And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there which had a withered hand.
ct: Jesus went into the synagogue again, and a man with a paralyzed hand was there.

kjv: And they watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.
ct: The religious leaders watched him closely, looking for an excuse to accuse him if he healed the man on the Sabbath.

kjv: And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.
ct: Jesus told the man with the paralyzed hand, "Stand up and come to the front."

kjv: And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they held their peace.
ct: Then he turned to the others and asked, "Which is lawful on the Sabbath — doing good or doing harm? Saving a life or killing?" No one said a word.

kjv: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
ct: Jesus looked around at them with anger, deeply grieved by how hard their hearts had become. Then he said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." The man stretched it out, and it was completely restored — as healthy as the other.

kjv: And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway took counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
ct: The Pharisees left immediately and began plotting with the Herodians about how to destroy Jesus.

kjv: But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judaea,
ct: Jesus withdrew with his disciples to the Sea of Galilee, and a massive crowd followed him — people from Galilee, Judaea,

kjv: And from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea, and from beyond Jordan; and they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came unto him.
ct: Jerusalem, Idumaea, the region beyond the Jordan, and the area around Tyre and Sidon. This enormous crowd came to him because they'd heard about everything he was doing.

kjv: And he spake to his disciples, that a small ship should wait on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.
ct: He told his disciples to have a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldn't crush him.

kjv: For he had healed many; insomuch that they pressed upon him for to touch him, as many as had plagues.
ct: He had healed so many people that everyone with diseases was pushing forward just to touch him.

kjv: And unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.
ct: Whenever unclean spirits saw him, they threw themselves down in front of him and screamed, "You are the Son of God!"

kjv: And he straitly charged them that they should not make him known.
ct: But he sternly ordered them not to reveal who he was.

kjv: And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth unto him whom he would: and they came unto him.
ct: Jesus went up on a mountainside and called the ones he wanted, and they came to him.

kjv: And he ordained twelve, that they should be with him, and that he might send them forth to preach,
ct: He appointed 12 of them to be his closest companions and to be sent out to preach.

kjv: And to have power to heal sicknesses, and to cast out devils:
ct: He also gave them authority to heal the sick and cast out demons.

kjv: And Simon he surnamed Peter;
ct: These are the 12 he chose: Simon, whom he named Peter;

kjv: And James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sons of thunder:
ct: James son of Zebedee and his brother John — Jesus nicknamed them Boanerges, which means "Sons of Thunder";

kjv: And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Canaanite,
ct: Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James son of Alphaeus; Thaddaeus; Simon the Canaanite;

kjv: And Judas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they went into an house.
ct: and Judas Iscariot, who would later betray him. Then they went into a house.

kjv: And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
ct: The crowd gathered again in such numbers that Jesus and his disciples couldn't even find time to eat.

kjv: And when his friends heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself.
ct: When his family heard what was going on, they went to take him home by force, because they said, "He's lost his mind."

kjv: And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils.
ct: The scribes who had come down from Jerusalem were saying, "He's possessed by Beelzebub! He drives out demons by the power of the prince of demons."

kjv: And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?
ct: So Jesus called them over and spoke to them using illustrations: "How can Satan drive out Satan?

kjv: And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
ct: If a kingdom fights against itself, that kingdom can't survive.

kjv: And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
ct: If a household is torn apart by conflict, that household won't last.

kjv: And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
ct: So if Satan has turned against himself and is divided, he can't stand either — his end has come.

kjv: No man can enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.
ct: No one can break into a strong man's house and steal his possessions unless he first ties up the strong man. Only then can he ransack the house.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:
ct: I tell you the truth: every sin and every blasphemy people commit can be forgiven.

kjv: But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never forgiveness, but is in danger of eternal damnation:
ct: But whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven — that person is guilty of an eternal sin."

kjv: Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.
ct: He said this because they were claiming he had an unclean spirit.

kjv: There came then his brethren and his mother, and, standing without, sent unto him, calling him.
ct: Then his mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent someone in to call him.

kjv: And the multitude sat about him, and they said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.
ct: A crowd was sitting around Jesus, and they told him, "Your mother and your brothers are outside asking for you."

kjv: And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?
ct: He replied, "Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?"

kjv: And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
ct: Then he looked at the people sitting in a circle around him and said, "Here are my mother and my brothers!

kjv: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.
ct: Anyone who does the will of God — that person is my brother, my sister, and my mother."

kjv: And he began again to teach by the sea side: and there was gathered unto him a great multitude, so that he entered into a ship, and sat in the sea; and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land.
ct: Jesus began teaching again beside the sea. Such a huge crowd gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat down on the water, while the entire crowd stood along the shore.

kjv: And he taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his doctrine,
ct: He taught them many things using parables, and as he was teaching he said,

kjv: Hearken; Behold, there went out a sower to sow:
ct: "Listen! A farmer went out to plant his seed.

kjv: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.
ct: As he scattered it, some seed fell along the path, and birds came and ate it up.

kjv: And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:
ct: Some fell on rocky ground where there wasn't much soil. It sprouted quickly because the soil was so shallow.

kjv: But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
ct: But when the sun came up, it was scorched, and since it had no roots, it withered and died.

kjv: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
ct: Some seed fell among thorn bushes. The thorns grew up and choked it out, so it produced nothing.

kjv: And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.
ct: But some seed fell on good soil. It grew up strong and produced a harvest — 30, 60, even 100 times what was planted."

kjv: And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
ct: Then he said, "If you have ears to hear, then listen carefully."

kjv: And when he was alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable.
ct: Later, when Jesus was alone, the 12 disciples and the others close to him asked him what the parable meant.
