ct: The next morning, as they walked past the fig tree, they saw it had completely withered from the roots up.

kjv: And Peter calling to remembrance saith unto him, Master, behold, the fig tree which thou cursedst is withered away.
ct: Peter remembered what had happened and said, "Rabbi, look! The fig tree you cursed has completely withered!"

kjv: And Jesus answering saith unto them, Have faith in God.
ct: Jesus replied, "Have faith in God.

kjv: For verily I say unto you, That whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; and shall not doubt in his heart, but shall believe that those things which he saith shall come to pass; he shall have whatsoever he saith.
ct: I tell you the truth: if anyone says to this mountain, 'Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,' and doesn't doubt in his heart but truly believes that what he says will happen, it will be done for him.

kjv: Therefore I say unto you, What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them.
ct: That's why I'm telling you — whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you've received it, and it will be yours.

kjv: And when ye stand praying, forgive, if ye have ought against any: that your Father also which is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.
ct: And when you stand praying, if you're holding anything against someone, forgive them — so that your Father in heaven will also forgive your sins.

kjv: But if ye do not forgive, neither will your Father which is in heaven forgive your trespasses.
ct: But if you refuse to forgive, your Father in heaven won't forgive your sins either."

kjv: And they come again to Jerusalem: and as he was walking in the temple, there come to him the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders,
ct: They returned to Jerusalem, and while Jesus was walking through the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders approached him.

kjv: And say unto him, By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority to do these things?
ct: They demanded, "What authority do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?"

kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
ct: Jesus said to them, "I'll ask you one question. Answer me, and I'll tell you by what authority I do these things.

kjv: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men? answer me.
ct: John's baptism — was it from heaven, or was it a human invention? Answer me."

kjv: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then did ye not believe him?
ct: They debated among themselves: "If we say, 'From heaven,' he'll ask, 'Then why didn't you believe him?'

kjv: But if we shall say, Of men; they feared the people: for all men counted John, that he was a prophet indeed.
ct: But if we say, 'A human invention' — " they were afraid of the crowd, because everyone believed John was truly a prophet.

kjv: And they answered and said unto Jesus, We cannot tell. And Jesus answering saith unto them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.
ct: So they answered Jesus, "We don't know." Jesus replied, "Then I'm not going to tell you by what authority I do these things either."

kjv: And he began to speak unto them by parables. A certain man planted a vineyard, and set an hedge about it, and digged a place for the winefat, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country.
ct: Then Jesus began teaching them with parables. He said, "A man planted a vineyard, built a wall around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and put up a watchtower. Then he rented it out to some tenant farmers and left for a distant country.

kjv: And at the season he sent to the husbandmen a servant, that he might receive from the husbandmen of the fruit of the vineyard.
ct: When harvest season came, he sent a servant to the tenants to collect his share of the vineyard's fruit.

kjv: And they caught him, and beat him, and sent him away empty.
ct: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him back with nothing.

kjv: And again he sent unto them another servant; and at him they cast stones, and wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully handled.
ct: So the owner sent another servant. This time they threw stones at him, cracked his head open, and sent him away humiliated.

kjv: And again he sent another; and him they killed, and many others; beating some, and killing some.
ct: He sent yet another servant, and they killed that one. He sent many more after that — some they beat, and some they killed.

kjv: Having yet therefore one son, his wellbeloved, he sent him also last unto them, saying, They will reverence my son.
ct: He had one person left to send: his beloved son. Finally he sent him, thinking, 'Surely they will respect my son.'

kjv: But those husbandmen said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance shall be our’s.
ct: But the tenants said to each other, 'This is the heir! Let's kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.'

kjv: And they took him, and killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.
ct: So they grabbed him, killed him, and threw his body out of the vineyard.

kjv: What shall therefore the lord of the vineyard do? he will come and destroy the husbandmen, and will give the vineyard unto others.
ct: Now what do you think the owner of the vineyard will do? He'll come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.

kjv: And have ye not read this scripture; The stone which the builders rejected is become the head of the corner:
ct: Haven't you read this Scripture: 'The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.

kjv: This was the Lord’s doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?
ct: This was the Lord's doing, and it is an amazing thing to see'?"

kjv: And they sought to lay hold on him, but feared the people: for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them: and they left him, and went their way.
ct: The religious leaders wanted to arrest him because they knew the parable was aimed at them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him alone and walked away.

kjv: And they send unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, to catch him in his words.
ct: Later they sent some Pharisees and Herodians to Jesus, hoping to trap him with his own words.

kjv: And when they were come, they say unto him, Master, we know that thou art true, and carest for no man: for thou regardest not the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth: Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar, or not?
ct: They came to him and said, "Teacher, we know you're an honest man who isn't swayed by anyone's opinion. You don't play favorites — you truly teach the way of God. So tell us: is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?"

kjv: Shall we give, or shall we not give? But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them, Why tempt ye me? bring me a penny, that I may see it.
ct: "Should we pay, or shouldn't we?" But Jesus saw right through their hypocrisy and said, "Why are you trying to trap me? Bring me a denarius so I can look at it."

kjv: And they brought it. And he saith unto them, Whose is this image and superscription? And they said unto him, Caesar’s.
ct: They brought him one. He asked them, "Whose face and name are stamped on this coin?" They answered, "Caesar's."

kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s. And they marvelled at him.
ct: Jesus replied, "Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar, and give to God what belongs to God." They were completely amazed by his answer.

kjv: Then come unto him the Sadducees, which say there is no resurrection; and they asked him, saying,
ct: Next, some Sadducees came to him — these are the ones who say there's no resurrection — and they posed a question.

kjv: Master, Moses wrote unto us, If a man’s brother die, and leave his wife behind him, and leave no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
ct: "Teacher, Moses wrote that if a man dies and leaves a wife but no children, his brother must marry the widow and have children to carry on his brother's name.

kjv: Now there were seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and dying left no seed.
ct: Now, there were 7 brothers. The first one married a woman but died without having any children.

kjv: And the second took her, and died, neither left he any seed: and the third likewise.
ct: The second brother married her and also died without children. The same thing happened with the third.

kjv: And the seven had her, and left no seed: last of all the woman died also.
ct: In fact, all 7 brothers married her and died without leaving any children. Finally, the woman herself died too.

kjv: In the resurrection therefore, when they shall rise, whose wife shall she be of them? for the seven had her to wife.
ct: So here's our question: at the resurrection, when they all rise from the dead, whose wife will she be? After all, all 7 of them were married to her."

kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, Do ye not therefore err, because ye know not the scriptures, neither the power of God?
ct: Jesus replied, "You're completely wrong, and here's why — you don't know the Scriptures, and you don't understand the power of God.

kjv: For when they shall rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage; but are as the angels which are in heaven.
ct: When people rise from the dead, they won't marry or be given in marriage. They'll be like the angels in heaven.

kjv: And as touching the dead, that they rise: have ye not read in the book of Moses, how in the bush God spake unto him, saying, I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?
ct: Now, as for whether the dead are raised — haven't you read in the book of Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to him, 'I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'?

kjv: He is not the God of the dead, but the God of the living: ye therefore do greatly err.
ct: He is not the God of the dead but of the living. You are badly mistaken."

kjv: And one of the scribes came, and having heard them reasoning together, and perceiving that he had answered them well, asked him, Which is the first commandment of all?
ct: One of the scribes had been listening to the whole debate. Impressed by how well Jesus answered, he asked him, "Which commandment is the greatest of all?"

kjv: And Jesus answered him, The first of all the commandments is, Hear, O Israel; The Lord our God is one Lord:
ct: Jesus answered, "The greatest is this: 'Hear, O Israel: the Lord our God, the Lord is one.

kjv: And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind, and with all thy strength: this is the first commandment.
ct: Love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your mind, and with all your strength.' This is the first commandment.

kjv: And the second is like, namely this, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. There is none other commandment greater than these.
ct: The second is this: 'Love your neighbor as yourself.' No commandment is greater than these."

kjv: And the scribe said unto him, Well, Master, thou hast said the truth: for there is one God; and there is none other but he:
ct: The scribe said, "Well said, Teacher. You're absolutely right that God is one and there is no other besides him.

kjv: And to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbour as himself, is more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.
ct: To love him with all your heart, all your understanding, all your soul, and all your strength, and to love your neighbor as yourself — that matters more than all the burnt offerings and sacrifices combined."

kjv: And when Jesus saw that he answered discreetly, he said unto him, Thou art not far from the kingdom of God. And no man after that durst ask him any question.
ct: When Jesus saw how wisely the man answered, he told him, "You are not far from the kingdom of God." After that, no one dared ask him any more questions.

kjv: And Jesus answered and said, while he taught in the temple, How say the scribes that Christ is the son of David?
ct: While teaching in the temple, Jesus raised a question: "How can the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David?

kjv: For David himself said by the Holy Ghost, The LORD said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool.
ct: David himself, speaking through the Holy Spirit, said: 'The LORD said to my Lord, sit at my right hand until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.'

kjv: David therefore himself calleth him Lord; and whence is he then his son? And the common people heard him gladly.
ct: If David himself calls him 'Lord,' then how can he simply be David's son?" The large crowd listened to him with delight.

kjv: And he said unto them in his doctrine, Beware of the scribes, which love to go in long clothing, and love salutations in the marketplaces,
ct: As he taught, he warned them, "Watch out for the scribes. They love to walk around in long, flowing robes and be greeted with respect in the marketplaces.

kjv: And the chief seats in the synagogues, and the uppermost rooms at feasts:
ct: They grab the best seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.

kjv: Which devour widows’ houses, and for a pretence make long prayers: these shall receive greater damnation.
ct: They cheat widows out of their homes and then cover it up with long, showy prayers. These men will be punished most severely."

kjv: And Jesus sat over against the treasury, and beheld how the people cast money into the treasury: and many that were rich cast in much.
ct: Jesus sat down across from the temple treasury and watched as people dropped in their money. Many wealthy people put in large amounts.

kjv: And there came a certain poor widow, and she threw in two mites, which make a farthing.
ct: Then a poor widow came and dropped in 2 tiny copper coins, worth almost nothing.

kjv: And he called unto him his disciples, and saith unto them, Verily I say unto you, That this poor widow hath cast more in, than all they which have cast into the treasury:
ct: Jesus called his disciples over and said, "I tell you the truth: this poor widow has put in more than everyone else who gave to the treasury.

kjv: For all they did cast in of their abundance; but she of her want did cast in all that she had, even all her living.
ct: All the others gave out of their wealth, but she, out of her poverty, gave everything she had — every last coin she had to live on."

kjv: And as he went out of the temple, one of his disciples saith unto him, Master, see what manner of stones and what buildings are here!
ct: As Jesus was leaving the temple, one of his disciples said to him, "Teacher, look at these incredible stones and magnificent buildings!"

kjv: And Jesus answering said unto him, Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.
ct: Jesus replied, "You see all these great buildings? Not a single stone will be left standing on another. Every one of them will be torn down."

kjv: And as he sat upon the mount of Olives over against the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him privately,
ct: Later, Jesus sat on the Mount of Olives, looking across at the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew came to him privately and asked,

kjv: Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign when all these things shall be fulfilled?
ct: "Tell us, when will this happen? And what will be the sign that all of this is about to take place?"

kjv: And Jesus answering them began to say, Take heed lest any man deceive you:
ct: Jesus began to tell them, "Be on your guard so that no one deceives you.

kjv: For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.
ct: Many will come using my name, claiming, 'I am the Christ,' and they will deceive many people.

kjv: And when ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars, be ye not troubled: for such things must needs be; but the end shall not be yet.
ct: When you hear about wars and rumors of wars, don't be alarmed. These things have to happen, but it won't be the end yet.

kjv: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be earthquakes in divers places, and there shall be famines and troubles: these are the beginnings of sorrows.
ct: Nations will go to war against each other, and kingdoms will clash. There will be earthquakes in many places, along with famines and upheaval. But all of this is just the beginning — like the first pains of childbirth.

kjv: But take heed to yourselves: for they shall deliver you up to councils; and in the synagogues ye shall be beaten: and ye shall be brought before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them.
ct: Watch out for yourselves. You'll be handed over to local courts and beaten in synagogues. You'll stand trial before governors and kings because of me, and your testimony will be a witness to them.

kjv: And the gospel must first be published among all nations.
ct: But before the end comes, the gospel must be proclaimed to every nation.

kjv: But when they shall lead you, and deliver you up, take no thought beforehand what ye shall speak, neither do ye premeditate: but whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, that speak ye: for it is not ye that speak, but the Holy Ghost.
ct: When they arrest you and put you on trial, don't worry ahead of time about what to say. Don't rehearse your words. Just speak whatever is given to you in that moment, because it won't be you speaking — it will be the Holy Spirit.

kjv: Now the brother shall betray the brother to death, and the father the son; and children shall rise up against their parents, and shall cause them to be put to death.
ct: Brothers will hand over their own brothers to be killed. Fathers will betray their children. Children will turn against their parents and have them put to death.

kjv: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake: but he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.
ct: Everyone will hate you because you follow me. But whoever stands firm to the end will be saved.

kjv: But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not, (let him that readeth understand,) then let them that be in Judaea flee to the mountains:
ct: When you see the abomination of desolation — the thing Daniel the prophet spoke about — standing where it should never be (let the reader understand what this means), then everyone in Judea must flee to the mountains.

kjv: And let him that is on the housetop not go down into the house, neither enter therein, to take any thing out of his house:
ct: If you're on the roof of your house, don't go back inside to grab anything.

kjv: And let him that is in the field not turn back again for to take up his garment.
ct: If you're out in the field, don't go back to get your cloak.

kjv: But woe to them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!
ct: How terrible it will be for pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days!

kjv: And pray ye that your flight be not in the winter.
ct: Pray that your escape doesn't happen in winter.

kjv: For in those days shall be affliction, such as was not from the beginning of the creation which God created unto this time, neither shall be.
ct: Because the suffering in those days will be worse than anything the world has seen from the moment God created it until now — and nothing like it will ever happen again.

kjv: And except that the Lord had shortened those days, no flesh should be saved: but for the elect’s sake, whom he hath chosen, he hath shortened the days.
ct: If the Lord hadn't cut those days short, no one on earth would survive. But for the sake of his chosen people, he has shortened them.

kjv: And then if any man shall say to you, Lo, here is Christ; or, lo, he is there; believe him not:
ct: If anyone tells you at that time, 'Look, here is the Christ!' or 'He's over there!' — don't believe it.

kjv: For false Christs and false prophets shall rise, and shall shew signs and wonders, to seduce, if it were possible, even the elect.
ct: False christs and false prophets will appear and perform signs and wonders to deceive — if it were possible — even God's chosen people.

kjv: But take ye heed: behold, I have foretold you all things.
ct: So stay alert. I've warned you about all of this ahead of time.

kjv: But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light,
ct: In those days, after that time of suffering, the sun will go dark and the moon will stop shining.

kjv: And the stars of heaven shall fall, and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken.
ct: Stars will fall from the sky, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.

kjv: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
ct: Then everyone will see the Son of Man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.

kjv: And then shall he send his angels, and shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from the uttermost part of the earth to the uttermost part of heaven.
ct: He will send out his angels to gather his chosen people from every direction — from the farthest corners of the earth to the farthest reaches of the sky.

kjv: Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When her branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is near:
ct: "Learn this lesson from the fig tree: when its branches turn tender and its leaves begin to sprout, you know summer is close.

kjv: So ye in like manner, when ye shall see these things come to pass, know that it is nigh, even at the doors.
ct: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you'll know the time is near — right at the door.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, that this generation shall not pass, till all these things be done.
ct: I tell you the truth: this generation will not pass away before all these things take place.

kjv: Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.
ct: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.

kjv: But of that day and that hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels which are in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.
ct: No one knows the day or the hour when this will happen — not the angels in heaven, not even the Son — only the Father.

kjv: Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.
ct: Stay alert, keep watch, and pray, because you don't know when the time will come.

kjv: For the Son of man is as a man taking a far journey, who left his house, and gave authority to his servants, and to every man his work, and commanded the porter to watch.
ct: It's like a man who goes on a long journey. Before leaving, he puts his servants in charge, assigns each one a task, and tells the doorkeeper to stay alert.

kjv: Watch ye therefore: for ye know not when the master of the house cometh, at even, or at midnight, or at the cockcrowing, or in the morning:
ct: So stay watchful, because you don't know when the master of the house will return — whether in the evening, at midnight, at the rooster's crow, or at dawn.

kjv: Lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.
ct: Don't let him arrive suddenly and find you asleep.

kjv: And what I say unto you I say unto all, Watch.
ct: What I'm telling you, I'm telling everyone: Stay watchful!"

kjv: After two days was the feast of the passover, and of unleavened bread: and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might take him by craft, and put him to death.
ct: It was 2 days before the Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a way to arrest Jesus through some trick and have him killed.

kjv: But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar of the people.
ct: "Not during the festival," they said, "or the people might riot."

kjv: And being in Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at meat, there came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment of spikenard very precious; and she brake the box, and poured it on his head.
ct: While Jesus was in Bethany, eating at the home of Simon the leper, a woman came in carrying an alabaster jar of pure nard — an extremely expensive perfume. She broke the jar open and poured it over his head.

kjv: And there were some that had indignation within themselves, and said, Why was this waste of the ointment made?
ct: Some of those present were outraged. "Why would anyone waste perfume like that?" they said.

kjv: For it might have been sold for more than three hundred pence, and have been given to the poor. And they murmured against her.
ct: "That could have been sold for more than 300 denarii and the money given to the poor!" They scolded her harshly.

kjv: And Jesus said, Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me.
ct: But Jesus said, "Leave her alone. Why are you giving her a hard time? She's done something beautiful for me.

kjv: For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always.
ct: You'll always have the poor among you, and you can help them whenever you want. But you won't always have me.

kjv: She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying.
ct: She did what she could — she anointed my body ahead of time for burial.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.
ct: I tell you the truth: wherever the gospel is preached in the whole world, what this woman has done will also be told, and people will remember her for it."

kjv: And Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went unto the chief priests, to betray him unto them.
ct: Then Judas Iscariot, one of the 12 disciples, went to the chief priests to hand Jesus over to them.

kjv: And when they heard it, they were glad, and promised to give him money. And he sought how he might conveniently betray him.
ct: When they heard his offer, they were thrilled and promised to pay him. From that point on, Judas watched for the right moment to betray him.

kjv: And the first day of unleavened bread, when they killed the passover, his disciples said unto him, Where wilt thou that we go and prepare that thou mayest eat the passover?
ct: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, his disciples asked him, "Where do you want us to go and prepare the Passover meal for you?"

kjv: And he sendeth forth two of his disciples, and saith unto them, Go ye into the city, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him.
ct: He sent 2 of his disciples ahead, telling them, "Go into the city, and you'll meet a man carrying a pitcher of water. Follow him.

kjv: And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
ct: Whatever house he enters, tell the owner, 'The Teacher asks: Where is the guest room where I can eat the Passover with my disciples?'

kjv: And he will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared: there make ready for us.
ct: He'll show you a large upstairs room, already furnished and ready. Prepare the meal for us there."

kjv: And his disciples went forth, and came into the city, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.
ct: The disciples left, went into the city, and found everything exactly as Jesus had told them. They prepared the Passover meal.

kjv: And in the evening he cometh with the twelve.
ct: That evening, Jesus arrived with the 12.

kjv: And as they sat and did eat, Jesus said, Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.
ct: While they were sitting at the table eating, Jesus said, "I tell you the truth: one of you eating with me right now is going to betray me."

kjv: And they began to be sorrowful, and to say unto him one by one, Is it I? and another said, Is it I?
ct: They were deeply upset, and one by one they asked him, "It's not me, is it?" And another said, "Surely not me?"

kjv: And he answered and said unto them, It is one of the twelve, that dippeth with me in the dish.
ct: He told them, "It's one of the 12 — someone who is dipping bread into the dish with me right now.

kjv: The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had never been born.
ct: The Son of Man will go to his death just as the Scriptures say. But how terrible it will be for the man who betrays the Son of Man! It would have been better for that man if he had never been born."

kjv: And as they did eat, Jesus took bread, and blessed, and brake it, and gave to them, and said, Take, eat: this is my body.
ct: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, gave thanks, broke it, and handed it to them. "Take it and eat," he said. "This is my body."

kjv: And he took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave it to them: and they all drank of it.
ct: Then he took a cup, gave thanks, and passed it to them. They all drank from it.

kjv: And he said unto them, This is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many.
ct: He told them, "This is my blood of the new covenant, poured out for many.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, I will drink no more of the fruit of the vine, until that day that I drink it new in the kingdom of God.
ct: I tell you the truth: I won't drink the fruit of the vine again until the day I drink it new in the kingdom of God."

kjv: And when they had sung an hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.
ct: After singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

kjv: And Jesus saith unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered.
ct: Jesus told them, "Tonight, every one of you will fall away because of me. For the Scripture says, 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.'

kjv: But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.
ct: But after I have risen, I'll go ahead of you to Galilee."

kjv: But Peter said unto him, Although all shall be offended, yet will not I.
ct: Peter spoke up: "Even if everyone else falls away, I never will."

kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this day, even in this night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.
ct: Jesus said to him, "I tell you the truth: today — this very night — before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me 3 times."

kjv: But he spake the more vehemently, If I should die with thee, I will not deny thee in any wise. Likewise also said they all.
ct: But Peter insisted even more forcefully, "Even if I have to die with you, I will never deny you!" All the others said the same thing.

kjv: And they came to a place which was named Gethsemane: and he saith to his disciples, Sit ye here, while I shall pray.
ct: They came to a place called Gethsemane, and Jesus told his disciples, "Sit here while I pray."

kjv: And he taketh with him Peter and James and John, and began to be sore amazed, and to be very heavy;
ct: He took Peter, James, and John with him, and a wave of deep anguish and distress came over him.

kjv: And saith unto them, My soul is exceeding sorrowful unto death: tarry ye here, and watch.
ct: He said to them, "My soul is crushed with grief — to the point of death. Stay here and keep watch."

kjv: And he went forward a little, and fell on the ground, and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
ct: Going a little farther, he fell to the ground and prayed that if it were possible, this hour might pass him by.

kjv: And he said, Abba, Father, all things are possible unto thee; take away this cup from me: nevertheless not what I will, but what thou wilt.
ct: "Abba, Father," he said, "everything is possible for you. Take this cup away from me. Yet not what I want, but what you want."

kjv: And he cometh, and findeth them sleeping, and saith unto Peter, Simon, sleepest thou? couldest not thou watch one hour?
ct: He came back and found them sleeping. "Simon, are you asleep?" he said to Peter. "Couldn't you keep watch for just one hour?

kjv: Watch ye and pray, lest ye enter into temptation. The spirit truly is ready, but the flesh is weak.
ct: Stay awake and pray so that you won't fall into temptation. The spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak."

kjv: And again he went away, and prayed, and spake the same words.
ct: He went away again and prayed, saying the same words.

kjv: And when he returned, he found them asleep again, (for their eyes were heavy,) neither wist they what to answer him.
ct: When he returned, he found them sleeping again — their eyes were so heavy they couldn't keep them open. They didn't know what to say to him.

kjv: And he cometh the third time, and saith unto them, Sleep on now, and take your rest: it is enough, the hour is come; behold, the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
ct: He came back a 3rd time and said, "Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough! The hour has come. Look — the Son of Man is being handed over to sinners.

kjv: Rise up, let us go; lo, he that betrayeth me is at hand.
ct: Get up, let's go! My betrayer is here."

kjv: And immediately, while he yet spake, cometh Judas, one of the twelve, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
ct: The words were barely out of his mouth when Judas — one of the 12 — arrived with a large mob armed with swords and clubs, sent by the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.

kjv: And he that betrayed him had given them a token, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is he; take him, and lead him away safely.
ct: Judas had arranged a signal with them: "The one I kiss is the man you want. Grab him and take him away under guard."

kjv: And as soon as he was come, he goeth straightway to him, and saith, Master, master; and kissed him.
ct: The moment he arrived, Judas walked straight up to Jesus and said, "Rabbi! Rabbi!" and kissed him.

kjv: And they laid their hands on him, and took him.
ct: The men seized Jesus and held him.

kjv: And one of them that stood by drew a sword, and smote a servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
ct: One of the bystanders drew a sword, struck the high priest's servant, and cut off his ear.

kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and with staves to take me?
ct: Jesus said to them, "Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest me, as if I were some kind of criminal?

kjv: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.
ct: Day after day I was right there with you, teaching in the temple, and you never arrested me. But this is happening so that the Scriptures will be fulfilled."

kjv: And they all forsook him, and fled.
ct: Then every one of his disciples abandoned him and ran.

kjv: And there followed him a certain young man, having a linen cloth cast about his naked body; and the young men laid hold on him:
ct: A young man had been following Jesus, wearing nothing but a linen cloth. The mob grabbed at him,

kjv: And he left the linen cloth, and fled from them naked.
ct: but he slipped out of the cloth and ran away naked.

kjv: And they led Jesus away to the high priest: and with him were assembled all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes.
ct: They took Jesus to the high priest, where all the chief priests, elders, and scribes had gathered.

kjv: And Peter followed him afar off, even into the palace of the high priest: and he sat with the servants, and warmed himself at the fire.
ct: Peter followed at a distance, all the way into the courtyard of the high priest's residence. He sat down with the guards and warmed himself by the fire.

kjv: And the chief priests and all the council sought for witness against Jesus to put him to death; and found none.
ct: The chief priests and the entire council were trying to find testimony against Jesus so they could execute him, but they couldn't find any.

kjv: For many bare false witness against him, but their witness agreed not together.
ct: Many people gave false testimony against him, but their stories contradicted each other.

kjv: And there arose certain, and bare false witness against him, saying,
ct: Then some men stood up and gave this false testimony:

kjv: We heard him say, I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and within three days I will build another made without hands.
ct: "We heard him say, 'I will tear down this temple made by human hands, and in 3 days I will build another one, not made by human hands.'"

kjv: But neither so did their witness agree together.
ct: But even on this point, their stories didn't match.

kjv: And the high priest stood up in the midst, and asked Jesus, saying, Answerest thou nothing? what is it which these witness against thee?
ct: The high priest stood up before the assembly and demanded of Jesus, "Aren't you going to answer? What do you have to say about these accusations?"

kjv: But he held his peace, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, and said unto him, Art thou the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?
ct: But Jesus stayed silent and gave no answer. The high priest pressed him again: "Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed One?"

kjv: And Jesus said, I am: and ye shall see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.
ct: Jesus said, "I am. And you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven."

kjv: Then the high priest rent his clothes, and saith, What need we any further witnesses?
ct: The high priest tore his clothes and said, "Why do we need any more witnesses?

kjv: Ye have heard the blasphemy: what think ye? And they all condemned him to be guilty of death.
ct: You've heard the blasphemy! What's your verdict?" They all condemned him as deserving death.

kjv: And some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to buffet him, and to say unto him, Prophesy: and the servants did strike him with the palms of their hands.
ct: Some began spitting on him. They blindfolded him, punched him, and taunted, "Prophesy! Tell us who hit you!" The guards took him and slapped him.

kjv: And as Peter was beneath in the palace, there cometh one of the maids of the high priest:
ct: While Peter was down in the courtyard below, one of the high priest's servant girls came by.

kjv: And when she saw Peter warming himself, she looked upon him, and said, And thou also wast with Jesus of Nazareth.
ct: She noticed Peter warming himself, looked closely at him, and said, "You were with Jesus of Nazareth too."

kjv: But he denied, saying, I know not, neither understand I what thou sayest. And he went out into the porch; and the cock crew.
ct: But Peter denied it. "I don't know what you're talking about," he said. He walked out to the entryway, and the rooster crowed.

kjv: And a maid saw him again, and began to say to them that stood by, This is one of them.
ct: The servant girl saw him there and started telling the people standing around, "This man is one of them."

kjv: And he denied it again. And a little after, they that stood by said again to Peter, Surely thou art one of them: for thou art a Galilaean, and thy speech agreeth thereto.
ct: Peter denied it again. A little later, the bystanders said to Peter, "You're definitely one of them — you're a Galilean. Your accent gives you away."

kjv: But he began to curse and to swear, saying, I know not this man of whom ye speak.
ct: Peter began to call down curses on himself and swore, "I don't know this man you're talking about!"

kjv: And the second time the cock crew. And Peter called to mind the word that Jesus said unto him, Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice. And when he thought thereon, he wept.
ct: Immediately the rooster crowed a 2nd time. Then Peter remembered what Jesus had told him: "Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me 3 times." And he broke down and wept.

kjv: And straightway in the morning the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council, and bound Jesus, and carried him away, and delivered him to Pilate.
ct: At dawn, the chief priests met with the elders, the scribes, and the entire council. They bound Jesus, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate.

kjv: And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answering said unto him, Thou sayest it.
ct: Pilate asked him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" Jesus replied, "You have said it yourself."

kjv: And the chief priests accused him of many things: but he answered nothing.
ct: The chief priests piled accusation after accusation against him, but Jesus said nothing in response.

kjv: And Pilate asked him again, saying, Answerest thou nothing? behold how many things they witness against thee.
ct: Pilate questioned him again: "Don't you have anything to say? Look at how many charges they're bringing against you!"

kjv: But Jesus yet answered nothing; so that Pilate marvelled.
ct: But Jesus still didn't answer, which amazed Pilate.

kjv: Now at that feast he released unto them one prisoner, whomsoever they desired.
ct: Now it was customary during the festival for the governor to release one prisoner — whichever one the crowd requested.

kjv: And there was one named Barabbas, which lay bound with them that had made insurrection with him, who had committed murder in the insurrection.
ct: A man named Barabbas was in prison along with other rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.

kjv: And the multitude crying aloud began to desire him to do as he had ever done unto them.
ct: The crowd came forward and began asking Pilate to follow his usual custom of releasing a prisoner.

kjv: But Pilate answered them, saying, Will ye that I release unto you the King of the Jews?
ct: Pilate responded, "Do you want me to release the King of the Jews to you?"

kjv: For he knew that the chief priests had delivered him for envy.
ct: He could tell that the chief priests had handed Jesus over because they were jealous of him.

kjv: But the chief priests moved the people, that he should rather release Barabbas unto them.
ct: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to demand that Pilate release Barabbas instead.

kjv: And Pilate answered and said again unto them, What will ye then that I shall do unto him whom ye call the King of the Jews?
ct: Pilate asked them again, "Then what do you want me to do with the man you call the King of the Jews?"

kjv: And they cried out again, Crucify him.
ct: They shouted back, "Crucify him!"

kjv: Then Pilate said unto them, Why, what evil hath he done? And they cried out the more exceedingly, Crucify him.
ct: "Why? What crime has he committed?" Pilate asked. But they only screamed louder, "Crucify him!"

kjv: And so Pilate, willing to content the people, released Barabbas unto them, and delivered Jesus, when he had scourged him, to be crucified.
ct: Wanting to satisfy the crowd, Pilate released Barabbas to them. Then he had Jesus flogged and handed him over to be crucified.

kjv: And the soldiers led him away into the hall, called Praetorium; and they call together the whole band.
ct: The soldiers led Jesus into the courtyard of the governor's headquarters, called the Praetorium, and assembled the entire cohort around him.

kjv: And they clothed him with purple, and platted a crown of thorns, and put it about his head,
ct: They dressed him in a purple robe, twisted together a crown of thorns, and pressed it onto his head.

kjv: And began to salute him, Hail, King of the Jews!
ct: Then they began mocking him with salutes: "Hail, King of the Jews!"

kjv: And they smote him on the head with a reed, and did spit upon him, and bowing their knees worshipped him.
ct: They beat him on the head with a stick, spit on him, and dropped to their knees in fake worship.

kjv: And when they had mocked him, they took off the purple from him, and put his own clothes on him, and led him out to crucify him.
ct: When they were done mocking him, they stripped off the purple robe, put his own clothes back on him, and led him out to be crucified.

kjv: And they compel one Simon a Cyrenian, who passed by, coming out of the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to bear his cross.
ct: A man named Simon from Cyrene — the father of Alexander and Rufus — was passing by on his way in from the countryside. The soldiers forced him to carry Jesus' cross.

kjv: And they bring him unto the place Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, The place of a skull.
ct: They brought Jesus to a place called Golgotha, which means "The Place of the Skull."

kjv: And they gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh: but he received it not.
ct: They offered him wine mixed with myrrh, but he refused to drink it.

kjv: And when they had crucified him, they parted his garments, casting lots upon them, what every man should take.
ct: After they crucified him, the soldiers divided up his clothes by casting lots to see who would get each piece.

kjv: And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.
ct: It was 9 in the morning when they crucified him.

kjv: And the superscription of his accusation was written over, THE KING OF THE JEWS.
ct: A sign was posted above him stating the charge against him: THE KING OF THE JEWS.

kjv: And with him they crucify two thieves; the one on his right hand, and the other on his left.
ct: They also crucified 2 criminals with him — one on his right and one on his left.

kjv: And the scripture was fulfilled, which saith, And he was numbered with the transgressors.
ct: This fulfilled the Scripture that says, "He was counted with transgressors."

kjv: And they that passed by railed on him, wagging their heads, and saying, Ah, thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days,
ct: People walking by hurled insults at him, shaking their heads and saying, "Ha! You who were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in 3 days —

kjv: Save thyself, and come down from the cross.
ct: save yourself and come down from the cross!"

kjv: Likewise also the chief priests mocking said among themselves with the scribes, He saved others; himself he cannot save.
ct: The chief priests and scribes mocked him the same way among themselves: "He saved others, but he can't save himself.

kjv: Let Christ the King of Israel descend now from the cross, that we may see and believe. And they that were crucified with him reviled him.
ct: Let this 'Christ,' this 'King of Israel,' come down from the cross right now so we can see it and believe!" Even the men crucified alongside him hurled insults at him.

kjv: And when the sixth hour was come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.
ct: At noon, darkness fell over the entire land and lasted until 3 in the afternoon.

kjv: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani? which is, being interpreted, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
ct: At 3 in the afternoon, Jesus cried out in a loud voice, "Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?" — which means, "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"

kjv: And some of them that stood by, when they heard it, said, Behold, he calleth Elias.
ct: Some of the bystanders heard him and said, "Listen — he's calling for Elijah."

kjv: And one ran and filled a spunge full of vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink, saying, Let alone; let us see whether Elias will come to take him down.
ct: One man ran and soaked a sponge in sour wine, stuck it on a stick, and held it up for Jesus to drink. "Leave him alone," he said. "Let's see if Elijah comes to take him down."

kjv: And Jesus cried with a loud voice, and gave up the ghost.
ct: Then Jesus let out a loud cry and breathed his last.

kjv: And the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom.
ct: At that moment, the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.

kjv: And when the centurion, which stood over against him, saw that he so cried out, and gave up the ghost, he said, Truly this man was the Son of God.
ct: The Roman centurion standing right across from Jesus saw how he died and said, "Truly, this man was the Son of God!"

kjv: There were also women looking on afar off: among whom was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;
ct: A group of women watched from a distance. Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James the younger and Joses, and Salome.

kjv: (Who also, when he was in Galilee, followed him, and ministered unto him;) and many other women which came up with him unto Jerusalem.
ct: These women had followed Jesus and served him when he was in Galilee. Many other women who had traveled with him to Jerusalem were there as well.

kjv: And now when the even was come, because it was the preparation, that is, the day before the sabbath,
ct: That evening — since it was Preparation Day, the day before the Sabbath —

kjv: Joseph of Arimathaea, an honourable counsellor, which also waited for the kingdom of God, came, and went in boldly unto Pilate, and craved the body of Jesus.
ct: Joseph of Arimathaea, a respected member of the council who was himself waiting for God's Kingdom, gathered his courage, went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus' body.

kjv: And Pilate marvelled if he were already dead: and calling unto him the centurion, he asked him whether he had been any while dead.
ct: Pilate was surprised that Jesus could already be dead. He summoned the centurion and asked if Jesus had been dead for long.

kjv: And when he knew it of the centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.
ct: Once the centurion confirmed it, Pilate released the body to Joseph.

kjv: And he bought fine linen, and took him down, and wrapped him in the linen, and laid him in a sepulchre which was hewn out of a rock, and rolled a stone unto the door of the sepulchre.
ct: Joseph bought a linen cloth, took the body down from the cross, wrapped it in the cloth, and placed it in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. Then he rolled a stone across the entrance.

kjv: And Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses beheld where he was laid.
ct: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses watched carefully and saw where he was laid.

kjv: And when the sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, had bought sweet spices, that they might come and anoint him.
ct: After the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so they could go and anoint Jesus' body.

kjv: And very early in the morning the first day of the week, they came unto the sepulchre at the rising of the sun.
ct: Very early on Sunday morning, just as the sun was rising, they headed to the tomb.

kjv: And they said among themselves, Who shall roll us away the stone from the door of the sepulchre?
ct: On the way, they asked each other, "Who's going to roll that stone away from the entrance of the tomb for us?"

kjv: And when they looked, they saw that the stone was rolled away: for it was very great.
ct: But when they looked up, they saw that the stone had already been rolled away — and it was massive.

kjv: And entering into the sepulchre, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, clothed in a long white garment; and they were affrighted.
ct: They stepped inside the tomb and saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed in a long white robe. They were terrified.

kjv: And he saith unto them, Be not affrighted: Ye seek Jesus of Nazareth, which was crucified: he is risen; he is not here: behold the place where they laid him.
ct: He told them, "Don't be afraid. You're looking for Jesus of Nazareth, the one who was crucified. He has risen! He isn't here. Look — this is where they laid his body."

kjv: But go your way, tell his disciples and Peter that he goeth before you into Galilee: there shall ye see him, as he said unto you.
ct: "Now go tell his disciples — and Peter — that he is going ahead of you to Galilee. You'll see him there, just as he told you."

kjv: And they went out quickly, and fled from the sepulchre; for they trembled and were amazed: neither said they any thing to any man; for they were afraid.
ct: The women rushed out and fled from the tomb, shaking and overwhelmed. They didn't say a word to anyone, because they were terrified.

kjv: Now when Jesus was risen early the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had cast seven devils.
ct: After Jesus rose early on Sunday morning, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, the woman he had freed from 7 demons.

kjv: And she went and told them that had been with him, as they mourned and wept.
ct: She went and told his companions, who were grieving and weeping.

kjv: And they, when they had heard that he was alive, and had been seen of her, believed not.
ct: But when they heard that he was alive and that she had seen him, they refused to believe it.

kjv: After that he appeared in another form unto two of them, as they walked, and went into the country.
ct: After that, Jesus appeared in a different form to 2 of them as they were walking out into the countryside.

kjv: And they went and told it unto the residue: neither believed they them.
ct: They went back and told the others, but no one believed them either.

kjv: Afterward he appeared unto the eleven as they sat at meat, and upbraided them with their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they believed not them which had seen him after he was risen.
ct: Later, Jesus appeared to the 11 disciples while they were eating together. He rebuked them for their stubborn unbelief, because they had refused to believe the people who saw him after he had risen.

kjv: And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature.
ct: Then he told them, "Go into all the world and preach the good news to everyone.

kjv: He that believeth and is baptized shall be saved; but he that believeth not shall be damned.
ct: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever refuses to believe will be condemned.

kjv: And these signs shall follow them that believe; In my name shall they cast out devils; they shall speak with new tongues;
ct: These signs will accompany those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons and speak in new languages.

kjv: They shall take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it shall not hurt them; they shall lay hands on the sick, and they shall recover.
ct: They will pick up serpents, and if they drink anything deadly, it won't harm them. They will place their hands on the sick, and the sick will be healed."

kjv: So then after the Lord had spoken unto them, he was received up into heaven, and sat on the right hand of God.
ct: After the Lord Jesus finished speaking to them, he was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.

kjv: And they went forth, and preached every where, the Lord working with them, and confirming the word with signs following. Amen.
ct: The disciples went out and preached everywhere, and the Lord worked alongside them, confirming his word through the miraculous signs that followed. Amen.

kjv: Forasmuch as many have taken in hand to set forth in order a declaration of those things which are most surely believed among us,
ct: Many people have already attempted to write an orderly account of the events that have taken place among us.

kjv: Even as they delivered them unto us, which from the beginning were eyewitnesses, and ministers of the word;
ct: They based their accounts on what was handed down to us by those who were eyewitnesses from the beginning and who became servants of the word.

kjv: It seemed good to me also, having had perfect understanding of all things from the very first, to write unto thee in order, most excellent Theophilus,
ct: So I also decided, after carefully investigating everything from the very start, to write it all down for you in an orderly way, most excellent Theophilus,

kjv: That thou mightest know the certainty of those things, wherein thou hast been instructed.
ct: so that you can be completely confident about the things you've been taught.

kjv: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judaea, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the course of Abia: and his wife was of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elisabeth.
ct: During the reign of King Herod of Judea, there was a priest named Zacharias who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elisabeth was a descendant of Aaron.

kjv: And they were both righteous before God, walking in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord blameless.
ct: Both of them were righteous before God, carefully following all the LORD's commandments and regulations without fault.

kjv: And they had no child, because that Elisabeth was barren, and they both were now well stricken in years.
ct: But they had no children because Elisabeth couldn't conceive, and by now they were both very old.

kjv: And it came to pass, that while he executed the priest’s office before God in the order of his course,
ct: One day, when Zacharias's division was on duty, he was serving as priest before God.

kjv: According to the custom of the priest’s office, his lot was to burn incense when he went into the temple of the Lord.
ct: Following the custom of the priesthood, he was chosen by lot to enter the temple of the Lord and burn incense.

kjv: And the whole multitude of the people were praying without at the time of incense.
ct: A large crowd of worshipers was praying outside during the time the incense was being offered.

kjv: And there appeared unto him an angel of the Lord standing on the right side of the altar of incense.
ct: Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the incense altar.

kjv: And when Zacharias saw him, he was troubled, and fear fell upon him.
ct: When Zacharias saw the angel, he was shaken, and fear gripped him.

kjv: But the angel said unto him, Fear not, Zacharias: for thy prayer is heard; and thy wife Elisabeth shall bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name John.
ct: But the angel said to him, "Don't be afraid, Zacharias. Your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elisabeth will give birth to a son, and you are to name him John."

kjv: And thou shalt have joy and gladness; and many shall rejoice at his birth.
ct: "He will bring you great joy and gladness, and many people will celebrate his birth."

kjv: For he shall be great in the sight of the Lord, and shall drink neither wine nor strong drink; and he shall be filled with the Holy Ghost, even from his mother’s womb.
ct: "He will be great in the sight of the Lord. He must never drink wine or any other alcoholic drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before he is born."

kjv: And many of the children of Israel shall he turn to the Lord their God.
ct: "He will bring many of the people of Israel back to the Lord their God."

kjv: And he shall go before him in the spirit and power of Elias, to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just; to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
ct: "He will go ahead of the Lord with the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of fathers back to their children and to lead the disobedient toward the wisdom of the righteous — preparing a people who are ready for the Lord."

kjv: And Zacharias said unto the angel, Whereby shall I know this? for I am an old man, and my wife well stricken in years.
ct: Zacharias said to the angel, "How can I be sure of this? I'm an old man, and my wife is well along in years."

kjv: And the angel answering said unto him, I am Gabriel, that stand in the presence of God; and am sent to speak unto thee, and to shew thee these glad tidings.
ct: The angel answered, "I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I was sent to speak to you and bring you this good news."

kjv: And, behold, thou shalt be dumb, and not able to speak, until the day that these things shall be performed, because thou believest not my words, which shall be fulfilled in their season.
ct: "But now, because you didn't believe my words — which will come true at the right time — you will be unable to speak. You'll remain silent until the day these things happen."

kjv: And the people waited for Zacharias, and marvelled that he tarried so long in the temple.
ct: Meanwhile, the people outside were waiting for Zacharias and wondering why he was taking so long in the temple.

kjv: And when he came out, he could not speak unto them: and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple: for he beckoned unto them, and remained speechless.
ct: When he finally came out, he couldn't speak to them. They realized he must have seen a vision inside the temple, because he kept trying to communicate with gestures but remained unable to say a word.

kjv: And it came to pass, that, as soon as the days of his ministration were accomplished, he departed to his own house.
ct: When his time of service at the temple was finished, he went back home.

kjv: And after those days his wife Elisabeth conceived, and hid herself five months, saying,
ct: Soon after, his wife Elisabeth became pregnant. She stayed in seclusion for 5 months, saying,

kjv: Thus hath the Lord dealt with me in the days wherein he looked on me, to take away my reproach among men.
ct: "The Lord has done this for me! He has looked on me with favor and taken away the disgrace I carried among people."

kjv: And in the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent from God unto a city of Galilee, named Nazareth,
ct: In the 6th month of Elisabeth's pregnancy, God sent the angel Gabriel to a town in Galilee called Nazareth,

kjv: To a virgin espoused to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David; and the virgin’s name was Mary.
ct: to a virgin who was engaged to a man named Joseph, a descendant of David. The virgin's name was Mary.

kjv: And the angel came in unto her, and said, Hail, thou that art highly favoured, the Lord is with thee: blessed art thou among women.
ct: The angel came to her and said, "Greetings, you who are highly favored! The Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women!"

kjv: And when she saw him, she was troubled at his saying, and cast in her mind what manner of salutation this should be.
ct: Mary was deeply troubled by his words and tried to figure out what kind of greeting this could be.

kjv: And the angel said unto her, Fear not, Mary: for thou hast found favour with God.
ct: The angel said to her, "Don't be afraid, Mary. You have found favor with God."

kjv: And, behold, thou shalt conceive in thy womb, and bring forth a son, and shalt call his name JESUS.
ct: "You will become pregnant and give birth to a son, and you are to name him Jesus."

kjv: He shall be great, and shall be called the Son of the Highest: and the Lord God shall give unto him the throne of his father David:
ct: "He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his ancestor David."

kjv: And he shall reign over the house of Jacob for ever; and of his kingdom there shall be no end.
ct: "He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and his kingdom will never end."

kjv: Then said Mary unto the angel, How shall this be, seeing I know not a man?
ct: Mary asked the angel, "How can this happen? I am a virgin."

kjv: And the angel answered and said unto her, The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee, and the power of the Highest shall overshadow thee: therefore also that holy thing which shall be born of thee shall be called the Son of God.
ct: The angel answered, "The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. For that reason, the holy child born to you will be called the Son of God."

kjv: And, behold, thy cousin Elisabeth, she hath also conceived a son in her old age: and this is the sixth month with her, who was called barren.
ct: "And listen — your relative Elisabeth has also conceived a son in her old age. She who was called barren is now in her 6th month."

kjv: For with God nothing shall be impossible.
ct: "For nothing is impossible with God."

kjv: And Mary said, Behold the handmaid of the Lord; be it unto me according to thy word. And the angel departed from her.
ct: Mary said, "I am the Lord's servant. Let it happen to me just as you've said." Then the angel left her.

kjv: And Mary arose in those days, and went into the hill country with haste, into a city of Juda;
ct: In those days, Mary got up and hurried to a town in the hill country of Judah.

kjv: And entered into the house of Zacharias, and saluted Elisabeth.
ct: She entered Zacharias's house and greeted Elisabeth.

kjv: And it came to pass, that, when Elisabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe leaped in her womb; and Elisabeth was filled with the Holy Ghost:
ct: The moment Elisabeth heard Mary's greeting, the baby leaped inside her womb, and Elisabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.

kjv: And she spake out with a loud voice, and said, Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.
ct: She cried out in a loud voice, "Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the child you carry!"

kjv: And whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
ct: "Why am I so honored that the mother of my Lord would come to visit me?"

kjv: For, lo, as soon as the voice of thy salutation sounded in mine ears, the babe leaped in my womb for joy.
ct: "The instant the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby in my womb jumped for joy!"

kjv: And blessed is she that believed: for there shall be a performance of those things which were told her from the Lord.
ct: "Blessed is the woman who believed that the Lord would fulfill what he promised her!"

kjv: And Mary said, My soul doth magnify the Lord,
ct: Then Mary said, "My soul magnifies the Lord,

kjv: And my spirit hath rejoiced in God my Saviour.
ct: and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,

kjv: For he hath regarded the low estate of his handmaiden: for, behold, from henceforth all generations shall call me blessed.
ct: because he has noticed the humble state of his servant. From now on, every generation will call me blessed,

kjv: For he that is mighty hath done to me great things; and holy is his name.
ct: because the Mighty One has done great things for me. Holy is his name.

kjv: And his mercy is on them that fear him from generation to generation.
ct: His mercy extends from generation to generation to those who fear him.

kjv: He hath shewed strength with his arm; he hath scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.
ct: He has shown the strength of his arm. He has scattered those who are proud in the thoughts of their hearts.

kjv: He hath put down the mighty from their seats, and exalted them of low degree.
ct: He has pulled the mighty down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.

kjv: He hath filled the hungry with good things; and the rich he hath sent empty away.
ct: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away with nothing.

kjv: He hath holpen his servant Israel, in remembrance of his mercy;
ct: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy,

kjv: As he spake to our fathers, to Abraham, and to his seed for ever.
ct: just as he promised our ancestors — to Abraham and his descendants forever."

kjv: And Mary abode with her about three months, and returned to her own house.
ct: Mary stayed with Elisabeth for about 3 months and then returned home.

kjv: Now Elisabeth’s full time came that she should be delivered; and she brought forth a son.
ct: When the time came for Elisabeth to have her baby, she gave birth to a son.

kjv: And her neighbours and her cousins heard how the Lord had shewed great mercy upon her; and they rejoiced with her.
ct: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy, and they celebrated with her.

kjv: And it came to pass, that on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child; and they called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.
ct: On the 8th day, they came to circumcise the child, and they were going to name him Zacharias after his father.

kjv: And his mother answered and said, Not so; but he shall be called John.
ct: But his mother spoke up: "No! He is to be called John."

kjv: And they said unto her, There is none of thy kindred that is called by this name.
ct: They said to her, "But no one in your family has that name."

kjv: And they made signs to his father, how he would have him called.
ct: So they made signs to his father, asking what he wanted the child to be named.

kjv: And he asked for a writing table, and wrote, saying, His name is John. And they marvelled all.
ct: He asked for a writing tablet and wrote, "His name is John." Everyone was amazed.

kjv: And his mouth was opened immediately, and his tongue loosed, and he spake, and praised God.
ct: Instantly, his mouth was opened and his tongue was freed, and he began to speak, praising God.

kjv: And fear came on all that dwelt round about them: and all these sayings were noised abroad throughout all the hill country of Judaea.
ct: Fear came over everyone living in the area, and people talked about all these events throughout the entire hill country of Judea.

kjv: And all they that heard them laid them up in their hearts, saying, What manner of child shall this be! And the hand of the Lord was with him.
ct: Everyone who heard about it took it to heart, asking, "What is this child going to become?" For the hand of the Lord was clearly with him.

kjv: And his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Ghost, and prophesied, saying,
ct: Then his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied:

kjv: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel; for he hath visited and redeemed his people,
ct: "Praise the Lord, the God of Israel, because he has come to his people and set them free.

kjv: And hath raised up an horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David;
ct: He has raised up a mighty savior for us from the house of his servant David,

kjv: As he spake by the mouth of his holy prophets, which have been since the world began:
ct: just as he promised long ago through his holy prophets.

kjv: That we should be saved from our enemies, and from the hand of all that hate us;
ct: He promised salvation from our enemies and from the power of all who hate us.

kjv: To perform the mercy promised to our fathers, and to remember his holy covenant;
ct: He has shown mercy to our ancestors and remembered his holy covenant —

kjv: The oath which he sware to our father Abraham,
ct: the oath he swore to our father Abraham —

kjv: That he would grant unto us, that we being delivered out of the hand of our enemies might serve him without fear,
ct: to rescue us from the grip of our enemies so we could serve him without fear,

kjv: In holiness and righteousness before him, all the days of our life.
ct: living in holiness and righteousness before him all the days of our lives.

kjv: And thou, child, shalt be called the prophet of the Highest: for thou shalt go before the face of the Lord to prepare his ways;
ct: And you, my child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, because you will go ahead of the Lord to prepare the way for him,

kjv: To give knowledge of salvation unto his people by the remission of their sins,
ct: giving his people the knowledge of salvation through the forgiveness of their sins,

kjv: Through the tender mercy of our God; whereby the dayspring from on high hath visited us,
ct: because of the tender mercy of our God. A new dawn from heaven will break upon us,

kjv: To give light to them that sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.
ct: shining light on those sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, and guiding our feet onto the path of peace."

kjv: And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit, and was in the deserts till the day of his shewing unto Israel.
ct: The child grew up and became strong in spirit. He lived out in the wilderness until the day he appeared publicly to Israel.

kjv: And it came to pass in those days, that there went out a decree from Caesar Augustus, that all the world should be taxed.
ct: Around that time, Caesar Augustus issued a decree requiring a census of the entire Roman world.

kjv: (And this taxing was first made when Cyrenius was governor of Syria.)
ct: This was the first census taken while Quirinius was governor of Syria.

kjv: And all went to be taxed, every one into his own city.
ct: Everyone had to travel to their ancestral hometown to be registered.

kjv: And Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judaea, unto the city of David, which is called Bethlehem; (because he was of the house and lineage of David:)
ct: So Joseph traveled from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to the city of David called Bethlehem, because he was a descendant of David's family line.

kjv: To be taxed with Mary his espoused wife, being great with child.
ct: He went to register there along with Mary, who was pledged to marry him and was expecting a child.

kjv: And so it was, that, while they were there, the days were accomplished that she should be delivered.
ct: While they were in Bethlehem, the time came for her baby to be born.

kjv: And she brought forth her firstborn son, and wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger; because there was no room for them in the inn.
ct: She gave birth to her firstborn son, wrapped him snugly in strips of cloth, and laid him in a manger, because there was no room for them at the inn.

kjv: And there were in the same country shepherds abiding in the field, keeping watch over their flock by night.
ct: Nearby, shepherds were out in the fields, keeping watch over their flocks through the night.

kjv: And, lo, the angel of the Lord came upon them, and the glory of the Lord shone round about them: and they were sore afraid.
ct: Suddenly an angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord blazed all around them. They were terrified.

kjv: And the angel said unto them, Fear not: for, behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy, which shall be to all people.
ct: But the angel said to them, "Don't be afraid! I'm here to bring you wonderful news that will fill everyone with great joy."

kjv: For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Saviour, which is Christ the Lord.
ct: "Today in the city of David, a Savior has been born for you — he is Christ the Lord."

kjv: And this shall be a sign unto you; Ye shall find the babe wrapped in swaddling clothes, lying in a manger.
ct: "Here's how you'll recognize him: you'll find a baby wrapped in strips of cloth, lying in a manger."

kjv: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God, and saying,
ct: Then all at once, a massive army of angels appeared alongside the first, praising God and saying,

kjv: Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.
ct: "Glory to God in the highest heavens, and on earth peace and good will toward men."

kjv: And it came to pass, as the angels were gone away from them into heaven, the shepherds said one to another, Let us now go even unto Bethlehem, and see this thing which is come to pass, which the Lord hath made known unto us.
ct: After the angels left them and returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, "Let's go to Bethlehem right now and see this thing the Lord has told us about!"

kjv: And they came with haste, and found Mary, and Joseph, and the babe lying in a manger.
ct: They hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.

kjv: And when they had seen it, they made known abroad the saying which was told them concerning this child.
ct: After seeing the child, they spread the word everywhere about what the angel had told them about him.

kjv: And all they that heard it wondered at those things which were told them by the shepherds.
ct: Everyone who heard the shepherds' story was amazed.

kjv: But Mary kept all these things, and pondered them in her heart.
ct: But Mary treasured all these things, turning them over and over in her heart.

kjv: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, as it was told unto them.
ct: The shepherds headed back to their flocks, praising and glorifying God for everything they had heard and seen — it was exactly as the angel had told them.

kjv: And when eight days were accomplished for the circumcising of the child, his name was called JESUS, which was so named of the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
ct: On the 8th day, when it was time for the child to be circumcised, they named him Jesus — the name the angel had given him before he was even conceived.

kjv: And when the days of her purification according to the law of Moses were accomplished, they brought him to Jerusalem, to present him to the Lord;
ct: When the time came for the purification ceremony required by the law of Moses, Joseph and Mary brought Jesus to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord.

kjv: (As it is written in the law of the LORD, Every male that openeth the womb shall be called holy to the Lord;)
ct: This was in keeping with what is written in the law of the LORD: "Every firstborn male shall be set apart as holy to the Lord."

kjv: And to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.
ct: They also offered the sacrifice required by the law of the Lord: "A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons."

kjv: And, behold, there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simeon; and the same man was just and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was upon him.
ct: Now there was a man in Jerusalem named Simeon. He was righteous and deeply devoted to God, eagerly waiting for God to comfort and rescue Israel. The Holy Spirit rested on him,

kjv: And it was revealed unto him by the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.
ct: and the Holy Spirit had revealed to him that he would not die before he had seen the Lord's Christ.

kjv: And he came by the Spirit into the temple: and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him after the custom of the law,
ct: Led by the Spirit, Simeon went into the temple that day. When Mary and Joseph brought the child Jesus in to carry out the customary requirements of the law,

kjv: Then took he him up in his arms, and blessed God, and said,
ct: Simeon took the baby in his arms, praised God, and said,

kjv: Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word:
ct: "Lord, now you can let your servant die in peace, just as you promised."

kjv: For mine eyes have seen thy salvation,
ct: "My own eyes have seen your salvation —"
