kjv: Which thou hast prepared before the face of all people;
ct: "which you have prepared for all people to see —"

kjv: A light to lighten the Gentiles, and the glory of thy people Israel.
ct: "a light to reveal truth to the Gentiles, and the glory of your people Israel."

kjv: And Joseph and his mother marvelled at those things which were spoken of him.
ct: Joseph and his mother were amazed at the things Simeon said about the child.

kjv: And Simeon blessed them, and said unto Mary his mother, Behold, this child is set for the fall and rising again of many in Israel; and for a sign which shall be spoken against;
ct: Then Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, "Listen carefully: this child will cause the falling and rising of many in Israel. He will be a sign that people will speak against."

kjv: (Yea, a sword shall pierce through thy own soul also,) that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.
ct: "And a sword will pierce your own soul as well — so that the hidden thoughts of many hearts will be exposed."

kjv: And there was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Aser: she was of a great age, and had lived with an husband seven years from her virginity;
ct: There was also a prophetess named Anna, the daughter of Phanuel, from the tribe of Asher. She was very old — she had been married for 7 years before her husband died,

kjv: And she was a widow of about fourscore and four years, which departed not from the temple, but served God with fastings and prayers night and day.
ct: and she had been a widow for about 84 years. She never left the temple, worshiping God day and night with fasting and prayer.

kjv: And she coming in that instant gave thanks likewise unto the Lord, and spake of him to all them that looked for redemption in Jerusalem.
ct: She came up to them at that very moment, gave thanks to the Lord, and began telling everyone who was waiting for God's redemption in Jerusalem about this child.

kjv: And when they had performed all things according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city Nazareth.
ct: Once Joseph and Mary had completed everything required by the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their hometown of Nazareth.

kjv: And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit, filled with wisdom: and the grace of God was upon him.
ct: The child grew and became strong in spirit, filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was on him.

kjv: Now his parents went to Jerusalem every year at the feast of the passover.
ct: Every year, Jesus' parents traveled to Jerusalem for the feast of the Passover.

kjv: And when he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem after the custom of the feast.
ct: When Jesus was 12 years old, they went up to Jerusalem as usual for the festival.

kjv: And when they had fulfilled the days, as they returned, the child Jesus tarried behind in Jerusalem; and Joseph and his mother knew not of it.
ct: After the celebration ended, they started the journey home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didn't realize it.

kjv: But they, supposing him to have been in the company, went a day’s journey; and they sought him among their kinsfolk and acquaintance.
ct: Assuming he was somewhere in the traveling group, they went a full day's journey before they started looking for him among their relatives and friends.

kjv: And when they found him not, they turned back again to Jerusalem, seeking him.
ct: When they couldn't find him, they turned around and went back to Jerusalem to search for him.

kjv: And it came to pass, that after three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the doctors, both hearing them, and asking them questions.
ct: After 3 days, they finally found him in the temple, sitting among the religious teachers, listening to them and asking questions.

kjv: And all that heard him were astonished at his understanding and answers.
ct: Everyone who heard him was stunned by his understanding and his answers.

kjv: And when they saw him, they were amazed: and his mother said unto him, Son, why hast thou thus dealt with us? behold, thy father and I have sought thee sorrowing.
ct: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said to him, "Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been searching for you everywhere, worried sick!"

kjv: And he said unto them, How is it that ye sought me? wist ye not that I must be about my Father’s business?
ct: He replied, "Why were you searching for me? Didn't you know that I had to be in my Father's house?"

kjv: And they understood not the saying which he spake unto them.
ct: But they didn't understand what he meant.

kjv: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth, and was subject unto them: but his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.
ct: Then he went back to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother held all these things close in her heart.

kjv: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favour with God and man.
ct: Jesus grew in wisdom and stature, and in favor with both God and people.

kjv: Now in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judaea, and Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip tetrarch of Ituraea and of the region of Trachonitis, and Lysanias the tetrarch of Abilene,
ct: It was the 15th year of Emperor Tiberius Caesar's reign. Pontius Pilate was governor of Judaea, Herod was tetrarch of Galilee, his brother Philip was tetrarch of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was tetrarch of Abilene.

kjv: Annas and Caiaphas being the high priests, the word of God came unto John the son of Zacharias in the wilderness.
ct: Annas and Caiaphas were serving as high priests. During this time, the word of God came to John, the son of Zacharias, out in the wilderness.

kjv: And he came into all the country about Jordan, preaching the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins;
ct: John traveled throughout the region around the Jordan River, preaching that people should be baptized as a sign of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.

kjv: As it is written in the book of the words of Esaias the prophet, saying, The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
ct: This fulfilled what was written in the book of the prophet Esaias: "A voice is calling out in the wilderness, 'Prepare the way of the Lord! Make his paths straight.

kjv: Every valley shall be filled, and every mountain and hill shall be brought low; and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough ways shall be made smooth;
ct: Every valley will be filled in, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked roads will be straightened, and the rough paths will be made smooth.

kjv: And all flesh shall see the salvation of God.
ct: And all people everywhere will see the salvation of God.'"

kjv: Then said he to the multitude that came forth to be baptized of him, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
ct: Crowds of people came out to be baptized by John, but he confronted them: "You brood of vipers! Who warned you to run from the coming wrath?

kjv: Bring forth therefore fruits worthy of repentance, and begin not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham to our father: for I say unto you, That God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.
ct: Prove your repentance by the way you live. And don't start telling yourselves, 'We're safe because Abraham is our ancestor.' Let me tell you — God could turn these stones into children of Abraham!

kjv: And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees: every tree therefore which bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.
ct: The axe is already at the root of the trees. Every tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into the fire."

kjv: And the people asked him, saying, What shall we do then?
ct: The people in the crowd asked him, "Then what should we do?"

kjv: He answereth and saith unto them, He that hath two coats, let him impart to him that hath none; and he that hath meat, let him do likewise.
ct: He answered, "If you have 2 coats, give one to someone who has none. If you have food, share it."

kjv: Then came also publicans to be baptized, and said unto him, Master, what shall we do?
ct: Even tax collectors came to be baptized and asked him, "Teacher, what should we do?"

kjv: And he said unto them, Exact no more than that which is appointed you.
ct: He told them, "Don't collect anything more than what you're authorized to collect."

kjv: And the soldiers likewise demanded of him, saying, And what shall we do? And he said unto them, Do violence to no man, neither accuse any falsely; and be content with your wages.
ct: Some soldiers asked him too, "What about us? What should we do?" He said, "Don't extort money from anyone or make false accusations. Be satisfied with your pay."

kjv: And as the people were in expectation, and all men mused in their hearts of John, whether he were the Christ, or not;
ct: The people were filled with anticipation, and everyone was wondering in their hearts whether John might be the Christ.

kjv: John answered, saying unto them all, I indeed baptize you with water; but one mightier than I cometh, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to unloose: he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire:
ct: John addressed them all: "I baptize you with water, but someone far more powerful than me is coming. I'm not even worthy to untie the straps of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.

kjv: Whose fan is in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and will gather the wheat into his garner; but the chaff he will burn with fire unquenchable.
ct: He has his winnowing fork in hand, ready to thoroughly clean his threshing floor. He'll gather the wheat into his barn, but the chaff he'll burn with fire that never goes out."

kjv: And many other things in his exhortation preached he unto the people.
ct: With many other urgent appeals like these, John proclaimed the good news to the people.

kjv: But Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him for Herodias his brother Philip’s wife, and for all the evils which Herod had done,
ct: But when John rebuked Herod the tetrarch for marrying Herodias, his brother Philip's wife, and for all the other evil things Herod had done,

kjv: Added yet this above all, that he shut up John in prison.
ct: Herod added one more crime to the list — he locked John up in prison.

kjv: Now when all the people were baptized, it came to pass, that Jesus also being baptized, and praying, the heaven was opened,
ct: Before that happened, when all the people were being baptized, Jesus was baptized too. As he was praying, heaven opened up,

kjv: And the Holy Ghost descended in a bodily shape like a dove upon him, and a voice came from heaven, which said, Thou art my beloved Son; in thee I am well pleased.
ct: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in a visible form like a dove. Then a voice spoke from heaven: "You are my beloved Son; in you I am well pleased."

kjv: And Jesus himself began to be about thirty years of age, being (as was supposed) the son of Joseph, which was the son of Heli,
ct: Jesus was about 30 years old when he began his ministry. He was known as the son of Joseph, who was the son of Heli,

kjv: Which was the son of Matthat, which was the son of Levi, which was the son of Melchi, which was the son of Janna, which was the son of Joseph,
ct: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Janna, the son of Joseph,

kjv: Which was the son of Mattathias, which was the son of Amos, which was the son of Naum, which was the son of Esli, which was the son of Nagge,
ct: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Naum, the son of Esli, the son of Nagge,

kjv: Which was the son of Maath, which was the son of Mattathias, which was the son of Semei, which was the son of Joseph, which was the son of Juda,
ct: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semei, the son of Joseph, the son of Juda,

kjv: Which was the son of Joanna, which was the son of Rhesa, which was the son of Zorobabel, which was the son of Salathiel, which was the son of Neri,
ct: the son of Joanna, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zorobabel, the son of Salathiel, the son of Neri,

kjv: Which was the son of Melchi, which was the son of Addi, which was the son of Cosam, which was the son of Elmodam, which was the son of Er,
ct: the son of Melchi, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmodam, the son of Er,

kjv: Which was the son of Jose, which was the son of Eliezer, which was the son of Jorim, which was the son of Matthat, which was the son of Levi,
ct: the son of Jose, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,

kjv: Which was the son of Simeon, which was the son of Juda, which was the son of Joseph, which was the son of Jonan, which was the son of Eliakim,
ct: the son of Simeon, the son of Juda, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonan, the son of Eliakim,

kjv: Which was the son of Melea, which was the son of Menan, which was the son of Mattatha, which was the son of Nathan, which was the son of David,
ct: the son of Melea, the son of Menan, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,

kjv: Which was the son of Jesse, which was the son of Obed, which was the son of Booz, which was the son of Salmon, which was the son of Naasson,
ct: the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Booz, the son of Salmon, the son of Naasson,

kjv: Which was the son of Aminadab, which was the son of Aram, which was the son of Esrom, which was the son of Phares, which was the son of Juda,
ct: the son of Aminadab, the son of Aram, the son of Esrom, the son of Phares, the son of Juda,

kjv: Which was the son of Jacob, which was the son of Isaac, which was the son of Abraham, which was the son of Thara, which was the son of Nachor,
ct: the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Thara, the son of Nachor,

kjv: Which was the son of Saruch, which was the son of Ragau, which was the son of Phalec, which was the son of Heber, which was the son of Sala,
ct: the son of Saruch, the son of Ragau, the son of Phalec, the son of Heber, the son of Sala,

kjv: Which was the son of Cainan, which was the son of Arphaxad, which was the son of Sem, which was the son of Noe, which was the son of Lamech,
ct: the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Sem, the son of Noe, the son of Lamech,

kjv: Which was the son of Mathusala, which was the son of Enoch, which was the son of Jared, which was the son of Maleleel, which was the son of Cainan,
ct: the son of Mathusala, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Maleleel, the son of Cainan,

kjv: Which was the son of Enos, which was the son of Seth, which was the son of Adam, which was the son of God.
ct: the son of Enos, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.

kjv: And Jesus being full of the Holy Ghost returned from Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness,
ct: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan River and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.

kjv: Being forty days tempted of the devil. And in those days he did eat nothing: and when they were ended, he afterward hungered.
ct: For 40 days the devil tempted him there. He ate nothing during that entire time, and when it was over, he was starving.

kjv: And the devil said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, command this stone that it be made bread.
ct: The devil said to him, "If you are the Son of God, tell this stone to turn into bread."

kjv: And Jesus answered him, saying, It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.
ct: Jesus answered, "It is written: 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.'"

kjv: And the devil, taking him up into an high mountain, shewed unto him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
ct: Then the devil led him up to a high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a single instant.

kjv: And the devil said unto him, All this power will I give thee, and the glory of them: for that is delivered unto me; and to whomsoever I will I give it.
ct: The devil told him, "I will give you all this authority and all their glory, because it has been handed over to me, and I can give it to anyone I choose.

kjv: If thou therefore wilt worship me, all shall be thine.
ct: So if you worship me, it will all be yours."

kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto him, Get thee behind me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.
ct: Jesus answered, "Get behind me, Satan! For it is written: 'You shall worship the Lord your God, and you shall serve him only.'"

kjv: And he brought him to Jerusalem, and set him on a pinnacle of the temple, and said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down from hence:
ct: Then the devil brought him to Jerusalem and placed him on the highest point of the temple. He said, "If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down from here.

kjv: For it is written, He shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee:
ct: For it is written: 'He will command his angels to watch over you and keep you safe,'

kjv: And in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.
ct: and, 'They will lift you up in their hands so that you won't even strike your foot against a stone.'"

kjv: And Jesus answering said unto him, It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.
ct: Jesus replied, "It is said: 'You shall not tempt the Lord your God.'"

kjv: And when the devil had ended all the temptation, he departed from him for a season.
ct: When the devil had finished every temptation, he left Jesus alone — but only until the next opportunity.

kjv: And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee: and there went out a fame of him through all the region round about.
ct: Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and news about him spread throughout the entire region.

kjv: And he taught in their synagogues, being glorified of all.
ct: He taught in their synagogues, and everyone praised him.

kjv: And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up: and, as his custom was, he went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and stood up for to read.
ct: He traveled to Nazareth, the town where he'd grown up. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he always did and stood up to read.

kjv: And there was delivered unto him the book of the prophet Esaias. And when he had opened the book, he found the place where it was written,
ct: The scroll of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. He unrolled it and found the passage that says:

kjv: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach deliverance to the captives, and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty them that are bruised,
ct: "The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to bring good news to the poor. He has sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to announce freedom for the captives, sight for the blind, and to set free those who are crushed,

kjv: To preach the acceptable year of the Lord.
ct: and to declare the year of the Lord's favor."

kjv: And he closed the book, and he gave it again to the minister, and sat down. And the eyes of all them that were in the synagogue were fastened on him.
ct: He rolled up the scroll, handed it back to the attendant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.

kjv: And he began to say unto them, This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears.
ct: Then he said, "Today, this Scripture has been fulfilled right as you heard it."

kjv: And all bare him witness, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth. And they said, Is not this Joseph’s son?
ct: Everyone spoke well of him and marveled at the gracious words that came from his mouth. But then they asked, "Wait — isn't this Joseph's son?"

kjv: And he said unto them, Ye will surely say unto me this proverb, Physician, heal thyself: whatsoever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in thy country.
ct: He said to them, "No doubt you'll quote this proverb at me: 'Doctor, heal yourself!' You'll say, 'Do here in your hometown what we've heard you did in Capernaum.'"

kjv: And he said, Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country.
ct: Then he added, "I tell you the truth — no prophet is accepted in his own hometown.

kjv: But I tell you of a truth, many widows were in Israel in the days of Elias, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, when great famine was throughout all the land;
ct: Believe me, there were many widows in Israel during the time of Elijah, when the sky was shut for 3 and a half years and a severe famine struck the whole land.

kjv: But unto none of them was Elias sent, save unto Sarepta, a city of Sidon, unto a woman that was a widow.
ct: Yet Elijah wasn't sent to any of them. Instead, he was sent to a widow in Zarephath, in the region of Sidon.

kjv: And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Eliseus the prophet; and none of them was cleansed, saving Naaman the Syrian.
ct: And there were many lepers in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, yet not one of them was healed — only Naaman, a Syrian."

kjv: And all they in the synagogue, when they heard these things, were filled with wrath,
ct: When the people in the synagogue heard this, they were furious.

kjv: And rose up, and thrust him out of the city, and led him unto the brow of the hill whereon their city was built, that they might cast him down headlong.
ct: They jumped to their feet, forced him out of the city, and dragged him to the edge of the hill their town was built on, intending to throw him off the cliff.

kjv: But he passing through the midst of them went his way,
ct: But Jesus walked right through the middle of the crowd and went on his way.

kjv: And came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and taught them on the sabbath days.
ct: He went down to Capernaum, a city in Galilee, and taught the people on the Sabbath.

kjv: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for his word was with power.
ct: They were amazed at his teaching because his words carried real authority.

kjv: And in the synagogue there was a man, which had a spirit of an unclean devil, and cried out with a loud voice,
ct: In the synagogue there was a man possessed by the spirit of an unclean demon, and he screamed at the top of his voice,

kjv: Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art; the Holy One of God.
ct: "Leave us alone! What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — the Holy One of God!"

kjv: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him. And when the devil had thrown him in the midst, he came out of him, and hurt him not.
ct: Jesus commanded him sharply, "Be silent and come out of him!" The demon threw the man down in front of everyone, then came out without hurting him.

kjv: And they were all amazed, and spake among themselves, saying, What a word is this! for with authority and power he commandeth the unclean spirits, and they come out.
ct: Everyone was stunned. They said to each other, "What kind of authority is this? He commands unclean spirits with power, and they obey him!"

kjv: And the fame of him went out into every place of the country round about.
ct: News about him spread to every corner of the surrounding region.

kjv: And he arose out of the synagogue, and entered into Simon’s house. And Simon’s wife’s mother was taken with a great fever; and they besought him for her.
ct: Jesus left the synagogue and went to Simon's house. Simon's mother-in-law was suffering from a severe fever, and they begged Jesus to help her.

kjv: And he stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her: and immediately she arose and ministered unto them.
ct: He stood over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. She got up immediately and began serving them.

kjv: Now when the sun was setting, all they that had any sick with divers diseases brought them unto him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.
ct: As the sun was going down, people brought everyone they knew who was sick with all kinds of diseases to Jesus. He placed his hands on each one of them and healed them.

kjv: And devils also came out of many, crying out, and saying, Thou art Christ the Son of God. And he rebuking them suffered them not to speak: for they knew that he was Christ.
ct: Demons also came out of many people, shouting, "You are Christ, the Son of God!" But he rebuked them and wouldn't let them speak, because they knew he was the Christ.

kjv: And when it was day, he departed and went into a desert place: and the people sought him, and came unto him, and stayed him, that he should not depart from them.
ct: At daybreak, Jesus left and went to a remote place. The crowds searched for him, and when they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving.

kjv: And he said unto them, I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.
ct: But he told them, "I have to preach the good news of God's Kingdom to other towns too. That's why I was sent."

kjv: And he preached in the synagogues of Galilee.
ct: So he continued preaching in the synagogues throughout Galilee.

kjv: And it came to pass, that, as the people pressed upon him to hear the word of God, he stood by the lake of Gennesaret,
ct: One day, as crowds were pressing in around Jesus to hear the word of God, he was standing by the lake of Gennesaret.

kjv: And saw two ships standing by the lake: but the fishermen were gone out of them, and were washing their nets.
ct: He noticed 2 boats at the water's edge. The fishermen had left them and were washing their nets.

kjv: And he entered into one of the ships, which was Simon’s, and prayed him that he would thrust out a little from the land. And he sat down, and taught the people out of the ship.
ct: He climbed into one of the boats — it belonged to Simon — and asked him to push out a little from shore. Then he sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.

kjv: Now when he had left speaking, he said unto Simon, Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught.
ct: When he finished speaking, he told Simon, "Take the boat out into deep water and let down your nets for a catch."

kjv: And Simon answering said unto him, Master, we have toiled all the night, and have taken nothing: nevertheless at thy word I will let down the net.
ct: Simon replied, "Master, we worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, I'll let down the net."

kjv: And when they had this done, they inclosed a great multitude of fishes: and their net brake.
ct: When they did, they hauled in such an enormous number of fish that their net started tearing apart.

kjv: And they beckoned unto their partners, which were in the other ship, that they should come and help them. And they came, and filled both the ships, so that they began to sink.
ct: They signaled to their partners in the other boat to come help. They came, and both boats were filled so full of fish that they started to sink.

kjv: When Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, Depart from me; for I am a sinful man, O Lord.
ct: When Simon Peter saw what had happened, he dropped to his knees in front of Jesus and said, "Go away from me, Lord — I'm a sinful man!"

kjv: For he was astonished, and all that were with him, at the draught of the fishes which they had taken:
ct: He and everyone with him were completely stunned by the size of the catch they had pulled in.

kjv: And so was also James, and John, the sons of Zebedee, which were partners with Simon. And Jesus said unto Simon, Fear not; from henceforth thou shalt catch men.
ct: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, who were Simon's fishing partners, were just as amazed. But Jesus told Simon, "Don't be afraid. From now on, you'll be catching people."

kjv: And when they had brought their ships to land, they forsook all, and followed him.
ct: As soon as they brought their boats to shore, they left everything behind and followed him.

kjv: And it came to pass, when he was in a certain city, behold a man full of leprosy: who seeing Jesus fell on his face, and besought him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
ct: While Jesus was in one of the towns, a man covered with leprosy saw him, fell facedown on the ground, and begged, "Lord, if you're willing, you can make me clean."

kjv: And he put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will: be thou clean. And immediately the leprosy departed from him.
ct: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him. "I am willing," he said. "Be clean." Instantly the leprosy vanished.

kjv: And he charged him to tell no man: but go, and shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
ct: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. "Instead," he said, "go show yourself to the priest and make the offering for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will serve as proof to everyone."

kjv: But so much the more went there a fame abroad of him: and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.
ct: But word about Jesus spread even faster, and huge crowds gathered to hear him and to be healed of their diseases.

kjv: And he withdrew himself into the wilderness, and prayed.
ct: Jesus, however, often slipped away to deserted places to pray.

kjv: And it came to pass on a certain day, as he was teaching, that there were Pharisees and doctors of the law sitting by, which were come out of every town of Galilee, and Judaea, and Jerusalem: and the power of the Lord was present to heal them.
ct: One day while Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee, from Judaea, and from Jerusalem. The power of the Lord was present for him to heal.

kjv: And, behold, men brought in a bed a man which was taken with a palsy: and they sought means to bring him in, and to lay him before him.
ct: Some men arrived carrying a paralyzed man on a mat, trying to find a way to bring him inside and lay him in front of Jesus.

kjv: And when they could not find by what way they might bring him in because of the multitude, they went upon the housetop, and let him down through the tiling with his couch into the midst before Jesus.
ct: When they couldn't get through the crowd, they went up on the roof, removed some tiles, and lowered the man on his mat right into the middle of the room in front of Jesus.

kjv: And when he saw their faith, he said unto him, Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.
ct: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man, "Friend, your sins are forgiven."

kjv: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, Who is this which speaketh blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?
ct: The scribes and the Pharisees immediately started arguing among themselves: "Who does this man think he is, speaking blasphemies? Only God can forgive sins!"

kjv: But when Jesus perceived their thoughts, he answering said unto them, What reason ye in your hearts?
ct: But Jesus knew exactly what they were thinking. He said to them, "Why are you questioning this in your hearts?"

kjv: Whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Rise up and walk?
ct: "Which is easier to say — 'Your sins are forgiven,' or 'Get up and walk'?"

kjv: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power upon earth to forgive sins, (he said unto the sick of the palsy,) I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy couch, and go into thine house.
ct: "But so that you'll know the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins" — he turned to the paralyzed man — "I'm telling you: get up, pick up your mat, and go home."

kjv: And immediately he rose up before them, and took up that whereon he lay, and departed to his own house, glorifying God.
ct: Right then and there, the man stood up in front of everyone, picked up the mat he'd been lying on, and went home praising God.

kjv: And they were all amazed, and they glorified God, and were filled with fear, saying, We have seen strange things to day.
ct: Everyone was completely amazed and gave glory to God. They were filled with fear and kept saying, "We've seen incredible things today!"

kjv: And after these things he went forth, and saw a publican, named Levi, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he said unto him, Follow me.
ct: After this, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at his tax booth. He said to him, "Follow me."

kjv: And he left all, rose up, and followed him.
ct: Levi got up, left everything, and followed him.

kjv: And Levi made him a great feast in his own house: and there was a great company of publicans and of others that sat down with them.
ct: Then Levi threw a huge banquet for Jesus at his house. A large crowd of tax collectors and others were there eating with them.

kjv: But their scribes and Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, Why do ye eat and drink with publicans and sinners?
ct: The Pharisees and their scribes complained to his disciples, "Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?"

kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, They that are whole need not a physician; but they that are sick.
ct: Jesus answered them, "Healthy people don't need a doctor — sick people do.

kjv: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
ct: I didn't come to call the righteous. I came to call sinners to repentance."

kjv: And they said unto him, Why do the disciples of John fast often, and make prayers, and likewise the disciples of the Pharisees; but thine eat and drink?
ct: They pressed him further: "John's disciples fast and pray regularly, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees. But your disciples just eat and drink. Why is that?"

kjv: And he said unto them, Can ye make the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them?
ct: Jesus replied, "Can you make wedding guests fast while the groom is still with them?

kjv: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
ct: But the time will come when the groom is taken away from them. When that day comes, they will fast."

kjv: And he spake also a parable unto them; No man putteth a piece of a new garment upon an old; if otherwise, then both the new maketh a rent, and the piece that was taken out of the new agreeth not with the old.
ct: He also told them a parable: "Nobody tears a patch from a new garment to sew it on an old one. If you did, you'd ruin the new garment, and the new patch wouldn't match the old fabric anyway.

kjv: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles; else the new wine will burst the bottles, and be spilled, and the bottles shall perish.
ct: And nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If you did, the new wine would burst the skins — the wine would spill out and the skins would be ruined.

kjv: But new wine must be put into new bottles; and both are preserved.
ct: New wine has to go into fresh wineskins, and then both are preserved.

kjv: No man also having drunk old wine straightway desireth new: for he saith, The old is better.
ct: And no one who has been drinking aged wine wants new wine right away, because they say, 'The old is better.'"

kjv: And it came to pass on the second sabbath after the first, that he went through the corn fields; and his disciples plucked the ears of corn, and did eat, rubbing them in their hands.
ct: One Sabbath, Jesus was walking through the grain fields, and his disciples were picking heads of grain, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.

kjv: And certain of the Pharisees said unto them, Why do ye that which is not lawful to do on the sabbath days?
ct: Some of the Pharisees confronted them: "Why are you doing what's not allowed on the Sabbath?"

kjv: And Jesus answering them said, Have ye not read so much as this, what David did, when himself was an hungred, and they which were with him;
ct: Jesus answered them, "Haven't you even read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?

kjv: How he went into the house of God, and did take and eat the shewbread, and gave also to them that were with him; which it is not lawful to eat but for the priests alone?
ct: He went into God's house, took the sacred bread that only priests are allowed to eat, and shared it with the men who were with him."

kjv: And he said unto them, That the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
ct: Then he told them, "The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath."

kjv: And it came to pass also on another sabbath, that he entered into the synagogue and taught: and there was a man whose right hand was withered.
ct: On another Sabbath, Jesus went into the synagogue and began teaching. A man was there whose right hand was shriveled up.

kjv: And the scribes and Pharisees watched him, whether he would heal on the sabbath day; that they might find an accusation against him.
ct: The scribes and Pharisees were watching Jesus closely, hoping he would heal on the Sabbath so they could bring charges against him.

kjv: But he knew their thoughts, and said to the man which had the withered hand, Rise up, and stand forth in the midst. And he arose and stood forth.
ct: But Jesus knew exactly what they were thinking. He said to the man with the shriveled hand, "Get up and stand here in front of everyone." The man stood up and came forward.

kjv: Then said Jesus unto them, I will ask you one thing; Is it lawful on the sabbath days to do good, or to do evil? to save life, or to destroy it?
ct: Then Jesus said to them, "Let me ask you something: Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do harm? To save a life or to destroy one?"

kjv: And looking round about upon them all, he said unto the man, Stretch forth thy hand. And he did so: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
ct: He looked around at all of them, then said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." He did, and his hand was completely restored — as healthy as the other one.

kjv: And they were filled with madness; and communed one with another what they might do to Jesus.
ct: But the Pharisees and scribes were furious, and they began discussing with each other what they could do to Jesus.

kjv: And it came to pass in those days, that he went out into a mountain to pray, and continued all night in prayer to God.
ct: Around that time, Jesus went up on a mountain to pray, and he spent the entire night in prayer to God.

kjv: And when it was day, he called unto him his disciples: and of them he chose twelve, whom also he named apostles;
ct: When morning came, he called his disciples together and chose 12 of them, whom he named apostles:

kjv: Simon, (whom he also named Peter,) and Andrew his brother, James and John, Philip and Bartholomew,
ct: Simon, whom he also named Peter; Andrew, Peter's brother; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew;

kjv: Matthew and Thomas, James the son of Alphaeus, and Simon called Zelotes,
ct: Matthew; Thomas; James son of Alphaeus; Simon, known as the Zealot;

kjv: And Judas the brother of James, and Judas Iscariot, which also was the traitor.
ct: Judas son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who would later betray him.

kjv: And he came down with them, and stood in the plain, and the company of his disciples, and a great multitude of people out of all Judaea and Jerusalem, and from the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, which came to hear him, and to be healed of their diseases;
ct: Jesus came down the mountain with them and stood on a level area. A large crowd of his disciples was there, along with a huge number of people from all over Judaea, Jerusalem, and the coastal region of Tyre and Sidon. They had come to hear him and to be healed of their diseases.

kjv: And they that were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were healed.
ct: Those tormented by unclean spirits were also healed.

kjv: And the whole multitude sought to touch him: for there went virtue out of him, and healed them all.
ct: The entire crowd was trying to touch him, because power was flowing out of him and healing everyone.

kjv: And he lifted up his eyes on his disciples, and said, Blessed be ye poor: for yours is the kingdom of God.
ct: Jesus looked up at his disciples and said, "Blessed are you who are poor, because the kingdom of God belongs to you.

kjv: Blessed are ye that hunger now: for ye shall be filled. Blessed are ye that weep now: for ye shall laugh.
ct: Blessed are you who are hungry now, because you will be satisfied. Blessed are you who weep now, because you will laugh.

kjv: Blessed are ye, when men shall hate you, and when they shall separate you from their company, and shall reproach you, and cast out your name as evil, for the Son of man’s sake.
ct: Blessed are you when people hate you, shut you out, insult you, and reject your name as evil — all because of the Son of Man.

kjv: Rejoice ye in that day, and leap for joy: for, behold, your reward is great in heaven: for in the like manner did their fathers unto the prophets.
ct: When that happens, rejoice! Jump for joy! Your reward in heaven is great. After all, their ancestors treated the prophets the same way.

kjv: But woe unto you that are rich! for ye have received your consolation.
ct: But how terrible it will be for you who are rich, because you've already received all the comfort you're going to get.

kjv: Woe unto you that are full! for ye shall hunger. Woe unto you that laugh now! for ye shall mourn and weep.
ct: How terrible for you who are well-fed now, because you will go hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.

kjv: Woe unto you, when all men shall speak well of you! for so did their fathers to the false prophets.
ct: How terrible for you when everyone speaks well of you, because that's exactly how their ancestors treated the false prophets.

kjv: But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you,
ct: But to those of you who are listening, I say this: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.

kjv: Bless them that curse you, and pray for them which despitefully use you.
ct: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.

kjv: And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek offer also the other; and him that taketh away thy cloak forbid not to take thy coat also.
ct: If someone strikes you on one cheek, offer the other one too. If someone takes your cloak, don't stop them from taking your coat as well.

kjv: Give to every man that asketh of thee; and of him that taketh away thy goods ask them not again.
ct: Give to everyone who asks you, and if someone takes what belongs to you, don't demand it back.

kjv: And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise.
ct: Treat others the way you want them to treat you.

kjv: For if ye love them which love you, what thank have ye? for sinners also love those that love them.
ct: If you only love people who love you back, what credit is that to you? Even sinners love those who love them.

kjv: And if ye do good to them which do good to you, what thank have ye? for sinners also do even the same.
ct: If you only do good to people who do good to you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners do that much.

kjv: And if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive, what thank have ye? for sinners also lend to sinners, to receive as much again.
ct: If you only lend to people you expect to pay you back, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, expecting to be repaid in full.

kjv: But love ye your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing again; and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be the children of the Highest: for he is kind unto the unthankful and to the evil.
ct: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend without expecting anything in return. Your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High, because he is kind even to the ungrateful and the evil.

kjv: Be ye therefore merciful, as your Father also is merciful.
ct: Be merciful, just as your Father is merciful.

kjv: Judge not, and ye shall not be judged: condemn not, and ye shall not be condemned: forgive, and ye shall be forgiven:
ct: Don't judge others, and you won't be judged. Don't condemn others, and you won't be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.

kjv: Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom. For with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again.
ct: Give, and it will be given back to you — pressed down, shaken together, and overflowing. It will be poured right into your lap. Because whatever measure you use for others, that same measure will be used for you."

kjv: And he spake a parable unto them, Can the blind lead the blind? shall they not both fall into the ditch?
ct: He also told them a parable: "Can a blind person lead another blind person? Won't they both fall into a pit?

kjv: The disciple is not above his master: but every one that is perfect shall be as his master.
ct: A student isn't above his teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will become like his teacher.

kjv: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother’s eye, but perceivest not the beam that is in thine own eye?
ct: Why do you notice the tiny speck in your brother's eye but ignore the wooden beam in your own?

kjv: Either how canst thou say to thy brother, Brother, let me pull out the mote that is in thine eye, when thou thyself beholdest not the beam that is in thine own eye? Thou hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine own eye, and then shalt thou see clearly to pull out the mote that is in thy brother’s eye.
ct: How can you say to your brother, 'Brother, let me get that speck out of your eye,' when you can't even see the beam in your own eye? You hypocrite! First remove the beam from your own eye, and then you'll be able to see clearly to remove the speck from your brother's eye.

kjv: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit; neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
ct: A good tree doesn't produce rotten fruit, and a rotten tree doesn't produce good fruit.

kjv: For every tree is known by his own fruit. For of thorns men do not gather figs, nor of a bramble bush gather they grapes.
ct: Every tree is identified by its own fruit. You don't pick figs from thornbushes, and you don't harvest grapes from a bramble bush.

kjv: A good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is good; and an evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is evil: for of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaketh.
ct: A good person produces good things from the goodness stored up in their heart, and an evil person produces evil things from the evil stored up in theirs. Whatever fills your heart is what your mouth will speak.

kjv: And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things which I say?
ct: Why do you call me 'Lord, Lord,' but don't do what I say?

kjv: Whosoever cometh to me, and heareth my sayings, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like:
ct: Let me show you what it's like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.

kjv: He is like a man which built an house, and digged deep, and laid the foundation on a rock: and when the flood arose, the stream beat vehemently upon that house, and could not shake it: for it was founded upon a rock.
ct: That person is like someone building a house who dug deep and laid the foundation on solid rock. When a flood came and the river slammed against that house, it couldn't be shaken, because it was built on rock.

kjv: But he that heareth, and doeth not, is like a man that without a foundation built an house upon the earth; against which the stream did beat vehemently, and immediately it fell; and the ruin of that house was great.
ct: But the person who hears my words and doesn't act on them is like someone who built a house on bare ground with no foundation. The moment the river hit it, the house collapsed, and the destruction was total."

kjv: Now when he had ended all his sayings in the audience of the people, he entered into Capernaum.
ct: When Jesus had finished saying all of this to the crowd, he went to Capernaum.

kjv: And a certain centurion’s servant, who was dear unto him, was sick, and ready to die.
ct: There, a Roman centurion had a servant he valued deeply, but the servant was sick and close to death.

kjv: And when he heard of Jesus, he sent unto him the elders of the Jews, beseeching him that he would come and heal his servant.
ct: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to him, asking Jesus to come and heal his servant.

kjv: And when they came to Jesus, they besought him instantly, saying, That he was worthy for whom he should do this:
ct: They came to Jesus and pleaded with him urgently. "This man deserves your help," they said.

kjv: For he loveth our nation, and he hath built us a synagogue.
ct: "He loves our people, and he's the one who built our synagogue."

kjv: Then Jesus went with them. And when he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying unto him, Lord, trouble not thyself: for I am not worthy that thou shouldest enter under my roof:
ct: So Jesus went with them. But when he was getting close to the house, the centurion sent friends with a message: "Lord, don't go to the trouble. I'm not worthy to have you come into my home.

kjv: Wherefore neither thought I myself worthy to come unto thee: but say in a word, and my servant shall be healed.
ct: That's why I didn't even feel worthy to come to you myself. Just say the word, and my servant will be healed.

kjv: For I also am a man set under authority, having under me soldiers, and I say unto one, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.
ct: I understand how authority works — I'm a man under authority myself, with soldiers under me. I tell one, 'Go,' and he goes. I tell another, 'Come,' and he comes. I tell my servant, 'Do this,' and he does it."

kjv: When Jesus heard these things, he marvelled at him, and turned him about, and said unto the people that followed him, I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.
ct: When Jesus heard this, he was amazed. He turned to the crowd following him and said, "I'm telling you, I haven't found faith this great anywhere in Israel."

kjv: And they that were sent, returning to the house, found the servant whole that had been sick.
ct: When the messengers returned to the house, they found the servant completely well.

kjv: And it came to pass the day after, that he went into a city called Nain; and many of his disciples went with him, and much people.
ct: The next day, Jesus traveled to a town called Nain. A large crowd and many of his disciples went along with him.

kjv: Now when he came nigh to the gate of the city, behold, there was a dead man carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow: and much people of the city was with her.
ct: As he approached the town gate, a funeral procession was coming out. The dead man was the only son of a widow, and a large crowd from the town was with her.

kjv: And when the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said unto her, Weep not.
ct: When the Lord saw her, his heart broke for her. "Don't cry," he said.

kjv: And he came and touched the bier: and they that bare him stood still. And he said, Young man, I say unto thee, Arise.
ct: Then he walked up and touched the coffin, and the men carrying it stopped. He said, "Young man, I tell you — get up!"

kjv: And he that was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he delivered him to his mother.
ct: The dead man sat up and began to talk! Then Jesus gave him back to his mother.

kjv: And there came a fear on all: and they glorified God, saying, That a great prophet is risen up among us; and, That God hath visited his people.
ct: Everyone was gripped with fear, and they praised God. "A great prophet has risen among us!" they said. "God has come to help his people!"

kjv: And this rumour of him went forth throughout all Judaea, and throughout all the region round about.
ct: News about Jesus spread throughout all of Judea and the entire surrounding region.

kjv: And the disciples of John shewed him of all these things.
ct: John's disciples told him about everything that was happening.

kjv: And John calling unto him two of his disciples sent them to Jesus, saying, Art thou he that should come? or look we for another?
ct: So John called 2 of his disciples and sent them to Jesus with this question: "Are you the one we've been waiting for, or should we be looking for someone else?"

kjv: When the men were come unto him, they said, John Baptist hath sent us unto thee, saying, Art thou he that should come? or look we for another?
ct: When the men reached Jesus, they said, "John the Baptist sent us to ask you: 'Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else?'"

kjv: And in that same hour he cured many of their infirmities and plagues, and of evil spirits; and unto many that were blind he gave sight.
ct: Right then and there, Jesus healed many people of diseases, afflictions, and evil spirits, and he restored sight to many who were blind.

kjv: Then Jesus answering said unto them, Go your way, and tell John what things ye have seen and heard; how that the blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, to the poor the gospel is preached.
ct: Then Jesus told them, "Go back and report to John what you've seen and heard: the blind can see, the lame are walking, lepers are healed, the deaf can hear, the dead are being raised, and the good news is being preached to the poor.

kjv: And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.
ct: And blessed is the person who doesn't lose faith in me."

kjv: And when the messengers of John were departed, he began to speak unto the people concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness for to see? A reed shaken with the wind?
ct: After John's messengers left, Jesus began talking to the crowd about John. "What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A blade of grass swaying in the wind?

kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? Behold, they which are gorgeously apparelled, and live delicately, are in kings’ courts.
ct: Then what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? People who wear expensive clothing and live in luxury are found in palaces.

kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? Yea, I say unto you, and much more than a prophet.
ct: So what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes — and I tell you, he's far more than a prophet.

kjv: This is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
ct: He is the one Scripture talks about when it says, 'Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare the way before you.'

kjv: For I say unto you, Among those that are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptist: but he that is least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.
ct: I tell you, no one ever born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least important person in the kingdom of God is greater than he is."

kjv: And all the people that heard him, and the publicans, justified God, being baptized with the baptism of John.
ct: When the common people and the tax collectors heard this, they acknowledged that God was right, since they had been baptized by John.

kjv: But the Pharisees and lawyers rejected the counsel of God against themselves, being not baptized of him.
ct: But the Pharisees and the experts in the law rejected God's purpose for them, because they had refused to be baptized by John.

kjv: And the Lord said, Whereunto then shall I liken the men of this generation? and to what are they like?
ct: "So what should I compare the people of this generation to? What are they like?

kjv: They are like unto children sitting in the marketplace, and calling one to another, and saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned to you, and ye have not wept.
ct: They're like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other: 'We played music for you, and you didn't dance! We sang a funeral song, and you didn't cry!'

kjv: For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine; and ye say, He hath a devil.
ct: John the Baptist came fasting — not eating bread or drinking wine — and you said, 'He has a demon.'

kjv: The Son of man is come eating and drinking; and ye say, Behold a gluttonous man, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners!
ct: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and you say, 'Look at him! A glutton and a drunk, hanging around with tax collectors and sinners!'

kjv: But wisdom is justified of all her children.
ct: But wisdom proves itself right by the lives of those who follow it."

kjv: And one of the Pharisees desired him that he would eat with him. And he went into the Pharisee’s house, and sat down to meat.
ct: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to dinner. So Jesus went to his house and took his place at the table.

kjv: And, behold, a woman in the city, which was a sinner, when she knew that Jesus sat at meat in the Pharisee’s house, brought an alabaster box of ointment,
ct: A woman in that town who had lived a sinful life found out that Jesus was eating at the Pharisee's house. She brought an alabaster jar of perfumed ointment

kjv: And stood at his feet behind him weeping, and began to wash his feet with tears, and did wipe them with the hairs of her head, and kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.
ct: and stood behind him at his feet, weeping. Her tears fell on his feet, and she wiped them with her hair, kissing his feet over and over and pouring the ointment on them.

kjv: Now when the Pharisee which had bidden him saw it, he spake within himself, saying, This man, if he were a prophet, would have known who and what manner of woman this is that toucheth him: for she is a sinner.
ct: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this, he thought to himself, "If this man were really a prophet, he'd know what kind of woman is touching him. She's a sinner."

kjv: And Jesus answering said unto him, Simon, I have somewhat to say unto thee. And he saith, Master, say on.
ct: Jesus spoke up. "Simon, I have something to tell you." "Go ahead, Teacher," Simon replied.

kjv: There was a certain creditor which had two debtors: the one owed five hundred pence, and the other fifty.
ct: "A moneylender had 2 people who owed him money. One owed 500 denarii, and the other owed 50.

kjv: And when they had nothing to pay, he frankly forgave them both. Tell me therefore, which of them will love him most?
ct: Neither of them could pay him back, so he forgave both debts. Now, which of them do you think will love him more?"

kjv: Simon answered and said, I suppose that he, to whom he forgave most. And he said unto him, Thou hast rightly judged.
ct: Simon answered, "I'd guess the one who had the bigger debt forgiven." Jesus said, "You're exactly right."

kjv: And he turned to the woman, and said unto Simon, Seest thou this woman? I entered into thine house, thou gavest me no water for my feet: but she hath washed my feet with tears, and wiped them with the hairs of her head.
ct: Then he turned toward the woman and said to Simon, "Do you see this woman? When I came into your house, you didn't offer me water to wash my feet. But she has washed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.

kjv: Thou gavest me no kiss: but this woman since the time I came in hath not ceased to kiss my feet.
ct: You didn't greet me with a kiss, but from the moment I walked in, she hasn't stopped kissing my feet.

kjv: My head with oil thou didst not anoint: but this woman hath anointed my feet with ointment.
ct: You didn't anoint my head with oil, but she has poured ointment on my feet.

kjv: Wherefore I say unto thee, Her sins, which are many, are forgiven; for she loved much: but to whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little.
ct: So I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven — and her great love proves it. But whoever has been forgiven only a little loves only a little."

kjv: And he said unto her, Thy sins are forgiven.
ct: Then Jesus said to her, "Your sins are forgiven."

kjv: And they that sat at meat with him began to say within themselves, Who is this that forgiveth sins also?
ct: The other guests began whispering to each other, "Who does he think he is, forgiving sins?"

kjv: And he said to the woman, Thy faith hath saved thee; go in peace.
ct: But Jesus said to the woman, "Your faith has saved you. Go in peace."

kjv: And it came to pass afterward, that he went throughout every city and village, preaching and shewing the glad tidings of the kingdom of God: and the twelve were with him,
ct: After this, Jesus traveled through cities and villages, preaching and announcing the good news of the kingdom of God. The 12 disciples were with him,

kjv: And certain women, which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities, Mary called Magdalene, out of whom went seven devils,
ct: along with certain women who had been healed of evil spirits and diseases. Among them was Mary, called Magdalene, from whom 7 demons had been driven out;

kjv: And Joanna the wife of Chuza Herod’s steward, and Susanna, and many others, which ministered unto him of their substance.
ct: Joanna, the wife of Chuza, who was Herod's household manager; Susanna; and many others. These women supported Jesus and his disciples out of their own resources.

kjv: And when much people were gathered together, and were come to him out of every city, he spake by a parable:
ct: A huge crowd was gathering, with people streaming in from every town. Jesus told them a parable:

kjv: A sower went out to sow his seed: and as he sowed, some fell by the way side; and it was trodden down, and the fowls of the air devoured it.
ct: "A farmer went out to plant his seed. As he scattered it, some fell along the path, where it was trampled underfoot, and the birds came and ate it up.

kjv: And some fell upon a rock; and as soon as it was sprung up, it withered away, because it lacked moisture.
ct: Some fell on rocky ground, and as soon as it sprouted, it withered because it had no moisture.

kjv: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprang up with it, and choked it.
ct: Some fell among thorn bushes, and the thorns grew up alongside it and choked it out.

kjv: And other fell on good ground, and sprang up, and bare fruit an hundredfold. And when he had said these things, he cried, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
ct: But some fell on good soil, and it grew and produced a crop 100 times what was planted." After saying this, he called out, "If you have ears to hear, then listen!"

kjv: And his disciples asked him, saying, What might this parable be?
ct: His disciples asked him, "What does this parable mean?"

kjv: And he said, Unto you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God: but to others in parables; that seeing they might not see, and hearing they might not understand.
ct: He said, "You have been given the ability to understand the mysteries of the kingdom of God. But for others, these truths come in parables, so that 'seeing, they may not truly see, and hearing, they may not understand.'"

kjv: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
ct: "Here's what the parable means: The seed is the word of God.

kjv: Those by the way side are they that hear; then cometh the devil, and taketh away the word out of their hearts, lest they should believe and be saved.
ct: The seed along the path represents people who hear the word, but then the devil comes and snatches it from their hearts so they won't believe and be saved.

kjv: They on the rock are they, which, when they hear, receive the word with joy; and these have no root, which for a while believe, and in time of temptation fall away.
ct: The seed on rocky ground represents people who hear the word and receive it with joy, but they have no root. They believe for a while, but when temptation comes, they fall away.

kjv: And that which fell among thorns are they, which, when they have heard, go forth, and are choked with cares and riches and pleasures of this life, and bring no fruit to perfection.
ct: The seed that fell among thorns represents people who hear the word, but as they go through life, they get choked by worries, wealth, and pleasures, and they never produce mature fruit.

kjv: But that on the good ground are they, which in an honest and good heart, having heard the word, keep it, and bring forth fruit with patience.
ct: But the seed on good soil represents people with an honest and good heart who hear the word, hold on to it, and produce fruit through patience."

kjv: No man, when he hath lighted a candle, covereth it with a vessel, or putteth it under a bed; but setteth it on a candlestick, that they which enter in may see the light.
ct: "No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or shoves it under a bed. Instead, they put it on a stand so that everyone who comes in can see the light.

kjv: For nothing is secret, that shall not be made manifest; neither any thing hid, that shall not be known and come abroad.
ct: Nothing is secret that won't eventually be revealed, and nothing is hidden that won't be made known and brought into the open.

kjv: Take heed therefore how ye hear: for whosoever hath, to him shall be given; and whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he seemeth to have.
ct: So pay close attention to how you listen. Whoever has understanding will be given more, but whoever doesn't have it — even what they think they have will be taken away."

kjv: Then came to him his mother and his brethren, and could not come at him for the press.
ct: Jesus' mother and brothers came to see him, but they couldn't get to him because of the crowd.

kjv: And it was told him by certain which said, Thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to see thee.
ct: Someone told him, "Your mother and your brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you."

kjv: And he answered and said unto them, My mother and my brethren are these which hear the word of God, and do it.
ct: He replied, "My mother and my brothers are those who hear the word of God and put it into practice."

kjv: Now it came to pass on a certain day, that he went into a ship with his disciples: and he said unto them, Let us go over unto the other side of the lake. And they launched forth.
ct: One day Jesus got into a boat with his disciples and said, "Let's cross over to the other side of the lake." So they set out.

kjv: But as they sailed he fell asleep: and there came down a storm of wind on the lake; and they were filled with water, and were in jeopardy.
ct: As they sailed, Jesus fell asleep. Then a fierce windstorm swept down on the lake, and the boat was filling with water. They were in serious danger.

kjv: And they came to him, and awoke him, saying, Master, master, we perish. Then he arose, and rebuked the wind and the raging of the water: and they ceased, and there was a calm.
ct: The disciples rushed to him and woke him up, shouting, "Master, Master, we're going to drown!" He got up and commanded the wind and the raging waves to stop. They obeyed, and everything became perfectly calm.

kjv: And he said unto them, Where is your faith? And they being afraid wondered, saying one to another, What manner of man is this! for he commandeth even the winds and water, and they obey him.
ct: He turned to them and asked, "Where is your faith?" Shaken and amazed, they said to each other, "Who is this man? He gives orders to the wind and the water, and they obey him!"

kjv: And they arrived at the country of the Gadarenes, which is over against Galilee.
ct: They sailed to the region of the Gadarenes, which is across the lake from Galilee.

kjv: And when he went forth to land, there met him out of the city a certain man, which had devils long time, and ware no clothes, neither abode in any house, but in the tombs.
ct: When Jesus stepped ashore, a man from the town met him who had been possessed by demons for a long time. He wore no clothes and didn't live in a house — he lived among the tombs.

kjv: When he saw Jesus, he cried out, and fell down before him, and with a loud voice said, What have I to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God most high? I beseech thee, torment me not.
ct: When he saw Jesus, he screamed and threw himself down before him, shouting, "What do you want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I'm begging you, don't torment me!"

kjv: (For he had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. For oftentimes it had caught him: and he was kept bound with chains and in fetters; and he brake the bands, and was driven of the devil into the wilderness.)
ct: He said this because Jesus had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of him. The spirit had seized him many times before. Even when he was chained hand and foot under guard, he would snap the chains and be driven by the demon into desolate places.

kjv: And Jesus asked him, saying, What is thy name? And he said, Legion: because many devils were entered into him.
ct: Jesus asked him, "What is your name?" He answered, "Legion" — because many demons had entered him.

kjv: And they besought him that he would not command them to go out into the deep.
ct: The demons kept begging Jesus not to send them into the abyss.

kjv: And there was there an herd of many swine feeding on the mountain: and they besought him that he would suffer them to enter into them. And he suffered them.
ct: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the mountainside nearby. The demons begged Jesus to let them enter the pigs, and he gave them permission.

kjv: Then went the devils out of the man, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the lake, and were choked.
ct: The demons came out of the man and went into the pigs. The entire herd stampeded down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.

kjv: When they that fed them saw what was done, they fled, and went and told it in the city and in the country.
ct: When the men tending the pigs saw what happened, they ran off and reported it throughout the town and the surrounding countryside.

kjv: Then they went out to see what was done; and came to Jesus, and found the man, out of whom the devils were departed, sitting at the feet of Jesus, clothed, and in his right mind: and they were afraid.
ct: People came out to see what had happened. When they reached Jesus, they found the man the demons had left — sitting at Jesus' feet, dressed, and completely sane. This terrified them.

kjv: They also which saw it told them by what means he that was possessed of the devils was healed.
ct: The eyewitnesses told everyone how the demon-possessed man had been healed.

kjv: Then the whole multitude of the country of the Gadarenes round about besought him to depart from them; for they were taken with great fear: and he went up into the ship, and returned back again.
ct: Then the entire population of the Gadarene region begged Jesus to leave, because they were overwhelmed with fear. So he got into the boat and headed back.

kjv: Now the man out of whom the devils were departed besought him that he might be with him: but Jesus sent him away, saying,
ct: The man who had been freed from the demons begged to go with him, but Jesus sent him away, saying,

kjv: Return to thine own house, and shew how great things God hath done unto thee. And he went his way, and published throughout the whole city how great things Jesus had done unto him.
ct: "Go home and tell everyone what God has done for you." So the man went through the whole city, declaring what Jesus had done for him.

kjv: And it came to pass, that, when Jesus was returned, the people gladly received him: for they were all waiting for him.
ct: When Jesus returned to the other side, the crowd welcomed him eagerly because they had all been waiting for him.

kjv: And, behold, there came a man named Jairus, and he was a ruler of the synagogue: and he fell down at Jesus’ feet, and besought him that he would come into his house:
ct: Just then, a man named Jairus came forward. He was a synagogue leader. He fell at Jesus' feet and pleaded with him to come to his house,

kjv: For he had one only daughter, about twelve years of age, and she lay a dying. But as he went the people thronged him.
ct: because his only daughter, about 12 years old, was dying. As Jesus made his way there, the crowds pressed in around him from every side.

kjv: And a woman having an issue of blood twelve years, which had spent all her living upon physicians, neither could be healed of any,
ct: In the crowd was a woman who had been suffering from bleeding for 12 years. She had spent everything she had on doctors, but no one could heal her.

kjv: Came behind him, and touched the border of his garment: and immediately her issue of blood stanched.
ct: She came up behind Jesus and touched the edge of his garment. Instantly, her bleeding stopped.

kjv: And Jesus said, Who touched me? When all denied, Peter and they that were with him said, Master, the multitude throng thee and press thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?
ct: Jesus asked, "Who touched me?" Everyone denied it, and Peter said, "Master, the whole crowd is pushing and pressing against you. How can you ask, 'Who touched me?'"

kjv: And Jesus said, Somebody hath touched me: for I perceive that virtue is gone out of me.
ct: But Jesus said, "Someone deliberately touched me. I felt power go out from me."

kjv: And when the woman saw that she was not hid, she came trembling, and falling down before him, she declared unto him before all the people for what cause she had touched him, and how she was healed immediately.
ct: When the woman realized she couldn't hide, she came forward trembling and fell at his feet. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been healed instantly.

kjv: And he said unto her, Daughter, be of good comfort: thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace.
ct: Jesus said to her, "Daughter, take heart. Your faith has made you well. Go in peace."

kjv: While he yet spake, there cometh one from the ruler of the synagogue’s house, saying to him, Thy daughter is dead; trouble not the Master.
ct: While Jesus was still speaking, someone arrived from Jairus' house with a message: "Your daughter is dead. Don't bother the Teacher anymore."

kjv: But when Jesus heard it, he answered him, saying, Fear not: believe only, and she shall be made whole.
ct: But when Jesus heard this, he told Jairus, "Don't be afraid. Just believe, and she will be healed."

kjv: And when he came into the house, he suffered no man to go in, save Peter, and James, and John, and the father and the mother of the maiden.
ct: When he arrived at the house, he didn't let anyone go inside with him except Peter, James, John, and the girl's father and mother.

kjv: And all wept, and bewailed her: but he said, Weep not; she is not dead, but sleepeth.
ct: Everyone there was weeping and mourning for her. Jesus said, "Stop crying. She isn't dead — she's sleeping."

kjv: And they laughed him to scorn, knowing that she was dead.
ct: They laughed at him, because they knew she was dead.

kjv: And he put them all out, and took her by the hand, and called, saying, Maid, arise.
ct: But Jesus sent them all outside. He took the girl by the hand and called out, "Child, get up!"

kjv: And her spirit came again, and she arose straightway: and he commanded to give her meat.
ct: Her spirit returned, and she stood up immediately. Then Jesus told them to give her something to eat.

kjv: And her parents were astonished: but he charged them that they should tell no man what was done.
ct: Her parents were astonished, but Jesus strictly instructed them not to tell anyone what had happened.

kjv: Then he called his twelve disciples together, and gave them power and authority over all devils, and to cure diseases.
ct: Jesus called his 12 disciples together and gave them power and authority over all demons, along with the ability to cure diseases.

kjv: And he sent them to preach the kingdom of God, and to heal the sick.
ct: Then he sent them out to preach the kingdom of God and to heal the sick.

kjv: And he said unto them, Take nothing for your journey, neither staves, nor scrip, neither bread, neither money; neither have two coats apiece.
ct: He told them, "Don't take anything for the journey — no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money. Don't even pack an extra coat.

kjv: And whatsoever house ye enter into, there abide, and thence depart.
ct: Whatever house you enter, stay there until you leave that town.

kjv: And whosoever will not receive you, when ye go out of that city, shake off the very dust from your feet for a testimony against them.
ct: If a town won't welcome you, shake the dust off your feet as you leave — as a witness against them."

kjv: And they departed, and went through the towns, preaching the gospel, and healing every where.
ct: So they set out, traveling from village to village, preaching the good news and healing people everywhere they went.

kjv: Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by him: and he was perplexed, because that it was said of some, that John was risen from the dead;
ct: Herod the tetrarch heard about everything that was happening, and he was deeply troubled. Some people were saying that John had risen from the dead.

kjv: And of some, that Elias had appeared; and of others, that one of the old prophets was risen again.
ct: Others claimed that Elijah had appeared, and still others said one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.

kjv: And Herod said, John have I beheaded: but who is this, of whom I hear such things? And he desired to see him.
ct: Herod said, "I had John beheaded. So who is this man I keep hearing about?" And he kept trying to see Jesus.

kjv: And the apostles, when they were returned, told him all that they had done. And he took them, and went aside privately into a desert place belonging to the city called Bethsaida.
ct: When the apostles returned, they told Jesus everything they had done. He took them and withdrew privately to a remote area near the town of Bethsaida.

kjv: And the people, when they knew it, followed him: and he received them, and spake unto them of the kingdom of God, and healed them that had need of healing.
ct: But the crowds found out and followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about the kingdom of God, and healed those who needed healing.

kjv: And when the day began to wear away, then came the twelve, and said unto him, Send the multitude away, that they may go into the towns and country round about, and lodge, and get victuals: for we are here in a desert place.
