ct: As the day was winding down, the 12 came to him and said, "Send the crowd away so they can go to the surrounding villages and farms to find food and a place to stay. We're out here in the middle of nowhere."

kjv: But he said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they said, We have no more but five loaves and two fishes; except we should go and buy meat for all this people.
ct: But Jesus said, "You feed them." They replied, "All we have is 5 loaves of bread and 2 fish — unless you want us to go buy food for all these people."

kjv: For they were about five thousand men. And he said to his disciples, Make them sit down by fifties in a company.
ct: There were about 5,000 men in the crowd. Jesus told his disciples, "Have them sit down in groups of about 50."

kjv: And they did so, and made them all sit down.
ct: They did as he said and got everyone seated.

kjv: Then he took the five loaves and the two fishes, and looking up to heaven, he blessed them, and brake, and gave to the disciples to set before the multitude.
ct: Jesus took the 5 loaves and the 2 fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke them into pieces, and handed them to the disciples to distribute to the crowd.

kjv: And they did eat, and were all filled: and there was taken up of fragments that remained to them twelve baskets.
ct: Everyone ate and was completely satisfied. Afterward, the disciples picked up 12 baskets full of leftover pieces.

kjv: And it came to pass, as he was alone praying, his disciples were with him: and he asked them, saying, Whom say the people that I am?
ct: One time when Jesus was praying alone — with his disciples nearby — he asked them, "Who do the crowds say I am?"

kjv: They answering said, John the Baptist; but some say, Elias; and others say, that one of the old prophets is risen again.
ct: They answered, "Some say John the Baptist. Others say Elijah. And others think one of the ancient prophets has risen again."

kjv: He said unto them, But whom say ye that I am? Peter answering said, The Christ of God.
ct: "But who do you say I am?" he asked. Peter answered, "The Christ of God."

kjv: And he straitly charged them, and commanded them to tell no man that thing;
ct: Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone this.

kjv: Saying, The Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be slain, and be raised the third day.
ct: He said, "The Son of Man must suffer many things. He will be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes. He will be killed, and on the 3rd day he will be raised to life."

kjv: And he said to them all, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily, and follow me.
ct: Then he said to all of them, "If anyone wants to follow me, he must deny himself, take up his cross daily, and follow me.

kjv: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: but whosoever will lose his life for my sake, the same shall save it.
ct: Whoever tries to hold on to his life will lose it, but whoever gives up his life for my sake will save it.

kjv: For what is a man advantaged, if he gain the whole world, and lose himself, or be cast away?
ct: What good is it if someone gains the whole world but loses or destroys himself?

kjv: For whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words, of him shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he shall come in his own glory, and in his Father’s, and of the holy angels.
ct: If anyone is ashamed of me and my words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.

kjv: But I tell you of a truth, there be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the kingdom of God.
ct: I'm telling you the truth: some of you standing here right now will not taste death before you see the kingdom of God."

kjv: And it came to pass about an eight days after these sayings, he took Peter and John and James, and went up into a mountain to pray.
ct: About 8 days after he said these things, Jesus took Peter, John, and James and went up on a mountain to pray.

kjv: And as he prayed, the fashion of his countenance was altered, and his raiment was white and glistering.
ct: While he was praying, the appearance of his face changed, and his clothes became brilliant white, blazing with light.

kjv: And, behold, there talked with him two men, which were Moses and Elias:
ct: Suddenly, 2 men appeared and were talking with him — they were Moses and Elijah.

kjv: Who appeared in glory, and spake of his decease which he should accomplish at Jerusalem.
ct: They appeared in glory and spoke with Jesus about his departure, which he was about to carry out in Jerusalem.

kjv: But Peter and they that were with him were heavy with sleep: and when they were awake, they saw his glory, and the two men that stood with him.
ct: Peter and the others had been overcome with sleep, but when they fully woke up, they saw his glory and the 2 men standing with him.

kjv: And it came to pass, as they departed from him, Peter said unto Jesus, Master, it is good for us to be here: and let us make three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias: not knowing what he said.
ct: As Moses and Elijah were about to leave, Peter blurted out, "Master, it's wonderful that we're here! Let us build 3 shelters — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah." He didn't really know what he was saying.

kjv: While he thus spake, there came a cloud, and overshadowed them: and they feared as they entered into the cloud.
ct: While he was still speaking, a cloud rolled in and covered them. The disciples were terrified as the cloud surrounded them.

kjv: And there came a voice out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloved Son: hear him.
ct: Then a voice came from the cloud: "This is my beloved Son. Listen to him!"

kjv: And when the voice was past, Jesus was found alone. And they kept it close, and told no man in those days any of those things which they had seen.
ct: When the voice had spoken, Jesus was standing there alone. The disciples kept this to themselves and told no one at that time what they had seen.

kjv: And it came to pass, that on the next day, when they were come down from the hill, much people met him.
ct: The next day, when they came down from the mountain, a large crowd met them.

kjv: And, behold, a man of the company cried out, saying, Master, I beseech thee, look upon my son: for he is mine only child.
ct: A man in the crowd shouted, "Teacher, I'm begging you — please look at my son! He's my only child.

kjv: And, lo, a spirit taketh him, and he suddenly crieth out; and it teareth him that he foameth again, and bruising him hardly departeth from him.
ct: A spirit seizes him and he suddenly screams. It throws him into convulsions until he foams at the mouth. It batters him and barely ever leaves him alone.

kjv: And I besought thy disciples to cast him out; and they could not.
ct: I begged your disciples to drive it out, but they couldn't."

kjv: And Jesus answering said, O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you, and suffer you? Bring thy son hither.
ct: Jesus replied, "You faithless and twisted generation, how long must I stay with you and put up with you? Bring your son here."

kjv: And as he was yet a coming, the devil threw him down, and tare him. And Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the child, and delivered him again to his father.
ct: Even as the boy was coming forward, the demon slammed him to the ground and threw him into violent convulsions. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.

kjv: And they were all amazed at the mighty power of God. But while they wondered every one at all things which Jesus did, he said unto his disciples,
ct: Everyone was stunned by the mighty power of God. While they were all marveling at everything Jesus was doing, he said to his disciples,

kjv: Let these sayings sink down into your ears: for the Son of man shall be delivered into the hands of men.
ct: "Let these words sink deep into your ears: the Son of Man is going to be handed over to men."

kjv: But they understood not this saying, and it was hid from them, that they perceived it not: and they feared to ask him of that saying.
ct: But they didn't understand what he meant. The meaning was hidden from them so they couldn't grasp it, and they were afraid to ask him about it.

kjv: Then there arose a reasoning among them, which of them should be greatest.
ct: An argument broke out among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.

kjv: And Jesus, perceiving the thought of their heart, took a child, and set him by him,
ct: Jesus, knowing what they were thinking in their hearts, took a little child and had the child stand beside him.

kjv: And said unto them, Whosoever shall receive this child in my name receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me receiveth him that sent me: for he that is least among you all, the same shall be great.
ct: Then he told them, "Whoever welcomes this little child in my name welcomes me. And whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me. The one who is the least among all of you — that person is truly great."

kjv: And John answered and said, Master, we saw one casting out devils in thy name; and we forbad him, because he followeth not with us.
ct: John spoke up and said, "Master, we saw someone driving out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isn't part of our group."

kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Forbid him not: for he that is not against us is for us.
ct: Jesus told him, "Don't stop him. Whoever is not against you is for you."

kjv: And it came to pass, when the time was come that he should be received up, he stedfastly set his face to go to Jerusalem,
ct: As the time drew near for Jesus to be taken up to heaven, he resolutely set out for Jerusalem.

kjv: And sent messengers before his face: and they went, and entered into a village of the Samaritans, to make ready for him.
ct: He sent messengers ahead of him, and they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.

kjv: And they did not receive him, because his face was as though he would go to Jerusalem.
ct: But the people there refused to welcome him because he was heading toward Jerusalem.

kjv: And when his disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, wilt thou that we command fire to come down from heaven, and consume them, even as Elias did?
ct: When his disciples James and John saw this, they said, "Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to destroy them, just like Elijah did?"

kjv: But he turned, and rebuked them, and said, Ye know not what manner of spirit ye are of.
ct: But Jesus turned and rebuked them. "You don't even realize what kind of spirit is driving you to say that," he said.

kjv: For the Son of man is not come to destroy men’s lives, but to save them. And they went to another village.
ct: "The Son of Man didn't come to destroy people's lives but to save them." Then they moved on to another village.

kjv: And it came to pass, that, as they went in the way, a certain man said unto him, Lord, I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest.
ct: As they were walking along the road, a man said to him, "Lord, I'll follow you wherever you go."

kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.
ct: Jesus replied, "Foxes have dens and birds have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head."

kjv: And he said unto another, Follow me. But he said, Lord, suffer me first to go and bury my father.
ct: To another man he said, "Follow me." But the man replied, "Lord, let me go home first and bury my father."

kjv: Jesus said unto him, Let the dead bury their dead: but go thou and preach the kingdom of God.
ct: Jesus told him, "Let the dead bury their own dead. You go and proclaim the kingdom of God."

kjv: And another also said, Lord, I will follow thee; but let me first go bid them farewell, which are at home at my house.
ct: Yet another person said, "Lord, I'll follow you, but first let me go say goodbye to my family."

kjv: And Jesus said unto him, No man, having put his hand to the plough, and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God.
ct: Jesus said to him, "No one who puts his hand to the plow and then looks back is fit for the kingdom of God."

kjv: After these things the LORD appointed other seventy also, and sent them two and two before his face into every city and place, whither he himself would come.
ct: After this, the Lord appointed 70 others and sent them out in pairs ahead of him to every town and place he planned to visit.

kjv: Therefore said he unto them, The harvest truly is great, but the labourers are few: pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he would send forth labourers into his harvest.
ct: He told them, "The harvest is huge, but there aren't enough workers. So pray and ask the Lord of the harvest to send more workers into his fields.

kjv: Go your ways: behold, I send you forth as lambs among wolves.
ct: Now go! But understand this — I'm sending you out like lambs into a pack of wolves.

kjv: Carry neither purse, nor scrip, nor shoes: and salute no man by the way.
ct: Don't carry a money bag, a travel pack, or extra sandals. And don't stop to chat with anyone along the road.

kjv: And into whatsoever house ye enter, first say, Peace be to this house.
ct: When you enter a house, the first thing you should say is, 'Peace be to this house.'

kjv: And if the son of peace be there, your peace shall rest upon it: if not, it shall turn to you again.
ct: If someone there is open to peace, your blessing will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will come back to you.

kjv: And in the same house remain, eating and drinking such things as they give: for the labourer is worthy of his hire. Go not from house to house.
ct: Stay in that same house, and eat and drink whatever they offer you, because a worker deserves to be paid. Don't keep moving from house to house.

kjv: And into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you, eat such things as are set before you:
ct: When you enter a town and the people welcome you, eat whatever food they put in front of you.

kjv: And heal the sick that are therein, and say unto them, The kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.
ct: Heal the sick who are there, and tell them, 'The kingdom of God has come near to you.'

kjv: But into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you not, go your ways out into the streets of the same, and say,
ct: But when you enter a town and they don't welcome you, go out into the streets and say,

kjv: Even the very dust of your city, which cleaveth on us, we do wipe off against you: notwithstanding be ye sure of this, that the kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.
ct: 'We're wiping off even the dust of your town that clings to our feet as a witness against you. But know this — the kingdom of God has come near to you.'

kjv: But I say unto you, that it shall be more tolerable in that day for Sodom, than for that city.
ct: I'm telling you, on judgment day it will be more bearable for Sodom than for that town.

kjv: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon, which have been done in you, they had a great while ago repented, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
ct: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible for you, Bethsaida! If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, those cities would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.

kjv: But it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment, than for you.
ct: But on judgment day, it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon than for you.

kjv: And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted to heaven, shalt be thrust down to hell.
ct: And you, Capernaum — do you think you'll be lifted up to heaven? No, you'll be thrown down to hell.

kjv: He that heareth you heareth me; and he that despiseth you despiseth me; and he that despiseth me despiseth him that sent me.
ct: Whoever listens to you is listening to me. Whoever rejects you is rejecting me. And whoever rejects me is rejecting the one who sent me."

kjv: And the seventy returned again with joy, saying, Lord, even the devils are subject unto us through thy name.
ct: The 70 came back overjoyed. "Lord," they said, "even the demons obey us when we use your name!"

kjv: And he said unto them, I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven.
ct: Jesus replied, "I watched Satan fall from heaven like a bolt of lightning.

kjv: Behold, I give unto you power to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy: and nothing shall by any means hurt you.
ct: Listen — I've given you authority to trample on snakes and scorpions and to overpower every force of the enemy. Nothing will harm you in any way.

kjv: Notwithstanding in this rejoice not, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rather rejoice, because your names are written in heaven.
ct: But don't celebrate because the spirits submit to you. Instead, celebrate because your names are written in heaven."

kjv: In that hour Jesus rejoiced in spirit, and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes: even so, Father; for so it seemed good in thy sight.
ct: In that very moment, Jesus was filled with joy through the Holy Spirit and said, "I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you've hidden these things from the wise and educated and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, this is exactly what pleased you."

kjv: All things are delivered to me of my Father: and no man knoweth who the Son is, but the Father; and who the Father is, but the Son, and he to whom the Son will reveal him.
ct: Then turning to his disciples, he said, "My Father has entrusted everything to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, and no one knows who the Father is except the Son — and anyone the Son chooses to reveal him to."

kjv: And he turned him unto his disciples, and said privately, Blessed are the eyes which see the things that ye see:
ct: Then he turned to his disciples privately and said, "How blessed are the eyes that see what you're seeing!

kjv: For I tell you, that many prophets and kings have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.
ct: I tell you, many prophets and kings longed to see what you're seeing but never saw it, and to hear what you're hearing but never heard it."

kjv: And, behold, a certain lawyer stood up, and tempted him, saying, Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?
ct: One day an expert in the law stood up to test Jesus. "Teacher," he asked, "what must I do to inherit eternal life?"

kjv: He said unto him, What is written in the law? how readest thou?
ct: Jesus said to him, "What does the law say? How do you read it?"

kjv: And he answering said, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind; and thy neighbour as thyself.
ct: The man answered, "'Love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your strength, and with all your mind' — and 'love your neighbor as yourself.'"

kjv: And he said unto him, Thou hast answered right: this do, and thou shalt live.
ct: "That's the right answer," Jesus told him. "Do this, and you will live."

kjv: But he, willing to justify himself, said unto Jesus, And who is my neighbour?
ct: But the man wanted to justify himself, so he asked Jesus, "And who exactly is my neighbor?"

kjv: And Jesus answering said, A certain man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among thieves, which stripped him of his raiment, and wounded him, and departed, leaving him half dead.
ct: Jesus answered with a story: "A man was traveling the road from Jerusalem down to Jericho when he was attacked by robbers. They stripped him, beat him, and left him lying there half dead.

kjv: And by chance there came down a certain priest that way: and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
ct: A priest happened to be going down that same road. When he saw the man, he crossed to the other side and kept walking.

kjv: And likewise a Levite, when he was at the place, came and looked on him, and passed by on the other side.
ct: A Levite came along too. He walked over, looked at the man, and then crossed to the other side and kept going.

kjv: But a certain Samaritan, as he journeyed, came where he was: and when he saw him, he had compassion on him,
ct: Then a Samaritan came traveling down the road. When he saw the man, his heart broke for him.

kjv: And went to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring in oil and wine, and set him on his own beast, and brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
ct: He went to him, bandaged his wounds, and poured oil and wine on them to help them heal. Then he lifted the man onto his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.

kjv: And on the morrow when he departed, he took out two pence, and gave them to the host, and said unto him, Take care of him; and whatsoever thou spendest more, when I come again, I will repay thee.
ct: The next morning before he left, he gave the innkeeper 2 denarii and said, 'Take care of him. If you spend more than this, I'll pay you back when I return.'

kjv: Which now of these three, thinkest thou, was neighbour unto him that fell among the thieves?
ct: Now which of these 3 men do you think was a neighbor to the man who was attacked by robbers?"

kjv: And he said, He that shewed mercy on him. Then said Jesus unto him, Go, and do thou likewise.
ct: The expert in the law replied, "The one who showed him mercy." Jesus said to him, "Go and do the same."

kjv: Now it came to pass, as they went, that he entered into a certain village: and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.
ct: As Jesus and his disciples continued their journey, he came to a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.

kjv: And she had a sister called Mary, which also sat at Jesus’ feet, and heard his word.
ct: Martha had a sister named Mary, who sat at Jesus' feet and listened to what he was teaching.

kjv: But Martha was cumbered about much serving, and came to him, and said, Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone? bid her therefore that she help me.
ct: But Martha was overwhelmed with all the preparations. She came to Jesus and said, "Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come help me!"

kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto her, Martha, Martha, thou art careful and troubled about many things:
ct: "Martha, Martha," Jesus answered, "you're worried and stressed about so many things.

kjv: But one thing is needful: and Mary hath chosen that good part, which shall not be taken away from her.
ct: But only one thing really matters. Mary has chosen what's best, and it won't be taken away from her."

kjv: And it came to pass, that, as he was praying in a certain place, when he ceased, one of his disciples said unto him, Lord, teach us to pray, as John also taught his disciples.
ct: One day, Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said to him, "Lord, teach us to pray, just like John taught his disciples."

kjv: And he said unto them, When ye pray, say, Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done, as in heaven, so in earth.
ct: He told them, "When you pray, say: 'Father in heaven, may your name be honored as holy. May your kingdom come. May your will be done on earth, just as it is in heaven.

kjv: Give us day by day our daily bread.
ct: Give us each day the bread we need.

kjv: And forgive us our sins; for we also forgive every one that is indebted to us. And lead us not into temptation; but deliver us from evil.
ct: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who wrongs us. Don't lead us into temptation, but deliver us from evil.'"

kjv: And he said unto them, Which of you shall have a friend, and shall go unto him at midnight, and say unto him, Friend, lend me three loaves;
ct: Then he said to them, "Imagine you go to a friend's house at midnight and say, 'Friend, lend me 3 loaves of bread.

kjv: For a friend of mine in his journey is come to me, and I have nothing to set before him?
ct: A friend of mine just showed up from a journey, and I don't have anything to offer him.'

kjv: And he from within shall answer and say, Trouble me not: the door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot rise and give thee.
ct: And your friend calls out from inside, 'Don't bother me. The door is already locked, and my children are in bed with me. I can't get up and give you anything.'

kjv: I say unto you, Though he will not rise and give him, because he is his friend, yet because of his importunity he will rise and give him as many as he needeth.
ct: I'm telling you, even if he won't get up because of friendship alone, he will get up and give you whatever you need because of your bold persistence.

kjv: And I say unto you, Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.
ct: "So I tell you: keep asking, and it will be given to you. Keep seeking, and you will find. Keep knocking, and the door will be opened for you.

kjv: For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.
ct: Because everyone who asks receives. Everyone who seeks finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door will be opened.

kjv: If a son shall ask bread of any of you that is a father, will he give him a stone? or if he ask a fish, will he for a fish give him a serpent?
ct: "What father among you, if his son asks for bread, would hand him a stone? Or if he asks for a fish, would give him a snake instead?

kjv: Or if he shall ask an egg, will he offer him a scorpion?
ct: Or if he asks for an egg, would hand him a scorpion?

kjv: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children: how much more shall your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him?
ct: So if you — flawed as you are — know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him?"

kjv: And he was casting out a devil, and it was dumb. And it came to pass, when the devil was gone out, the dumb spake; and the people wondered.
ct: Jesus was driving out a demon that had made a man unable to speak. When the demon left, the man began talking, and the crowd was amazed.

kjv: But some of them said, He casteth out devils through Beelzebub the chief of the devils.
ct: But some of them said, "He drives out demons by the power of Beelzebub, the ruler of demons."

kjv: And others, tempting him, sought of him a sign from heaven.
ct: Others, trying to test him, demanded that he perform a miraculous sign from heaven.

kjv: But he, knowing their thoughts, said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and a house divided against a house falleth.
ct: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said to them, "Any kingdom torn apart by civil war will collapse. A household divided against itself will fall.

kjv: If Satan also be divided against himself, how shall his kingdom stand? because ye say that I cast out devils through Beelzebub.
ct: So if Satan is fighting against himself, how can his kingdom survive? I ask because you're claiming I drive out demons by Beelzebub's power.

kjv: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your sons cast them out? therefore shall they be your judges.
ct: And if I drive out demons by Beelzebub, then by whose power do your own followers drive them out? They themselves will prove you wrong.

kjv: But if I with the finger of God cast out devils, no doubt the kingdom of God is come upon you.
ct: But if I drive out demons by the finger of God, then the kingdom of God has arrived right here among you.

kjv: When a strong man armed keepeth his palace, his goods are in peace:
ct: "When a strong man is fully armed and guards his estate, everything he owns is safe.

kjv: But when a stronger than he shall come upon him, and overcome him, he taketh from him all his armour wherein he trusted, and divideth his spoils.
ct: But when someone even stronger attacks and overpowers him, that stronger one strips away all the armor he relied on and carries off his possessions.

kjv: He that is not with me is against me: and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.
ct: "Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever doesn't gather with me scatters.

kjv: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest; and finding none, he saith, I will return unto my house whence I came out.
ct: "When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through barren places looking for rest. When it finds none, it says, 'I'll go back to the home I left.'

kjv: And when he cometh, he findeth it swept and garnished.
ct: When it returns, it finds the place swept clean and put in order.

kjv: Then goeth he, and taketh to him seven other spirits more wicked than himself; and they enter in, and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first.
ct: Then it goes and brings 7 other spirits more wicked than itself, and they all move in and settle there. That person ends up far worse off than before."

kjv: And it came to pass, as he spake these things, a certain woman of the company lifted up her voice, and said unto him, Blessed is the womb that bare thee, and the paps which thou hast sucked.
ct: While he was saying these things, a woman in the crowd raised her voice and called out, "Blessed is the mother who gave birth to you and nursed you!"

kjv: But he said, Yea rather, blessed are they that hear the word of God, and keep it.
ct: But Jesus replied, "Even more blessed are those who hear the word of God and obey it."

kjv: And when the people were gathered thick together, he began to say, This is an evil generation: they seek a sign; and there shall no sign be given it, but the sign of Jonas the prophet.
ct: As the crowds grew larger, Jesus began to say, "This is an evil generation. It demands a miraculous sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of Jonah the prophet.

kjv: For as Jonas was a sign unto the Ninevites, so shall also the Son of man be to this generation.
ct: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Son of Man will be a sign to this generation.

kjv: The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and condemn them: for she came from the utmost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.
ct: The Queen of the South will stand up at the judgment alongside the people of this generation and condemn them, because she traveled from the ends of the earth to hear Solomon's wisdom — and someone greater than Solomon is standing right here.

kjv: The men of Nineve shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.
ct: The people of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment alongside this generation and condemn it, because they repented when Jonah preached to them — and someone greater than Jonah is standing right here.

kjv: No man, when he hath lighted a candle, putteth it in a secret place, neither under a bushel, but on a candlestick, that they which come in may see the light.
ct: "No one lights a lamp and then hides it away or puts it under a basket. Instead, they set it on a stand so that everyone who comes in can see the light.

kjv: The light of the body is the eye: therefore when thine eye is single, thy whole body also is full of light; but when thine eye is evil, thy body also is full of darkness.
ct: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.

kjv: Take heed therefore that the light which is in thee be not darkness.
ct: So be careful that the light inside you hasn't actually become darkness.

kjv: If thy whole body therefore be full of light, having no part dark, the whole shall be full of light, as when the bright shining of a candle doth give thee light.
ct: If your whole body is full of light, with no dark corner left, it will be completely radiant — like when a lamp shines its bright light on you."

kjv: And as he spake, a certain Pharisee besought him to dine with him: and he went in, and sat down to meat.
ct: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him to eat at his house. So Jesus went in and took his place at the table.

kjv: And when the Pharisee saw it, he marvelled that he had not first washed before dinner.
ct: The Pharisee was surprised to see that Jesus didn't perform the ritual hand-washing before the meal.

kjv: And the Lord said unto him, Now do ye Pharisees make clean the outside of the cup and the platter; but your inward part is full of ravening and wickedness.
ct: The Lord said to him, "You Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and the plate, but inside you're full of greed and wickedness.

kjv: Ye fools, did not he that made that which is without make that which is within also?
ct: You fools! Didn't the one who made the outside also make the inside?

kjv: But rather give alms of such things as ye have; and, behold, all things are clean unto you.
ct: Instead, give generously to the poor from what you have, and then everything will be clean for you.

kjv: But woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye tithe mint and rue and all manner of herbs, and pass over judgment and the love of God: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.
ct: "But how terrible it will be for you Pharisees! You carefully tithe your mint, rue, and every little herb, but you completely ignore justice and the love of God. You should have practiced these things without neglecting the others.

kjv: Woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues, and greetings in the markets.
ct: "How terrible it will be for you Pharisees! You love the most important seats in the synagogues and being greeted with respect in the marketplaces.

kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are as graves which appear not, and the men that walk over them are not aware of them.
ct: "How terrible it will be for you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You're like unmarked graves that people walk over without even knowing it."

kjv: Then answered one of the lawyers, and said unto him, Master, thus saying thou reproachest us also.
ct: One of the experts in the law spoke up and said, "Teacher, when you say these things, you're insulting us too."

kjv: And he said, Woe unto you also, ye lawyers! for ye lade men with burdens grievous to be borne, and ye yourselves touch not the burdens with one of your fingers.
ct: Jesus replied, "How terrible it will be for you experts in the law as well! You pile heavy burdens on people's backs that are almost impossible to carry, but you won't lift a single finger to help them.

kjv: Woe unto you! for ye build the sepulchres of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.
ct: "How terrible it will be for you! You build impressive tombs for the prophets, but it was your own ancestors who killed them.

kjv: Truly ye bear witness that ye allow the deeds of your fathers: for they indeed killed them, and ye build their sepulchres.
ct: By doing this, you show that you approve of what your ancestors did. They murdered the prophets, and you build their monuments.

kjv: Therefore also said the wisdom of God, I will send them prophets and apostles, and some of them they shall slay and persecute:
ct: "That's why the wisdom of God declared, 'I will send them prophets and apostles. Some of them they will kill, and others they will persecute.'

kjv: That the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;
ct: As a result, this generation will be held accountable for the blood of every prophet shed since the world began —

kjv: From the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharias, which perished between the altar and the temple: verily I say unto you, It shall be required of this generation.
ct: from the blood of Abel all the way to the blood of Zachariah, who was killed between the altar and the sanctuary. Yes, I'm telling you, this generation will answer for all of it.

kjv: Woe unto you, lawyers! for ye have taken away the key of knowledge: ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered.
ct: "How terrible it will be for you experts in the law! You've taken away the key to knowledge. You didn't enter in yourselves, and you blocked those who were trying to enter."

kjv: And as he said these things unto them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to urge him vehemently, and to provoke him to speak of many things:
ct: After Jesus left that place, the scribes and the Pharisees became furiously hostile toward him, bombarding him with questions about all kinds of topics,

kjv: Laying wait for him, and seeking to catch something out of his mouth, that they might accuse him.
ct: setting traps for him and trying to catch him saying something they could use against him.

kjv: In the mean time, when there were gathered together an innumerable multitude of people, insomuch that they trode one upon another, he began to say unto his disciples first of all, Beware ye of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
ct: Meanwhile, a crowd of thousands had gathered — so many that people were trampling each other. Jesus turned first to his disciples and said, "Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

kjv: For there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; neither hid, that shall not be known.
ct: Nothing that's covered up will stay hidden forever, and nothing kept secret will remain unknown.

kjv: Therefore whatsoever ye have spoken in darkness shall be heard in the light; and that which ye have spoken in the ear in closets shall be proclaimed upon the housetops.
ct: Whatever you've whispered in the dark will be heard in broad daylight, and what you've said behind closed doors will be shouted from the rooftops.

kjv: And I say unto you my friends, Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.
ct: "I'm telling you, my friends — don't be afraid of people who can kill the body but can't do anything more than that.

kjv: But I will forewarn you whom ye shall fear: Fear him, which after he hath killed hath power to cast into hell; yea, I say unto you, Fear him.
ct: Let me tell you who to fear: fear the one who, after killing, has the power to throw you into hell. Yes, I'm telling you — fear him.

kjv: Are not five sparrows sold for two farthings, and not one of them is forgotten before God?
ct: "Aren't 5 sparrows sold for just 2 small coins? Yet not a single one of them is forgotten by God.

kjv: But even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not therefore: ye are of more value than many sparrows.
ct: In fact, every hair on your head has been counted. So don't be afraid — you are worth far more than many sparrows.

kjv: Also I say unto you, Whosoever shall confess me before men, him shall the Son of man also confess before the angels of God:
ct: "I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me publicly will be acknowledged by the Son of Man before the angels of God.

kjv: But he that denieth me before men shall be denied before the angels of God.
ct: But whoever denies me in front of others will be denied before the angels of God.

kjv: And whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but unto him that blasphemeth against the Holy Ghost it shall not be forgiven.
ct: Anyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.

kjv: And when they bring you unto the synagogues, and unto magistrates, and powers, take ye no thought how or what thing ye shall answer, or what ye shall say:
ct: When they drag you before synagogues, rulers, and authorities, don't worry about how to defend yourselves or what to say.

kjv: For the Holy Ghost shall teach you in the same hour what ye ought to say.
ct: The Holy Spirit will teach you in that very moment what you need to say."

kjv: And one of the company said unto him, Master, speak to my brother, that he divide the inheritance with me.
ct: Someone in the crowd called out, "Teacher, tell my brother to split the inheritance with me."

kjv: And he said unto him, Man, who made me a judge or a divider over you?
ct: Jesus replied, "Friend, who appointed me as a judge or mediator between you two?"

kjv: And he said unto them, Take heed, and beware of covetousness: for a man’s life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth.
ct: Then he said to them all, "Be on your guard against every kind of greed, because a person's life isn't measured by how much they own."

kjv: And he spake a parable unto them, saying, The ground of a certain rich man brought forth plentifully:
ct: He told them a parable: "A rich man's land produced an enormous harvest.

kjv: And he thought within himself, saying, What shall I do, because I have no room where to bestow my fruits?
ct: He thought to himself, 'What am I going to do? I don't have enough room to store all my crops.'

kjv: And he said, This will I do: I will pull down my barns, and build greater; and there will I bestow all my fruits and my goods.
ct: Then he said, 'Here's what I'll do — I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, and I'll store all my grain and goods there.

kjv: And I will say to my soul, Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years; take thine ease, eat, drink, and be merry.
ct: Then I'll say to my soul, "Soul, you've got plenty stored up for years to come. Relax! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself."'

kjv: But God said unto him, Thou fool, this night thy soul shall be required of thee: then whose shall those things be, which thou hast provided?
ct: "But God said to him, 'You fool! Tonight your soul will be demanded from you. Then who will get everything you've stored up?'

kjv: So is he that layeth up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.
ct: That's how it is for anyone who hoards wealth for themselves but isn't rich toward God."

kjv: And he said unto his disciples, Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat; neither for the body, what ye shall put on.
ct: Then Jesus said to his disciples, "That's why I tell you — stop worrying about your life, what you'll eat, or about your body, what you'll wear.

kjv: The life is more than meat, and the body is more than raiment.
ct: Life is about more than food, and the body is about more than clothing.

kjv: Consider the ravens: for they neither sow nor reap; which neither have storehouse nor barn; and God feedeth them: how much more are ye better than the fowls?
ct: Look at the ravens: they don't plant or harvest, they have no storeroom or barn, yet God feeds them. How much more valuable are you than birds!

kjv: And which of you with taking thought can add to his stature one cubit?
ct: Which of you can add a single cubit to your height by worrying about it?

kjv: If ye then be not able to do that thing which is least, why take ye thought for the rest?
ct: If you can't even do something that small, why worry about the rest?

kjv: Consider the lilies how they grow: they toil not, they spin not; and yet I say unto you, that Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
ct: Look at how the lilies grow. They don't work or weave cloth. Yet I tell you, not even Solomon in all his glory was dressed as beautifully as one of these.

kjv: If then God so clothe the grass, which is to day in the field, and to morrow is cast into the oven; how much more will he clothe you, O ye of little faith?
ct: If that's how God clothes the grass — here in the field today, thrown into the oven tomorrow — how much more will he clothe you, O you of little faith?

kjv: And seek not ye what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink, neither be ye of doubtful mind.
ct: So don't obsess over what you'll eat or what you'll drink. Don't let your mind be consumed with worry.

kjv: For all these things do the nations of the world seek after: and your Father knoweth that ye have need of these things.
ct: The nations of the world chase after all these things, but your Father already knows you need them.

kjv: But rather seek ye the kingdom of God; and all these things shall be added unto you.
ct: Instead, seek the kingdom of God, and all these things will be given to you as well.

kjv: Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father’s good pleasure to give you the kingdom.
ct: Don't be afraid, little flock, because it is your Father's joy to give you the kingdom.

kjv: Sell that ye have, and give alms; provide yourselves bags which wax not old, a treasure in the heavens that faileth not, where no thief approacheth, neither moth corrupteth.
ct: Sell your possessions and give to the poor. Make for yourselves purses that never wear out — a treasure in the heavens that never runs out, where no thief can reach it and no moth can destroy it.

kjv: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
ct: Because wherever your treasure is, that's where your heart will be too.

kjv: Let your loins be girded about, and your lights burning;
ct: "Stay dressed and ready for action, with your lamps burning.

kjv: And ye yourselves like unto men that wait for their lord, when he will return from the wedding; that when he cometh and knocketh, they may open unto him immediately.
ct: Be like servants waiting for their master to return from a wedding feast, so that when he arrives and knocks, they can open the door for him right away.

kjv: Blessed are those servants, whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching: verily I say unto you, that he shall gird himself, and make them to sit down to meat, and will come forth and serve them.
ct: How blessed are those servants whose master finds them alert and waiting when he comes! I tell you the truth — he will put on an apron, seat them at the table, and come out to serve them himself.

kjv: And if he shall come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find them so, blessed are those servants.
ct: Whether he comes at midnight or even later, those servants are blessed if he finds them ready.

kjv: And this know, that if the goodman of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched, and not have suffered his house to be broken through.
ct: But understand this: if the homeowner had known what hour the thief was coming, he would have stayed awake and never let his house be broken into.

kjv: Be ye therefore ready also: for the Son of man cometh at an hour when ye think not.
ct: So you must be ready too, because the Son of Man is coming at an hour you don't expect."

kjv: Then Peter said unto him, Lord, speakest thou this parable unto us, or even to all?
ct: Peter asked, "Lord, are you telling this parable just to us, or to everyone?"

kjv: And the Lord said, Who then is that faithful and wise steward, whom his lord shall make ruler over his household, to give them their portion of meat in due season?
ct: The Lord replied, "Who then is the faithful and wise manager that the master puts in charge of his household to give them their food at the proper time?

kjv: Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.
ct: That servant is blessed if the master finds him doing exactly that when he arrives.

kjv: Of a truth I say unto you, that he will make him ruler over all that he hath.
ct: I tell you the truth — the master will put him in charge of everything he owns.

kjv: But and if that servant say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming; and shall begin to beat the menservants and maidens, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken;
ct: But suppose that servant says to himself, 'My master is taking a long time coming,' and starts beating the other servants, both men and women, and starts eating and drinking until he's drunk.

kjv: The lord of that servant will come in a day when he looketh not for him, and at an hour when he is not aware, and will cut him in sunder, and will appoint him his portion with the unbelievers.
ct: That servant's master will show up on a day he doesn't expect, at an hour he doesn't know. The master will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unfaithful.

kjv: And that servant, which knew his lord’s will, and prepared not himself, neither did according to his will, shall be beaten with many stripes.
ct: The servant who knew his master's wishes but didn't get ready or carry them out will receive a severe beating.

kjv: But he that knew not, and did commit things worthy of stripes, shall be beaten with few stripes. For unto whomsoever much is given, of him shall be much required: and to whom men have committed much, of him they will ask the more.
ct: But the one who didn't know and did things deserving punishment will receive a lighter beating. From everyone who has been given much, much will be demanded. And from the one entrusted with much, even more will be expected.

kjv: I am come to send fire on the earth; and what will I, if it be already kindled?
ct: "I came to set the earth on fire, and how I wish it were already blazing!

kjv: But I have a baptism to be baptized with; and how am I straitened till it be accomplished!
ct: But I have a baptism to undergo, and how distressed I am until it's completed!

kjv: Suppose ye that I am come to give peace on earth? I tell you, Nay; but rather division:
ct: Do you think I came to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you — I came to bring division.

kjv: For from henceforth there shall be five in one house divided, three against two, and two against three.
ct: From now on, a family of 5 will be split apart — 3 against 2 and 2 against 3.

kjv: The father shall be divided against the son, and the son against the father; the mother against the daughter, and the daughter against the mother; the mother in law against her daughter in law, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.
ct: Fathers will turn against sons and sons against fathers, mothers against daughters and daughters against mothers, mothers-in-law against daughters-in-law and daughters-in-law against mothers-in-law."

kjv: And he said also to the people, When ye see a cloud rise out of the west, straightway ye say, There cometh a shower; and so it is.
ct: Then he said to the crowds, "When you see clouds forming in the west, you immediately say, 'Rain is coming,' and it does.

kjv: And when ye see the south wind blow, ye say, There will be heat; and it cometh to pass.
ct: When the south wind blows, you say, 'It's going to be scorching hot,' and it is.

kjv: Ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky and of the earth; but how is it that ye do not discern this time?
ct: You hypocrites! You know how to read the weather by looking at the earth and sky, so why can't you read the signs of this present time?

kjv: Yea, and why even of yourselves judge ye not what is right?
ct: And why don't you judge for yourselves what is right?

kjv: When thou goest with thine adversary to the magistrate, as thou art in the way, give diligence that thou mayest be delivered from him; lest he hale thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and the officer cast thee into prison.
ct: When you're on your way to court with someone who has a case against you, do everything you can to settle it before you get there. Otherwise, they may drag you before the judge, the judge will hand you over to the officer, and the officer will throw you in prison.

kjv: I tell thee, thou shalt not depart thence, till thou hast paid the very last mite.
ct: I tell you, you won't get out until you've paid back the very last penny."

kjv: There were present at that season some that told him of the Galilaeans, whose blood Pilate had mingled with their sacrifices.
ct: Around that time, some people told Jesus about a group of Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices — their own blood mixed with the blood of their offerings.

kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, Suppose ye that these Galilaeans were sinners above all the Galilaeans, because they suffered such things?
ct: Jesus responded, "Do you think those Galileans were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because this happened to them?

kjv: I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.
ct: Not at all. But I'm telling you, unless you repent, every one of you will perish the same way.

kjv: Or those eighteen, upon whom the tower in Siloam fell, and slew them, think ye that they were sinners above all men that dwelt in Jerusalem?
ct: And what about the 18 people who died when the tower in Siloam collapsed on them? Do you think they were guiltier than everyone else living in Jerusalem?

kjv: I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.
ct: Not at all. But I'm telling you, unless you repent, every one of you will perish the same way."

kjv: He spake also this parable; A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard; and he came and sought fruit thereon, and found none.
ct: Then he told them this parable: "A man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He went to check it for fruit but didn't find any.

kjv: Then said he unto the dresser of his vineyard, Behold, these three years I come seeking fruit on this fig tree, and find none: cut it down; why cumbereth it the ground?
ct: So he told the gardener, 'Look, I've come looking for fruit on this fig tree for 3 years now and haven't found a single piece. Cut it down — why should it take up space in the soil?'

kjv: And he answering said unto him, Lord, let it alone this year also, till I shall dig about it, and dung it:
ct: The gardener replied, 'Sir, give it one more year. Let me dig around it and add fertilizer.

kjv: And if it bear fruit, well: and if not, then after that thou shalt cut it down.
ct: If it produces fruit next year, great. But if not, then you can cut it down.'"

kjv: And he was teaching in one of the synagogues on the sabbath.
ct: One Sabbath day, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue.

kjv: And, behold, there was a woman which had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years, and was bowed together, and could in no wise lift up herself.
ct: A woman was there who had been crippled by an evil spirit for 18 years. She was hunched over and completely unable to stand up straight.

kjv: And when Jesus saw her, he called her to him, and said unto her, Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity.
ct: When Jesus noticed her, he called her over and said, "Woman, you are set free from your condition."

kjv: And he laid his hands on her: and immediately she was made straight, and glorified God.
ct: He placed his hands on her, and instantly she straightened up and began praising God.

kjv: And the ruler of the synagogue answered with indignation, because that Jesus had healed on the sabbath day, and said unto the people, There are six days in which men ought to work: in them therefore come and be healed, and not on the sabbath day.
ct: But the synagogue leader was furious that Jesus had healed someone on the Sabbath. He turned to the crowd and said, "There are 6 days for work. Come and get healed on one of those days — not on the Sabbath!"

kjv: The Lord then answered him, and said, Thou hypocrite, doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or his ass from the stall, and lead him away to watering?
ct: The Lord fired back, "You hypocrites! Every one of you unties your ox or donkey from its stall on the Sabbath and leads it out to get water.

kjv: And ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan hath bound, lo, these eighteen years, be loosed from this bond on the sabbath day?
ct: So why shouldn't this woman — a daughter of Abraham whom Satan has kept bound for 18 long years — be set free from her chains on the Sabbath?"

kjv: And when he had said these things, all his adversaries were ashamed: and all the people rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him.
ct: His response put all his critics to shame, while the rest of the crowd celebrated the incredible things he was doing.

kjv: Then said he, Unto what is the kingdom of God like? and whereunto shall I resemble it?
ct: Then Jesus asked, "What is the kingdom of God like? What can I compare it to?

kjv: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and cast into his garden; and it grew, and waxed a great tree; and the fowls of the air lodged in the branches of it.
ct: It's like a mustard seed that a man planted in his garden. It grew and became a large tree, and birds came and nested in its branches."

kjv: And again he said, Whereunto shall I liken the kingdom of God?
ct: He continued, "What else can I compare the kingdom of God to?

kjv: It is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.
ct: It's like yeast that a woman mixed into 3 measures of flour. Eventually, the yeast worked its way through the entire batch of dough."

kjv: And he went through the cities and villages, teaching, and journeying toward Jerusalem.
ct: Jesus continued traveling through towns and villages, teaching as he made his way toward Jerusalem.

kjv: Then said one unto him, Lord, are there few that be saved? And he said unto them,
ct: Someone asked him, "Lord, will only a few people be saved?" He answered them,

kjv: Strive to enter in at the strait gate: for many, I say unto you, will seek to enter in, and shall not be able.
ct: "Fight to get through the narrow door, because many people will try to enter and won't be able to.

kjv: When once the master of the house is risen up, and hath shut to the door, and ye begin to stand without, and to knock at the door, saying, Lord, Lord, open unto us; and he shall answer and say unto you, I know you not whence ye are:
ct: Once the master of the house gets up and locks the door, you'll be left standing outside knocking and calling, 'Lord, Lord, open up for us!' But he'll answer, 'I don't know you or where you come from.'

kjv: Then shall ye begin to say, We have eaten and drunk in thy presence, and thou hast taught in our streets.
ct: Then you'll protest, 'But we ate and drank with you! You taught in our streets!'

kjv: But he shall say, I tell you, I know you not whence ye are; depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity.
ct: And he'll reply, 'I'm telling you, I don't know where you come from. Get away from me, all of you who practice evil.'

kjv: There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth, when ye shall see Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and all the prophets, in the kingdom of God, and you yourselves thrust out.
ct: You'll weep and grind your teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets inside the kingdom of God — while you yourselves are thrown out.

kjv: And they shall come from the east, and from the west, and from the north, and from the south, and shall sit down in the kingdom of God.
ct: People will come from east and west, from north and south, and take their places at the table in the kingdom of God.

kjv: And, behold, there are last which shall be first, and there are first which shall be last.
ct: Pay attention: some who are last will end up first, and some who are first will end up last."

kjv: The same day there came certain of the Pharisees, saying unto him, Get thee out, and depart hence: for Herod will kill thee.
ct: That same day, some Pharisees came to Jesus and warned him, "You need to leave this area — Herod wants to kill you."

kjv: And he said unto them, Go ye, and tell that fox, Behold, I cast out devils, and I do cures to day and to morrow, and the third day I shall be perfected.
ct: Jesus replied, "Go tell that fox, 'Look, I'm driving out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the 3rd day I will finish my work.'

kjv: Nevertheless I must walk to day, and to morrow, and the day following: for it cannot be that a prophet perish out of Jerusalem.
ct: But I must keep moving today, tomorrow, and the next day — because no prophet can die outside of Jerusalem."

kjv: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, which killest the prophets, and stonest them that are sent unto thee; how often would I have gathered thy children together, as a hen doth gather her brood under her wings, and ye would not!
ct: "Jerusalem, Jerusalem — you murder the prophets and stone the messengers God sends to you! How many times I wanted to gather your children together the way a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you refused!

kjv: Behold, your house is left unto you desolate: and verily I say unto you, Ye shall not see me, until the time come when ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.
ct: Look, your house is abandoned and desolate. I tell you, you won't see me again until the day comes when you say, 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!'"

kjv: And it came to pass, as he went into the house of one of the chief Pharisees to eat bread on the sabbath day, that they watched him.
ct: One Sabbath, Jesus went to eat at the home of a prominent Pharisee leader. The other guests were watching him closely.

kjv: And, behold, there was a certain man before him which had the dropsy.
ct: Right there in front of him was a man suffering from severe swelling in his body.

kjv: And Jesus answering spake unto the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath day?
ct: Jesus turned to the experts in the law and the Pharisees and asked, "Does the law allow healing on the Sabbath, or not?"

kjv: And they held their peace. And he took him, and healed him, and let him go;
ct: They refused to answer. So Jesus took hold of the man, healed him, and sent him on his way.

kjv: And answered them, saying, Which of you shall have an ass or an ox fallen into a pit, and will not straightway pull him out on the sabbath day?
ct: Then he said to them, "If your son or your ox fell into a well on the Sabbath, wouldn't you pull him out immediately?"

kjv: And they could not answer him again to these things.
ct: They had nothing to say to that.

kjv: And he put forth a parable to those which were bidden, when he marked how they chose out the chief rooms; saying unto them,
ct: Jesus noticed how the guests were scrambling for the best seats at the table, so he told them this parable:

kjv: When thou art bidden of any man to a wedding, sit not down in the highest room; lest a more honourable man than thou be bidden of him;
ct: "When someone invites you to a wedding feast, don't take the seat of honor. Someone more important than you may have been invited,

kjv: And he that bade thee and him come and say to thee, Give this man place; and thou begin with shame to take the lowest room.
ct: and the host will come over and say, 'Please give your seat to this person.' Then you'll have to move to the worst seat in the room, completely embarrassed.

kjv: But when thou art bidden, go and sit down in the lowest room; that when he that bade thee cometh, he may say unto thee, Friend, go up higher: then shalt thou have worship in the presence of them that sit at meat with thee.
ct: Instead, when you're invited, sit in the lowest place. That way, when the host sees you, he'll say, 'Friend, come sit up here!' And everyone at the table will see you honored.

kjv: For whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.
ct: Because everyone who lifts himself up will be brought low, and everyone who humbles himself will be lifted up."

kjv: Then said he also to him that bade him, When thou makest a dinner or a supper, call not thy friends, nor thy brethren, neither thy kinsmen, nor thy rich neighbours; lest they also bid thee again, and a recompence be made thee.
ct: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him, "When you host a lunch or dinner, don't just invite your friends, your brothers, your relatives, or your wealthy neighbors — because they'll return the favor, and you'll already have your reward.

kjv: But when thou makest a feast, call the poor, the maimed, the lame, the blind:
ct: Instead, when you throw a feast, invite the poor, the disabled, the lame, and the blind.

kjv: And thou shalt be blessed; for they cannot recompense thee: for thou shalt be recompensed at the resurrection of the just.
ct: Then you will be blessed, because they have no way to repay you. God himself will repay you at the resurrection of the righteous."

kjv: And when one of them that sat at meat with him heard these things, he said unto him, Blessed is he that shall eat bread in the kingdom of God.
ct: One of the guests at the table heard this and said to Jesus, "What a blessing it will be to feast in the kingdom of God!"

kjv: Then said he unto him, A certain man made a great supper, and bade many:
ct: Jesus replied, "A man once prepared a grand banquet and invited many guests.

kjv: And sent his servant at supper time to say to them that were bidden, Come; for all things are now ready.
ct: When everything was ready, he sent his servant to tell those who had been invited, 'Come! Everything is prepared.'

kjv: And they all with one consent began to make excuse. The first said unto him, I have bought a piece of ground, and I must needs go and see it: I pray thee have me excused.
ct: But one after another, they all started making excuses. The first one said, 'I just bought a piece of land and I need to go look at it. Please excuse me.'

kjv: And another said, I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I go to prove them: I pray thee have me excused.
ct: Another said, 'I just bought 5 yoke of oxen and I'm on my way to try them out. Please excuse me.'

kjv: And another said, I have married a wife, and therefore I cannot come.
ct: Another said, 'I just got married, so I can't come.'

kjv: So that servant came, and shewed his lord these things. Then the master of the house being angry said to his servant, Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in hither the poor, and the maimed, and the halt, and the blind.
ct: The servant came back and reported all of this to his master. The master was furious. He told his servant, 'Go out right now into the streets and alleys of the city and bring back the poor, the disabled, the blind, and the lame.'

kjv: And the servant said, Lord, it is done as thou hast commanded, and yet there is room.
ct: The servant returned and said, 'Sir, I've done what you ordered, but there's still room.'

kjv: And the lord said unto the servant, Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.
ct: The master told him, 'Then go out to the roads and country lanes and urge people to come in, so that my house will be full.

kjv: For I say unto you, That none of those men which were bidden shall taste of my supper.
ct: Because I'm telling you, not one of those people who were originally invited will get even a taste of my banquet.'"

kjv: And there went great multitudes with him: and he turned, and said unto them,
ct: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. He turned around and said to them,

kjv: If any man come to me, and hate not his father, and mother, and wife, and children, and brethren, and sisters, yea, and his own life also, he cannot be my disciple.
ct: "If anyone comes to me but isn't willing to put me above his father, mother, wife, children, brothers, sisters — and even his own life — he cannot be my disciple.

kjv: And whosoever doth not bear his cross, and come after me, cannot be my disciple.
ct: Anyone who doesn't carry his own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.

kjv: For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have sufficient to finish it?
ct: Suppose you want to build a tower. Wouldn't you first sit down and figure out the cost to make sure you can finish it?

kjv: Lest haply, after he hath laid the foundation, and is not able to finish it, all that behold it begin to mock him,
ct: Otherwise, you might lay the foundation and then run out of money. Everyone who sees it will laugh at you,

kjv: Saying, This man began to build, and was not able to finish.
ct: saying, 'This guy started building and couldn't even finish!'

kjv: Or what king, going to make war against another king, sitteth not down first, and consulteth whether he be able with ten thousand to meet him that cometh against him with twenty thousand?
ct: Or suppose a king is about to go to war against another king. Won't he first sit down and consider whether his 10,000 soldiers can defeat the one coming against him with 20,000?

kjv: Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sendeth an ambassage, and desireth conditions of peace.
ct: If he can't win, he'll send a delegation while the enemy is still far away to negotiate terms of peace.

kjv: So likewise, whosoever he be of you that forsaketh not all that he hath, he cannot be my disciple.
ct: In the same way, anyone who doesn't give up everything he has cannot be my disciple.

kjv: Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be seasoned?
ct: Salt is useful. But if salt loses its flavor, how can you make it salty again?

kjv: It is neither fit for the land, nor yet for the dunghill; but men cast it out. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
ct: It's useless — no good for the soil, no good even for the manure pile. People just throw it away. If you have ears to hear, then listen!"

kjv: Then drew near unto him all the publicans and sinners for to hear him.
ct: Tax collectors and sinners of all kinds were crowding around Jesus to listen to him.

kjv: And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.
ct: The Pharisees and the teachers of the law complained, "This man welcomes sinners and even eats with them."

kjv: And he spake this parable unto them, saying,
ct: So Jesus told them this parable:

kjv: What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?
ct: "Suppose one of you has 100 sheep and loses one of them. Wouldn't you leave the 99 out in the open and go searching for the lost one until you find it?

kjv: And when he hath found it, he layeth it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
ct: When you find it, you'd lift it onto your shoulders, overjoyed.

kjv: And when he cometh home, he calleth together his friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost.
ct: Then you'd go home and call your friends and neighbors together and say, 'Celebrate with me! I found my lost sheep!'

kjv: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.
ct: I'm telling you, there is more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over 99 righteous people who don't need repentance.

kjv: Either what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find it?
ct: Or imagine a woman who has 10 silver coins and loses one. Wouldn't she light a lamp, sweep the entire house, and search carefully until she finds it?

kjv: And when she hath found it, she calleth her friends and her neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the piece which I had lost.
ct: When she finds it, she calls her friends and neighbors together and says, 'Celebrate with me! I found the coin I lost!'

kjv: Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.
ct: In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents."

kjv: And he said, A certain man had two sons:
ct: Then Jesus said, "A man had 2 sons.

kjv: And the younger of them said to his father, Father, give me the portion of goods that falleth to me. And he divided unto them his living.
ct: The younger one said to his father, 'Father, give me my share of the inheritance now.' So the father divided his wealth between them.

kjv: And not many days after the younger son gathered all together, and took his journey into a far country, and there wasted his substance with riotous living.
ct: A few days later, the younger son packed up everything he had and left for a distant country, where he blew through all his money on wild living.

kjv: And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land; and he began to be in want.
ct: After he had spent everything, a severe famine hit that country, and he found himself with nothing.

kjv: And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.
ct: Desperate, he talked a local citizen into giving him a job, and the man sent him out to his fields to feed pigs.

kjv: And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat: and no man gave unto him.
ct: He was so hungry he would have gladly eaten the pods the pigs were eating, but nobody gave him a thing.

kjv: And when he came to himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger!
ct: Finally he came to his senses and said, 'My father's hired workers have more food than they can eat, and here I am starving to death!

kjv: I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and before thee,
ct: I'll get up and go back to my father and tell him, "Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.

kjv: And am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.
ct: I don't deserve to be called your son anymore. Just treat me like one of your hired workers."'

kjv: And he arose, and came to his father. But when he was yet a great way off, his father saw him, and had compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.
ct: So he got up and headed home to his father. But while he was still a long way off, his father spotted him. His heart broke with compassion, and he ran to his son, embraced him, and kissed him.

kjv: And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son.
ct: The son said to him, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I don't deserve to be called your son anymore.'

kjv: But the father said to his servants, Bring forth the best robe, and put it on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet:
ct: But the father turned to his servants and said, 'Quick! Bring out the finest robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.

kjv: And bring hither the fatted calf, and kill it; and let us eat, and be merry:
ct: Bring the fattened calf and slaughter it. Let's have a feast and celebrate!

kjv: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.
ct: Because this son of mine was dead, and now he's alive again. He was lost, and now he's found.' And the celebration began.

kjv: Now his elder son was in the field: and as he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard musick and dancing.
ct: Meanwhile, the older son was out working in the field. As he approached the house, he heard music and dancing.

kjv: And he called one of the servants, and asked what these things meant.
ct: He called over one of the servants and asked what was going on.

kjv: And he said unto him, Thy brother is come; and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe and sound.
ct: The servant told him, 'Your brother has come home, and your father slaughtered the fattened calf because he got him back safe and sound.'

kjv: And he was angry, and would not go in: therefore came his father out, and intreated him.
ct: The older son was furious and refused to go inside. So his father came out and pleaded with him.

kjv: And he answering said to his father, Lo, these many years do I serve thee, neither transgressed I at any time thy commandment: and yet thou never gavest me a kid, that I might make merry with my friends:
ct: But he fired back at his father, 'Look, I've slaved for you all these years and never once disobeyed you. Yet you never even gave me a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.

kjv: But as soon as this thy son was come, which hath devoured thy living with harlots, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.
ct: But the moment this son of yours shows up — the one who burned through your money on prostitutes — you slaughter the fattened calf for him!'

kjv: And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine.
ct: His father said to him, 'Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.

kjv: It was meet that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.
ct: But we had to celebrate and be glad, because this brother of yours was dead and is alive again. He was lost, and now he's found.'"

kjv: And he said also unto his disciples, There was a certain rich man, which had a steward; and the same was accused unto him that he had wasted his goods.
ct: Jesus also told his disciples this story: "A wealthy man had a manager who was accused of squandering his master's wealth.

kjv: And he called him, and said unto him, How is it that I hear this of thee? give an account of thy stewardship; for thou mayest be no longer steward.
ct: So the rich man called him in and said, 'What's this I'm hearing about you? Turn in your financial records, because you're fired.'

kjv: Then the steward said within himself, What shall I do? for my lord taketh away from me the stewardship: I cannot dig; to beg I am ashamed.
ct: "The manager thought to himself, 'What am I going to do now? My master is taking my job away. I'm not strong enough for manual labor, and I'm too proud to beg.

kjv: I am resolved what to do, that, when I am put out of the stewardship, they may receive me into their houses.
ct: I know what I'll do — I'll make sure people owe me favors, so that when I lose this job, they'll welcome me into their homes.'

kjv: So he called every one of his lord’s debtors unto him, and said unto the first, How much owest thou unto my lord?
ct: So he called in each person who owed his master money. He asked the first one, 'How much do you owe my master?'

kjv: And he said, An hundred measures of oil. And he said unto him, Take thy bill, and sit down quickly, and write fifty.
ct: The man answered, '100 measures of oil.' The manager told him, 'Here, take your bill, sit down right now, and change it to 50.'

kjv: Then said he to another, And how much owest thou? And he said, An hundred measures of wheat. And he said unto him, Take thy bill, and write fourscore.
ct: Then he asked another, 'And how much do you owe?' He said, '100 measures of wheat.' The manager told him, 'Take your bill and change it to 80.'
