kjv: And the lord commended the unjust steward, because he had done wisely: for the children of this world are in their generation wiser than the children of light.
ct: "The master actually praised the dishonest manager for being so shrewd. And here's the truth: people of this world are more clever in dealing with their own kind than the children of light are.

kjv: And I say unto you, Make to yourselves friends of the mammon of unrighteousness; that, when ye fail, they may receive you into everlasting habitations.
ct: So I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves, so that when it's gone, they'll welcome you into eternal dwellings.

kjv: He that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much: and he that is unjust in the least is unjust also in much.
ct: Whoever is faithful with very little will also be faithful with a lot. And whoever is dishonest with very little will also be dishonest with a lot.

kjv: If therefore ye have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?
ct: So if you haven't been faithful with worldly wealth, who is going to trust you with true riches?

kjv: And if ye have not been faithful in that which is another man’s, who shall give you that which is your own?
ct: And if you haven't been faithful with what belongs to someone else, who is going to give you something of your own?

kjv: No servant can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.
ct: No servant can serve two masters. Either he'll hate one and love the other, or he'll be devoted to one and look down on the other. You cannot serve both God and money."

kjv: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things: and they derided him.
ct: The Pharisees, who loved money, heard all of this and mocked him.

kjv: And he said unto them, Ye are they which justify yourselves before men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God.
ct: Jesus said to them, "You make yourselves look righteous in front of people, but God knows your hearts. What people value most highly is an abomination to God.

kjv: The law and the prophets were until John: since that time the kingdom of God is preached, and every man presseth into it.
ct: The Law and the prophets carried authority until John came. Since then, the good news of God's kingdom is being proclaimed, and everyone is pressing hard to get into it.

kjv: And it is easier for heaven and earth to pass, than one tittle of the law to fail.
ct: But it would be easier for heaven and earth to disappear than for the smallest detail of the Law to lose its force.

kjv: Whosoever putteth away his wife, and marrieth another, committeth adultery: and whosoever marrieth her that is put away from her husband committeth adultery.
ct: Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery, and anyone who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.

kjv: There was a certain rich man, which was clothed in purple and fine linen, and fared sumptuously every day:
ct: "There was a rich man who dressed in purple robes and the finest linen, living in luxury every single day.

kjv: And there was a certain beggar named Lazarus, which was laid at his gate, full of sores,
ct: At his gate lay a beggar named Lazarus, covered with sores.

kjv: And desiring to be fed with the crumbs which fell from the rich man’s table: moreover the dogs came and licked his sores.
ct: Lazarus longed to eat even the scraps that fell from the rich man's table. To make matters worse, dogs would come and lick his sores.

kjv: And it came to pass, that the beggar died, and was carried by the angels into Abraham’s bosom: the rich man also died, and was buried;
ct: Eventually the beggar died, and angels carried him to Abraham's side. The rich man also died and was buried.

kjv: And in hell he lift up his eyes, being in torments, and seeth Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.
ct: In Hades, where he was in torment, the rich man looked up and saw Abraham far away, with Lazarus right beside him.

kjv: And he cried and said, Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue; for I am tormented in this flame.
ct: He cried out, 'Father Abraham, have mercy on me! Send Lazarus to dip just the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because I am in agony in this fire.'

kjv: But Abraham said, Son, remember that thou in thy lifetime receivedst thy good things, and likewise Lazarus evil things: but now he is comforted, and thou art tormented.
ct: "But Abraham replied, 'Son, remember that during your lifetime you had everything you wanted, while Lazarus had nothing but suffering. Now he is comforted here, and you are in agony.

kjv: And beside all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed: so that they which would pass from hence to you cannot; neither can they pass to us, that would come from thence.
ct: Beyond all that, a massive chasm has been set between us and you. No one who wants to cross from here to you can do it, and no one can cross from there to us.'

kjv: Then he said, I pray thee therefore, father, that thou wouldest send him to my father’s house:
ct: "The rich man said, 'Then I beg you, father, send Lazarus to my father's house.

kjv: For I have five brethren; that he may testify unto them, lest they also come into this place of torment.
ct: I have 5 brothers. Let him warn them so they don't end up in this place of torment too.'

kjv: Abraham saith unto him, They have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.
ct: "But Abraham replied, 'They have Moses and the prophets. Let them listen to them.'

kjv: And he said, Nay, father Abraham: but if one went unto them from the dead, they will repent.
ct: "'No, father Abraham,' he said, 'but if someone goes to them from the dead, they will repent.'

kjv: And he said unto him, If they hear not Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded, though one rose from the dead.
ct: "Abraham told him, 'If they won't listen to Moses and the prophets, they won't be convinced even if someone rises from the dead.'"

kjv: Then said he unto the disciples, It is impossible but that offences will come: but woe unto him, through whom they come!
ct: Jesus said to his disciples, "It's inevitable that things will come along that cause people to sin, but how terrible it will be for the person who causes them!

kjv: It were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he cast into the sea, than that he should offend one of these little ones.
ct: It would be better for that person to have a millstone tied around their neck and be thrown into the sea than to cause one of these little ones to stumble.

kjv: Take heed to yourselves: If thy brother trespass against thee, rebuke him; and if he repent, forgive him.
ct: So watch yourselves. If a fellow believer sins against you, confront them about it. And if they repent, forgive them.

kjv: And if he trespass against thee seven times in a day, and seven times in a day turn again to thee, saying, I repent; thou shalt forgive him.
ct: Even if they sin against you 7 times in a single day, and each time they come back and say, 'I repent,' you must forgive them."

kjv: And the apostles said unto the Lord, Increase our faith.
ct: The apostles said to the Lord, "Increase our faith."

kjv: And the Lord said, If ye had faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye might say unto this sycamine tree, Be thou plucked up by the root, and be thou planted in the sea; and it should obey you.
ct: The Lord replied, "If you had faith even as small as a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, 'Uproot yourself and plant yourself in the sea,' and it would obey you.

kjv: But which of you, having a servant plowing or feeding cattle, will say unto him by and by, when he is come from the field, Go and sit down to meat?
ct: Suppose one of you has a servant who's been out plowing or tending sheep. When he comes in from the field, would you say, 'Come sit down right away and eat'?

kjv: And will not rather say unto him, Make ready wherewith I may sup, and gird thyself, and serve me, till I have eaten and drunken; and afterward thou shalt eat and drink?
ct: Of course not. You'd say, 'Get my dinner ready, then put on your apron and serve me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink.'

kjv: Doth he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded him? I trow not.
ct: Would you thank the servant for doing what he was told to do? I don't think so.

kjv: So likewise ye, when ye shall have done all those things which are commanded you, say, We are unprofitable servants: we have done that which was our duty to do.
ct: It's the same with you. When you've done everything you were commanded to do, say, 'We are unworthy servants. We've simply done our duty.'"

kjv: And it came to pass, as he went to Jerusalem, that he passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee.
ct: As Jesus was making his way to Jerusalem, he traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.

kjv: And as he entered into a certain village, there met him ten men that were lepers, which stood afar off:
ct: When he entered a certain village, 10 men with leprosy met him. They kept their distance,

kjv: And they lifted up their voices, and said, Jesus, Master, have mercy on us.
ct: but they called out loudly, "Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!"

kjv: And when he saw them, he said unto them, Go shew yourselves unto the priests. And it came to pass, that, as they went, they were cleansed.
ct: When he saw them, he said, "Go show yourselves to the priests." And as they were on their way, they were healed.

kjv: And one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, and with a loud voice glorified God,
ct: One of them, when he realized he'd been healed, came running back, praising God at the top of his voice.

kjv: And fell down on his face at his feet, giving him thanks: and he was a Samaritan.
ct: He threw himself facedown at Jesus' feet, thanking him over and over. And this man was a Samaritan.

kjv: And Jesus answering said, Were there not ten cleansed? but where are the nine?
ct: Jesus responded, "Weren't all 10 healed? Where are the other 9?

kjv: There are not found that returned to give glory to God, save this stranger.
ct: Was no one willing to come back and give glory to God except this foreigner?"

kjv: And he said unto him, Arise, go thy way: thy faith hath made thee whole.
ct: Then he told the man, "Stand up and go on your way. Your faith has healed you."

kjv: And when he was demanded of the Pharisees, when the kingdom of God should come, he answered them and said, The kingdom of God cometh not with observation:
ct: Some Pharisees asked Jesus when the kingdom of God would come. He answered, "The kingdom of God doesn't arrive with visible signs you can point to.

kjv: Neither shall they say, Lo here! or, lo there! for, behold, the kingdom of God is within you.
ct: No one will say, 'Look, it's here!' or 'It's over there!' because the kingdom of God is already among you."

kjv: And he said unto the disciples, The days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and ye shall not see it.
ct: Then he said to his disciples, "The time is coming when you'll long to see even one of the days of the Son of Man, but you won't see it.

kjv: And they shall say to you, See here; or, see there: go not after them, nor follow them.
ct: People will tell you, 'Look, he's here!' or 'He's over there!' Don't chase after them or follow them.

kjv: For as the lightning, that lighteneth out of the one part under heaven, shineth unto the other part under heaven; so shall also the Son of man be in his day.
ct: Because just as lightning flashes and lights up the entire sky from one horizon to the other, that's how it will be when the Son of Man comes.

kjv: But first must he suffer many things, and be rejected of this generation.
ct: But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by this generation.

kjv: And as it was in the days of Noe, so shall it be also in the days of the Son of man.
ct: When the Son of Man returns, it will be just like it was in the days of Noah.

kjv: They did eat, they drank, they married wives, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark, and the flood came, and destroyed them all.
ct: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up until the day Noah entered the ark. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.

kjv: Likewise also as it was in the days of Lot; they did eat, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they builded;
ct: It will be the same as it was in the days of Lot. People were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building—

kjv: But the same day that Lot went out of Sodom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven, and destroyed them all.
ct: but on the very day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.

kjv: Even thus shall it be in the day when the Son of man is revealed.
ct: That's exactly how it will be on the day the Son of Man is revealed.

kjv: In that day, he which shall be upon the housetop, and his stuff in the house, let him not come down to take it away: and he that is in the field, let him likewise not return back.
ct: On that day, if you're on the rooftop, don't go back inside to grab your belongings. If you're out in the field, don't turn back for anything.

kjv: Remember Lot’s wife.
ct: Remember Lot's wife!

kjv: Whosoever shall seek to save his life shall lose it; and whosoever shall lose his life shall preserve it.
ct: Whoever tries to hold on to their life will lose it, but whoever lets go of their life will preserve it.

kjv: I tell you, in that night there shall be two men in one bed; the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left.
ct: I tell you, on that night 2 people will be in one bed—one will be taken and the other left behind.

kjv: Two women shall be grinding together; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
ct: 2 women will be grinding grain together—one will be taken and the other left behind.

kjv: Two men shall be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
ct: 2 men will be working in a field—one will be taken and the other left behind."

kjv: And they answered and said unto him, Where, Lord? And he said unto them, Wheresoever the body is, thither will the eagles be gathered together.
ct: The disciples asked him, "Where will this happen, Lord?" He answered, "Where there's a dead body, the vultures will gather."

kjv: And he spake a parable unto them to this end, that men ought always to pray, and not to faint;
ct: Jesus told his disciples a parable to show them that they should always pray and never give up.

kjv: Saying, There was in a city a judge, which feared not God, neither regarded man:
ct: He said, "There was a judge in a certain city who didn't fear God and didn't care what anyone thought of him.

kjv: And there was a widow in that city; and she came unto him, saying, Avenge me of mine adversary.
ct: A widow in that city kept coming to him, pleading, 'Give me justice against the person who has wronged me!'

kjv: And he would not for a while: but afterward he said within himself, Though I fear not God, nor regard man;
ct: For a long time the judge refused. But eventually he said to himself, 'I don't fear God, and I don't care about people's opinions.

kjv: Yet because this widow troubleth me, I will avenge her, lest by her continual coming she weary me.
ct: But this widow keeps bothering me. I'm going to give her justice, or she'll wear me out with her constant visits.'"

kjv: And the Lord said, Hear what the unjust judge saith.
ct: Then the Lord said, "Pay attention to what this corrupt judge said.

kjv: And shall not God avenge his own elect, which cry day and night unto him, though he bear long with them?
ct: Won't God bring justice for his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he keep putting them off?

kjv: I tell you that he will avenge them speedily. Nevertheless when the Son of man cometh, shall he find faith on the earth?
ct: I tell you, he will bring them justice quickly. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?"

kjv: And he spake this parable unto certain which trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and despised others:
ct: Jesus also told this parable to some people who were confident in their own righteousness and looked down on everyone else.

kjv: Two men went up into the temple to pray; the one a Pharisee, and the other a publican.
ct: "Two men went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee, and the other was a tax collector.

kjv: The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himself, God, I thank thee, that I am not as other men are, extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even as this publican.
ct: The Pharisee stood by himself and prayed, 'God, I thank you that I'm not like other people — cheaters, sinners, adulterers — or even like this tax collector.

kjv: I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all that I possess.
ct: I fast twice a week, and I give a tenth of everything I earn.'

kjv: And the publican, standing afar off, would not lift up so much as his eyes unto heaven, but smote upon his breast, saying, God be merciful to me a sinner.
ct: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldn't even look up toward heaven. Instead, he beat his chest and said, 'God, have mercy on me, a sinner.'

kjv: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other: for every one that exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.
ct: I tell you, this man — not the Pharisee — went home right with God. Because everyone who lifts himself up will be brought low, and everyone who humbles himself will be lifted up."

kjv: And they brought unto him also infants, that he would touch them: but when his disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
ct: People were bringing their babies to Jesus so he could touch them. When the disciples saw this, they told the people to stop.

kjv: But Jesus called them unto him, and said, Suffer little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.
ct: But Jesus called the children over and said, "Let the little children come to me. Don't stop them, because the kingdom of God belongs to people like these.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child shall in no wise enter therein.
ct: I tell you the truth: anyone who doesn't receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it."

kjv: And a certain ruler asked him, saying, Good Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?
ct: One of the Jewish leaders asked him, "Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?"

kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? none is good, save one, that is, God.
ct: Jesus replied, "Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone.

kjv: Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honour thy father and thy mother.
ct: You know the commandments: 'Don't commit adultery,' 'Don't murder,' 'Don't steal,' 'Don't give false testimony,' 'Honor your father and mother.'"

kjv: And he said, All these have I kept from my youth up.
ct: The man said, "I've kept all of these since I was young."

kjv: Now when Jesus heard these things, he said unto him, Yet lackest thou one thing: sell all that thou hast, and distribute unto the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow me.
ct: When Jesus heard this, he told him, "There's still one thing you're missing. Sell everything you own and give the money to the poor. You'll have treasure in heaven. Then come and follow me."

kjv: And when he heard this, he was very sorrowful: for he was very rich.
ct: When the man heard this, he became deeply sad, because he was extremely wealthy.

kjv: And when Jesus saw that he was very sorrowful, he said, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!
ct: Jesus saw how sad he was and said, "How hard it is for rich people to enter the kingdom of God!

kjv: For it is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
ct: It's easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God."

kjv: And they that heard it said, Who then can be saved?
ct: The people who heard this asked, "Then who can possibly be saved?"

kjv: And he said, The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.
ct: Jesus answered, "What is impossible for people is possible with God."

kjv: Then Peter said, Lo, we have left all, and followed thee.
ct: Peter spoke up and said, "We've left everything behind to follow you."

kjv: And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children, for the kingdom of God’s sake,
ct: Jesus told them, "I tell you the truth: anyone who has left home, wife, brothers, parents, or children for the sake of the kingdom of God

kjv: Who shall not receive manifold more in this present time, and in the world to come life everlasting.
ct: will receive many times more in this life, and in the age to come, eternal life."

kjv: Then he took unto him the twelve, and said unto them, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, and all things that are written by the prophets concerning the Son of man shall be accomplished.
ct: Jesus pulled the 12 disciples aside and told them, "Listen — we're going up to Jerusalem, and everything the prophets wrote about the Son of Man is about to come true.

kjv: For he shall be delivered unto the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and spitefully entreated, and spitted on:
ct: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. They will mock him, insult him, and spit on him.

kjv: And they shall scourge him, and put him to death: and the third day he shall rise again.
ct: They will whip him and kill him. But on the 3rd day, he will rise again."

kjv: And they understood none of these things: and this saying was hid from them, neither knew they the things which were spoken.
ct: The disciples didn't understand any of this. The meaning was hidden from them, and they couldn't grasp what he was talking about.

kjv: And it came to pass, that as he was come nigh unto Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the way side begging:
ct: As Jesus approached Jericho, a blind man was sitting beside the road, begging.

kjv: And hearing the multitude pass by, he asked what it meant.
ct: When the blind man heard a crowd passing by, he asked what was going on.

kjv: And they told him, that Jesus of Nazareth passeth by.
ct: They told him, "Jesus of Nazareth is coming through."

kjv: And he cried, saying, Jesus, thou son of David, have mercy on me.
ct: He shouted, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!"

kjv: And they which went before rebuked him, that he should hold his peace: but he cried so much the more, Thou son of David, have mercy on me.
ct: The people at the front of the crowd told him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"

kjv: And Jesus stood, and commanded him to be brought unto him: and when he was come near, he asked him,
ct: Jesus stopped and ordered the man to be brought to him. When the man came close, Jesus asked him,

kjv: Saying, What wilt thou that I shall do unto thee? And he said, Lord, that I may receive my sight.
ct: "What do you want me to do for you?" He answered, "Lord, I want to see again."

kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Receive thy sight: thy faith hath saved thee.
ct: Jesus said to him, "Receive your sight. Your faith has healed you."

kjv: And immediately he received his sight, and followed him, glorifying God: and all the people, when they saw it, gave praise unto God.
ct: Instantly the man could see. He followed Jesus, praising God. And when all the people saw what happened, they praised God too.

kjv: And Jesus entered and passed through Jericho.
ct: Jesus entered Jericho and was making his way through the city.

kjv: And, behold, there was a man named Zacchaeus, which was the chief among the publicans, and he was rich.
ct: There was a man there named Zacchaeus. He was a chief tax collector and very wealthy.

kjv: And he sought to see Jesus who he was; and could not for the press, because he was little of stature.
ct: Zacchaeus was trying to get a look at Jesus, but he couldn't see over the crowd because he was short.

kjv: And he ran before, and climbed up into a sycomore tree to see him: for he was to pass that way.
ct: So he ran ahead and climbed a sycamore tree along the route where Jesus was about to pass.

kjv: And when Jesus came to the place, he looked up, and saw him, and said unto him, Zacchaeus, make haste, and come down; for to day I must abide at thy house.
ct: When Jesus reached that spot, he looked up and said, "Zacchaeus, come down quickly! I need to stay at your house today."

kjv: And he made haste, and came down, and received him joyfully.
ct: Zacchaeus scrambled down immediately and welcomed Jesus with great joy.

kjv: And when they saw it, they all murmured, saying, That he was gone to be guest with a man that is a sinner.
ct: Everyone who saw this began to grumble, saying, "He's gone to be the guest of a sinner."

kjv: And Zacchaeus stood, and said unto the Lord; Behold, Lord, the half of my goods I give to the poor; and if I have taken any thing from any man by false accusation, I restore him fourfold.
ct: But Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, "Lord, I'm giving half of everything I own to the poor. And if I've cheated anyone, I'll pay them back 4 times as much."

kjv: And Jesus said unto him, This day is salvation come to this house, forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham.
ct: Jesus said to him, "Today, salvation has come to this house, because this man too is a son of Abraham.

kjv: For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.
ct: The Son of Man came to seek and to save those who are lost."

kjv: And as they heard these things, he added and spake a parable, because he was nigh to Jerusalem, and because they thought that the kingdom of God should immediately appear.
ct: While the people were listening, Jesus told them a parable, because he was near Jerusalem and they expected God's Kingdom to appear at any moment.

kjv: He said therefore, A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.
ct: He said, "A nobleman traveled to a distant country to be appointed king and then return.

kjv: And he called his ten servants, and delivered them ten pounds, and said unto them, Occupy till I come.
ct: Before leaving, he called 10 of his servants, gave each of them one mina, and told them, 'Put this money to work until I come back.'

kjv: But his citizens hated him, and sent a message after him, saying, We will not have this man to reign over us.
ct: But his own people hated him and sent a delegation after him with this message: 'We don't want this man ruling over us.'

kjv: And it came to pass, that when he was returned, having received the kingdom, then he commanded these servants to be called unto him, to whom he had given the money, that he might know how much every man had gained by trading.
ct: "After he was appointed king and returned home, he summoned the servants he had given the money to, so he could find out what each one had earned.

kjv: Then came the first, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained ten pounds.
ct: The first servant came and said, 'Lord, your one mina has earned 10 more minas.'

kjv: And he said unto him, Well, thou good servant: because thou hast been faithful in a very little, have thou authority over ten cities.
ct: The king replied, 'Well done, good servant! Because you were faithful with something so small, you will now govern 10 cities.'

kjv: And the second came, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained five pounds.
ct: The second servant came and said, 'Lord, your mina has earned 5 more minas.'

kjv: And he said likewise to him, Be thou also over five cities.
ct: The king told him, 'You will govern 5 cities.'

kjv: And another came, saying, Lord, behold, here is thy pound, which I have kept laid up in a napkin:
ct: Then another servant came and said, 'Lord, here is your mina. I kept it wrapped up in a cloth.

kjv: For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that thou layedst not down, and reapest that thou didst not sow.
ct: I was afraid of you because you're a harsh man. You collect what you didn't deposit and harvest what you didn't plant.'

kjv: And he saith unto him, Out of thine own mouth will I judge thee, thou wicked servant. Thou knewest that I was an austere man, taking up that I laid not down, and reaping that I did not sow:
ct: The king said, 'I'll judge you by your own words, you wicked servant! So you knew I was a harsh man, collecting what I didn't deposit and harvesting what I didn't plant?

kjv: Wherefore then gavest not thou my money into the bank, that at my coming I might have required mine own with usury?
ct: Then why didn't you at least put my money in the bank? That way I could have collected it with interest when I returned.'

kjv: And he said unto them that stood by, Take from him the pound, and give it to him that hath ten pounds.
ct: Then he said to those standing nearby, 'Take the mina from him and give it to the one who has 10 minas.'

kjv: (And they said unto him, Lord, he hath ten pounds.)
ct: They said, 'Lord, he already has 10 minas!'

kjv: For I say unto you, That unto every one which hath shall be given; and from him that hath not, even that he hath shall be taken away from him.
ct: 'I tell you, everyone who has will be given more. But the one who has nothing — even what he has will be taken away.

kjv: But those mine enemies, which would not that I should reign over them, bring hither, and slay them before me.
ct: As for those enemies of mine who didn't want me to reign over them — bring them here and execute them in front of me.'"

kjv: And when he had thus spoken, he went before, ascending up to Jerusalem.
ct: After saying this, Jesus continued on ahead, heading up toward Jerusalem.

kjv: And it came to pass, when he was come nigh to Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount called the mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,
ct: As he approached Bethphage and Bethany near the Mount of Olives, he sent 2 of his disciples ahead.

kjv: Saying, Go ye into the village over against you; in the which at your entering ye shall find a colt tied, whereon yet never man sat: loose him, and bring him hither.
ct: He told them, "Go into the village just ahead. As you enter it, you'll find a young donkey tied up that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.

kjv: And if any man ask you, Why do ye loose him? thus shall ye say unto him, Because the Lord hath need of him.
ct: If anyone asks you, 'Why are you untying it?' just say, 'The Lord needs it.'"

kjv: And they that were sent went their way, and found even as he had said unto them.
ct: The 2 disciples went and found everything exactly as Jesus had told them.

kjv: And as they were loosing the colt, the owners thereof said unto them, Why loose ye the colt?
ct: As they were untying the colt, its owners asked them, "Why are you untying the colt?"

kjv: And they said, The Lord hath need of him.
ct: They answered, "The Lord needs it."

kjv: And they brought him to Jesus: and they cast their garments upon the colt, and they set Jesus thereon.
ct: They brought the colt to Jesus, threw their cloaks over it, and helped Jesus onto it.

kjv: And as he went, they spread their clothes in the way.
ct: As he rode along, people spread their cloaks on the road in front of him.

kjv: And when he was come nigh, even now at the descent of the mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works that they had seen;
ct: As he reached the downward slope of the Mount of Olives, the entire crowd of disciples burst into joyful praise, thanking God loudly for all the miracles they had witnessed.

kjv: Saying, Blessed be the King that cometh in the name of the Lord: peace in heaven, and glory in the highest.
ct: They shouted, "Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory in the highest!"

kjv: And some of the Pharisees from among the multitude said unto him, Master, rebuke thy disciples.
ct: Some Pharisees in the crowd called out to him, "Teacher, tell your disciples to stop!"

kjv: And he answered and said unto them, I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out.
ct: Jesus replied, "I tell you, if they kept silent, the very stones would cry out."

kjv: And when he was come near, he beheld the city, and wept over it,
ct: As he came closer and saw the city of Jerusalem, he wept over it.

kjv: Saying, If thou hadst known, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things which belong unto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes.
ct: He said, "If only you had recognized today what would bring you peace! But now it is hidden from your eyes.

kjv: For the days shall come upon thee, that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee, and compass thee round, and keep thee in on every side,
ct: The days are coming when your enemies will build siege walls around you, surround you, and close in on you from every side.

kjv: And shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.
ct: They will crush you to the ground — you and your children within your walls. They won't leave one stone standing on another, because you did not recognize the time when God came to save you."

kjv: And he went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold therein, and them that bought;
ct: Then Jesus entered the temple and began driving out those who were buying and selling there.

kjv: Saying unto them, It is written, My house is the house of prayer: but ye have made it a den of thieves.
ct: He told them, "Scripture says, 'My house will be a house of prayer,' but you have turned it into a den of robbers!"

kjv: And he taught daily in the temple. But the chief priests and the scribes and the chief of the people sought to destroy him,
ct: Every day he taught in the temple. Meanwhile, the chief priests, the scribes, and the leading citizens were looking for a way to kill him.

kjv: And could not find what they might do: for all the people were very attentive to hear him.
ct: But they couldn't figure out how to do it, because all the people hung on every word he said.

kjv: And it came to pass, that on one of those days, as he taught the people in the temple, and preached the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes came upon him with the elders,
ct: One day, while Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, scribes, and elders approached him.

kjv: And spake unto him, saying, Tell us, by what authority doest thou these things? or who is he that gave thee this authority?
ct: They demanded, "Tell us — by what authority are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority?"

kjv: And he answered and said unto them, I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:
ct: Jesus replied, "Let me ask you a question first. Answer me this:

kjv: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?
ct: Was John's baptism from heaven, or was it merely a human invention?"

kjv: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then believed ye him not?
ct: They debated among themselves, saying, "If we say 'from heaven,' he'll ask, 'Then why didn't you believe him?'

kjv: But and if we say, Of men; all the people will stone us: for they be persuaded that John was a prophet.
ct: But if we say 'from humans,' the whole crowd will stone us, because they're convinced John was a prophet."

kjv: And they answered, that they could not tell whence it was.
ct: So they answered that they didn't know where it came from.

kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
ct: Jesus said to them, "Then I won't tell you by what authority I do these things either."

kjv: Then began he to speak to the people this parable; A certain man planted a vineyard, and let it forth to husbandmen, and went into a far country for a long time.
ct: He then told the people this parable: "A man planted a vineyard, rented it out to some tenant farmers, and left for a distant country for a long time.

kjv: And at the season he sent a servant to the husbandmen, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard: but the husbandmen beat him, and sent him away empty.
ct: When harvest season came, he sent a servant to the farmers to collect his share of the vineyard's produce. But the farmers beat the servant and sent him away with nothing.

kjv: And again he sent another servant: and they beat him also, and entreated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.
ct: So the owner sent another servant. They beat him too, humiliated him, and sent him away with nothing.

kjv: And again he sent a third: and they wounded him also, and cast him out.
ct: He sent a third servant, and they wounded him and threw him out.

kjv: Then said the lord of the vineyard, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: it may be they will reverence him when they see him.
ct: Then the owner of the vineyard said, 'What should I do? I'll send my beloved son. Surely they'll respect him.'

kjv: But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir: come, let us kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.
ct: But when the farmers saw the son coming, they schemed together: 'This is the heir! Let's kill him so the inheritance will be ours.'

kjv: So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do unto them?
ct: So they dragged him out of the vineyard and killed him. Now, what will the owner of the vineyard do to them?

kjv: He shall come and destroy these husbandmen, and shall give the vineyard to others. And when they heard it, they said, God forbid.
ct: He will come and destroy those farmers and give the vineyard to others." When the people heard this, they said, "May that never happen!"

kjv: And he beheld them, and said, What is this then that is written, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner?
ct: But Jesus looked straight at them and said, "Then what does this Scripture mean: 'The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone'?

kjv: Whosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.
ct: Everyone who stumbles over that stone will be shattered, and anyone it falls on will be ground to dust."

kjv: And the chief priests and the scribes the same hour sought to lay hands on him; and they feared the people: for they perceived that he had spoken this parable against them.
ct: The chief priests and scribes wanted to arrest him right then and there, but they were afraid of the people — because they knew he had aimed this parable directly at them.

kjv: And they watched him, and sent forth spies, which should feign themselves just men, that they might take hold of his words, that so they might deliver him unto the power and authority of the governor.
ct: So they kept a close watch on him and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. Their goal was to catch him saying something they could use to hand him over to the governor's authority.

kjv: And they asked him, saying, Master, we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly, neither acceptest thou the person of any, but teachest the way of God truly:
ct: These spies said, "Teacher, we know you speak and teach what is right. You don't show favoritism but truly teach God's way.

kjv: Is it lawful for us to give tribute unto Caesar, or no?
ct: So tell us — is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?"

kjv: But he perceived their craftiness, and said unto them, Why tempt ye me?
ct: But Jesus saw right through their scheme and said, "Why are you trying to trap me?

kjv: Shew me a penny. Whose image and superscription hath it? They answered and said, Caesar’s.
ct: Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?" They answered, "Caesar's."

kjv: And he said unto them, Render therefore unto Caesar the things which be Caesar’s, and unto God the things which be God’s.
ct: He told them, "Then give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar, and give to God what belongs to God."

kjv: And they could not take hold of his words before the people: and they marvelled at his answer, and held their peace.
ct: They couldn't trap him with his words in front of the people. Stunned by his answer, they fell silent.

kjv: Then came to him certain of the Sadducees, which deny that there is any resurrection; and they asked him,
ct: Then some Sadducees — who deny that there is any resurrection — came to him with a question.

kjv: Saying, Master, Moses wrote unto us, If any man’s brother die, having a wife, and he die without children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
ct: They said, "Teacher, Moses wrote that if a man dies leaving a wife but no children, his brother must marry the widow and have children to carry on the dead man's family line.

kjv: There were therefore seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and died without children.
ct: Now, there were 7 brothers. The first married a woman and died without children.

kjv: And the second took her to wife, and he died childless.
ct: The second brother married her, and he also died without children.

kjv: And the third took her; and in like manner the seven also: and they left no children, and died.
ct: The third married her, and so on through all 7 — none of them had children, and all of them died.

kjv: Last of all the woman died also.
ct: Finally, the woman died too.

kjv: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them is she? for seven had her to wife.
ct: So in the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All 7 brothers were married to her."

kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, The children of this world marry, and are given in marriage:
ct: Jesus answered, "People in this present age marry and are given in marriage.

kjv: But they which shall be accounted worthy to obtain that world, and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage:
ct: But those who are counted worthy to reach the age to come and the resurrection from the dead will neither marry nor be given in marriage.

kjv: Neither can they die any more: for they are equal unto the angels; and are the children of God, being the children of the resurrection.
ct: They can never die again — they are like the angels. They are children of God, because they are children of the resurrection.

kjv: Now that the dead are raised, even Moses shewed at the bush, when he calleth the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
ct: Even Moses proved that the dead are raised. At the burning bush, he called the Lord 'the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.'

kjv: For he is not a God of the dead, but of the living: for all live unto him.
ct: He is not the God of the dead but of the living, because to him everyone is alive."

kjv: Then certain of the scribes answering said, Master, thou hast well said.
ct: Some of the scribes responded, "Teacher, that was an excellent answer."

kjv: And after that they durst not ask him any question at all.
ct: After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.

kjv: And he said unto them, How say they that Christ is David’s son?
ct: Then Jesus asked them, "Why do people say that the Christ is David's son?

kjv: And David himself saith in the book of Psalms, The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
ct: David himself says in the book of Psalms: 'The LORD said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand,

kjv: Till I make thine enemies thy footstool.
ct: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet."'

kjv: David therefore calleth him Lord, how is he then his son?
ct: David calls him 'Lord' — so how can he be David's son?"

kjv: Then in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples,
ct: While all the people were listening, Jesus said to his disciples,

kjv: Beware of the scribes, which desire to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the markets, and the highest seats in the synagogues, and the chief rooms at feasts;
ct: "Watch out for the scribes. They love to walk around in flowing robes, soak up greetings in the marketplaces, claim the best seats in the synagogues, and take the places of honor at banquets.

kjv: Which devour widows’ houses, and for a shew make long prayers: the same shall receive greater damnation.
ct: They cheat widows out of their homes and then put on a show with long prayers. They will be punished most severely."

kjv: And he looked up, and saw the rich men casting their gifts into the treasury.
ct: Jesus looked up and watched the wealthy dropping their gifts into the temple treasury.

kjv: And he saw also a certain poor widow casting in thither two mites.
ct: Then he noticed a poor widow put in 2 small copper coins.

kjv: And he said, Of a truth I say unto you, that this poor widow hath cast in more than they all:
ct: He said, "I'm telling you the truth — this poor widow has given more than all of them.

kjv: For all these have of their abundance cast in unto the offerings of God: but she of her penury hath cast in all the living that she had.
ct: Everyone else gave out of their surplus, but she gave out of her poverty — every last penny she had to live on."

kjv: And as some spake of the temple, how it was adorned with goodly stones and gifts, he said,
ct: Some of his followers were admiring the temple, talking about its beautiful stonework and the gifts that decorated it. Jesus said,

kjv: As for these things which ye behold, the days will come, in the which there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.
ct: "These things you're looking at? The day is coming when not a single stone will be left standing on another. Every one of them will be torn down."

kjv: And they asked him, saying, Master, but when shall these things be? and what sign will there be when these things shall come to pass?
ct: They asked him, "Teacher, when will this happen? What sign should we look for to know it's about to take place?"

kjv: And he said, Take heed that ye be not deceived: for many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and the time draweth near: go ye not therefore after them.
ct: He said, "Be careful not to be deceived. Many will come using my name, claiming, 'I am Christ,' and saying, 'The time has come!' Don't follow them.

kjv: But when ye shall hear of wars and commotions, be not terrified: for these things must first come to pass; but the end is not by and by.
ct: When you hear about wars and uprisings, don't panic. These things have to happen first, but the end won't come right away."

kjv: Then said he unto them, Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom:
ct: Then he told them, "Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.

kjv: And great earthquakes shall be in divers places, and famines, and pestilences; and fearful sights and great signs shall there be from heaven.
ct: There will be massive earthquakes, famines, and plagues in many places. Terrifying events and powerful signs will appear in the sky.

kjv: But before all these, they shall lay their hands on you, and persecute you, delivering you up to the synagogues, and into prisons, being brought before kings and rulers for my name’s sake.
ct: But before any of that happens, people will seize you and persecute you. They'll drag you into synagogues and throw you in prison. You'll be brought before kings and governors because of my name.

kjv: And it shall turn to you for a testimony.
ct: This will give you the chance to share your testimony.

kjv: Settle it therefore in your hearts, not to meditate before what ye shall answer:
ct: So make up your minds right now not to rehearse your defense ahead of time,

kjv: For I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to gainsay nor resist.
ct: because I will give you the words and the wisdom that none of your enemies will be able to argue against or refute.

kjv: And ye shall be betrayed both by parents, and brethren, and kinsfolks, and friends; and some of you shall they cause to be put to death.
ct: You'll be betrayed by your own parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. Some of you will even be put to death.

kjv: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake.
ct: Everyone will hate you because of my name.

kjv: But there shall not an hair of your head perish.
ct: But not a single hair on your head will be lost forever.

kjv: In your patience possess ye your souls.
ct: Stand firm, and you will win your souls.

kjv: And when ye shall see Jerusalem compassed with armies, then know that the desolation thereof is nigh.
ct: "When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, you'll know its destruction is near.

kjv: Then let them which are in Judaea flee to the mountains; and let them which are in the midst of it depart out; and let not them that are in the countries enter thereinto.
ct: At that point, anyone in Judea should flee to the mountains. Anyone inside the city should get out. Anyone in the countryside should not go into the city.

kjv: For these be the days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.
ct: Those will be days of vengeance, when everything written in Scripture will be fulfilled.

kjv: But woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck, in those days! for there shall be great distress in the land, and wrath upon this people.
ct: How terrible it will be for pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days! There will be overwhelming suffering across the land and fierce judgment on this people.

kjv: And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be led away captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.
ct: They will be killed by the sword and taken as prisoners to every nation. Jerusalem will be trampled by the Gentiles until the era of the Gentiles comes to an end.

kjv: And there shall be signs in the sun, and in the moon, and in the stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, with perplexity; the sea and the waves roaring;
ct: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On earth, nations will be gripped with panic and confusion as the seas rage and roar.

kjv: Men’s hearts failing them for fear, and for looking after those things which are coming on the earth: for the powers of heaven shall be shaken.
ct: People will faint from terror, dreading what is coming upon the world, because the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

kjv: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
ct: Then everyone will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.

kjv: And when these things begin to come to pass, then look up, and lift up your heads; for your redemption draweth nigh.
ct: When these things start to happen, stand up and lift your heads high, because your redemption is drawing near."

kjv: And he spake to them a parable; Behold the fig tree, and all the trees;
ct: Then he gave them an illustration: "Look at the fig tree — or any tree, for that matter.

kjv: When they now shoot forth, ye see and know of your own selves that summer is now nigh at hand.
ct: When you see the leaves starting to bud, you know for yourselves that summer is almost here.

kjv: So likewise ye, when ye see these things come to pass, know ye that the kingdom of God is nigh at hand.
ct: In the same way, when you see all these things happening, you'll know that the kingdom of God is near.

kjv: Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass away, till all be fulfilled.
ct: I tell you the truth: this generation will not pass away until all of this has taken place.

kjv: Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.
ct: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.

kjv: And take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life, and so that day come upon you unawares.
ct: "Watch yourselves. Don't let your hearts get weighed down with wild living, drunkenness, and the worries of everyday life, or that day will catch you completely off guard.

kjv: For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.
ct: It will spring like a trap on everyone living on the face of the earth.

kjv: Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man.
ct: Stay alert at all times, and pray that you'll have the strength to escape everything that's coming and to stand before the Son of Man."

kjv: And in the day time he was teaching in the temple; and at night he went out, and abode in the mount that is called the mount of Olives.
ct: During the day, Jesus taught in the temple. Each evening, he left the city and spent the night on the Mount of Olives.

kjv: And all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple, for to hear him.
ct: Every morning, all the people came to the temple early to hear him.

kjv: Now the feast of unleavened bread drew nigh, which is called the Passover.
ct: The Festival of Unleavened Bread — also known as Passover — was getting close.

kjv: And the chief priests and scribes sought how they might kill him; for they feared the people.
ct: The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a way to kill Jesus, but they were afraid of how the people might react.

kjv: Then entered Satan into Judas surnamed Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve.
ct: Then Satan entered into Judas, the one called Iscariot, who was one of the 12 disciples.

kjv: And he went his way, and communed with the chief priests and captains, how he might betray him unto them.
ct: Judas went and met with the chief priests and the temple guards to discuss how he could hand Jesus over to them.

kjv: And they were glad, and covenanted to give him money.
ct: They were thrilled and agreed to pay him money for it.

kjv: And he promised, and sought opportunity to betray him unto them in the absence of the multitude.
ct: Judas accepted the deal and began watching for the right moment to betray Jesus when no crowd was around.

kjv: Then came the day of unleavened bread, when the passover must be killed.
ct: Then the day of Unleavened Bread arrived — the day when the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.

kjv: And he sent Peter and John, saying, Go and prepare us the passover, that we may eat.
ct: Jesus sent Peter and John ahead, saying, "Go and prepare the Passover meal for us so we can eat together."

kjv: And they said unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare?
ct: They asked him, "Where do you want us to prepare it?"

kjv: And he said unto them, Behold, when ye are entered into the city, there shall a man meet you, bearing a pitcher of water; follow him into the house where he entereth in.
ct: He told them, "When you enter the city, a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him into the house he enters.

kjv: And ye shall say unto the goodman of the house, The Master saith unto thee, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
ct: Tell the owner of the house, 'The Teacher asks you: Where is the guest room where I can eat the Passover with my disciples?'

kjv: And he shall shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.
ct: He'll show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Prepare the meal there."

kjv: And they went, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.
ct: They went and found everything exactly as Jesus had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.

kjv: And when the hour was come, he sat down, and the twelve apostles with him.
ct: When the time came, Jesus sat down at the table with his 12 apostles.

kjv: And he said unto them, With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer:
ct: He said to them, "I have deeply longed to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.

kjv: For I say unto you, I will not any more eat thereof, until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God.
ct: I'm telling you, I won't eat it again until it finds its fulfillment in the kingdom of God."

kjv: And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and said, Take this, and divide it among yourselves:
ct: Then he took a cup, gave thanks to God, and said, "Take this and share it among yourselves.

kjv: For I say unto you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come.
ct: I'm telling you, I won't drink from the fruit of the vine again until the kingdom of God comes."

kjv: And he took bread, and gave thanks, and brake it, and gave unto them, saying, This is my body which is given for you: this do in remembrance of me.
ct: Then he took bread, gave thanks, broke it, and handed it to them, saying, "This is my body, given for you. Do this to remember me."

kjv: Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood, which is shed for you.
ct: In the same way, after supper he took the cup and said, "This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.

kjv: But, behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table.
ct: But look — the hand of the one who will betray me is right here at this table with me.

kjv: And truly the Son of man goeth, as it was determined: but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed!
ct: The Son of Man will go down the path that has been determined for him, but how terrible it will be for the man who betrays him!"

kjv: And they began to enquire among themselves, which of them it was that should do this thing.
ct: The disciples began asking each other which one of them could possibly do such a thing.

kjv: And there was also a strife among them, which of them should be accounted the greatest.
ct: An argument also broke out among them about which of them should be considered the greatest.

kjv: And he said unto them, The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and they that exercise authority upon them are called benefactors.
ct: Jesus said to them, "The kings of the nations lord it over their people, and those in authority over them call themselves 'benefactors.'

kjv: But ye shall not be so: but he that is greatest among you, let him be as the younger; and he that is chief, as he that doth serve.
ct: But that's not how it should be with you. Instead, the greatest among you should act like the youngest, and the leader should act like a servant.

kjv: For whether is greater, he that sitteth at meat, or he that serveth? is not he that sitteth at meat? but I am among you as he that serveth.
ct: Think about it — who's greater, the one sitting at the table or the one serving the food? Obviously the one at the table. But I am among you as one who serves.

kjv: Ye are they which have continued with me in my temptations.
ct: You are the ones who have stood by me through my trials.

kjv: And I appoint unto you a kingdom, as my Father hath appointed unto me;
ct: So I grant you a kingdom, just as my Father granted one to me,

kjv: That ye may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
ct: so that you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom. You will sit on thrones, judging the 12 tribes of Israel."

kjv: And the Lord said, Simon, Simon, behold, Satan hath desired to have you, that he may sift you as wheat:
ct: The Lord said, "Simon, Simon, listen — Satan has demanded permission to sift all of you like wheat.

kjv: But I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not: and when thou art converted, strengthen thy brethren.
ct: But I have prayed for you, that your faith won't fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers."

kjv: And he said unto him, Lord, I am ready to go with thee, both into prison, and to death.
ct: Peter said to him, "Lord, I'm ready to go with you to prison and even to death!"

kjv: And he said, I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow this day, before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me.
ct: Jesus replied, "I'm telling you, Peter — the rooster won't crow today before you have denied 3 times that you even know me."

kjv: And he said unto them, When I sent you without purse, and scrip, and shoes, lacked ye any thing? And they said, Nothing.
ct: Then he asked them, "When I sent you out without a purse, a bag, or sandals, did you lack anything?" They answered, "Nothing."

kjv: Then said he unto them, But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one.
ct: He said, "But now, if you have a purse, take it — and a bag too. If you don't have a sword, sell your cloak and buy one.

kjv: For I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the transgressors: for the things concerning me have an end.
ct: I'm telling you, what was written about me must be fulfilled: 'He was counted among the criminals.' Everything written about me is reaching its conclusion."

kjv: And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords. And he said unto them, It is enough.
ct: They said, "Lord, look — here are 2 swords." He replied, "That's enough."

kjv: And he came out, and went, as he was wont, to the mount of Olives; and his disciples also followed him.
ct: Jesus left and went to the Mount of Olives, as he usually did. His disciples followed him.

kjv: And when he was at the place, he said unto them, Pray that ye enter not into temptation.
ct: When he reached the place, he told them, "Pray that you won't fall into temptation."

kjv: And he was withdrawn from them about a stone’s cast, and kneeled down, and prayed,
ct: Then he pulled away from them about a stone's throw, knelt down, and prayed.

kjv: Saying, Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup from me: nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.
ct: "Father, if you are willing, take this cup away from me. But not my will — yours be done."

kjv: And there appeared an angel unto him from heaven, strengthening him.
ct: An angel from heaven appeared to him and strengthened him.

kjv: And being in an agony he prayed more earnestly: and his sweat was as it were great drops of blood falling down to the ground.
ct: In deep agony, he prayed even more intensely, and his sweat fell to the ground like great drops of blood.

kjv: And when he rose up from prayer, and was come to his disciples, he found them sleeping for sorrow,
ct: When he got up from praying and went back to his disciples, he found them asleep, exhausted from grief.

kjv: And said unto them, Why sleep ye? rise and pray, lest ye enter into temptation.
ct: He said to them, "Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you won't fall into temptation."

kjv: And while he yet spake, behold a multitude, and he that was called Judas, one of the twelve, went before them, and drew near unto Jesus to kiss him.
ct: While he was still speaking, a crowd appeared. Leading them was Judas, one of the 12, and he walked up to Jesus to kiss him.

kjv: But Jesus said unto him, Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?
ct: But Jesus said to him, "Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?"

kjv: When they which were about him saw what would follow, they said unto him, Lord, shall we smite with the sword?
ct: When the disciples around him saw what was about to happen, they asked, "Lord, should we fight with our swords?"

kjv: And one of them smote the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.
ct: One of them struck the high priest's servant and cut off his right ear.

kjv: And Jesus answered and said, Suffer ye thus far. And he touched his ear, and healed him.
ct: But Jesus said, "Stop — no more of this!" Then he touched the man's ear and healed him.

kjv: Then Jesus said unto the chief priests, and captains of the temple, and the elders, which were come to him, Be ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and staves?
ct: Jesus said to the chief priests, temple guards, and elders who had come for him, "Have you come out with swords and clubs as if I were a criminal?

kjv: When I was daily with you in the temple, ye stretched forth no hands against me: but this is your hour, and the power of darkness.
ct: Every day I was with you in the temple and you never laid a hand on me. But this is your hour — the hour when darkness reigns."

kjv: Then took they him, and led him, and brought him into the high priest’s house. And Peter followed afar off.
ct: They seized Jesus and led him away to the high priest's house. Peter followed at a distance.

kjv: And when they had kindled a fire in the midst of the hall, and were set down together, Peter sat down among them.
ct: They lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together. Peter sat down among them.

kjv: But a certain maid beheld him as he sat by the fire, and earnestly looked upon him, and said, This man was also with him.
ct: A servant girl noticed him sitting by the firelight. She stared at him closely and said, "This man was with him too."

kjv: And he denied him, saying, Woman, I know him not.
ct: But Peter denied it, saying, "Woman, I don't know him."

kjv: And after a little while another saw him, and said, Thou art also of them. And Peter said, Man, I am not.
ct: A little later, someone else saw him and said, "You're one of them too!" Peter replied, "Man, I am not!"

kjv: And about the space of one hour after another confidently affirmed, saying, Of a truth this fellow also was with him: for he is a Galilaean.
ct: About an hour later, another person insisted, "There's no doubt — this man was with him. He's a Galilean!"

kjv: And Peter said, Man, I know not what thou sayest. And immediately, while he yet spake, the cock crew.
ct: Peter said, "Man, I don't know what you're talking about!" Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.

kjv: And the Lord turned, and looked upon Peter. And Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.
ct: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter. Then Peter remembered what the Lord had said to him: "Before the rooster crows today, you will deny me 3 times."

kjv: And Peter went out, and wept bitterly.
ct: Peter went outside and wept bitterly.

kjv: And the men that held Jesus mocked him, and smote him.
ct: The men guarding Jesus mocked him and beat him.

kjv: And when they had blindfolded him, they struck him on the face, and asked him, saying, Prophesy, who is it that smote thee?
ct: They blindfolded him, hit him in the face, and taunted him: "Prophesy! Who hit you?"

kjv: And many other things blasphemously spake they against him.
ct: They hurled all kinds of insults at him.

kjv: And as soon as it was day, the elders of the people and the chief priests and the scribes came together, and led him into their council, saying,
ct: At daybreak, the council of elders — both chief priests and scribes — assembled and had Jesus brought before them.

kjv: Art thou the Christ? tell us. And he said unto them, If I tell you, ye will not believe:
ct: They demanded, "If you are the Christ, tell us." He answered, "If I tell you, you won't believe me.

kjv: And if I also ask you, ye will not answer me, nor let me go.
ct: And if I ask you a question, you won't answer me or let me go.

kjv: Hereafter shall the Son of man sit on the right hand of the power of God.
ct: But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God."

kjv: Then said they all, Art thou then the Son of God? And he said unto them, Ye say that I am.
ct: They all asked, "So you are the Son of God?" He replied, "You say that I am."

kjv: And they said, What need we any further witness? for we ourselves have heard of his own mouth.
ct: They said, "Why do we need any more testimony? We've heard it straight from his own mouth!"

kjv: And the whole multitude of them arose, and led him unto Pilate.
ct: Then the entire assembly rose and brought Jesus before Pilate.

kjv: And they began to accuse him, saying, We found this fellow perverting the nation, and forbidding to give tribute to Caesar, saying that he himself is Christ a King.
ct: They began accusing him, saying, "We caught this man misleading our nation, telling people not to pay taxes to Caesar, and claiming to be Christ, a king."

kjv: And Pilate asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answered him and said, Thou sayest it.
ct: Pilate asked him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" Jesus answered, "You said it yourself."

kjv: Then said Pilate to the chief priests and to the people, I find no fault in this man.
ct: Pilate turned to the chief priests and the crowd and said, "I find nothing wrong with this man."

kjv: And they were the more fierce, saying, He stirreth up the people, teaching throughout all Jewry, beginning from Galilee to this place.
ct: But they pressed harder, insisting, "He stirs up trouble among the people with his teaching all across Judea — starting in Galilee and now here!"

kjv: When Pilate heard of Galilee, he asked whether the man were a Galilaean.
ct: When Pilate heard the word "Galilee," he asked whether the man was a Galilean.

kjv: And as soon as he knew that he belonged unto Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who himself also was at Jerusalem at that time.
ct: Once he learned that Jesus fell under Herod's jurisdiction, he sent him over to Herod, who happened to be in Jerusalem at the time.

kjv: And when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceeding glad: for he was desirous to see him of a long season, because he had heard many things of him; and he hoped to have seen some miracle done by him.
ct: Herod was thrilled to see Jesus. He had wanted to meet him for a long time because he'd heard so much about him, and he was hoping to watch him perform some kind of miracle.

kjv: Then he questioned with him in many words; but he answered him nothing.
ct: Herod bombarded him with question after question, but Jesus didn't answer a single one.

kjv: And the chief priests and scribes stood and vehemently accused him.
ct: Meanwhile, the chief priests and the scribes stood nearby, hurling fierce accusations at him.

kjv: And Herod with his men of war set him at nought, and mocked him, and arrayed him in a gorgeous robe, and sent him again to Pilate.
ct: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt and mocked him. They dressed him in an elegant robe and sent him back to Pilate.

kjv: And the same day Pilate and Herod were made friends together: for before they were at enmity between themselves.
ct: That very day, Herod and Pilate became friends — before this, they had been enemies.

kjv: And Pilate, when he had called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people,
ct: Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.

kjv: Said unto them, Ye have brought this man unto me, as one that perverteth the people: and, behold, I, having examined him before you, have found no fault in this man touching those things whereof ye accuse him:
ct: He told them, "You brought this man to me, claiming he was leading the people astray. I've examined him right here in front of you, and I find no basis for any of the charges you're bringing against him."

kjv: No, nor yet Herod: for I sent you to him; and, lo, nothing worthy of death is done unto him.
ct: "Neither did Herod, because he sent him back to us. Clearly, this man has done nothing that deserves death."

kjv: I will therefore chastise him, and release him.
ct: "So I'll have him flogged and then let him go."

kjv: (For of necessity he must release one unto them at the feast.)
ct: (It was customary for Pilate to release one prisoner to the people during the feast.)

kjv: And they cried out all at once, saying, Away with this man, and release unto us Barabbas:
ct: But the whole crowd shouted together, "Get rid of this man! Release Barabbas to us!"

kjv: (Who for a certain sedition made in the city, and for murder, was cast into prison.)
ct: Barabbas had been thrown in prison for starting a revolt in the city and for murder.

kjv: Pilate therefore, willing to release Jesus, spake again to them.
ct: Pilate wanted to release Jesus, so he appealed to the crowd again.

kjv: But they cried, saying, Crucify him, crucify him.
ct: But they kept shouting, "Crucify him! Crucify him!"

kjv: And he said unto them the third time, Why, what evil hath he done? I have found no cause of death in him: I will therefore chastise him, and let him go.
ct: A third time Pilate spoke to them: "Why? What crime has he committed? I've found no reason to execute him. I'll have him flogged and release him."

kjv: And they were instant with loud voices, requiring that he might be crucified. And the voices of them and of the chief priests prevailed.
ct: But they kept screaming louder and louder, demanding that Jesus be crucified. Eventually their voices — along with those of the chief priests — won out.

kjv: And Pilate gave sentence that it should be as they required.
ct: Pilate gave in and handed down the ruling they wanted.

kjv: And he released unto them him that for sedition and murder was cast into prison, whom they had desired; but he delivered Jesus to their will.
ct: He released the man they asked for — the one imprisoned for revolt and murder — and handed Jesus over to be dealt with as they wished.

kjv: And as they led him away, they laid hold upon one Simon, a Cyrenian, coming out of the country, and on him they laid the cross, that he might bear it after Jesus.
ct: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed a man named Simon from Cyrene who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.

kjv: And there followed him a great company of people, and of women, which also bewailed and lamented him.
ct: A huge crowd followed, including women who were weeping and mourning for him.

kjv: But Jesus turning unto them said, Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children.
ct: Jesus turned to them and said, "Daughters of Jerusalem, don't weep for me. Weep for yourselves and for your children."

kjv: For, behold, the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the paps which never gave suck.
ct: "Because the days are coming when people will say, 'Blessed are the women who never had children — the wombs that never carried a baby and the breasts that never nursed.'"

kjv: Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us.
ct: "Then people will beg the mountains, 'Fall on us!' and plead with the hills, 'Cover us!'"

kjv: For if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?
ct: "For if they do this when the tree is green, what will happen when it's dry?"

kjv: And there were also two other, malefactors, led with him to be put to death.
ct: Two criminals were also led out with him to be executed.

kjv: And when they were come to the place, which is called Calvary, there they crucified him, and the malefactors, one on the right hand, and the other on the left.
ct: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him there along with the 2 criminals — one on his right and one on his left.

kjv: Then said Jesus, Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do. And they parted his raiment, and cast lots.
ct: Jesus said, "Father, forgive them, for they don't know what they're doing." Meanwhile, the soldiers divided up his clothes by casting lots.

kjv: And the people stood beholding. And the rulers also with them derided him, saying, He saved others; let him save himself, if he be Christ, the chosen of God.
ct: The people stood watching. The rulers sneered at him, saying, "He saved others — let him save himself if he really is Christ, God's chosen one!"

kjv: And the soldiers also mocked him, coming to him, and offering him vinegar,
ct: The soldiers joined in the mockery too, coming up to him and offering him sour wine.

kjv: And saying, If thou be the king of the Jews, save thyself.
ct: They taunted, "If you're the King of the Jews, save yourself!"

kjv: And a superscription also was written over him in letters of Greek, and Latin, and Hebrew, THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
ct: Above him was a sign written in Greek, Latin, and Hebrew: THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.

kjv: And one of the malefactors which were hanged railed on him, saying, If thou be Christ, save thyself and us.
ct: One of the criminals hanging beside him hurled insults at him: "Aren't you the Christ? Save yourself — and us while you're at it!"

kjv: But the other answering rebuked him, saying, Dost not thou fear God, seeing thou art in the same condemnation?
ct: But the other criminal rebuked him: "Don't you even fear God? You're facing the same death sentence he is."

kjv: And we indeed justly; for we receive the due reward of our deeds: but this man hath done nothing amiss.
ct: "We deserve what we're getting — we earned this punishment. But this man has done nothing wrong."

kjv: And he said unto Jesus, Lord, remember me when thou comest into thy kingdom.
ct: Then he said, "Jesus, Lord, remember me when you come into your kingdom."

kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Verily I say unto thee, To day shalt thou be with me in paradise.
ct: Jesus told him, "I promise you, today you will be with me in Paradise."

kjv: And it was about the sixth hour, and there was a darkness over all the earth until the ninth hour.
ct: It was now about noon, and darkness fell over the entire land until 3 in the afternoon.

kjv: And the sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was rent in the midst.
ct: The sun stopped shining, and the curtain in the temple was torn right down the middle.

kjv: And when Jesus had cried with a loud voice, he said, Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit: and having said thus, he gave up the ghost.
ct: Jesus cried out in a loud voice, "Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!" After saying this, he breathed his last.

kjv: Now when the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, Certainly this was a righteous man.
ct: When the Roman centurion saw what had happened, he praised God and said, "There is no doubt — this was a righteous man."

kjv: And all the people that came together to that sight, beholding the things which were done, smote their breasts, and returned.
ct: The crowds who had gathered to watch, after seeing everything that took place, beat their chests in grief and went home.

kjv: And all his acquaintance, and the women that followed him from Galilee, stood afar off, beholding these things.
