ct: All of Jesus' friends, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance watching everything.

kjv: And, behold, there was a man named Joseph, a counsellor; and he was a good man, and a just:
ct: Now there was a man named Joseph, a member of the Jewish council, who was a good and righteous man.

kjv: (The same had not consented to the counsel and deed of them;) he was of Arimathaea, a city of the Jews: who also himself waited for the kingdom of God.
ct: He had not agreed with the council's decision or their actions. He was from the town of Arimathaea in Judea, and he was waiting for the kingdom of God.

kjv: This man went unto Pilate, and begged the body of Jesus.
ct: Joseph went to Pilate and asked for Jesus' body.

kjv: And he took it down, and wrapped it in linen, and laid it in a sepulchre that was hewn in stone, wherein never man before was laid.
ct: He took it down from the cross, wrapped it in linen cloth, and placed it in a tomb carved out of rock where no one had ever been buried.

kjv: And that day was the preparation, and the sabbath drew on.
ct: It was Preparation Day, and the Sabbath was about to begin.

kjv: And the women also, which came with him from Galilee, followed after, and beheld the sepulchre, and how his body was laid.
ct: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and saw the tomb and how his body was placed inside.

kjv: And they returned, and prepared spices and ointments; and rested the sabbath day according to the commandment.
ct: Then they went home and prepared spices and ointments. But when the Sabbath came, they rested as the commandment required.

kjv: Now upon the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they came unto the sepulchre, bringing the spices which they had prepared, and certain others with them.
ct: Very early on Sunday morning, the women went to the tomb, bringing the spices they had prepared. Several others came with them.

kjv: And they found the stone rolled away from the sepulchre.
ct: When they arrived, they found the stone had been rolled away from the entrance.

kjv: And they entered in, and found not the body of the Lord Jesus.
ct: They went inside, but the body of the Lord Jesus was not there.

kjv: And it came to pass, as they were much perplexed thereabout, behold, two men stood by them in shining garments:
ct: While they stood there, completely baffled, two men suddenly appeared beside them, dressed in clothes that blazed with light.

kjv: And as they were afraid, and bowed down their faces to the earth, they said unto them, Why seek ye the living among the dead?
ct: The women were terrified and dropped their faces to the ground. The men said to them, "Why are you looking for the living among the dead?

kjv: He is not here, but is risen: remember how he spake unto you when he was yet in Galilee,
ct: He isn't here — he has risen! Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee:

kjv: Saying, The Son of man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.
ct: that the Son of Man had to be handed over to sinful men, be crucified, and rise again on the 3rd day."

kjv: And they remembered his words,
ct: Then they remembered his words.

kjv: And returned from the sepulchre, and told all these things unto the eleven, and to all the rest.
ct: They hurried back from the tomb and reported everything to the 11 apostles and everyone else who was with them.

kjv: It was Mary Magdalene, and Joanna, and Mary the mother of James, and other women that were with them, which told these things unto the apostles.
ct: The women who told the apostles were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and several others who had gone with them.

kjv: And their words seemed to them as idle tales, and they believed them not.
ct: But the apostles thought it was nonsense and didn't believe them.

kjv: Then arose Peter, and ran unto the sepulchre; and stooping down, he beheld the linen clothes laid by themselves, and departed, wondering in himself at that which was come to pass.
ct: Peter, however, jumped up and ran to the tomb. He bent down and looked inside, but all he saw were the linen burial cloths lying by themselves. He went home, stunned and wondering what had happened.

kjv: And, behold, two of them went that same day to a village called Emmaus, which was from Jerusalem about threescore furlongs.
ct: That same day, 2 of the followers were walking to a village called Emmaus, about 60 stadia from Jerusalem.

kjv: And they talked together of all these things which had happened.
ct: They were deep in conversation about everything that had happened.

kjv: And it came to pass, that, while they communed together and reasoned, Jesus himself drew near, and went with them.
ct: As they talked and debated back and forth, Jesus himself came up and began walking alongside them.

kjv: But their eyes were holden that they should not know him.
ct: But something kept their eyes from recognizing him.

kjv: And he said unto them, What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?
ct: He asked them, "What are you two discussing so seriously as you walk?" They stopped, their faces heavy with sadness.

kjv: And the one of them, whose name was Cleopas, answering said unto him, Art thou only a stranger in Jerusalem, and hast not known the things which are come to pass there in these days?
ct: One of them, named Cleopas, replied, "Are you the only visitor in Jerusalem who hasn't heard about what's happened there these past few days?"

kjv: And he said unto them, What things? And they said unto him, Concerning Jesus of Nazareth, which was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people:
ct: "What things?" he asked. They said, "About Jesus of Nazareth. He was a prophet who did incredible things and spoke with great power before God and all the people.

kjv: And how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him to be condemned to death, and have crucified him.
ct: But our chief priests and rulers handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.

kjv: But we trusted that it had been he which should have redeemed Israel: and beside all this, to day is the third day since these things were done.
ct: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. On top of all that, today is the 3rd day since it happened.

kjv: Yea, and certain women also of our company made us astonished, which were early at the sepulchre;
ct: Then some women from our group shocked us. They went to the tomb early this morning,

kjv: And when they found not his body, they came, saying, that they had also seen a vision of angels, which said that he was alive.
ct: and when they didn't find his body, they came back saying they had seen a vision of angels who told them he was alive.

kjv: And certain of them which were with us went to the sepulchre, and found it even so as the women had said: but him they saw not.
ct: Some of our people went to the tomb and found it exactly as the women described — but they didn't see him."

kjv: Then he said unto them, O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken:
ct: Jesus said to them, "How foolish you are! How slow your hearts are to believe everything the prophets spoke about!

kjv: Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?
ct: Didn't the Christ have to suffer all these things before entering into his glory?"

kjv: And beginning at Moses and all the prophets, he expounded unto them in all the scriptures the things concerning himself.
ct: Then, starting with Moses and working through all the prophets, he explained to them everything in the Scriptures that pointed to himself.

kjv: And they drew nigh unto the village, whither they went: and he made as though he would have gone further.
ct: As they approached the village where they were headed, Jesus acted as if he were going to continue on.

kjv: But they constrained him, saying, Abide with us: for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent. And he went in to tarry with them.
ct: But they urged him, "Stay with us — it's almost evening and the day is nearly over." So he went inside to stay with them.

kjv: And it came to pass, as he sat at meat with them, he took bread, and blessed it, and brake, and gave to them.
ct: When he sat down to eat with them, he took the bread, gave thanks, broke it, and handed it to them.

kjv: And their eyes were opened, and they knew him; and he vanished out of their sight.
ct: In that moment, their eyes were opened and they recognized him — and then he vanished from their sight.

kjv: And they said one to another, Did not our heart burn within us, while he talked with us by the way, and while he opened to us the scriptures?
ct: They looked at each other and said, "Weren't our hearts burning inside us while he talked with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?"

kjv: And they rose up the same hour, and returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and them that were with them,
ct: They got up immediately and rushed back to Jerusalem, where they found the 11 and the others gathered together.

kjv: Saying, The Lord is risen indeed, and hath appeared to Simon.
ct: Everyone was saying, "It's true! The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon!"

kjv: And they told what things were done in the way, and how he was known of them in breaking of bread.
ct: Then the 2 told their story — what had happened on the road and how they recognized Jesus when he broke the bread.

kjv: And as they thus spake, Jesus himself stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, Peace be unto you.
ct: While they were still talking about this, Jesus himself stood right there among them and said, "Peace be to you."

kjv: But they were terrified and affrighted, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.
ct: They were terrified and panic-stricken, thinking they were seeing a spirit.

kjv: And he said unto them, Why are ye troubled? and why do thoughts arise in your hearts?
ct: He said to them, "Why are you so shaken? Why do doubts fill your hearts?

kjv: Behold my hands and my feet, that it is I myself: handle me, and see; for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have.
ct: Look at my hands and my feet — it's really me. Touch me and see for yourselves. A spirit doesn't have flesh and bones like I do."

kjv: And when he had thus spoken, he shewed them his hands and his feet.
ct: As he said this, he held out his hands and showed them his feet.

kjv: And while they yet believed not for joy, and wondered, he said unto them, Have ye here any meat?
ct: They still couldn't believe it — not because they doubted, but because the joy was too overwhelming. So he asked them, "Do you have anything here to eat?"

kjv: And they gave him a piece of a broiled fish, and of an honeycomb.
ct: They gave him a piece of broiled fish and some honeycomb.

kjv: And he took it, and did eat before them.
ct: He took it and ate it right in front of them.

kjv: And he said unto them, These are the words which I spake unto you, while I was yet with you, that all things must be fulfilled, which were written in the law of Moses, and in the prophets, and in the psalms, concerning me.
ct: Then he told them, "This is what I was telling you while I was still with you: everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled."

kjv: Then opened he their understanding, that they might understand the scriptures,
ct: Then he opened their minds so they could understand the Scriptures.

kjv: And said unto them, Thus it is written, and thus it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise from the dead the third day:
ct: He said to them, "This is what was written: the Christ would suffer and rise from the dead on the 3rd day,

kjv: And that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name among all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
ct: and repentance and the forgiveness of sins would be proclaimed in his name to all nations, starting from Jerusalem.

kjv: And ye are witnesses of these things.
ct: You are witnesses of all these things.

kjv: And, behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem, until ye be endued with power from on high.
ct: Now listen — I am sending you what my Father promised. But stay here in Jerusalem until you are clothed with power from on high."

kjv: And he led them out as far as to Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.
ct: Then he led them out to the area near Bethany. He raised his hands and blessed them.

kjv: And it came to pass, while he blessed them, he was parted from them, and carried up into heaven.
ct: While he was blessing them, he was taken from them and carried up into heaven.

kjv: And they worshipped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy:
ct: They worshiped him, then returned to Jerusalem overflowing with joy.

kjv: And were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.
ct: They spent their time continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.

kjv: There was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews:
ct: There was a man named Nicodemus, a Pharisee and a member of the Jewish ruling council.

kjv: The same came to Jesus by night, and said unto him, Rabbi, we know that thou art a teacher come from God: for no man can do these miracles that thou doest, except God be with him.
ct: He came to Jesus at night and said, "Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher sent from God, because no one could perform the miracles you do unless God were with him."

kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.
ct: Jesus replied, "Truly, truly, I tell you, unless a person is born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God."

kjv: Nicodemus saith unto him, How can a man be born when he is old? can he enter the second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?
ct: Nicodemus asked him, "How can a man be born when he is old? He can't go back into his mother's womb and be born a second time, can he?"

kjv: Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.
ct: Jesus answered, "Truly, truly, I tell you, unless a person is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God.

kjv: That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
ct: What is born from the flesh is flesh, and what is born from the Spirit is spirit.

kjv: Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be born again.
ct: Don't be amazed that I told you, 'You must be born again.'

kjv: The wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh, and whither it goeth: so is every one that is born of the Spirit.
ct: The wind blows wherever it pleases. You hear its sound, but you don't know where it comes from or where it's going. It is the same with everyone born of the Spirit."

kjv: Nicodemus answered and said unto him, How can these things be?
ct: Nicodemus responded, "How is this possible?"

kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things?
ct: Jesus said to him, "You are Israel's teacher, and you don't understand these things?

kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that we do know, and testify that we have seen; and ye receive not our witness.
ct: Truly, truly, I tell you, we speak about what we know, and we testify to what we have seen, yet you people do not accept our testimony.

kjv: If I have told you earthly things, and ye believe not, how shall ye believe, if I tell you of heavenly things?
ct: If I told you about earthly things and you don't believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?

kjv: And no man hath ascended up to heaven, but he that came down from heaven, even the Son of man which is in heaven.
ct: No one has gone up into heaven except the one who came down from heaven — the Son of Man, who is in heaven.

kjv: And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up:
ct: Just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so the Son of Man must be lifted up,

kjv: That whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
ct: so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.

kjv: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
ct: For God loved the world so much that he gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.

kjv: For God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved.
ct: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but so that the world might be saved through him.

kjv: He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already, because he hath not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.
ct: Whoever believes in him is not condemned, but whoever does not believe stands condemned already, because he has not believed in the name of God's one and only Son.

kjv: And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil.
ct: This is the basis for condemnation: light has come into the world, but people loved the darkness instead of the light because their actions were evil.

kjv: For every one that doeth evil hateth the light, neither cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved.
ct: Everyone who does evil hates the light and refuses to come into the light, for fear that his deeds will be exposed.

kjv: But he that doeth truth cometh to the light, that his deeds may be made manifest, that they are wrought in God.
ct: But whoever lives by the truth comes into the light, so that it may be clearly seen that his deeds have been done through God."

kjv: After these things came Jesus and his disciples into the land of Judaea; and there he tarried with them, and baptized.
ct: After this, Jesus and his disciples went into the Judean countryside, where he spent time with them and baptized.

kjv: And John also was baptizing in Aenon near to Salim, because there was much water there: and they came, and were baptized.
ct: John was also baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water there. People kept coming and being baptized,

kjv: For John was not yet cast into prison.
ct: since John had not yet been thrown into prison.

kjv: Then there arose a question between some of John’s disciples and the Jews about purifying.
ct: Then a dispute arose between some of John's disciples and a certain Jew over the matter of purification.

kjv: And they came unto John, and said unto him, Rabbi, he that was with thee beyond Jordan, to whom thou barest witness, behold, the same baptizeth, and all men come to him.
ct: They went to John and said, "Rabbi, the man who was with you across the Jordan — the one you testified about — look, he is baptizing, and everyone is going to him."

kjv: John answered and said, A man can receive nothing, except it be given him from heaven.
ct: John answered, "A person cannot receive anything unless it is given to him from heaven.

kjv: Ye yourselves bear me witness, that I said, I am not the Christ, but that I am sent before him.
ct: You yourselves can confirm that I said, 'I am not the Christ, but I have been sent ahead of him.'

kjv: He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, which standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice: this my joy therefore is fulfilled.
ct: The bride belongs to the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom stands nearby and listens for him, and he is overjoyed when he hears the bridegroom's voice. That is my joy, and it is now complete.

kjv: He must increase, but I must decrease.
ct: He must become greater, and I must become less."

kjv: He that cometh from above is above all: he that is of the earth is earthly, and speaketh of the earth: he that cometh from heaven is above all.
ct: The one who comes from above is above all. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks from an earthly perspective. The one who comes from heaven is above all.

kjv: And what he hath seen and heard, that he testifieth; and no man receiveth his testimony.
ct: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, yet no one accepts his testimony.

kjv: He that hath received his testimony hath set to his seal that God is true.
ct: Whoever does accept his testimony has certified that God is true.

kjv: For he whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God: for God giveth not the Spirit by measure unto him.
ct: For the one God has sent speaks God's words, because God gives the Spirit without limit.

kjv: The Father loveth the Son, and hath given all things into his hand.
ct: The Father loves the Son and has placed everything in his hands.

kjv: He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.
ct: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life, but whoever rejects the Son will not see life — instead, the wrath of God remains on him.

kjv: The former treatise have I made, O Theophilus, of all that Jesus began both to do and teach,
ct: In my first book, Theophilus, I wrote about everything Jesus began to do and teach,

kjv: Until the day in which he was taken up, after that he through the Holy Ghost had given commandments unto the apostles whom he had chosen:
ct: right up until the day he was taken up into heaven. Before he left, he gave instructions through the Holy Spirit to the apostles he had chosen.

kjv: To whom also he shewed himself alive after his passion by many infallible proofs, being seen of them forty days, and speaking of the things pertaining to the kingdom of God:
ct: After his suffering and death, he showed himself alive to these apostles with many convincing proofs. Over a period of 40 days he appeared to them and talked with them about the kingdom of God.

kjv: And, being assembled together with them, commanded them that they should not depart from Jerusalem, but wait for the promise of the Father, which, saith he, ye have heard of me.
ct: During one of these gatherings, he gave them this command: "Don't leave Jerusalem. Wait here for the gift the Father promised — the one I told you about.

kjv: For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost not many days hence.
ct: John baptized with water, but in just a few days, you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit."

kjv: When they therefore were come together, they asked of him, saying, Lord, wilt thou at this time restore again the kingdom to Israel?
ct: So when they were all together, they asked him, "Lord, is this the time when you're going to restore the kingdom to Israel?"

kjv: And he said unto them, It is not for you to know the times or the seasons, which the Father hath put in his own power.
ct: He told them, "It's not for you to know the times or dates that the Father has set by his own authority.

kjv: But ye shall receive power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judaea, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.
ct: But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes upon you. And you will be my witnesses — in Jerusalem, throughout all Judaea and Samaria, and to the farthest corners of the earth."

kjv: And when he had spoken these things, while they beheld, he was taken up; and a cloud received him out of their sight.
ct: After he said this, they watched as he was lifted up, and a cloud took him out of their sight.

kjv: And while they looked stedfastly toward heaven as he went up, behold, two men stood by them in white apparel;
ct: They stood there staring into the sky as he went up. Suddenly, 2 men dressed in white appeared beside them.

kjv: Which also said, Ye men of Galilee, why stand ye gazing up into heaven? this same Jesus, which is taken up from you into heaven, shall so come in like manner as ye have seen him go into heaven.
ct: "Men of Galilee," they said, "why are you standing here looking up at the sky? This same Jesus, who has been taken from you into heaven, will come back in exactly the same way you've seen him go."

kjv: Then returned they unto Jerusalem from the mount called Olivet, which is from Jerusalem a sabbath day’s journey.
ct: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is about a Sabbath day's journey from the city.

kjv: And when they were come in, they went up into an upper room, where abode both Peter, and James, and John, and Andrew, Philip, and Thomas, Bartholomew, and Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, and Simon Zelotes, and Judas the brother of James.
ct: When they arrived, they went to the upstairs room where they had been staying. Those present were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James son of Alphaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas son of James.

kjv: These all continued with one accord in prayer and supplication, with the women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brethren.
ct: They all joined together constantly in prayer, along with the women, Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.

kjv: And in those days Peter stood up in the midst of the disciples, and said, (the number of names together were about an hundred and twenty,)
ct: During those days, Peter stood up among the believers — a group of about 120 people — and said,

kjv: Men and brethren, this scripture must needs have been fulfilled, which the Holy Ghost by the mouth of David spake before concerning Judas, which was guide to them that took Jesus.
ct: "Brothers, the Scripture had to be fulfilled that the Holy Spirit spoke long ago through David about Judas, the one who guided the mob to arrest Jesus.

kjv: For he was numbered with us, and had obtained part of this ministry.
ct: Judas was one of us. He had been given his share in this ministry.

kjv: Now this man purchased a field with the reward of iniquity; and falling headlong, he burst asunder in the midst, and all his bowels gushed out.
ct: But with the money he earned for his wickedness, he bought a field. There he fell headfirst, his body split open, and his intestines spilled out.

kjv: And it was known unto all the dwellers at Jerusalem; insomuch as that field is called in their proper tongue, Aceldama, that is to say, The field of blood.
ct: Everyone in Jerusalem heard about this, so they named that field Aceldama in their own language, which means 'The Field of Blood.'

kjv: For it is written in the book of Psalms, Let his habitation be desolate, and let no man dwell therein: and his bishoprick let another take.
ct: Peter continued, "It's written in the book of Psalms: 'Let his home become deserted; let no one live in it,' and also, 'Let someone else take his position.'

kjv: Wherefore of these men which have companied with us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,
ct: So we need to choose someone who has been with us the entire time the Lord Jesus was among us —

kjv: Beginning from the baptism of John, unto that same day that he was taken up from us, must one be ordained to be a witness with us of his resurrection.
ct: from the time of John's baptism all the way until the day Jesus was taken up from us. This person must join us as a witness of his resurrection."

kjv: And they appointed two, Joseph called Barsabas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.
ct: They nominated 2 men: Joseph called Barsabas (also known as Justus) and Matthias.

kjv: And they prayed, and said, Thou, Lord, which knowest the hearts of all men, shew whether of these two thou hast chosen,
ct: Then they prayed, "Lord, you know everyone's heart. Show us which of these 2 you have chosen

kjv: That he may take part of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas by transgression fell, that he might go to his own place.
ct: to take over this ministry and apostleship that Judas abandoned to go where he belongs."

kjv: And they gave forth their lots; and the lot fell upon Matthias; and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.
ct: Then they cast lots, and the lot fell to Matthias. He was added to the 11 apostles.

kjv: And when the day of Pentecost was fully come, they were all with one accord in one place.
ct: When the day of Pentecost arrived, all the believers were gathered together in one place.

kjv: And suddenly there came a sound from heaven as of a rushing mighty wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting.
ct: Suddenly a sound came from heaven like a powerful, rushing wind, and it filled the entire house where they were sitting.

kjv: And there appeared unto them cloven tongues like as of fire, and it sat upon each of them.
ct: Then what looked like flames of fire appeared, splitting apart and resting on each one of them.

kjv: And they were all filled with the Holy Ghost, and began to speak with other tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance.
ct: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking in other languages as the Spirit gave them the ability.

kjv: And there were dwelling at Jerusalem Jews, devout men, out of every nation under heaven.
ct: Now there were devout Jews living in Jerusalem who had come from every nation under heaven.

kjv: Now when this was noised abroad, the multitude came together, and were confounded, because that every man heard them speak in his own language.
ct: When the sound rang out, a crowd gathered — and they were completely bewildered, because each person heard the believers speaking in his own native language.

kjv: And they were all amazed and marvelled, saying one to another, Behold, are not all these which speak Galilaeans?
ct: Stunned and amazed, they said to each other, "Wait — aren't all these people who are speaking Galileans?

kjv: And how hear we every man in our own tongue, wherein we were born?
ct: Then how is it that each of us hears them in the language we grew up speaking?

kjv: Parthians, and Medes, and Elamites, and the dwellers in Mesopotamia, and in Judaea, and Cappadocia, in Pontus, and Asia,
ct: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, people from Mesopotamia, Judaea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,

kjv: Phrygia, and Pamphylia, in Egypt, and in the parts of Libya about Cyrene, and strangers of Rome, Jews and proselytes,
ct: Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, the parts of Libya near Cyrene, visitors from Rome — both Jews and converts to Judaism —

kjv: Cretes and Arabians, we do hear them speak in our tongues the wonderful works of God.
ct: Cretans and Arabians — we all hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages!"

kjv: And they were all amazed, and were in doubt, saying one to another, What meaneth this?
ct: Everyone was amazed and confused, asking each other, "What does this mean?"

kjv: Others mocking said, These men are full of new wine.
ct: But others made fun of them, saying, "They've just had too much wine."

kjv: But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice, and said unto them, Ye men of Judaea, and all ye that dwell at Jerusalem, be this known unto you, and hearken to my words:
ct: Then Peter stood up with the 11 apostles, raised his voice, and addressed the crowd: "People of Judaea and everyone living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say.

kjv: For these are not drunken, as ye suppose, seeing it is but the third hour of the day.
ct: These people aren't drunk, as you're assuming. It's only 9 in the morning!

kjv: But this is that which was spoken by the prophet Joel;
ct: No, this is what the prophet Joel spoke about:

kjv: And it shall come to pass in the last days, saith God, I will pour out of my Spirit upon all flesh: and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams:
ct: 'In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your young men will see visions, and your old men will dream dreams.

kjv: And on my servants and on my handmaidens I will pour out in those days of my Spirit; and they shall prophesy:
ct: Even on my servants, both men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy.

kjv: And I will shew wonders in heaven above, and signs in the earth beneath; blood, and fire, and vapour of smoke:
ct: I will show wonders in the heavens above and signs on the earth below — blood, fire, and billowing smoke.

kjv: The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before that great and notable day of the Lord come:
ct: The sun will turn to darkness and the moon to blood before the great and glorious day of the Lord arrives.

kjv: And it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved.
ct: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.'

kjv: Ye men of Israel, hear these words; Jesus of Nazareth, a man approved of God among you by miracles and wonders and signs, which God did by him in the midst of you, as ye yourselves also know:
ct: "People of Israel, listen! Jesus of Nazareth was a man whose authority God confirmed to you through miracles, wonders, and signs that God performed through him right here among you — as you yourselves know.

kjv: Him, being delivered by the determinate counsel and foreknowledge of God, ye have taken, and by wicked hands have crucified and slain:
ct: This man was handed over to you by God's deliberate plan and foreknowledge, and you used lawless men to nail him to a cross and kill him.

kjv: Whom God hath raised up, having loosed the pains of death: because it was not possible that he should be holden of it.
ct: But God raised him from the dead, freeing him from the agony of death, because death could not hold him.

kjv: For David speaketh concerning him, I foresaw the Lord always before my face, for he is on my right hand, that I should not be moved:
ct: David said this about him: 'I always kept the Lord before me. Because he is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.

kjv: Therefore did my heart rejoice, and my tongue was glad; moreover also my flesh shall rest in hope:
ct: That is why my heart is glad and my tongue rejoices. Even my body will rest in hope,

kjv: Because thou wilt not leave my soul in hell, neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
ct: because you will not abandon my soul to the grave, and you will not let your Holy One experience decay.

kjv: Thou hast made known to me the ways of life; thou shalt make me full of joy with thy countenance.
ct: You have shown me the path of life; you will fill me with joy in your presence.'

kjv: Men and brethren, let me freely speak unto you of the patriarch David, that he is both dead and buried, and his sepulchre is with us unto this day.
ct: "Brothers, let me speak plainly about the patriarch David: he died, he was buried, and his tomb is still here among us to this day.

kjv: Therefore being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him, that of the fruit of his loins, according to the flesh, he would raise up Christ to sit on his throne;
ct: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had sworn an oath to him — that one of his own descendants would sit on his throne as the Christ.

kjv: He seeing this before spake of the resurrection of Christ, that his soul was not left in hell, neither his flesh did see corruption.
ct: Seeing what was coming, David spoke about the resurrection of the Christ — that his soul would not be abandoned to the grave, and his body would not experience decay.

kjv: This Jesus hath God raised up, whereof we all are witnesses.
ct: God raised this Jesus to life, and every one of us is a witness to it.

kjv: Therefore being by the right hand of God exalted, and having received of the Father the promise of the Holy Ghost, he hath shed forth this, which ye now see and hear.
ct: He has been lifted to the highest place at God's right hand. He received the promised Holy Spirit from the Father and has poured out what you are now seeing and hearing.

kjv: For David is not ascended into the heavens: but he saith himself, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
ct: David himself did not ascend into the heavens, yet he said, 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand

kjv: Until I make thy foes thy footstool.
ct: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet."'

kjv: Therefore let all the house of Israel know assuredly, that God hath made that same Jesus, whom ye have crucified, both Lord and Christ.
ct: "So let everyone in Israel know this beyond any doubt: God has made this Jesus — the very one you crucified — both Lord and Christ."

kjv: Now when they heard this, they were pricked in their heart, and said unto Peter and to the rest of the apostles, Men and brethren, what shall we do?
ct: When the people heard this, it cut them to the heart. They cried out to Peter and the other apostles, "Brothers, what should we do?"

kjv: Then Peter said unto them, Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost.
ct: Peter answered, "Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.

kjv: For the promise is unto you, and to your children, and to all that are afar off, even as many as the LORD our God shall call.
ct: This promise is for you, for your children, and for everyone who is far away — for everyone the LORD our God will call."

kjv: And with many other words did he testify and exhort, saying, Save yourselves from this untoward generation.
ct: Peter kept urging them with many more words, pleading, "Save yourselves from this corrupt generation!"

kjv: Then they that gladly received his word were baptized: and the same day there were added unto them about three thousand souls.
ct: Those who accepted his message were baptized, and about 3,000 people were added to their number that day.

kjv: And they continued stedfastly in the apostles’ doctrine and fellowship, and in breaking of bread, and in prayers.
ct: They devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching, to fellowship with one another, to the breaking of bread, and to prayer.

kjv: And fear came upon every soul: and many wonders and signs were done by the apostles.
ct: A deep sense of fear came over everyone, and the apostles performed many wonders and signs.

kjv: And all that believed were together, and had all things common;
ct: All the believers stayed together and shared everything they had.

kjv: And sold their possessions and goods, and parted them to all men, as every man had need.
ct: They sold their property and possessions and distributed the money to anyone who was in need.

kjv: And they, continuing daily with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread from house to house, did eat their meat with gladness and singleness of heart,
ct: Every day they met together at the temple with united hearts, and they shared meals in each other's homes, eating with joy and genuine generosity,

kjv: Praising God, and having favour with all the people. And the Lord added to the church daily such as should be saved.
ct: praising God and enjoying the goodwill of all the people. And every day the Lord added to their number those who were being saved.

kjv: Now Peter and John went up together into the temple at the hour of prayer, being the ninth hour.
ct: One afternoon, Peter and John headed to the temple for the regular 3 o'clock prayer time.

kjv: And a certain man lame from his mother’s womb was carried, whom they laid daily at the gate of the temple which is called Beautiful, to ask alms of them that entered into the temple;
ct: Near the entrance was a man who had been unable to walk since birth. Every day, people carried him to the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could beg for money from those going in.

kjv: Who seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple asked an alms.
ct: When he saw Peter and John about to enter the temple, he asked them for money.

kjv: And Peter, fastening his eyes upon him with John, said, Look on us.
ct: Peter looked straight at him, and so did John. "Look at us," Peter said.

kjv: And he gave heed unto them, expecting to receive something of them.
ct: The man gave them his full attention, expecting them to give him something.

kjv: Then Peter said, Silver and gold have I none; but such as I have give I thee: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth rise up and walk.
ct: Peter said, "I don't have any silver or gold, but I'll give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, get up and walk!"

kjv: And he took him by the right hand, and lifted him up: and immediately his feet and ankle bones received strength.
ct: Then Peter grabbed the man's right hand and pulled him to his feet. Instantly, strength surged into his feet and ankles.

kjv: And he leaping up stood, and walked, and entered with them into the temple, walking, and leaping, and praising God.
ct: He jumped up, stood on his own, and started walking. He went into the temple with them — walking, leaping, and praising God.

kjv: And all the people saw him walking and praising God:
ct: Everyone there saw him walking around and praising God.

kjv: And they knew that it was he which sat for alms at the Beautiful gate of the temple: and they were filled with wonder and amazement at that which had happened unto him.
ct: They recognized him as the same man who always sat begging at the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely stunned by what had happened to him.

kjv: And as the lame man which was healed held Peter and John, all the people ran together unto them in the porch that is called Solomon’s, greatly wondering.
ct: The healed man clung to Peter and John, and a crowd came rushing toward them at Solomon's Porch, absolutely amazed.

kjv: And when Peter saw it, he answered unto the people, Ye men of Israel, why marvel ye at this? or why look ye so earnestly on us, as though by our own power or holiness we had made this man to walk?
ct: When Peter saw the crowd gathering, he spoke up: "People of Israel, why are you so shocked by this? Why are you staring at us like we made this man walk by our own power or godliness?

kjv: The God of Abraham, and of Isaac, and of Jacob, the God of our fathers, hath glorified his Son Jesus; whom ye delivered up, and denied him in the presence of Pilate, when he was determined to let him go.
ct: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — the God of our ancestors — has brought glory to his Son Jesus. You handed him over and rejected him right in front of Pilate, even though Pilate had decided to release him.

kjv: But ye denied the Holy One and the Just, and desired a murderer to be granted unto you;
ct: You rejected the Holy and Righteous One and demanded that a murderer be set free instead.

kjv: And killed the Prince of life, whom God hath raised from the dead; whereof we are witnesses.
ct: You killed the Prince of life, but God raised him from the dead — and we are witnesses to that.

kjv: And his name through faith in his name hath made this man strong, whom ye see and know: yea, the faith which is by him hath given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all.
ct: It's through faith in Jesus' name that this man was healed — this man you can see standing right here. The faith that comes through Jesus has given him complete healing, right before your eyes.

kjv: And now, brethren, I wot that through ignorance ye did it, as did also your rulers.
ct: Now, brothers, I know that you acted out of ignorance, and so did your leaders.

kjv: But those things, which God before had shewed by the mouth of all his prophets, that Christ should suffer, he hath so fulfilled.
ct: But God was fulfilling what he had announced long ago through all his prophets — that Christ would suffer.

kjv: Repent ye therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out, when the times of refreshing shall come from the presence of the Lord;
ct: So repent and turn back to God, so that your sins may be wiped away and times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord.

kjv: And he shall send Jesus Christ, which before was preached unto you:
ct: Then he will send Jesus, the Christ who was appointed for you.

kjv: Whom the heaven must receive until the times of restitution of all things, which God hath spoken by the mouth of all his holy prophets since the world began.
ct: Heaven must receive him until the time comes for God to restore all things — just as he promised long ago through his holy prophets.

kjv: For Moses truly said unto the fathers, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you.
ct: Moses himself told our ancestors, 'The Lord your God will raise up a prophet for you from among your own people, just like me. You must listen to everything he tells you.

kjv: And it shall come to pass, that every soul, which will not hear that prophet, shall be destroyed from among the people.
ct: Anyone who refuses to listen to that prophet will be completely cut off from God's people.'

kjv: Yea, and all the prophets from Samuel and those that follow after, as many as have spoken, have likewise foretold of these days.
ct: Every prophet from Samuel onward — all who have spoken — also pointed to these very days.

kjv: Ye are the children of the prophets, and of the covenant which God made with our fathers, saying unto Abraham, And in thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed.
ct: You are the heirs of those prophets and of the covenant God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, 'Through your offspring, all the families of the earth will be blessed.'

kjv: Unto you first God, having raised up his Son Jesus, sent him to bless you, in turning away every one of you from his iniquities.
ct: God raised up his Son Jesus and sent him to you first, to bless you by turning each of you away from your wicked ways."

kjv: And as they spake unto the people, the priests, and the captain of the temple, and the Sadducees, came upon them,
ct: While Peter and John were still speaking to the crowd, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees came up to them.

kjv: Being grieved that they taught the people, and preached through Jesus the resurrection from the dead.
ct: They were furious that Peter and John were teaching the people and proclaiming that Jesus had risen from the dead.

kjv: And they laid hands on them, and put them in hold unto the next day: for it was now eventide.
ct: They arrested them and threw them in jail until the next day, since it was already evening.

kjv: Howbeit many of them which heard the word believed; and the number of the men was about five thousand.
ct: But many of the people who heard the message believed, and the total number of male believers grew to about 5,000.

kjv: And it came to pass on the morrow, that their rulers, and elders, and scribes,
ct: The next morning, the Jewish rulers, elders, and teachers of the law assembled in Jerusalem.

kjv: And Annas the high priest, and Caiaphas, and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of the kindred of the high priest, were gathered together at Jerusalem.
ct: Annas the high priest was there, along with Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and everyone else from the high priest's family.

kjv: And when they had set them in the midst, they asked, By what power, or by what name, have ye done this?
ct: They brought Peter and John before them and demanded, "By what power or in whose name did you do this?"

kjv: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Ghost, said unto them, Ye rulers of the people, and elders of Israel,
ct: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Spirit, answered them, "Rulers of the people and elders of Israel,

kjv: If we this day be examined of the good deed done to the impotent man, by what means he is made whole;
ct: if we are being questioned today about an act of kindness shown to a crippled man — how he was healed —

kjv: Be it known unto you all, and to all the people of Israel, that by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom ye crucified, whom God raised from the dead, even by him doth this man stand here before you whole.
ct: then let every one of you and all the people of Israel know this: it was by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, the one you crucified and God raised from the dead, that this man stands before you completely healed.

kjv: This is the stone which was set at nought of you builders, which is become the head of the corner.
ct: Jesus is 'the stone you builders rejected, which has become the cornerstone.'

kjv: Neither is there salvation in any other: for there is none other name under heaven given among men, whereby we must be saved.
ct: There is no salvation through anyone else. There is no other name under heaven given to humanity by which we must be saved."

kjv: Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and perceived that they were unlearned and ignorant men, they marvelled; and they took knowledge of them, that they had been with Jesus.
ct: The council members were amazed at the boldness of Peter and John, especially when they realized these were ordinary men with no formal training. Then it clicked — these men had been with Jesus.

kjv: And beholding the man which was healed standing with them, they could say nothing against it.
ct: And since the man who had been healed was standing right there with them, the council had nothing they could say against it.

kjv: But when they had commanded them to go aside out of the council, they conferred among themselves,
ct: So they ordered Peter and John to step outside the council chamber and began debating among themselves.

kjv: Saying, What shall we do to these men? for that indeed a notable miracle hath been done by them is manifest to all them that dwell in Jerusalem; and we cannot deny it.
ct: "What are we going to do with these men?" they said. "Everyone in Jerusalem knows they performed an undeniable miracle. We can't pretend it didn't happen.

kjv: But that it spread no further among the people, let us straitly threaten them, that they speak henceforth to no man in this name.
ct: But to keep this from spreading any further among the people, let's warn them with serious threats never to speak to anyone in this name again."

kjv: And they called them, and commanded them not to speak at all nor teach in the name of Jesus.
ct: So they called Peter and John back in and ordered them to stop speaking or teaching in the name of Jesus — period.

kjv: But Peter and John answered and said unto them, Whether it be right in the sight of God to hearken unto you more than unto God, judge ye.
ct: But Peter and John replied, "You decide for yourselves whether it's right in God's sight to obey you rather than God.

kjv: For we cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard.
ct: Because we simply cannot stop talking about what we've seen and heard."

kjv: So when they had further threatened them, they let them go, finding nothing how they might punish them, because of the people: for all men glorified God for that which was done.
ct: After threatening them further, the council let them go. They couldn't figure out how to punish them without causing a riot, because everyone was praising God for what had happened.

kjv: For the man was above forty years old, on whom this miracle of healing was shewed.
ct: After all, the man who had been miraculously healed was over 40 years old.

kjv: And being let go, they went to their own company, and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said unto them.
ct: As soon as they were released, Peter and John went back to the other believers and told them everything the chief priests and elders had said.

kjv: And when they heard that, they lifted up their voice to God with one accord, and said, Lord, thou art God, which hast made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all that in them is:
ct: When the believers heard this, they raised their voices together in prayer to God: "Lord, you are God! You made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.

kjv: Who by the mouth of thy servant David hast said, Why did the heathen rage, and the people imagine vain things?
ct: You spoke through your servant David by the Holy Spirit when he said: 'Why do the nations rage, and the peoples plot useless schemes?

kjv: The kings of the earth stood up, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord, and against his Christ.
ct: The kings of the earth take their stand, and the rulers band together against the Lord and against his Christ.'

kjv: For of a truth against thy holy child Jesus, whom thou hast anointed, both Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles, and the people of Israel, were gathered together,
ct: And that's exactly what happened in this city. Herod and Pontius Pilate joined forces with the Gentiles and the people of Israel against your holy servant Jesus, the one you anointed.

kjv: For to do whatsoever thy hand and thy counsel determined before to be done.
ct: They carried out everything your power and purpose had already determined would take place.

kjv: And now, Lord, behold their threatenings: and grant unto thy servants, that with all boldness they may speak thy word,
ct: Now, Lord, see how they're threatening us, and give your servants the courage to speak your word with complete boldness.

kjv: By stretching forth thine hand to heal; and that signs and wonders may be done by the name of thy holy child Jesus.
ct: Stretch out your hand to heal, and let signs and wonders be performed through the name of your holy servant Jesus."

kjv: And when they had prayed, the place was shaken where they were assembled together; and they were all filled with the Holy Ghost, and they spake the word of God with boldness.
ct: When they finished praying, the place where they were meeting shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking the word of God with boldness.

kjv: And the multitude of them that believed were of one heart and of one soul: neither said any of them that ought of the things which he possessed was his own; but they had all things common.
ct: The whole community of believers was united in heart and soul. No one claimed that any of their possessions were their own — they shared everything.

kjv: And with great power gave the apostles witness of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus: and great grace was upon them all.
ct: With tremendous power, the apostles continued to testify about the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and God's grace was powerfully at work in all of them.

kjv: Neither was there any among them that lacked: for as many as were possessors of lands or houses sold them, and brought the prices of the things that were sold,
ct: No one among them was in need, because those who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money from the sales

kjv: And laid them down at the apostles’ feet: and distribution was made unto every man according as he had need.
ct: and place it at the apostles' feet. Then it was distributed to anyone who had a need.

kjv: And Joses, who by the apostles was surnamed Barnabas, (which is, being interpreted, The son of consolation,) a Levite, and of the country of Cyprus,
ct: One example was a Levite from Cyprus named Joses, whom the apostles called Barnabas — which means "Son of Encouragement."

kjv: Having land, sold it, and brought the money, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
ct: He sold a field he owned, brought the money, and laid it at the apostles' feet.

kjv: But a certain man named Ananias, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession,
ct: Now a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.

kjv: And kept back part of the price, his wife also being privy to it, and brought a certain part, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
ct: With his wife's full knowledge, he kept back some of the money for himself. Then he brought the rest and laid it at the apostles' feet.

kjv: But Peter said, Ananias, why hath Satan filled thine heart to lie to the Holy Ghost, and to keep back part of the price of the land?
ct: Peter said, "Ananias, why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit and keep back part of the money you received for the land?

kjv: Whiles it remained, was it not thine own? and after it was sold, was it not in thine own power? why hast thou conceived this thing in thine heart? thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.
ct: Before you sold it, wasn't it yours to keep? And after you sold it, wasn't the money yours to do with as you pleased? What made you think up this scheme in your heart? You haven't lied to people — you've lied to God."

kjv: And Ananias hearing these words fell down, and gave up the ghost: and great fear came on all them that heard these things.
ct: The moment Ananias heard these words, he collapsed and breathed his last. Everyone who heard about it was gripped with fear.

kjv: And the young men arose, wound him up, and carried him out, and buried him.
ct: Some young men got up, wrapped his body, carried him out, and buried him.

kjv: And it was about the space of three hours after, when his wife, not knowing what was done, came in.
ct: About 3 hours later, his wife came in, completely unaware of what had happened.

kjv: And Peter answered unto her, Tell me whether ye sold the land for so much? And she said, Yea, for so much.
ct: Peter asked her, "Tell me — did you and your husband sell the land for this amount?" She answered, "Yes, that was the price."

kjv: Then Peter said unto her, How is it that ye have agreed together to tempt the Spirit of the Lord? behold, the feet of them which have buried thy husband are at the door, and shall carry thee out.
ct: Peter said to her, "How could the two of you agree to test the Spirit of the Lord? Listen — the men who just buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out too."

kjv: Then fell she down straightway at his feet, and yielded up the ghost: and the young men came in, and found her dead, and, carrying her forth, buried her by her husband.
ct: Instantly she fell down at his feet and breathed her last. When the young men came in and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.

kjv: And great fear came upon all the church, and upon as many as heard these things.
ct: Deep fear swept through the entire church and everyone else who heard what had happened.

kjv: And by the hands of the apostles were many signs and wonders wrought among the people; (and they were all with one accord in Solomon’s porch.
ct: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. All the believers would gather together in Solomon's Porch.

kjv: And of the rest durst no man join himself to them: but the people magnified them.
ct: No outsider dared to join them, yet the people held them in high regard.

kjv: And believers were the more added to the Lord, multitudes both of men and women.)
ct: Still, more and more people — crowds of both men and women — put their faith in the Lord.

kjv: Insomuch that they brought forth the sick into the streets, and laid them on beds and couches, that at the least the shadow of Peter passing by might overshadow some of them.
ct: People even carried the sick out into the streets and laid them on cots and mats, hoping that at least Peter's shadow might fall on some of them as he walked by.

kjv: There came also a multitude out of the cities round about unto Jerusalem, bringing sick folks, and them which were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were healed every one.
ct: Crowds also poured in from the towns surrounding Jerusalem, bringing those who were sick or tormented by unclean spirits, and every one of them was healed.

kjv: Then the high priest rose up, and all they that were with him, (which is the sect of the Sadducees,) and were filled with indignation,
ct: This provoked the high priest and all his associates — the party of the Sadducees — and they were filled with jealousy.

kjv: And laid their hands on the apostles, and put them in the common prison.
ct: They arrested the apostles and threw them into the public jail.

kjv: But the angel of the Lord by night opened the prison doors, and brought them forth, and said,
ct: But during the night, an angel of the Lord opened the prison doors, led them out, and said,

kjv: Go, stand and speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life.
ct: "Go stand in the temple and tell the people everything about this new life."

kjv: And when they heard that, they entered into the temple early in the morning, and taught. But the high priest came, and they that were with him, and called the council together, and all the senate of the children of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.
ct: They obeyed, entering the temple at daybreak and beginning to teach. Meanwhile, the high priest and his associates convened the full council — the entire senate of Israel — and sent officers to the prison to bring the apostles before them.

kjv: But when the officers came, and found them not in the prison, they returned, and told,
ct: But when the officers arrived at the jail, the apostles were gone. They went back and reported,

kjv: Saying, The prison truly found we shut with all safety, and the keepers standing without before the doors: but when we had opened, we found no man within.
ct: "We found the prison securely locked, with the guards standing at their posts in front of the doors. But when we opened them, no one was inside!"

kjv: Now when the high priest and the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these things, they doubted of them whereunto this would grow.
ct: When the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests heard this, they were completely baffled, wondering where this would all lead.

kjv: Then came one and told them, saying, Behold, the men whom ye put in prison are standing in the temple, and teaching the people.
ct: Then someone arrived with news: "The men you locked up are standing in the temple, teaching the people!"

kjv: Then went the captain with the officers, and brought them without violence: for they feared the people, lest they should have been stoned.
ct: So the captain went with his officers and brought the apostles back — but without force, because they were afraid the crowd might stone them.

kjv: And when they had brought them, they set them before the council: and the high priest asked them,
ct: They brought them in and made them stand before the council. The high priest confronted them,

kjv: Saying, Did not we straitly command you that ye should not teach in this name? and, behold, ye have filled Jerusalem with your doctrine, and intend to bring this man’s blood upon us.
ct: saying, "Didn't we give you strict orders not to teach in this name? Yet you've flooded Jerusalem with your teaching, and you're trying to make us responsible for this man's death!"

kjv: Then Peter and the other apostles answered and said, We ought to obey God rather than men.
ct: Peter and the other apostles replied, "We must obey God rather than people.

kjv: The God of our fathers raised up Jesus, whom ye slew and hanged on a tree.
ct: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus back to life — the very one you killed by hanging him on a cross.

kjv: Him hath God exalted with his right hand to be a Prince and a Saviour, for to give repentance to Israel, and forgiveness of sins.
ct: God lifted him up to his right hand as Prince and Savior, to offer Israel the chance to repent and receive forgiveness of sins.

kjv: And we are his witnesses of these things; and so is also the Holy Ghost, whom God hath given to them that obey him.
ct: We are witnesses to all of this, and so is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to everyone who obeys him."

kjv: When they heard that, they were cut to the heart, and took counsel to slay them.
ct: When the council members heard this, they were furious and wanted to have them killed.

kjv: Then stood there up one in the council, a Pharisee, named Gamaliel, a doctor of the law, had in reputation among all the people, and commanded to put the apostles forth a little space;
ct: But a Pharisee named Gamaliel stood up in the council. He was a respected teacher of the law, honored by all the people. He ordered the apostles to be taken outside for a moment.

kjv: And said unto them, Ye men of Israel, take heed to yourselves what ye intend to do as touching these men.
ct: Then he addressed the council: "Men of Israel, think carefully about what you're planning to do with these men.

kjv: For before these days rose up Theudas, boasting himself to be somebody; to whom a number of men, about four hundred, joined themselves: who was slain; and all, as many as obeyed him, were scattered, and brought to nought.
ct: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be someone important. About 400 men rallied behind him. But he was killed, and all his followers scattered and disappeared.

kjv: After this man rose up Judas of Galilee in the days of the taxing, and drew away much people after him: he also perished; and all, even as many as obeyed him, were dispersed.
ct: After him, Judas of Galilee came along during the time of the census and attracted a following. He too was killed, and all his followers were scattered.

kjv: And now I say unto you, Refrain from these men, and let them alone: for if this counsel or this work be of men, it will come to nought:
ct: So my advice is this: stay away from these men and leave them alone. If what they're planning and doing is merely human, it will collapse on its own.

kjv: But if it be of God, ye cannot overthrow it; lest haply ye be found even to fight against God.
ct: But if it is from God, you won't be able to stop them. You'll only find yourselves fighting against God."

kjv: And to him they agreed: and when they had called the apostles, and beaten them, they commanded that they should not speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
ct: The council was persuaded. They called the apostles back in, had them beaten, ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and released them.

kjv: And they departed from the presence of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer shame for his name.
ct: The apostles left the council overjoyed that they had been considered worthy to suffer disgrace for the name of Jesus.

kjv: And daily in the temple, and in every house, they ceased not to teach and preach Jesus Christ.
ct: Every day, both in the temple and from house to house, they never stopped teaching and proclaiming that Jesus is the Christ.

kjv: Then said the high priest, Are these things so?
ct: The high priest asked Stephen, "Are these accusations true?"

kjv: And he said, Men, brethren, and fathers, hearken; The God of glory appeared unto our father Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Charran,
ct: Stephen replied, "Brothers and fathers, listen to me. The God of glory appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still living in Mesopotamia, before he moved to Haran.

kjv: And said unto him, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and come into the land which I shall shew thee.
ct: God told him, 'Leave your country and your relatives, and go to the land I will show you.'

kjv: Then came he out of the land of the Chaldaeans, and dwelt in Charran: and from thence, when his father was dead, he removed him into this land, wherein ye now dwell.
ct: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, God moved him to this land where you're living now.

kjv: And he gave him none inheritance in it, no, not so much as to set his foot on: yet he promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his seed after him, when as yet he had no child.
ct: God didn't give him any property here — not even a single foot of ground. But he promised to give the whole land to Abraham and his descendants after him, even though Abraham had no children at the time.

kjv: And God spake on this wise, That his seed should sojourn in a strange land; and that they should bring them into bondage, and entreat them evil four hundred years.
ct: God told him that his descendants would live as foreigners in a land that wasn't theirs, where they'd be enslaved and mistreated for 400 years.

kjv: And the nation to whom they shall be in bondage will I judge, said God: and after that shall they come forth, and serve me in this place.
ct: 'But I will judge the nation that enslaves them,' God said, 'and afterward they will come out and worship me in this place.'

kjv: And he gave him the covenant of circumcision: and so Abraham begat Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat the twelve patriarchs.
ct: Then God gave Abraham the covenant of circumcision. So Abraham became the father of Isaac and circumcised him on the 8th day. Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob became the father of the 12 patriarchs.

kjv: And the patriarchs, moved with envy, sold Joseph into Egypt: but God was with him,
ct: The patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, so they sold him into slavery in Egypt. But God was with him.

kjv: And delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he made him governor over Egypt and all his house.
ct: God rescued him from all his troubles and gave him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh, the king of Egypt. Pharaoh appointed him governor over all of Egypt and his entire royal household.

kjv: Now there came a dearth over all the land of Egypt and Chanaan, and great affliction: and our fathers found no sustenance.
ct: Then a famine struck all of Egypt and Canaan, bringing terrible suffering. Our ancestors couldn't find any food.

kjv: But when Jacob heard that there was corn in Egypt, he sent out our fathers first.
ct: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there on their first trip.

kjv: And at the second time Joseph was made known to his brethren; and Joseph’s kindred was made known unto Pharaoh.
ct: On their second visit, Joseph revealed his identity to his brothers, and Pharaoh learned about Joseph's family.

kjv: Then sent Joseph, and called his father Jacob to him, and all his kindred, threescore and fifteen souls.
ct: Joseph then sent for his father Jacob and his entire family — 75 people in all.

kjv: So Jacob went down into Egypt, and died, he, and our fathers,
ct: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he eventually died, along with our ancestors.

kjv: And were carried over into Sychem, and laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a sum of money of the sons of Emmor the father of Sychem.
ct: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased with silver from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.

kjv: But when the time of the promise drew nigh, which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,
ct: As the time drew near for God to fulfill the promise he had made to Abraham, our people in Egypt grew and multiplied greatly.

kjv: Till another king arose, which knew not Joseph.
ct: Eventually a new king came to power in Egypt who knew nothing about Joseph.

kjv: The same dealt subtilly with our kindred, and evil entreated our fathers, so that they cast out their young children, to the end they might not live.
ct: This king dealt treacherously with our people and oppressed our ancestors, forcing them to abandon their newborn babies so they would die.

kjv: In which time Moses was born, and was exceeding fair, and nourished up in his father’s house three months:
ct: It was during this time that Moses was born — an exceptionally beautiful child. His parents raised him in their home for 3 months.

kjv: And when he was cast out, Pharaoh’s daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own son.
ct: When he was finally abandoned, Pharaoh's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.

kjv: And Moses was learned in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in words and in deeds.
ct: Moses was educated in all the wisdom of the Egyptians and became powerful in both his words and his actions.

kjv: And when he was full forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brethren the children of Israel.
ct: When Moses was 40 years old, he felt compelled to visit his own people, the Israelites.

kjv: And seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him that was oppressed, and smote the Egyptian:
ct: He saw one of them being mistreated, so he stepped in to defend him. He struck the Egyptian and killed him to avenge the man who was being oppressed.

kjv: For he supposed his brethren would have understood how that God by his hand would deliver them: but they understood not.
ct: Moses assumed his fellow Israelites would realize that God was using him to rescue them, but they didn't understand.

kjv: And the next day he shewed himself unto them as they strove, and would have set them at one again, saying, Sirs, ye are brethren; why do ye wrong one to another?
ct: The next day, he came across two Israelites fighting and tried to make peace between them. 'Men, you're brothers!' he said. 'Why are you hurting each other?'

kjv: But he that did his neighbour wrong thrust him away, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge over us?
ct: But the one who was in the wrong shoved Moses away and said, 'Who appointed you as our ruler and judge?

kjv: Wilt thou kill me, as thou diddest the Egyptian yesterday?
ct: Are you going to kill me the way you killed that Egyptian yesterday?'

kjv: Then fled Moses at this saying, and was a stranger in the land of Madian, where he begat two sons.
ct: When Moses heard this, he fled the country and lived as a foreigner in the land of Midian, where he had 2 sons.

kjv: And when forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the wilderness of mount Sina an angel of the Lord in a flame of fire in a bush.
ct: 40 years later, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in the wilderness near Mount Sinai, in a flame of fire burning inside a bush.

kjv: When Moses saw it, he wondered at the sight: and as he drew near to behold it, the voice of the LORD came unto him,
ct: Moses was amazed at what he saw. As he moved closer to get a better look, the voice of the LORD spoke to him:

kjv: Saying, I am the God of thy fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Then Moses trembled, and durst not behold.
ct: 'I am the God of your ancestors — the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.' Moses was shaking with fear and didn't dare to look.

kjv: Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shoes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest is holy ground.
ct: The Lord told him, 'Take off your sandals, because the place where you're standing is holy ground.

kjv: I have seen, I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning, and am come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send thee into Egypt.
ct: I have clearly seen how my people are suffering in Egypt. I've heard their groaning, and I've come down to set them free. Now go — I'm sending you to Egypt.'

kjv: This Moses whom they refused, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge? the same did God send to be a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel which appeared to him in the bush.
ct: This is the same Moses they had rejected, saying, 'Who made you a ruler and judge?' Yet God sent him as both a ruler and a deliverer, through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

kjv: He brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.
ct: Moses led them out of Egypt after performing wonders and signs there, at the Red Sea, and in the wilderness over the course of 40 years.

kjv: This is that Moses, which said unto the children of Israel, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear.
ct: This is the same Moses who told the Israelites, 'The Lord your God will raise up a prophet for you from among your own people, one like me.'

kjv: This is he, that was in the church in the wilderness with the angel which spake to him in the mount Sina, and with our fathers: who received the lively oracles to give unto us:
ct: This is the man who stood in the assembly in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai and with our ancestors. He received living words from God to pass on to us.

kjv: To whom our fathers would not obey, but thrust him from them, and in their hearts turned back again into Egypt,
ct: But our ancestors refused to obey him. They rejected him and in their hearts turned back to Egypt,

kjv: Saying unto Aaron, Make us gods to go before us: for as for this Moses, which brought us out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.
ct: telling Aaron, 'Make us gods who will lead us, because this Moses who brought us out of Egypt — we don't know what's happened to him.'

kjv: And they made a calf in those days, and offered sacrifice unto the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their own hands.
ct: So they made an idol shaped like a calf, offered sacrifices to it, and celebrated what they had made with their own hands.

kjv: Then God turned, and gave them up to worship the host of heaven; as it is written in the book of the prophets, O ye house of Israel, have ye offered to me slain beasts and sacrifices by the space of forty years in the wilderness?
ct: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky. As it's written in the book of the prophets: 'House of Israel, did you bring me sacrifices and offerings during those 40 years in the wilderness?

kjv: Yea, ye took up the tabernacle of Moloch, and the star of your god Remphan, figures which ye made to worship them: and I will carry you away beyond Babylon.
ct: No — you carried along the shrine of Moloch and the star of your god Rephan, idols you made to worship. So I will send you into exile beyond Babylon.'

kjv: Our fathers had the tabernacle of witness in the wilderness, as he had appointed, speaking unto Moses, that he should make it according to the fashion that he had seen.
ct: Our ancestors had the tabernacle of the testimony with them in the wilderness. God had instructed Moses to build it exactly according to the pattern he had been shown.

kjv: Which also our fathers that came after brought in with Jesus into the possession of the Gentiles, whom God drave out before the face of our fathers, unto the days of David;
ct: The next generation of our ancestors brought that tabernacle with them when they entered the land under Joshua's leadership. God drove out the nations before them, and the tabernacle remained until the time of David.

kjv: Who found favour before God, and desired to find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
ct: David found favor with God and asked for the privilege of building a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.

kjv: But Solomon built him an house.
ct: But it was Solomon who actually built the house for God.

kjv: Howbeit the most High dwelleth not in temples made with hands; as saith the prophet,
