ct: That's why so many of you are weak and sick, and a number of you have died.

kjv: For if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.
ct: If we examined ourselves honestly, we wouldn't come under judgment.

kjv: But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.
ct: But when the Lord does judge us, he disciplines us so that we won't be condemned along with the rest of the world.

kjv: Wherefore, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, tarry one for another.
ct: So, my brothers and sisters, when you come together to eat, wait for each other.

kjv: And if any man hunger, let him eat at home; that ye come not together unto condemnation. And the rest will I set in order when I come.
ct: If anyone is really hungry, eat at home first — so that when you gather, it doesn't bring judgment on you. I'll sort out the other matters when I arrive.

kjv: Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I would not have you ignorant.
ct: Now, brothers, I want you to be well-informed about spiritual gifts.

kjv: Ye know that ye were Gentiles, carried away unto these dumb idols, even as ye were led.
ct: You remember that before you believed, you were pagans — pulled along and led astray by mute idols that couldn't even speak.

kjv: Wherefore I give you to understand, that no man speaking by the Spirit of God calleth Jesus accursed: and that no man can say that Jesus is the Lord, but by the Holy Ghost.
ct: So let me make this clear: no one speaking by the spirit of God ever says, "Jesus is cursed," and no one can say, "Jesus is Lord," except by the Holy Spirit.

kjv: Now there are diversities of gifts, but the same Spirit.
ct: There are different kinds of gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.

kjv: And there are differences of administrations, but the same Lord.
ct: There are different kinds of service, but they're all directed by the same Lord.

kjv: And there are diversities of operations, but it is the same God which worketh all in all.
ct: There are different ways God works through people, but it's the same God doing the work in all of them.

kjv: But the manifestation of the Spirit is given to every man to profit withal.
ct: The Spirit shows up in each person's life in a unique way, and it's always meant to benefit everyone.

kjv: For to one is given by the Spirit the word of wisdom; to another the word of knowledge by the same Spirit;
ct: The Spirit gives one person a message of wisdom, and another a message of knowledge through that same Spirit.

kjv: To another faith by the same Spirit; to another the gifts of healing by the same Spirit;
ct: To another the same Spirit gives extraordinary faith, and to yet another, gifts of healing.

kjv: To another the working of miracles; to another prophecy; to another discerning of spirits; to another divers kinds of tongues; to another the interpretation of tongues:
ct: One person receives the power to perform miracles, another the gift of prophecy, another the ability to tell the difference between spirits, another the ability to speak in different tongues, and another the ability to interpret those tongues.

kjv: But all these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit, dividing to every man severally as he will.
ct: But one and the same Spirit produces all of these gifts, distributing them to each person individually, exactly as he chooses.

kjv: For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also is Christ.
ct: A human body is a single unit, even though it has many parts. All those different parts still make up one body. It's the same way with Christ.

kjv: For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body, whether we be Jews or Gentiles, whether we be bond or free; and have been all made to drink into one Spirit.
ct: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body — whether Jewish or Greek, slave or free — and we were all given the same Spirit to drink.

kjv: For the body is not one member, but many.
ct: After all, a body isn't made up of just one part. It has many.

kjv: If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
ct: If the foot said, "I'm not a hand, so I don't belong to the body," that wouldn't actually make it any less a part of the body.

kjv: And if the ear shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
ct: If the ear said, "I'm not an eye, so I don't belong to the body," that wouldn't make it any less a part of the body either.

kjv: If the whole body were an eye, where were the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where were the smelling?
ct: If the whole body were an eye, how would you hear anything? If the whole body were an ear, how would you smell anything?

kjv: But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.
ct: But God placed each part in the body exactly where he wanted it.

kjv: And if they were all one member, where were the body?
ct: If everything were the same part, there wouldn't be a body at all!

kjv: But now are they many members, yet but one body.
ct: The reality is that there are many parts, but they form one body.

kjv: And the eye cannot say unto the hand, I have no need of thee: nor again the head to the feet, I have no need of you.
ct: The eye can't tell the hand, "I don't need you." The head can't tell the feet, "I don't need you."

kjv: Nay, much more those members of the body, which seem to be more feeble, are necessary:
ct: In fact, the parts of the body that seem the weakest turn out to be the most essential.

kjv: And those members of the body, which we think to be less honourable, upon these we bestow more abundant honour; and our uncomely parts have more abundant comeliness.
ct: The parts we consider less dignified, we treat with extra care. The parts that aren't presentable, we cover with special modesty.

kjv: For our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to that part which lacked:
ct: Our more presentable parts don't need that kind of attention. But God designed the body so that extra honor goes to the parts that lack it.

kjv: That there should be no schism in the body; but that the members should have the same care one for another.
ct: He did this so there would be no division in the body, but instead every part would care equally for all the others.

kjv: And whether one member suffer, all the members suffer with it; or one member be honoured, all the members rejoice with it.
ct: When one part suffers, every part suffers with it. When one part is honored, every part celebrates with it.

kjv: Now ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular.
ct: Together, you are the body of Christ, and each of you is an individual part of it.

kjv: And God hath set some in the church, first apostles, secondarily prophets, thirdly teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, diversities of tongues.
ct: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracle workers, then those with gifts of healing, those who help others, those who lead, and those who speak in different tongues.

kjv: Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of miracles?
ct: Is everyone an apostle? Is everyone a prophet? Is everyone a teacher? Does everyone work miracles?

kjv: Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?
ct: Does everyone have gifts of healing? Does everyone speak in tongues? Does everyone interpret?

kjv: But covet earnestly the best gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more excellent way.
ct: Eagerly pursue the greater gifts. But now let me show you a way that surpasses them all.

kjv: Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.
ct: If I could speak every human language and even the language of angels, but didn't have love, I'd be nothing more than a loud gong or a clanging cymbal.

kjv: And though I have the gift of prophecy, and understand all mysteries, and all knowledge; and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.
ct: If I had the gift of prophecy and understood every mystery and possessed all knowledge — if I had faith powerful enough to move mountains — but didn't have love, I would be nothing.

kjv: And though I bestow all my goods to feed the poor, and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.
ct: If I gave away everything I owned to feed the poor, and even surrendered my body to be burned, but didn't have love, I would gain nothing from it.

kjv: Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,
ct: Love is patient and kind. Love doesn't envy. It doesn't brag, and it isn't proud.

kjv: Doth not behave itself unseemly, seeketh not her own, is not easily provoked, thinketh no evil;
ct: It isn't rude, it doesn't demand its own way, it isn't easily angered, and it keeps no record of wrongs.

kjv: Rejoiceth not in iniquity, but rejoiceth in the truth;
ct: Love finds no joy in injustice but celebrates the truth.

kjv: Beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.
ct: It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, and endures all things.

kjv: Charity never faileth: but whether there be prophecies, they shall fail; whether there be tongues, they shall cease; whether there be knowledge, it shall vanish away.
ct: Love never fails. Prophecies will come to an end. Speaking in tongues will stop. Knowledge will fade away.

kjv: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part.
ct: Right now, we only know part of the picture, and we only prophesy part of the picture.

kjv: But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part shall be done away.
ct: But when what is perfect finally comes, everything partial will disappear.

kjv: When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things.
ct: When I was a child, I talked like a child, I understood like a child, I thought like a child. But when I grew up, I left those childish ways behind.

kjv: For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.
ct: Right now we see only a dim reflection, like looking into a clouded mirror — but one day we'll see face to face. Right now I know only part of the truth, but one day I'll know fully, just as God already knows me fully.

kjv: And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
ct: So three things will last forever: faith, hope, and love — and the greatest of these is love.

kjv: Follow after charity, and desire spiritual gifts, but rather that ye may prophesy.
ct: Pursue love above everything else, and eagerly desire spiritual gifts — especially the gift of prophecy.

kjv: For he that speaketh in an unknown tongue speaketh not unto men, but unto God: for no man understandeth him; howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries.
ct: Here's why: when someone speaks in an unknown tongue, they aren't speaking to people — they're speaking to God. No one understands them. In the spirit, they're speaking mysteries.

kjv: But he that prophesieth speaketh unto men to edification, and exhortation, and comfort.
ct: But the person who prophesies speaks directly to people, building them up, encouraging them, and bringing them comfort.

kjv: He that speaketh in an unknown tongue edifieth himself; but he that prophesieth edifieth the church.
ct: Speaking in an unknown tongue builds up the speaker, but prophesying builds up the whole church.

kjv: I would that ye all spake with tongues, but rather that ye prophesied: for greater is he that prophesieth than he that speaketh with tongues, except he interpret, that the church may receive edifying.
ct: I wish all of you could speak in tongues, but I'd much rather you prophesied. The person who prophesies does something greater than the one who speaks in tongues — unless that person also interprets what they've said, so the church can be built up.

kjv: Now, brethren, if I come unto you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, except I shall speak to you either by revelation, or by knowledge, or by prophesying, or by doctrine?
ct: Think about it, brothers and sisters: if I came to you speaking in tongues, what good would that do you? None — unless I also brought you a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching you could understand.

kjv: And even things without life giving sound, whether pipe or harp, except they give a distinction in the sounds, how shall it be known what is piped or harped?
ct: Consider even lifeless instruments like a flute or a harp. If they don't produce distinct notes, how will anyone recognize the melody being played?

kjv: For if the trumpet give an uncertain sound, who shall prepare himself to the battle?
ct: And if a trumpet blasts an unclear signal, who's going to get ready for battle?

kjv: So likewise ye, except ye utter by the tongue words easy to be understood, how shall it be known what is spoken? for ye shall speak into the air.
ct: It's the same with you. If the words coming out of your mouth aren't easy to understand, how will anyone know what you're saying? You'll just be talking into thin air.

kjv: There are, it may be, so many kinds of voices in the world, and none of them is without signification.
ct: There are countless languages in the world, and every one of them carries meaning.

kjv: Therefore if I know not the meaning of the voice, I shall be unto him that speaketh a barbarian, and he that speaketh shall be a barbarian unto me.
ct: But if I don't understand someone's language, I'm a foreigner to them, and they're a foreigner to me.

kjv: Even so ye, forasmuch as ye are zealous of spiritual gifts, seek that ye may excel to the edifying of the church.
ct: Since you're so eager for spiritual gifts, focus on the ones that build up the church — and strive to overflow with those.

kjv: Wherefore let him that speaketh in an unknown tongue pray that he may interpret.
ct: So anyone who speaks in an unknown tongue should pray for the ability to interpret what they've said.

kjv: For if I pray in an unknown tongue, my spirit prayeth, but my understanding is unfruitful.
ct: Because if I pray in an unknown tongue, my spirit is praying, but my mind gets nothing out of it.

kjv: What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also: I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also.
ct: So what should I do? I'll pray with my spirit, and I'll also pray with my mind. I'll sing with my spirit, and I'll also sing with my mind.

kjv: Else when thou shalt bless with the spirit, how shall he that occupieth the room of the unlearned say Amen at thy giving of thanks, seeing he understandeth not what thou sayest?
ct: Otherwise, if you're praising God only in the spirit, how can an outsider who doesn't understand say "Amen" to your prayer of thanks? They have no idea what you just said.

kjv: For thou verily givest thanks well, but the other is not edified.
ct: You might be giving a beautiful prayer of thanks, but the other person isn't being built up by it at all.

kjv: I thank my God, I speak with tongues more than ye all:
ct: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than any of you.

kjv: Yet in the church I had rather speak five words with my understanding, that by my voice I might teach others also, than ten thousand words in an unknown tongue.
ct: But in a church gathering, I'd rather speak 5 words that people can understand — so I can actually teach them something — than 10,000 words in an unknown tongue.

kjv: Brethren, be not children in understanding: howbeit in malice be ye children, but in understanding be men.
ct: Brothers and sisters, stop thinking like children about this. When it comes to evil, be as innocent as babies — but in your thinking, be mature adults.

kjv: In the law it is written, With men of other tongues and other lips will I speak unto this people; and yet for all that will they not hear me, saith the LORD.
ct: It's written in the Law: "Through people speaking foreign languages and through the lips of strangers I will speak to this people, and even then they won't listen to me," says the LORD.

kjv: Wherefore tongues are for a sign, not to them that believe, but to them that believe not: but prophesying serveth not for them that believe not, but for them which believe.
ct: So tongues serve as a sign not for believers, but for unbelievers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is a sign not for unbelievers, but for believers.

kjv: If therefore the whole church be come together into one place, and all speak with tongues, and there come in those that are unlearned, or unbelievers, will they not say that ye are mad?
ct: Picture this: the whole church gathers together and everyone starts speaking in tongues. If outsiders or people who don't understand walk in, won't they think you've all lost your minds?

kjv: But if all prophesy, and there come in one that believeth not, or one unlearned, he is convinced of all, he is judged of all:
ct: But if everyone is prophesying and an unbeliever or outsider walks in, they'll be convicted by what they hear. Every word will pierce their conscience.

kjv: And thus are the secrets of his heart made manifest; and so falling down on his face he will worship God, and report that God is in you of a truth.
ct: The hidden things in their heart will be exposed. They'll fall facedown and worship God, declaring, "God is truly here among you!"

kjv: How is it then, brethren? when ye come together, every one of you hath a psalm, hath a doctrine, hath a tongue, hath a revelation, hath an interpretation. Let all things be done unto edifying.
ct: So here's the point, brothers and sisters: when you gather together, one person has a psalm, another has a teaching, another has a revelation, another has a message in tongues, and another has an interpretation. Everything you do should build each other up.

kjv: If any man speak in an unknown tongue, let it be by two, or at the most by three, and that by course; and let one interpret.
ct: If anyone speaks in a tongue, limit it to 2 or at most 3 people, and they should take turns. Someone must interpret.

kjv: But if there be no interpreter, let him keep silence in the church; and let him speak to himself, and to God.
ct: If no interpreter is present, the tongue-speaker should stay silent in the church and speak only to themselves and to God.

kjv: Let the prophets speak two or three, and let the other judge.
ct: Let 2 or 3 prophets speak, and let the others carefully evaluate what is said.

kjv: If any thing be revealed to another that sitteth by, let the first hold his peace.
ct: If someone else sitting there receives a revelation, the first speaker should stop and let them share.

kjv: For ye may all prophesy one by one, that all may learn, and all may be comforted.
ct: You can all prophesy one at a time, so that everyone learns and everyone is encouraged.

kjv: And the spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets.
ct: The spirits of prophets are under the prophets' own control.

kjv: For God is not the author of confusion, but of peace, as in all churches of the saints.
ct: God is not a God of chaos but of peace — and that's how it should be in every church of the saints.

kjv: Let your women keep silence in the churches: for it is not permitted unto them to speak; but they are commanded to be under obedience, as also saith the law.
ct: Women should remain silent in the churches. They aren't permitted to speak but should be in submission, as the Law also says.

kjv: And if they will learn any thing, let them ask their husbands at home: for it is a shame for women to speak in the church.
ct: If they want to learn about something, they should ask their own husbands at home. It's considered shameful for a woman to speak in the church.

kjv: What? came the word of God out from you? or came it unto you only?
ct: What! Did the word of God originate with you? Or are you the only ones it reached?

kjv: If any man think himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him acknowledge that the things that I write unto you are the commandments of the Lord.
ct: If anyone considers themselves a prophet or spiritually gifted, they should recognize that what I'm writing to you is a commandment from the Lord.

kjv: But if any man be ignorant, let him be ignorant.
ct: If anyone chooses to ignore this, let them be ignored.

kjv: Wherefore, brethren, covet to prophesy, and forbid not to speak with tongues.
ct: So, brothers and sisters, eagerly desire the gift of prophecy, and don't forbid speaking in tongues.

kjv: Let all things be done decently and in order.
ct: But make sure everything is done properly and in an orderly way.

kjv: Moreover, brethren, I declare unto you the gospel which I preached unto you, which also ye have received, and wherein ye stand;
ct: Brothers, I want to remind you of the good news I preached to you — the message you received and the foundation on which you now stand.

kjv: By which also ye are saved, if ye keep in memory what I preached unto you, unless ye have believed in vain.
ct: This is the message that saves you, if you hold firmly to what I told you. Otherwise, your faith was for nothing.

kjv: For I delivered unto you first of all that which I also received, how that Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures;
ct: What I passed on to you was the most important thing I myself had received: that Christ died for our sins, just as the Scriptures said he would,

kjv: And that he was buried, and that he rose again the third day according to the scriptures:
ct: that he was buried, and that he was raised on the 3rd day, again just as the Scriptures said.

kjv: And that he was seen of Cephas, then of the twelve:
ct: After that, he appeared to Cephas, and then to the 12 apostles.

kjv: After that, he was seen of above five hundred brethren at once; of whom the greater part remain unto this present, but some are fallen asleep.
ct: Then he appeared to more than 500 believers at the same time. Most of them are still alive today, though some have died.

kjv: After that, he was seen of James; then of all the apostles.
ct: After that, he appeared to James, and then to all the apostles.

kjv: And last of all he was seen of me also, as of one born out of due time.
ct: Last of all, he appeared to me — like someone born at the wrong time.

kjv: For I am the least of the apostles, that am not meet to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
ct: I'm the least of the apostles. I don't even deserve to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.

kjv: But by the grace of God I am what I am: and his grace which was bestowed upon me was not in vain; but I laboured more abundantly than they all: yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.
ct: But by the grace of God, I am what I am. And his grace toward me wasn't wasted — I've worked harder than all the other apostles. Yet it wasn't really me doing the work; it was God's grace working through me.

kjv: Therefore whether it were I or they, so we preach, and so ye believed.
ct: So whether it was me or the other apostles who told you, this is what we all preach, and this is what you believed.

kjv: Now if Christ be preached that he rose from the dead, how say some among you that there is no resurrection of the dead?
ct: Now if we preach that Christ has been raised from the dead, how can some of you say there's no such thing as resurrection?

kjv: But if there be no resurrection of the dead, then is Christ not risen:
ct: If there's no resurrection of the dead, then not even Christ has been raised.

kjv: And if Christ be not risen, then is our preaching vain, and your faith is also vain.
ct: And if Christ hasn't been raised, then everything we preach is meaningless, and your faith is meaningless too.

kjv: Yea, and we are found false witnesses of God; because we have testified of God that he raised up Christ: whom he raised not up, if so be that the dead rise not.
ct: Worse than that, we'd be liars about God, because we testified that God raised Christ from the dead. But if the dead aren't raised, then God didn't raise him.

kjv: For if the dead rise not, then is not Christ raised:
ct: Because if the dead aren't raised, then Christ hasn't been raised either.

kjv: And if Christ be not raised, your faith is vain; ye are yet in your sins.
ct: And if Christ hasn't been raised, your faith is useless — you're still trapped in your sins.

kjv: Then they also which are fallen asleep in Christ are perished.
ct: And it would mean that everyone who died believing in Christ is lost forever.

kjv: If in this life only we have hope in Christ, we are of all men most miserable.
ct: If our hope in Christ is only for this life, we are the most pitiful people on earth.

kjv: But now is Christ risen from the dead, and become the firstfruits of them that slept.
ct: But the truth is, Christ has been raised from the dead — the first of a great harvest among those who have died.

kjv: For since by man came death, by man came also the resurrection of the dead.
ct: Since death came into the world through one man, the resurrection of the dead also comes through one man.

kjv: For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.
ct: Just as everyone dies because of Adam, everyone will be made alive because of Christ.

kjv: But every man in his own order: Christ the firstfruits; afterward they that are Christ’s at his coming.
ct: But there's an order to it: Christ was raised first, and then when he returns, those who belong to him will be raised.

kjv: Then cometh the end, when he shall have delivered up the kingdom to God, even the Father; when he shall have put down all rule and all authority and power.
ct: After that comes the end, when Christ hands the kingdom over to God the Father, after he has destroyed every ruler, every authority, and every power.

kjv: For he must reign, till he hath put all enemies under his feet.
ct: Because Christ must reign until he has put every enemy under his feet.

kjv: The last enemy that shall be destroyed is death.
ct: The last enemy to be destroyed is death itself.

kjv: For he hath put all things under his feet. But when he saith all things are put under him, it is manifest that he is excepted, which did put all things under him.
ct: For Scripture says, "He has put all things under his feet." Now when it says "all things" are under him, it's obvious that God himself — the one who put everything under Christ — is not included in that.

kjv: And when all things shall be subdued unto him, then shall the Son also himself be subject unto him that put all things under him, that God may be all in all.
ct: And when everything has been placed under Christ's authority, then the Son himself will submit to God, who gave him authority over all things. Then God will be all in all.

kjv: Else what shall they do which are baptized for the dead, if the dead rise not at all? why are they then baptized for the dead?
ct: Otherwise, what will people who are baptized for the dead accomplish? If the dead aren't raised at all, why would anyone be baptized on their behalf?

kjv: And why stand we in jeopardy every hour?
ct: And why would we risk our lives every single hour?

kjv: I protest by your rejoicing which I have in Christ Jesus our LORD, I die daily.
ct: Brothers, I face death every day — I swear it by the pride I have in you through Christ Jesus our Lord.

kjv: If after the manner of men I have fought with beasts at Ephesus, what advantageth it me, if the dead rise not? let us eat and drink; for to morrow we die.
ct: If I fought wild beasts in Ephesus just for human reasons, what did I gain? If the dead aren't raised, then we might as well say, "Let's eat and drink, because tomorrow we die."

kjv: Be not deceived: evil communications corrupt good manners.
ct: Don't be fooled: "Bad company corrupts good character."

kjv: Awake to righteousness, and sin not; for some have not the knowledge of God: I speak this to your shame.
ct: Come to your senses and stop sinning. Some of you don't even know God — I say that to your shame.

kjv: But some man will say, How are the dead raised up? and with what body do they come?
ct: But someone will ask, "How exactly are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have?"

kjv: Thou fool, that which thou sowest is not quickened, except it die:
ct: What a foolish question! When you plant a seed, it doesn't sprout to life unless it first dies in the ground.

kjv: And that which thou sowest, thou sowest not that body that shall be, but bare grain, it may chance of wheat, or of some other grain:
ct: And what you plant isn't the full plant that will grow — it's just a bare seed, maybe wheat or something else.

kjv: But God giveth it a body as it hath pleased him, and to every seed his own body.
ct: But God gives it whatever body he chooses, and he gives each kind of seed its own unique body.

kjv: All flesh is not the same flesh: but there is one kind of flesh of men, another flesh of beasts, another of fishes, and another of birds.
ct: Not all flesh is the same. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another, fish have another, and birds have yet another.

kjv: There are also celestial bodies, and bodies terrestrial: but the glory of the celestial is one, and the glory of the terrestrial is another.
ct: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies, but the glory of the heavenly is completely different from the glory of the earthly.

kjv: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars: for one star differeth from another star in glory.
ct: The sun has its own glory, the moon has a different glory, and the stars have still another. Even among stars, each one differs from the others in glory.

kjv: So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption:
ct: That's exactly how it is with the resurrection of the dead. The body that's buried is perishable; the body that's raised is imperishable.

kjv: It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in glory: it is sown in weakness; it is raised in power:
ct: It's buried in dishonor; it's raised in glory. It's buried in weakness; it's raised in power.

kjv: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body, and there is a spiritual body.
ct: It's buried as a natural body; it's raised as a spiritual body. If there's a natural body, there's also a spiritual body.

kjv: And so it is written, The first man Adam was made a living soul; the last Adam was made a quickening spirit.
ct: As Scripture says, "The first man, Adam, became a living soul." But the last Adam — Christ — became a life-giving spirit.

kjv: Howbeit that was not first which is spiritual, but that which is natural; and afterward that which is spiritual.
ct: The spiritual didn't come first. The natural came first, and then the spiritual.

kjv: The first man is of the earth, earthy: the second man is the Lord from heaven.
ct: The first man was made from the dust of the earth. The second man is the Lord from heaven.

kjv: As is the earthy, such are they also that are earthy: and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.
ct: Those who are made of dust are like the man of dust, and those who are heavenly are like the man from heaven.

kjv: And as we have borne the image of the earthy, we shall also bear the image of the heavenly.
ct: Just as we have carried the image of the man made from dust, we will also carry the image of the man from heaven.

kjv: Now this I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God; neither doth corruption inherit incorruption.
ct: Here's what I'm telling you, brothers: flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, and what is perishable cannot inherit what is imperishable.

kjv: Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed,
ct: Listen — I'm revealing a mystery to you: We won't all die, but we will all be transformed,

kjv: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump: for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.
ct: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the sound of the last trumpet. The trumpet will blast, the dead will be raised imperishable, and we will be transformed.

kjv: For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.
ct: Because this perishable body must be clothed with the imperishable, and this mortal body must be clothed with immortality.

kjv: So when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.
ct: When this perishable body has been clothed with the imperishable, and this mortal body has been clothed with immortality, then the Scripture will come true: "Death is swallowed up in victory."

kjv: O death, where is thy sting? O grave, where is thy victory?
ct: "Death, where is your sting? Grave, where is your victory?"

kjv: The sting of death is sin; and the strength of sin is the law.
ct: The sting of death is sin, and sin gets its power from the law.

kjv: But thanks be to God, which giveth us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
ct: But thank God! He gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.

kjv: Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye stedfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the Lord.
ct: So then, my dear brothers, stand firm. Don't let anything move you. Always give yourselves fully to the Lord's work, because you know that nothing you do for the Lord is ever wasted.

kjv: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, unto the church of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints which are in all Achaia:
ct: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and from Timothy our brother — to the church of God in Corinth, along with all the saints throughout Achaia:

kjv: Grace be to you and peace from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
ct: May grace and peace be yours from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

kjv: Blessed be God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies, and the God of all comfort;
ct: Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! He is the Father of mercy and the God of all comfort.

kjv: Who comforteth us in all our tribulation, that we may be able to comfort them which are in any trouble, by the comfort wherewith we ourselves are comforted of God.
ct: He comforts us in every hardship we face, so that we can comfort others going through any kind of trouble — using the same comfort God has given us.

kjv: For as the sufferings of Christ abound in us, so our consolation also aboundeth by Christ.
ct: The more we share in the sufferings of Christ, the more his comfort overflows into our lives.

kjv: And whether we be afflicted, it is for your consolation and salvation, which is effectual in the enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer: or whether we be comforted, it is for your consolation and salvation.
ct: When we suffer, it's for your comfort and salvation — and that salvation proves its power as you endure the same kinds of suffering we go through. When we're comforted, that's for your comfort and salvation too.

kjv: And our hope of you is stedfast, knowing, that as ye are partakers of the sufferings, so shall ye be also of the consolation.
ct: Our hope for you is rock-solid, because we know that just as you share in our sufferings, you'll also share in our comfort.

kjv: For we would not, brethren, have you ignorant of our trouble which came to us in Asia, that we were pressed out of measure, above strength, insomuch that we despaired even of life:
ct: Brothers and sisters, we want you to know about the hardship we faced in Asia. We were crushed beyond what we could bear — so completely overwhelmed that we didn't think we'd survive.

kjv: But we had the sentence of death in ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God which raiseth the dead:
ct: In fact, we felt like we'd already been handed a death sentence. But this happened so we would stop relying on ourselves and start trusting in God, who raises the dead.

kjv: Who delivered us from so great a death, and doth deliver: in whom we trust that he will yet deliver us;
ct: He rescued us from a deadly threat, and he continues to rescue us. We've placed our hope in him that he will keep rescuing us in the future.

kjv: Ye also helping together by prayer for us, that for the gift bestowed upon us by the means of many persons thanks may be given by many on our behalf.
ct: And you're helping us by praying for us! That way, when God answers the prayers of so many people on our behalf, many will give thanks to him for the gift he's given us.

kjv: For our rejoicing is this, the testimony of our conscience, that in simplicity and godly sincerity, not with fleshly wisdom, but by the grace of God, we have had our conversation in the world, and more abundantly to you-ward.
ct: Here's what we're proud of, and our conscience backs it up: we've conducted ourselves in this world — and especially toward you — with the holiness and sincerity that come from God, not with human cleverness, but through God's grace.

kjv: For we write none other things unto you, than what ye read or acknowledge; and I trust ye shall acknowledge even to the end;
ct: We aren't writing anything to you that you can't read and understand. I'm confident you'll fully grasp what we mean,

kjv: As also ye have acknowledged us in part, that we are your rejoicing, even as ye also are ours in the day of the Lord Jesus.
ct: just as you've already partly understood that we are your reason to be proud, and you are ours — on the day of the Lord Jesus.

kjv: And in this confidence I was minded to come unto you before, that ye might have a second benefit;
ct: With that confidence, I originally planned to visit you first so you could benefit from seeing me twice.

kjv: And to pass by you into Macedonia, and to come again out of Macedonia unto you, and of you to be brought on my way toward Judaea.
ct: My plan was to visit you on my way to Macedonia, then come back to you from Macedonia, and have you send me on my way to Judaea.

kjv: When I therefore was thus minded, did I use lightness? or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be yea yea, and nay nay?
ct: So when I made those plans, was I being flippant about it? Do I make my decisions the way the world does — saying 'Yes, yes' and 'No, no' at the same time?

kjv: But as God is true, our word toward you was not yea and nay.
ct: As surely as God is faithful, our message to you has never been 'yes' and 'no' at the same time.

kjv: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, even by me and Silvanus and Timotheus, was not yea and nay, but in him was yea.
ct: The Son of God, Jesus Christ — the one Silvanus, Timothy, and I proclaimed to you — was never 'yes' and 'no.' In him, it has always been 'Yes.'

kjv: For all the promises of God in him are yea, and in him Amen, unto the glory of God by us.
ct: Every single promise God has ever made finds its 'Yes' in Christ. That's why we say 'Amen' through him, bringing glory to God.

kjv: Now he which stablisheth us with you in Christ, and hath anointed us, is God;
ct: It is God who makes both us and you stand firm in Christ. He is the one who anointed us,

kjv: Who hath also sealed us, and given the earnest of the Spirit in our hearts.
ct: placed his seal of ownership on us, and put his Holy Spirit in our hearts as a guarantee of what's to come.

kjv: Moreover I call God for a record upon my soul, that to spare you I came not as yet unto Corinth.
ct: I call God as my witness — I stake my soul on it — the reason I haven't come to Corinth yet is that I wanted to spare you.

kjv: Not for that we have dominion over your faith, but are helpers of your joy: for by faith ye stand.
ct: We're not trying to control your faith. Instead, we're working alongside you for your joy, because you already stand firm in faith.

kjv: But I determined this with myself, that I would not come again to you in heaviness.
ct: I made up my mind that I wouldn't visit you again if it meant bringing more pain.

kjv: For if I make you sorry, who is he then that maketh me glad, but the same which is made sorry by me?
ct: After all, if I cause you grief, who's left to bring me joy? Only the very people I've hurt.

kjv: And I wrote this same unto you, lest, when I came, I should have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having confidence in you all, that my joy is the joy of you all.
ct: That's exactly why I wrote to you instead — so that when I did come, I wouldn't be heartbroken by the people who should be giving me joy. I'm confident that all of you feel the same way: when I'm happy, you're happy too.

kjv: For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote unto you with many tears; not that ye should be grieved, but that ye might know the love which I have more abundantly unto you.
ct: I wrote that letter through deep distress and a heavy heart, with tears streaming down my face. My goal wasn't to hurt you — it was to show you just how much I love you.

kjv: But if any have caused grief, he hath not grieved me, but in part: that I may not overcharge you all.
ct: Now, if someone among you caused pain, he didn't just hurt me — he hurt all of you to some degree. I say "to some degree" because I don't want to be too harsh about it.

kjv: Sufficient to such a man is this punishment, which was inflicted of many.
ct: The punishment the majority of you imposed on this person has been enough.

kjv: So that contrariwise ye ought rather to forgive him, and comfort him, lest perhaps such a one should be swallowed up with overmuch sorrow.
ct: So now it's time to do the opposite — forgive him and encourage him. Otherwise, he could be crushed by too much grief.

kjv: Wherefore I beseech you that ye would confirm your love toward him.
ct: I'm urging you: show him that your love for him is real.

kjv: For to this end also did I write, that I might know the proof of you, whether ye be obedient in all things.
ct: This was actually one of the reasons I wrote — to test you and see whether you'd follow through and be obedient in every way.

kjv: To whom ye forgive any thing, I forgive also: for if I forgave any thing, to whom I forgave it, for your sakes forgave I it in the person of Christ;
ct: When you forgive someone, I forgive them too. And whatever I've forgiven — if there was anything to forgive — I did it for your sake, in the presence of Christ.

kjv: Lest Satan should get an advantage of us: for we are not ignorant of his devices.
ct: We can't let Satan outmaneuver us. We know exactly how he operates.

kjv: Furthermore, when I came to Troas to preach Christ’s gospel, and a door was opened unto me of the Lord,
ct: When I arrived in Troas to preach the good news about Christ, the Lord opened a door of opportunity for me there.

kjv: I had no rest in my spirit, because I found not Titus my brother: but taking my leave of them, I went from thence into Macedonia.
ct: But my spirit couldn't find rest because my brother Titus wasn't there. So I said goodbye to the believers and headed on to Macedonia.

kjv: Now thanks be unto God, which always causeth us to triumph in Christ, and maketh manifest the savour of his knowledge by us in every place.
ct: But thank God! He always leads us in triumphant procession through Christ, and through us he spreads the fragrance of knowing him everywhere we go.

kjv: For we are unto God a sweet savour of Christ, in them that are saved, and in them that perish:
ct: To God, we are the sweet aroma of Christ — both among those who are being saved and among those who are perishing.

kjv: To the one we are the savour of death unto death; and to the other the savour of life unto life. And who is sufficient for these things?
ct: To those who are perishing, we smell like death leading to death. But to those being saved, we are the fragrance of life leading to life. And who is up to a task like that?

kjv: For we are not as many, which corrupt the word of God: but as of sincerity, but as of God, in the sight of God speak we in Christ.
ct: Unlike so many others, we don't peddle God's word for profit. Instead, we speak in Christ with complete sincerity — as people sent by God, standing in his presence.

kjv: Therefore seeing we have this ministry, as we have received mercy, we faint not;
ct: Since God has given us this ministry out of his mercy, we don't lose heart.

kjv: But have renounced the hidden things of dishonesty, not walking in craftiness, nor handling the word of God deceitfully; but by manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every man’s conscience in the sight of God.
ct: We've rejected every shameful, underhanded method. We don't use tricks, and we don't twist the word of God. Instead, by presenting the truth plainly, we let every person's conscience judge us in the sight of God.

kjv: But if our gospel be hid, it is hid to them that are lost:
ct: If our gospel is hidden from anyone, it's hidden from those who are perishing.

kjv: In whom the god of this world hath blinded the minds of them which believe not, lest the light of the glorious gospel of Christ, who is the image of God, should shine unto them.
ct: The god of this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers so that they can't see the light of the glorious gospel of Christ, who is the image of God.

kjv: For we preach not ourselves, but Christ Jesus the Lord; and ourselves your servants for Jesus’ sake.
ct: We don't preach about ourselves — we preach Christ Jesus as Lord. We're simply your servants for Jesus' sake.

kjv: For God, who commanded the light to shine out of darkness, hath shined in our hearts, to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.
ct: The same God who said, "Let light shine out of darkness," has made his light shine in our hearts, giving us the light of knowing God's glory as it shines in the face of Jesus Christ.

kjv: But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the power may be of God, and not of us.
ct: But we carry this treasure in clay jars, so that everyone can see the extraordinary power belongs to God and doesn't come from us.

kjv: We are troubled on every side, yet not distressed; we are perplexed, but not in despair;
ct: We're pressed hard on every side, but not crushed. We're confused, but not driven to despair.

kjv: Persecuted, but not forsaken; cast down, but not destroyed;
ct: We're persecuted, but never abandoned. We're knocked down, but never destroyed.

kjv: Always bearing about in the body the dying of the Lord Jesus, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our body.
ct: We always carry the death of the Lord Jesus in our bodies, so that the life of Jesus can also be seen in us.

kjv: For we which live are alway delivered unto death for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our mortal flesh.
ct: As long as we live, we're constantly being handed over to death for Jesus' sake, so that the life of Jesus becomes visible in our mortal bodies.

kjv: So then death worketh in us, but life in you.
ct: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.

kjv: We having the same spirit of faith, according as it is written, I believed, and therefore have I spoken; we also believe, and therefore speak;
ct: We share the same spirit of faith described in Scripture: "I believed, and so I spoke." We also believe, and that's why we speak.

kjv: Knowing that he which raised up the Lord Jesus shall raise up us also by Jesus, and shall present us with you.
ct: We know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus from the dead will raise us too along with Jesus, and will bring us together with you into his presence.

kjv: For all things are for your sakes, that the abundant grace might through the thanksgiving of many redound to the glory of God.
ct: All of this is for your benefit, so that as grace reaches more and more people, it will overflow into an ever-growing flood of thanksgiving that brings glory to God.

kjv: For which cause we faint not; but though our outward man perish, yet the inward man is renewed day by day.
ct: That's why we don't lose heart. Even though our outer selves are wasting away, our inner selves are being renewed day by day.

kjv: For our light affliction, which is but for a moment, worketh for us a far more exceeding and eternal weight of glory;
ct: Our present troubles are small and temporary, but they're producing for us a glory that is eternal and far outweighs them all.

kjv: While we look not at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen: for the things which are seen are temporal; but the things which are not seen are eternal.
ct: So we fix our eyes not on what we can see, but on what we can't see. What we can see is temporary, but what we can't see is eternal.

kjv: For we know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
ct: We know that when this earthly body — our temporary tent — is destroyed, God has a permanent home waiting for us in the heavens, a dwelling not built by human hands but lasting forever.

kjv: For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be clothed upon with our house which is from heaven:
ct: While we live in this body, we groan with longing, aching to be clothed with our heavenly dwelling.

kjv: If so be that being clothed we shall not be found naked.
ct: Because once we are clothed with it, we won't be left exposed and naked.

kjv: For we that are in this tabernacle do groan, being burdened: not for that we would be unclothed, but clothed upon, that mortality might be swallowed up of life.
ct: While we're still living in this tent, we groan under its weight — not because we want to be stripped of our body, but because we want to be covered with our new one, so that what is mortal gets swallowed up by life.

kjv: Now he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing is God, who also hath given unto us the earnest of the Spirit.
ct: God himself is the one who shaped us for this very purpose, and he gave us the Holy Spirit as a down payment guaranteeing what's to come.

kjv: Therefore we are always confident, knowing that, whilst we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord:
ct: So we live with constant confidence, knowing that as long as we're at home in this body, we are away from the Lord.

kjv: (For we walk by faith, not by sight:)
ct: After all, we walk by faith, not by sight.

kjv: We are confident, I say, and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.
ct: We are confident, and we would actually prefer to leave this body and be at home with the Lord.

kjv: Wherefore we labour, that, whether present or absent, we may be accepted of him.
ct: That's why we make it our goal — whether we're still in this body or away from it — to please him.

kjv: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, according to that he hath done, whether it be good or bad.
ct: Every one of us must stand before the judgment seat of Christ, where each person will receive what they deserve for what they did while living in their body — whether good or bad.

kjv: Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord, we persuade men; but we are made manifest unto God; and I trust also are made manifest in your consciences.
ct: Because we understand the fear of the Lord, we try to persuade people. God sees us for who we truly are, and I hope your consciences recognize that too.

kjv: For we commend not ourselves again unto you, but give you occasion to glory on our behalf, that ye may have somewhat to answer them which glory in appearance, and not in heart.
ct: We're not trying to promote ourselves to you again. Instead, we're giving you a reason to be proud of us, so you'll have an answer for those who brag about outward appearances rather than what's in the heart.

kjv: For whether we be beside ourselves, it is to God: or whether we be sober, it is for your cause.
ct: If we seem out of our minds, it's for God's sake. If we're thinking clearly, it's for your benefit.

kjv: For the love of Christ constraineth us; because we thus judge, that if one died for all, then were all dead:
ct: The love of Christ drives everything we do. Here's what we've concluded: one man died for everyone, which means everyone had died.

kjv: And that he died for all, that they which live should not henceforth live unto themselves, but unto him which died for them, and rose again.
ct: He died for all so that those who are alive would stop living for themselves and start living for him — the one who died for them and rose again.

kjv: Wherefore henceforth know we no man after the flesh: yea, though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now henceforth know we him no more.
ct: From now on, we don't evaluate anyone by human standards. Even though we once viewed Christ that way, we no longer see him like that.

kjv: Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.
ct: So if anyone is in Christ, they are a new creation. The old life is gone — look, everything has become new!

kjv: And all things are of God, who hath reconciled us to himself by Jesus Christ, and hath given to us the ministry of reconciliation;
ct: All of this comes from God, who brought us back to himself through Jesus Christ and then gave us the mission of bringing others back to him too.

kjv: To wit, that God was in Christ, reconciling the world unto himself, not imputing their trespasses unto them; and hath committed unto us the word of reconciliation.
ct: Here's the heart of it: God was in Christ, reconciling the world to himself, choosing not to count people's sins against them. And he entrusted us with this message of reconciliation.

kjv: Now then we are ambassadors for Christ, as though God did beseech you by us: we pray you in Christ’s stead, be ye reconciled to God.
ct: So we serve as ambassadors for Christ, as if God were making his appeal through us. On behalf of Christ, we beg you: be reconciled to God.

kjv: For he hath made him to be sin for us, who knew no sin; that we might be made the righteousness of God in him.
ct: God took the one who never sinned and made him become sin for us, so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.

kjv: We then, as workers together with him, beseech you also that ye receive not the grace of God in vain.
ct: As God's coworkers, we urge you: don't let the grace God has given you be wasted.

kjv: (For he saith, I have heard thee in a time accepted, and in the day of salvation have I succoured thee: behold, now is the accepted time; behold, now is the day of salvation.)
ct: For God says, "At just the right time, I heard you. On the day of salvation, I helped you." Listen — right now is the right time. Today is the day of salvation.

kjv: Giving no offence in any thing, that the ministry be not blamed:
ct: We do everything we can to avoid giving anyone a reason to find fault with our ministry.

kjv: But in all things approving ourselves as the ministers of God, in much patience, in afflictions, in necessities, in distresses,
ct: Instead, in every situation we prove ourselves to be true servants of God — through great endurance, through afflictions, hardships, and desperate circumstances,

kjv: In stripes, in imprisonments, in tumults, in labours, in watchings, in fastings;
ct: through beatings, imprisonments, and riots, through exhausting labor, sleepless nights, and hunger,

kjv: By pureness, by knowledge, by longsuffering, by kindness, by the Holy Ghost, by love unfeigned,
ct: through purity, knowledge, patience, and kindness, through the Holy Spirit and sincere love,

kjv: By the word of truth, by the power of God, by the armour of righteousness on the right hand and on the left,
ct: through the word of truth and the power of God, wielding the armor of righteousness in both hands — ready for any attack,

kjv: By honour and dishonour, by evil report and good report: as deceivers, and yet true;
ct: whether we're honored or disgraced, whether people slander us or praise us. Some call us frauds, but we speak the truth.

kjv: As unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and, behold, we live; as chastened, and not killed;
ct: We're treated as nobodies, yet everyone knows us. We face death constantly, and yet — look — we're still alive. We're beaten down but never destroyed.

kjv: As sorrowful, yet alway rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, and yet possessing all things.
ct: We carry deep sorrow, yet we're always filled with joy. We're poor, yet we make many people rich. We have nothing, yet we possess everything.

kjv: O ye Corinthians, our mouth is open unto you, our heart is enlarged.
ct: Corinthians, we've spoken to you with complete openness. We've thrown our hearts wide open to you.

kjv: Ye are not straitened in us, but ye are straitened in your own bowels.
ct: We haven't held anything back from you — but you've held back from us. The walls are on your side, not ours.

kjv: Now for a recompence in the same, (I speak as unto my children,) be ye also enlarged.
ct: So as a fair exchange — and I'm talking to you like a father talks to his children — open your hearts wide to us too.

kjv: Be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers: for what fellowship hath righteousness with unrighteousness? and what communion hath light with darkness?
ct: Don't tie yourselves to unbelievers in binding partnerships. What does righteousness have in common with wickedness? What does light have to do with darkness?

kjv: And what concord hath Christ with Belial? or what part hath he that believeth with an infidel?
ct: What agreement could Christ possibly have with Belial? What does a believer share with an unbeliever?

kjv: And what agreement hath the temple of God with idols? for ye are the temple of the living God; as God hath said, I will dwell in them, and walk in them; and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.
ct: How can the temple of God coexist with idols? Because you are the temple of the living God! As God himself said, "I will live in them and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people."

kjv: Wherefore come out from among them, and be ye separate, saith the Lord, and touch not the unclean thing; and I will receive you,
ct: That's why the Lord says, "Come out from among them and be separate. Don't touch anything unclean, and I will receive you.

kjv: And will be a Father unto you, and ye shall be my sons and daughters, saith the Lord Almighty.
ct: I will be a Father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters," says the Lord Almighty.

kjv: Having therefore these promises, dearly beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God.
ct: Since God has given us these promises, dear friends, let's purify ourselves from everything that contaminates our bodies and spirits, growing in holiness out of the fear of God.

kjv: Receive us; we have wronged no man, we have corrupted no man, we have defrauded no man.
ct: Make room for us in your hearts. We haven't wronged anyone, we haven't led anyone astray, and we haven't taken advantage of anyone.

kjv: I speak not this to condemn you: for I have said before, that ye are in our hearts to die and live with you.
ct: I'm not saying this to make you feel guilty. As I've told you before, you hold such a place in our hearts that we would live or die with you.

kjv: Great is my boldness of speech toward you, great is my glorying of you: I am filled with comfort, I am exceeding joyful in all our tribulation.
ct: I can speak to you with great confidence. I'm so proud of you. I'm filled with encouragement, and even in the middle of all our hardships, my joy is overflowing.

kjv: For, when we were come into Macedonia, our flesh had no rest, but we were troubled on every side; without were fightings, within were fears.
ct: When we arrived in Macedonia, we couldn't find a moment's rest. Trouble hit us from every direction — conflicts on the outside, fears on the inside.

kjv: Nevertheless God, that comforteth those that are cast down, comforted us by the coming of Titus;
ct: But God, who comforts those who are beaten down, comforted us through the arrival of Titus.

kjv: And not by his coming only, but by the consolation wherewith he was comforted in you, when he told us your earnest desire, your mourning, your fervent mind toward me; so that I rejoiced the more.
ct: It wasn't just his arrival that lifted our spirits — it was the encouragement he had received from you. He told us how much you longed to see us, how deeply you grieved, and how passionately you cared about me. That made me even more joyful.

kjv: For though I made you sorry with a letter, I do not repent, though I did repent: for I perceive that the same epistle hath made you sorry, though it were but for a season.
ct: Even though my letter caused you pain, I don't regret sending it — though I did for a while. I can see that the letter hurt you, but only for a short time.

kjv: Now I rejoice, not that ye were made sorry, but that ye sorrowed to repentance: for ye were made sorry after a godly manner, that ye might receive damage by us in nothing.
ct: Now I'm glad — not because you were hurt, but because that pain led you to repentance. Your grief was the kind God uses, so nothing I did caused you any real harm.

kjv: For godly sorrow worketh repentance to salvation not to be repented of: but the sorrow of the world worketh death.
ct: Godly sorrow leads to repentance and salvation, and no one ever regrets that. But the kind of sorrow the world produces leads to death.

kjv: For behold this selfsame thing, that ye sorrowed after a godly sort, what carefulness it wrought in you, yea, what clearing of yourselves, yea, what indignation, yea, what fear, yea, what vehement desire, yea, what zeal, yea, what revenge! In all things ye have approved yourselves to be clear in this matter.
ct: Just look at what this godly grief produced in you: such earnestness, such eagerness to clear your name, such outrage at the wrong, such reverent fear, such deep longing, such passion, such determination to see justice done! At every point you've proven yourselves innocent in this matter.

kjv: Wherefore, though I wrote unto you, I did it not for his cause that had done the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered wrong, but that our care for you in the sight of God might appear unto you.
ct: So when I wrote to you, it wasn't really about the person who did the wrong or the person who was wronged. It was so that your genuine devotion to us would become clear to you before God.

kjv: Therefore we were comforted in your comfort: yea, and exceedingly the more joyed we for the joy of Titus, because his spirit was refreshed by you all.
ct: That's why we were so encouraged. On top of our own encouragement, we were even more thrilled by how happy Titus was, because all of you had refreshed his spirit.

kjv: For if I have boasted any thing to him of you, I am not ashamed; but as we spake all things to you in truth, even so our boasting, which I made before Titus, is found a truth.
ct: I had bragged about you to Titus, and you didn't let me down. Everything we've ever told you has been true, and what I boasted about you to Titus turned out to be true as well.

kjv: And his inward affection is more abundant toward you, whilst he remembereth the obedience of you all, how with fear and trembling ye received him.
ct: His affection for you runs deeper than ever as he remembers how obedient you all were and how you welcomed him with such fear and trembling.

kjv: I rejoice therefore that I have confidence in you in all things.
ct: It fills me with joy that I can have complete confidence in you in every way.

kjv: Moreover, brethren, we do you to wit of the grace of God bestowed on the churches of Macedonia;
ct: Brothers, we want you to know about the grace God has given to the churches in Macedonia.

kjv: How that in a great trial of affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded unto the riches of their liberality.
ct: Even though they were going through severe hardship and crushing poverty, their overflowing joy produced an incredible wave of generosity.

kjv: For to their power, I bear record, yea, and beyond their power they were willing of themselves;
ct: I can personally testify that they gave as much as they could — and then went beyond what they could afford, entirely on their own initiative.

kjv: Praying us with much intreaty that we would receive the gift, and take upon us the fellowship of the ministering to the saints.
ct: They actually begged us for the privilege of sharing in this gift for God's people.

kjv: And this they did, not as we hoped, but first gave their own selves to the Lord, and unto us by the will of God.
ct: What they did went far beyond what we expected. First, they gave themselves to the Lord, and then to us, because that was God's will.

kjv: Insomuch that we desired Titus, that as he had begun, so he would also finish in you the same grace also.
ct: So we urged Titus — since he had already started this work among you — to help you complete this same act of grace.

kjv: Therefore, as ye abound in every thing, in faith, and utterance, and knowledge, and in all diligence, and in your love to us, see that ye abound in this grace also.
ct: You already excel in so many things: in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in wholehearted dedication, and in your love for us. Now make sure you also excel in this act of generosity.

kjv: I speak not by commandment, but by occasion of the forwardness of others, and to prove the sincerity of your love.
ct: I'm not giving you an order. But by pointing to the eagerness of others, I'm giving you a chance to prove that your love is genuine.

kjv: For ye know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that ye through his poverty might be rich.
ct: You already know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ: though he was rich, he became poor for your sake, so that through his poverty you could become rich.

kjv: And herein I give my advice: for this is expedient for you, who have begun before, not only to do, but also to be forward a year ago.
ct: Here's my advice — and it's for your own benefit: a year ago, you were the first not only to start giving but also to want to give.

kjv: Now therefore perform the doing of it; that as there was a readiness to will, so there may be a performance also out of that which ye have.
ct: Now it's time to finish what you started. You were so eager to begin — now match that eagerness by completing the work with whatever you have.

kjv: For if there be first a willing mind, it is accepted according to that a man hath, and not according to that he hath not.
ct: If you're truly willing to give, God accepts your gift based on what you have, not on what you don't have.

kjv: For I mean not that other men be eased, and ye burdened:
ct: I'm not trying to make life easy for others while making it hard for you.

kjv: But by an equality, that now at this time your abundance may be a supply for their want, that their abundance also may be a supply for your want: that there may be equality:
ct: The goal is fairness. Right now, your abundance can meet their need. And someday, their abundance may meet yours. That way, things balance out.

kjv: As it is written, He that had gathered much had nothing over; and he that had gathered little had no lack.
ct: As Scripture says, "The one who gathered a lot didn't end up with too much, and the one who gathered only a little didn't go without."

kjv: But thanks be to God, which put the same earnest care into the heart of Titus for you.
ct: Thank God — he put the same deep concern for you into the heart of Titus.

kjv: For indeed he accepted the exhortation; but being more forward, of his own accord he went unto you.
ct: Titus not only accepted our request, but he was so eager that he decided to come to you on his own.

kjv: And we have sent with him the brother, whose praise is in the gospel throughout all the churches;
ct: Along with him, we're sending a brother who is widely praised among all the churches for his work in spreading the gospel.

kjv: And not that only, but who was also chosen of the churches to travel with us with this grace, which is administered by us to the glory of the same Lord, and declaration of your ready mind:
ct: Beyond that, the churches chose him to travel with us as we carry out this generous gift — a service that brings glory to the Lord and demonstrates our eagerness to help.

kjv: Avoiding this, that no man should blame us in this abundance which is administered by us:
ct: We're being careful so that no one can criticize how we handle this large contribution.

kjv: Providing for honest things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.
ct: We want to do what is right — not only in the Lord's eyes, but also in the eyes of everyone watching.

kjv: And we have sent with them our brother, whom we have oftentimes proved diligent in many things, but now much more diligent, upon the great confidence which I have in you.
ct: We're also sending another brother along with them. We've tested his dedication many times in many situations, and now he's even more eager because of his great confidence in you.

kjv: Whether any do enquire of Titus, he is my partner and fellowhelper concerning you: or our brethren be enquired of, they are the messengers of the churches, and the glory of Christ.
ct: As for Titus, he's my partner and coworker in serving you. As for the other brothers, they are representatives of the churches and a reflection of Christ's glory.

kjv: Wherefore shew ye to them, and before the churches, the proof of your love, and of our boasting on your behalf.
ct: So show them the proof of your love, and show all the churches that our pride in you is well deserved.

kjv: For as touching the ministering to the saints, it is superfluous for me to write to you:
ct: I really don't need to write to you about the collection for God's people.

kjv: For I know the forwardness of your mind, for which I boast of you to them of Macedonia, that Achaia was ready a year ago; and your zeal hath provoked very many.
ct: I already know how eager you are to help. In fact, I've been bragging about you to the believers in Macedonia, telling them that you in Achaia have been ready to give since last year. Your enthusiasm has inspired many of them to give as well.

kjv: Yet have I sent the brethren, lest our boasting of you should be in vain in this behalf; that, as I said, ye may be ready:
ct: Still, I'm sending these brothers ahead so that our bragging about you won't turn out to be empty talk. I want you to actually be ready, just like I told them you would be.

kjv: Lest haply if they of Macedonia come with me, and find you unprepared, we (that we say not, ye) should be ashamed in this same confident boasting.
ct: Otherwise, if some Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we would be humiliated — to say nothing of how you'd feel — after all our confident boasting.

kjv: Therefore I thought it necessary to exhort the brethren, that they would go before unto you, and make up beforehand your bounty, whereof ye had notice before, that the same might be ready, as a matter of bounty, and not as of covetousness.
ct: That's why I thought it was important to ask these brothers to go ahead of me and help you finish organizing the generous gift you already promised. That way it will be ready as a true act of generosity, not something squeezed out of you at the last minute.

kjv: But this I say, He which soweth sparingly shall reap also sparingly; and he which soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully.
ct: Remember this: whoever plants only a few seeds will harvest only a small crop, but whoever plants generously will harvest generously.

kjv: Every man according as he purposeth in his heart, so let him give; not grudgingly, or of necessity: for God loveth a cheerful giver.
ct: Each of you should give whatever you've decided in your heart to give — not reluctantly, and not because you feel pressured. God loves a cheerful giver.

kjv: And God is able to make all grace abound toward you; that ye, always having all sufficiency in all things, may abound to every good work:
ct: God is able to pour out every kind of grace on you, so that in every situation you'll always have more than enough — not just for yourselves, but to overflow into every good thing you do.

kjv: (As it is written, He hath dispersed abroad; he hath given to the poor: his righteousness remaineth for ever.
ct: As Scripture says: "He scattered his gifts freely; he gave to the poor. His righteousness lasts forever."

kjv: Now he that ministereth seed to the sower both minister bread for your food, and multiply your seed sown, and increase the fruits of your righteousness;)
ct: The God who provides seed for the farmer and bread for food will also provide and multiply what you have to give, increasing the harvest that grows from your righteousness.

kjv: Being enriched in every thing to all bountifulness, which causeth through us thanksgiving to God.
ct: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous on every occasion, and your generosity — delivered through us — will cause people to thank God.

kjv: For the administration of this service not only supplieth the want of the saints, but is abundant also by many thanksgivings unto God;
ct: This act of service doesn't just meet the needs of God's people. It also overflows in wave after wave of thanksgiving to God.

kjv: Whiles by the experiment of this ministration they glorify God for your professed subjection unto the gospel of Christ, and for your liberal distribution unto them, and unto all men;
ct: Through the proof of your generosity, others will praise God because you live out your commitment to the gospel of Christ and because you share so freely with them and with everyone.

kjv: And by their prayer for you, which long after you for the exceeding grace of God in you.
ct: They will pray for you with deep affection, drawn to you because of the extraordinary grace God has given you.

kjv: Thanks be unto God for his unspeakable gift.
ct: Thanks be to God for his gift — it's too wonderful for words!

kjv: Now I Paul myself beseech you by the meekness and gentleness of Christ, who in presence am base among you, but being absent am bold toward you:
ct: I, Paul, am making a personal appeal to you — and I'm asking with the same humility and gentleness that Christ himself showed. Some of you say I'm timid when I'm there in person but bold when I write from a distance.

kjv: But I beseech you, that I may not be bold when I am present with that confidence, wherewith I think to be bold against some, which think of us as if we walked according to the flesh.
ct: I'm asking you: please don't force me to be as bold in person as I'm prepared to be against those who accuse us of living by worldly motives.

kjv: For though we walk in the flesh, we do not war after the flesh:
ct: Yes, we live in this world, but we don't fight our battles the way the world does.

kjv: (For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal, but mighty through God to the pulling down of strong holds;)
ct: The weapons we fight with aren't worldly weapons. They carry God's power — strong enough to tear down fortresses.

kjv: Casting down imaginations, and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of Christ;
ct: We tear down false arguments and every arrogant idea that sets itself up against the knowledge of God, and we take every thought captive and make it obey Christ.

kjv: And having in a readiness to revenge all disobedience, when your obedience is fulfilled.
ct: Once your obedience is complete, we'll be ready to deal with every act of disobedience that remains.

kjv: Do ye look on things after the outward appearance? If any man trust to himself that he is Christ’s, let him of himself think this again, that, as he is Christ’s, even so are we Christ’s.
ct: You're judging by appearances! If anyone is convinced that they belong to Christ, they need to think again — because we belong to Christ just as much as they do.

kjv: For though I should boast somewhat more of our authority, which the Lord hath given us for edification, and not for your destruction, I should not be ashamed:
ct: Even if I boast a little too much about the authority the Lord gave us, I won't be embarrassed — because he gave it to build you up, not to tear you down.

kjv: That I may not seem as if I would terrify you by letters.
ct: I don't want it to seem like I'm just trying to scare you with my letters.

kjv: For his letters, say they, are weighty and powerful; but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech contemptible.
ct: Some people are saying, "His letters are impressive and forceful, but in person he's unimpressive, and his speaking is terrible."

kjv: Let such an one think this, that, such as we are in word by letters when we are absent, such will we be also in deed when we are present.
ct: Anyone who says that should understand this: what we say in our letters when we're away is exactly what we'll do when we show up in person.

kjv: For we dare not make ourselves of the number, or compare ourselves with some that commend themselves: but they measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves among themselves, are not wise.
ct: We wouldn't dare put ourselves in the same category as those who promote themselves, or compare ourselves to them. When they measure themselves by their own standards and compare themselves only with each other, they show how foolish they are.

kjv: But we will not boast of things without our measure, but according to the measure of the rule which God hath distributed to us, a measure to reach even unto you.
ct: We won't boast beyond what's appropriate. Instead, we'll stay within the boundaries of the work God assigned to us — and that work includes reaching you.

kjv: For we stretch not ourselves beyond our measure, as though we reached not unto you: for we are come as far as to you also in preaching the gospel of Christ:
ct: We aren't overstepping our boundaries, as if our mission didn't extend to you. After all, we were the first to come all the way to you with the good news of Christ.

kjv: Not boasting of things without our measure, that is, of other men’s labours; but having hope, when your faith is increased, that we shall be enlarged by you according to our rule abundantly,
