ct: We don't take credit for work that others have done. Instead, our hope is that as your faith grows, our area of influence among you will expand even further —

kjv: To preach the gospel in the regions beyond you, and not to boast in another man’s line of things made ready to our hand.
ct: so that we can preach the good news in the regions beyond you, rather than boasting about work someone else has already accomplished.

kjv: But he that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.
ct: As Scripture says, "If anyone is going to boast, let them boast in the Lord."

kjv: For not he that commendeth himself is approved, but whom the Lord commendeth.
ct: After all, it's not the person who promotes themselves who is approved — it's the one the Lord promotes.

kjv: Would to God ye could bear with me a little in my folly: and indeed bear with me.
ct: I wish you'd put up with a little foolishness from me — and actually, you already are, so thank you.

kjv: For I am jealous over you with godly jealousy: for I have espoused you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
ct: I'm jealous for you, and it's a godly jealousy. I promised you to one husband — Christ — so I could present you to him as a pure virgin.

kjv: But I fear, lest by any means, as the serpent beguiled Eve through his subtilty, so your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.
ct: But I'm afraid that just as the serpent tricked Eve with his cunning, your minds might be led astray from the simple and pure devotion you have to Christ.

kjv: For if he that cometh preacheth another Jesus, whom we have not preached, or if ye receive another spirit, which ye have not received, or another gospel, which ye have not accepted, ye might well bear with him.
ct: Because if someone comes along and preaches a different Jesus than the one we preached, or offers you a different spirit than the one you received, or a different gospel than the one you accepted — you seem perfectly happy to put up with it!

kjv: For I suppose I was not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles.
ct: I don't consider myself the least bit inferior to those so-called "super-apostles."

kjv: But though I be rude in speech, yet not in knowledge; but we have been throughly made manifest among you in all things.
ct: I may not be a polished speaker, but I certainly know what I'm talking about. We've made that clear to you in every possible way.

kjv: Have I committed an offence in abasing myself that ye might be exalted, because I have preached to you the gospel of God freely?
ct: Was it wrong of me to humble myself so that you could be lifted up? Did I sin by preaching God's gospel to you free of charge?

kjv: I robbed other churches, taking wages of them, to do you service.
ct: I took financial support from other churches — essentially letting them pay my way — so I could serve you.

kjv: And when I was present with you, and wanted, I was chargeable to no man: for that which was lacking to me the brethren which came from Macedonia supplied: and in all things I have kept myself from being burdensome unto you, and so will I keep myself.
ct: When I was with you and ran short on money, I didn't burden any of you. The brothers who came from Macedonia covered what I needed. I've made it a point never to be a financial burden to you, and I intend to keep it that way.

kjv: As the truth of Christ is in me, no man shall stop me of this boasting in the regions of Achaia.
ct: As surely as the truth of Christ lives in me, no one in all of Achaia will take this boast away from me.

kjv: Wherefore? because I love you not? God knoweth.
ct: Why do I insist on this? Is it because I don't love you? God knows that's not true.

kjv: But what I do, that I will do, that I may cut off occasion from them which desire occasion; that wherein they glory, they may be found even as we.
ct: I'll keep doing exactly what I'm doing so I can cut the ground out from under those who are looking for an excuse to claim they work on the same terms we do.

kjv: For such are false apostles, deceitful workers, transforming themselves into the apostles of Christ.
ct: These people are false apostles — dishonest workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.

kjv: And no marvel; for Satan himself is transformed into an angel of light.
ct: That shouldn't surprise anyone. Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.

kjv: Therefore it is no great thing if his ministers also be transformed as the ministers of righteousness; whose end shall be according to their works.
ct: So it's no big deal if his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. In the end, they'll get exactly what their actions deserve.

kjv: I say again, Let no man think me a fool; if otherwise, yet as a fool receive me, that I may boast myself a little.
ct: Let me say it again: don't think I'm a fool. But even if you do, at least hear me out the way you'd humor a fool, so I can do a little boasting of my own.

kjv: That which I speak, I speak it not after the Lord, but as it were foolishly, in this confidence of boasting.
ct: What I'm about to say isn't something the Lord told me to say. I'm speaking like a fool, with this bold confidence of boasting.

kjv: Seeing that many glory after the flesh, I will glory also.
ct: Since so many others are boasting about their human credentials, I'll boast too.

kjv: For ye suffer fools gladly, seeing ye yourselves are wise.
ct: After all, you're so wise that you gladly put up with fools!

kjv: For ye suffer, if a man bring you into bondage, if a man devour you, if a man take of you, if a man exalt himself, if a man smite you on the face.
ct: You put up with anyone who enslaves you, exploits you, takes advantage of you, puts on airs, or even slaps you in the face.

kjv: I speak as concerning reproach, as though we had been weak. Howbeit whereinsoever any is bold, (I speak foolishly,) I am bold also.
ct: I'm ashamed to admit that we were apparently too "weak" for that kind of behavior. But whatever anyone else dares to boast about — I'm speaking foolishly here — I dare to boast about it too.

kjv: Are they Hebrews? so am I. Are they Israelites? so am I. Are they the seed of Abraham? so am I.
ct: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.

kjv: Are they ministers of Christ? (I speak as a fool) I am more; in labours more abundant, in stripes above measure, in prisons more frequent, in deaths oft.
ct: Are they servants of Christ? I know this sounds insane, but I'm an even better one. I've worked harder, been thrown in prison more often, been beaten beyond what anyone should survive, and faced death again and again.

kjv: Of the Jews five times received I forty stripes save one.
ct: 5 times the Jewish authorities gave me 39 lashes.

kjv: Thrice was I beaten with rods, once was I stoned, thrice I suffered shipwreck, a night and a day I have been in the deep;
ct: 3 times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. 3 times I survived shipwrecks. I once spent a full night and day adrift in the open sea.

kjv: In journeyings often, in perils of waters, in perils of robbers, in perils by mine own countrymen, in perils by the heathen, in perils in the city, in perils in the wilderness, in perils in the sea, in perils among false brethren;
ct: I've been constantly on the move, facing danger from rivers, danger from robbers, danger from my own people, danger from Gentiles, danger in the city, danger in the wilderness, danger at sea, and danger from people pretending to be fellow believers.

kjv: In weariness and painfulness, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness.
ct: I've known exhausting labor and hardship, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, going without food over and over, and exposure to cold without enough clothing.

kjv: Beside those things that are without, that which cometh upon me daily, the care of all the churches.
ct: On top of all that, I carry the daily pressure of my concern for all the churches.

kjv: Who is weak, and I am not weak? who is offended, and I burn not?
ct: When someone is weak, I feel that weakness. When someone is led into sin, it burns me up inside.

kjv: If I must needs glory, I will glory of the things which concern mine infirmities.
ct: If I have to boast, I'll boast about the things that show my weakness.

kjv: The God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which is blessed for evermore, knoweth that I lie not.
ct: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus Christ — who is praised forever — knows I'm not lying.

kjv: In Damascus the governor under Aretas the king kept the city of the Damascenes with a garrison, desirous to apprehend me:
ct: In Damascus, the governor serving under King Aretas posted guards around the city to have me arrested.

kjv: And through a window in a basket was I let down by the wall, and escaped his hands.
ct: But my friends lowered me in a basket through a window in the city wall, and I escaped his grasp.

kjv: It is not expedient for me doubtless to glory. I will come to visions and revelations of the Lord.
ct: Boasting about myself isn't really helpful, but I'm going to do it anyway. Let me tell you about visions and revelations the Lord has given me.

kjv: I knew a man in Christ above fourteen years ago, (whether in the body, I cannot tell; or whether out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;) such an one caught up to the third heaven.
ct: I know a man who belongs to Christ who, 14 years ago, was caught up to the third heaven. Whether he was there physically or only in spirit, I don't know — only God knows.

kjv: And I knew such a man, (whether in the body, or out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;)
ct: I know this man — whether he was in his body or out of it, I can't say; God knows —

kjv: How that he was caught up into paradise, and heard unspeakable words, which it is not lawful for a man to utter.
ct: was caught up into paradise and heard things too sacred for any human being to repeat.

kjv: Of such an one will I glory: yet of myself I will not glory, but in mine infirmities.
ct: I'll boast about someone like that, but I won't boast about myself — except when it comes to my weaknesses.

kjv: For though I would desire to glory, I shall not be a fool; for I will say the truth: but now I forbear, lest any man should think of me above that which he seeth me to be, or that he heareth of me.
ct: Even if I did choose to boast, I wouldn't be a fool for doing it, because I'd be telling the truth. But I'll hold back, so that no one thinks more of me than what they can see with their own eyes or hear from my own lips.

kjv: And lest I should be exalted above measure through the abundance of the revelations, there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, the messenger of Satan to buffet me, lest I should be exalted above measure.
ct: To keep me from becoming proud because of these incredible revelations, I was given a thorn in the flesh — a messenger of Satan to torment me and keep me humble.

kjv: For this thing I besought the Lord thrice, that it might depart from me.
ct: 3 times I begged the Lord to take it away from me.

kjv: And he said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness. Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may rest upon me.
ct: But he told me, "My grace is all you need. My power reaches its full strength in weakness." So I will gladly boast about my weaknesses, because then the power of Christ rests on me.

kjv: Therefore I take pleasure in infirmities, in reproaches, in necessities, in persecutions, in distresses for Christ’s sake: for when I am weak, then am I strong.
ct: That's why I'm actually glad when I face weakness, insults, hardships, persecution, and trouble for Christ's sake. Because when I am weak, that's when I am truly strong.

kjv: I am become a fool in glorying; ye have compelled me: for I ought to have been commended of you: for in nothing am I behind the very chiefest apostles, though I be nothing.
ct: I've been acting like a fool with all this boasting — but you forced me into it. You should have been the ones speaking up for me! I'm not the least bit inferior to those so-called "super apostles," even though I'm nothing on my own.

kjv: Truly the signs of an apostle were wrought among you in all patience, in signs, and wonders, and mighty deeds.
ct: The proof that I'm a genuine apostle was demonstrated right there among you — through patient endurance, miraculous signs, wonders, and displays of power.

kjv: For what is it wherein ye were inferior to other churches, except it be that I myself was not burdensome to you? forgive me this wrong.
ct: How were you treated worse than any other church? The only difference is that I never asked you for financial support. Please, forgive me for that terrible offense!

kjv: Behold, the third time I am ready to come to you; and I will not be burdensome to you: for I seek not yours, but you: for the children ought not to lay up for the parents, but the parents for the children.
ct: Look, this will be my 3rd visit to you, and I still won't be a burden. I'm not after your money — I'm after you. Children shouldn't have to save up for their parents; parents should provide for their children.

kjv: And I will very gladly spend and be spent for you; though the more abundantly I love you, the less I be loved.
ct: I will gladly spend everything I have — and exhaust myself completely — for your souls. If I love you more and more, does that mean you'll love me less and less?

kjv: But be it so, I did not burden you: nevertheless, being crafty, I caught you with guile.
ct: Fine, you agree I wasn't a financial burden. But some of you are saying, "He's clever — he tricked us through some scheme."

kjv: Did I make a gain of you by any of them whom I sent unto you?
ct: Really? Did I exploit you through anyone I sent to you?

kjv: I desired Titus, and with him I sent a brother. Did Titus make a gain of you? walked we not in the same spirit? walked we not in the same steps?
ct: I urged Titus to visit you and sent another brother along with him. Did Titus take advantage of you? Didn't we act with the same spirit? Didn't we follow the same path?

kjv: Again, think ye that we excuse ourselves unto you? we speak before God in Christ: but we do all things, dearly beloved, for your edifying.
ct: Have you been thinking this whole time that we're just defending ourselves to you? We speak before God as people who belong to Christ. Everything we do, dear friends, is to build you up.

kjv: For I fear, lest, when I come, I shall not find you such as I would, and that I shall be found unto you such as ye would not: lest there be debates, envyings, wraths, strifes, backbitings, whisperings, swellings, tumults:
ct: I'm honestly afraid that when I arrive, I won't like what I find — and you won't like how I respond. I'm worried there will be quarreling, jealousy, angry outbursts, selfish rivalries, slander, gossip, arrogance, and chaos.

kjv: And lest, when I come again, my God will humble me among you, and that I shall bewail many which have sinned already, and have not repented of the uncleanness and fornication and lasciviousness which they have committed.
ct: I'm afraid that when I come again, my God will humble me in front of you, and I'll end up grieving over many who sinned before and still haven't repented of their impurity, sexual sin, and shameless behavior.

kjv: This is the third time I am coming to you. In the mouth of two or three witnesses shall every word be established.
ct: This is the 3rd time I'm coming to visit you. As Scripture says, "Every matter must be confirmed by the testimony of 2 or 3 witnesses."

kjv: I told you before, and foretell you, as if I were present, the second time; and being absent now I write to them which heretofore have sinned, and to all other, that, if I come again, I will not spare:
ct: I already warned you during my 2nd visit, and now I'm warning you again while I'm away: when I come back, I won't go easy on those who sinned before or on anyone else who is sinning.

kjv: Since ye seek a proof of Christ speaking in me, which to you-ward is not weak, but is mighty in you.
ct: You keep demanding proof that Christ is speaking through me. Well, Christ isn't weak when he deals with you — he is powerful among you.

kjv: For though he was crucified through weakness, yet he liveth by the power of God. For we also are weak in him, but we shall live with him by the power of God toward you.
ct: Yes, he was crucified in weakness, but he lives by the power of God. In the same way, we share in his weakness, but by God's power we will live with him as we deal with you.

kjv: Examine yourselves, whether ye be in the faith; prove your own selves. Know ye not your own selves, how that Jesus Christ is in you, except ye be reprobates?
ct: Take a hard look at yourselves and test whether you're really living in the faith. Examine your own lives. Don't you realize that Jesus Christ is in you? — unless, of course, you fail the test.

kjv: But I trust that ye shall know that we are not reprobates.
ct: I'm confident that when you examine us, you'll see that we haven't failed it.

kjv: Now I pray to God that ye do no evil; not that we should appear approved, but that ye should do that which is honest, though we be as reprobates.
ct: I pray to God that you won't do anything wrong — not so we can look good, but so that you'll do what is right, even if it makes us look like we've failed.

kjv: For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth.
ct: We can't do anything against the truth — only for it.

kjv: For we are glad, when we are weak, and ye are strong: and this also we wish, even your perfection.
ct: We're actually glad when we are weak and you are strong. What we pray for above all is your growth to maturity.

kjv: Therefore I write these things being absent, lest being present I should use sharpness, according to the power which the Lord hath given me to edification, and not to destruction.
ct: That's why I'm writing all this while I'm still away — so that when I arrive, I won't have to be harsh with you. The Lord gave me authority to build you up, not to tear you down.

kjv: Finally, brethren, farewell. Be perfect, be of good comfort, be of one mind, live in peace; and the God of love and peace shall be with you.
ct: Finally, brothers and sisters, goodbye. Aim for maturity. Take comfort in each other. Be united in heart and mind. Live in peace. And the God of love and peace will be with you.

kjv: Greet one another with an holy kiss.
ct: Greet one another with a holy kiss.

kjv: All the saints salute you.
ct: All of God's people here send you their greetings.

kjv: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the communion of the Holy Ghost, be with you all. Amen.
ct: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with every one of you. Amen.

kjv: Paul, an apostle, (not of men, neither by man, but by Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead;)
ct: This letter is from Paul, an apostle — not appointed by any human authority or human institution, but by Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised him from the dead.

kjv: And all the brethren which are with me, unto the churches of Galatia:
ct: All the brothers and sisters here with me join me in writing to the churches throughout Galatia.

kjv: Grace be to you and peace from God the Father, and from our Lord Jesus Christ,
ct: May grace and peace be yours from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ,

kjv: Who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present evil world, according to the will of God and our Father:
ct: who gave himself for our sins to rescue us from this present evil age, just as God our Father planned.

kjv: To whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
ct: To God be glory forever and ever. Amen.

kjv: I marvel that ye are so soon removed from him that called you into the grace of Christ unto another gospel:
ct: I'm shocked that you're already turning away from the one who called you by the grace of Christ, and chasing after a different gospel.

kjv: Which is not another; but there be some that trouble you, and would pervert the gospel of Christ.
ct: There is no other gospel — it's just that certain people are stirring up confusion among you and trying to twist the gospel of Christ into something it's not.

kjv: But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him be accursed.
ct: But even if we ourselves — or an angel from heaven — were to preach a gospel different from the one we already gave you, let that person be cursed.

kjv: As we said before, so say I now again, If any man preach any other gospel unto you than that ye have received, let him be accursed.
ct: I've said it before, and I'll say it again: if anyone preaches a gospel to you that contradicts what you already received, let that person be cursed.

kjv: For do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please men? for if I yet pleased men, I should not be the servant of Christ.
ct: Am I trying to win the approval of people, or of God? Am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I wouldn't be a servant of Christ.

kjv: But I certify you, brethren, that the gospel which was preached of me is not after man.
ct: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that the gospel I preached to you didn't come from any human source.

kjv: For I neither received it of man, neither was I taught it, but by the revelation of Jesus Christ.
ct: I didn't receive it from any person, and no one taught it to me. It came to me through a direct revelation from Jesus Christ.

kjv: For ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the church of God, and wasted it:
ct: You've heard about my former life in Judaism — how I violently persecuted God's church and did everything I could to destroy it.

kjv: And profited in the Jews’ religion above many my equals in mine own nation, being more exceedingly zealous of the traditions of my fathers.
ct: I was advancing in Judaism faster than many of my peers, driven by an extreme devotion to the traditions of my ancestors.

kjv: But when it pleased God, who separated me from my mother’s womb, and called me by his grace,
ct: But God, who set me apart before I was even born and called me by his grace,

kjv: To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the heathen; immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood:
ct: chose to reveal his Son to me so that I could preach him among the Gentiles. When that happened, I didn't consult with anyone.

kjv: Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia, and returned again unto Damascus.
ct: I didn't go up to Jerusalem to meet with the apostles who came before me. Instead, I went to Arabia, and later returned to Damascus.

kjv: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, and abode with him fifteen days.
ct: Then, 3 years later, I went up to Jerusalem to visit Peter and stayed with him for 15 days.

kjv: But other of the apostles saw I none, save James the Lord’s brother.
ct: I didn't see any of the other apostles — only James, the Lord's brother.

kjv: Now the things which I write unto you, behold, before God, I lie not.
ct: I swear before God that everything I'm writing to you is the truth.

kjv: Afterwards I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia;
ct: After that, I traveled to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.

kjv: And was unknown by face unto the churches of Judaea which were in Christ:
ct: The churches in Judea that belonged to Christ had never met me in person.

kjv: But they had heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed.
ct: All they knew was what others told them: "The man who used to persecute us is now preaching the very faith he once tried to destroy."

kjv: And they glorified God in me.
ct: And they praised God because of what he had done in me.

kjv: Then fourteen years after I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, and took Titus with me also.
ct: 14 years later, I went up to Jerusalem again, this time with Barnabas, and I brought Titus along too.

kjv: And I went up by revelation, and communicated unto them that gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to them which were of reputation, lest by any means I should run, or had run, in vain.
ct: I went because God revealed to me that I should. While there, I laid out the gospel message I'd been preaching to the Gentiles. But I did this privately with the recognized leaders, because I wanted to make sure the work I was doing — and had already done — wasn't for nothing.

kjv: But neither Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised:
ct: And here's the key thing: not even Titus, who was with me and is a Greek, was forced to be circumcised.

kjv: And that because of false brethren unawares brought in, who came in privily to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage:
ct: The issue only came up because some fake believers had been sneaked in among us. They infiltrated our group to spy on the freedom we have in Christ Jesus, hoping to drag us back into slavery to the law.

kjv: To whom we gave place by subjection, no, not for an hour; that the truth of the gospel might continue with you.
ct: We didn't give in to them for even an hour. We stood firm so that the truth of the gospel would be preserved for you.

kjv: But of these who seemed to be somewhat, (whatsoever they were, it maketh no matter to me: God accepteth no man’s person:) for they who seemed to be somewhat in conference added nothing to me:
ct: As for those who were considered important leaders — whatever status they held makes no difference to me, because God doesn't play favorites — those respected leaders added nothing to my message.

kjv: But contrariwise, when they saw that the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed unto me, as the gospel of the circumcision was unto Peter;
ct: Instead, they saw the opposite: that God had entrusted me with the gospel for the uncircumcised, just as he had entrusted Peter with the gospel for the circumcised.

kjv: (For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, the same was mighty in me toward the Gentiles:)
ct: The same God who worked powerfully through Peter in his mission to the Jews was also working powerfully through me in my mission to the Gentiles.

kjv: And when James, Cephas, and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the grace that was given unto me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of fellowship; that we should go unto the heathen, and they unto the circumcision.
ct: When James, Cephas, and John — the men recognized as pillars of the church — saw the grace God had given me, they shook hands with Barnabas and me as partners. We agreed that we would go to the Gentiles, and they would go to the Jews.

kjv: Only they would that we should remember the poor; the same which I also was forward to do.
ct: Their only request was that we remember the poor, which was something I was already eager to do.

kjv: But when Peter was come to Antioch, I withstood him to the face, because he was to be blamed.
ct: But when Peter came to Antioch, I confronted him to his face, because he was clearly in the wrong.

kjv: For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision.
ct: Before certain people arrived from James, Peter had been eating with Gentile believers. But when those people showed up, he pulled back and separated himself, because he was afraid of the circumcision group.

kjv: And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation.
ct: The rest of the Jewish believers joined him in this hypocrisy, and it was so contagious that even Barnabas got swept up in it.

kjv: But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said unto Peter before them all, If thou, being a Jew, livest after the manner of Gentiles, and not as do the Jews, why compellest thou the Gentiles to live as do the Jews?
ct: When I saw that they weren't acting in line with the truth of the gospel, I said to Peter in front of everyone, "You're a Jew, but you've been living like a Gentile, not like a Jew. So how can you now turn around and force Gentiles to follow Jewish customs?"

kjv: We who are Jews by nature, and not sinners of the Gentiles,
ct: "We are Jews by birth, not 'Gentile sinners.'

kjv: Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified.
ct: Yet we know that no one is made right with God by obeying the law, but only through faith in Jesus Christ. So even we have put our faith in Christ Jesus, so that we could be made right with God through faith in Christ — not by keeping the law. Because no human being will ever be made right with God by obeying the law.

kjv: But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ, we ourselves also are found sinners, is therefore Christ the minister of sin? God forbid.
ct: But if, in seeking to be made right with God through Christ, we ourselves turn out to be sinners too, does that make Christ an agent of sin? Absolutely not!

kjv: For if I build again the things which I destroyed, I make myself a transgressor.
ct: If I rebuild the very system I tore down, then I prove that I was wrong to tear it down in the first place.

kjv: For I through the law am dead to the law, that I might live unto God.
ct: Through the law, I died to the law so that I could live for God.

kjv: I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.
ct: I have been crucified with Christ, and I no longer live for myself — Christ lives in me. The life I now live in this body, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me.

kjv: I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness come by the law, then Christ is dead in vain.
ct: I refuse to treat God's grace as meaningless. Because if we could be made right with God by keeping the law, then Christ died for nothing."

kjv: O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you, that ye should not obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ hath been evidently set forth, crucified among you?
ct: You foolish Galatians! Who has cast a spell on you? Jesus Christ was clearly portrayed as crucified right before your eyes!

kjv: This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
ct: I only want to know one thing from you: Did you receive the Holy Spirit by obeying the law, or by hearing the message and believing it?

kjv: Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?
ct: How can you be so foolish? You started your journey with the Holy Spirit — are you now trying to finish it by your own human effort?

kjv: Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.
ct: Did you go through all that suffering for nothing? I hope it wasn't for nothing.

kjv: He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you, doeth he it by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
ct: When God gives you his Holy Spirit and works miracles among you, does he do it because you obey the law, or because you heard the message and believed?

kjv: Even as Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.
ct: Think about Abraham — he believed God, and God counted that faith as righteousness.

kjv: Know ye therefore that they which are of faith, the same are the children of Abraham.
ct: So understand this: the people who live by faith are the true children of Abraham.

kjv: And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the heathen through faith, preached before the gospel unto Abraham, saying, In thee shall all nations be blessed.
ct: Scripture saw ahead of time that God would make the Gentiles right with himself through faith. That's why it announced the good news to Abraham in advance: "Through you, all nations will be blessed."

kjv: So then they which be of faith are blessed with faithful Abraham.
ct: So everyone who lives by faith shares in the same blessing that faithful Abraham received.

kjv: For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written, Cursed is every one that continueth not in all things which are written in the book of the law to do them.
ct: But everyone who relies on obeying the law lives under a curse. Scripture says, "Cursed is everyone who does not continue doing everything written in the book of the law."

kjv: But that no man is justified by the law in the sight of God, it is evident: for, The just shall live by faith.
ct: It's clear that no one is made right with God by the law, because Scripture says, "The righteous will live by faith."

kjv: And the law is not of faith: but, The man that doeth them shall live in them.
ct: The law isn't based on faith. Instead, it operates on a different principle: "The person who does these things will live by them."

kjv: Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree:
ct: Christ rescued us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse in our place. Scripture says, "Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree."

kjv: That the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
ct: He did this so that the blessing given to Abraham would come to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, and so that we could receive the promised Holy Spirit through faith.

kjv: Brethren, I speak after the manner of men; Though it be but a man’s covenant, yet if it be confirmed, no man disannulleth, or addeth thereto.
ct: Brothers and sisters, let me use an everyday example. Even with a human contract, once it's been signed and sealed, no one can cancel it or add conditions to it.

kjv: Now to Abraham and his seed were the promises made. He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.
ct: Now, God made his promises to Abraham and to his offspring. Notice that Scripture doesn't say "and to your offspring" as if referring to many people. It says "and to your offspring" — meaning one person, and that person is Christ.

kjv: And this I say, that the covenant, that was confirmed before of God in Christ, the law, which was four hundred and thirty years after, cannot disannul, that it should make the promise of none effect.
ct: Here's my point: God confirmed a covenant with Abraham. The law, which came 430 years later, can't override that covenant or cancel the promise.

kjv: For if the inheritance be of the law, it is no more of promise: but God gave it to Abraham by promise.
ct: If the inheritance depends on the law, then it's no longer based on a promise. But God gave it to Abraham as a free promise.

kjv: Wherefore then serveth the law? It was added because of transgressions, till the seed should come to whom the promise was made; and it was ordained by angels in the hand of a mediator.
ct: So what was the purpose of the law? It was added to expose sin and remained in effect until the promised offspring — Christ — arrived. The law was delivered through angels and given through a mediator.

kjv: Now a mediator is not a mediator of one, but God is one.
ct: Now, a mediator implies more than one party, but God is one.

kjv: Is the law then against the promises of God? God forbid: for if there had been a law given which could have given life, verily righteousness should have been by the law.
ct: Does that mean the law works against God's promises? Absolutely not! If a law had been given that could actually give life, then righteousness really would have come through the law.

kjv: But the scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.
ct: But Scripture declares that everything is trapped under the power of sin, so that the promise — given through faith in Jesus Christ — would be offered to everyone who believes.

kjv: But before faith came, we were kept under the law, shut up unto the faith which should afterwards be revealed.
ct: Before faith in Christ came, we were held prisoner by the law, locked up until this coming faith would be revealed.

kjv: Wherefore the law was our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ, that we might be justified by faith.
ct: So the law served as our guardian to lead us to Christ, so that we could be made right with God through faith.

kjv: But after that faith is come, we are no longer under a schoolmaster.
ct: But now that faith has come, we're no longer under that guardian.

kjv: For ye are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus.
ct: You are all children of God through faith in Christ Jesus.

kjv: For as many of you as have been baptized into Christ have put on Christ.
ct: Every one of you who was baptized into Christ has been clothed with Christ.

kjv: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all one in Christ Jesus.
ct: There is no longer Jew or Greek, slave or free, male or female — you are all one in Christ Jesus.

kjv: And if ye be Christ’s, then are ye Abraham’s seed, and heirs according to the promise.
ct: And if you belong to Christ, then you are Abraham's offspring and heirs of everything God promised.

kjv: Now I say, That the heir, as long as he is a child, differeth nothing from a servant, though he be lord of all;
ct: Here's what I mean: as long as an heir is still a child, he's no different from a slave — even though he technically owns everything.

kjv: But is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father.
ct: He's under the control of guardians and managers until the date his father has set.

kjv: Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elements of the world:
ct: It was the same with us. When we were spiritually immature, we were enslaved to the basic principles of this world.

kjv: But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law,
ct: But when the right time had fully come, God sent his Son, born of a woman, born under the law,

kjv: To redeem them that were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of sons.
ct: to set free everyone who was under the law, so that we could be adopted as God's own children.

kjv: And because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
ct: And because you are his children, God sent the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying out, "Abba, Father!"

kjv: Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
ct: So you are no longer a slave but a son or daughter. And if you are God's child, then you are also an heir through Christ.

kjv: Howbeit then, when ye knew not God, ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods.
ct: Before, when you didn't know God, you served things that aren't really gods at all.

kjv: But now, after that ye have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements, whereunto ye desire again to be in bondage?
ct: But now that you know God — or rather, now that God knows you — how can you turn back to those weak and worthless principles? Do you really want to be enslaved by them all over again?

kjv: Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years.
ct: You're observing special days, months, seasons, and years.

kjv: I am afraid of you, lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain.
ct: I'm afraid for you. I'm worried that all my hard work among you was for nothing.

kjv: Brethren, I beseech you, be as I am; for I am as ye are: ye have not injured me at all.
ct: Brothers and sisters, I'm begging you — become like me, because I became like you. You never did me any wrong.

kjv: Ye know how through infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you at the first.
ct: You know it was because of a physical illness that I first preached the gospel to you.

kjv: And my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not, nor rejected; but received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.
ct: Even though my condition was a trial for you, you didn't look down on me or turn me away. Instead, you welcomed me as if I were an angel of God — as if I were Christ Jesus himself.

kjv: Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record, that, if it had been possible, ye would have plucked out your own eyes, and have given them to me.
ct: What happened to that joy you had? I can honestly say that back then, you would have torn out your own eyes and given them to me if you could have.

kjv: Am I therefore become your enemy, because I tell you the truth?
ct: So have I become your enemy just because I tell you the truth?

kjv: They zealously affect you, but not well; yea, they would exclude you, that ye might affect them.
ct: Those people are working hard to win you over, but not for your good. They want to cut you off from us so that you'll become devoted to them instead.

kjv: But it is good to be zealously affected always in a good thing, and not only when I am present with you.
ct: It's always a good thing to be passionate about something worthwhile — not just when I'm there with you.

kjv: My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,
ct: My dear children, I feel like I'm going through the pain of childbirth with you all over again, and it won't stop until Christ is fully formed in you.

kjv: I desire to be present with you now, and to change my voice; for I stand in doubt of you.
ct: I wish I could be there with you right now and change my tone, because honestly, I don't know what to make of you.

kjv: Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, do ye not hear the law?
ct: Tell me something — you who want to live under the law, have you actually listened to what the law says?

kjv: For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a freewoman.
ct: Scripture says that Abraham had 2 sons: one by a slave woman and one by a free woman.

kjv: But he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman was by promise.
ct: The slave woman's son was born through ordinary human means, but the free woman's son was born because of God's promise.

kjv: Which things are an allegory: for these are the two covenants; the one from the mount Sinai, which gendereth to bondage, which is Agar.
ct: This story has a deeper meaning. The 2 women represent 2 covenants. One covenant comes from Mount Sinai and produces slaves — that's Hagar.

kjv: For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia, and answereth to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children.
ct: Hagar stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present city of Jerusalem, because Jerusalem and her people are living in slavery.

kjv: But Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.
ct: But the Jerusalem above is free, and she is our mother.

kjv: For it is written, Rejoice, thou barren that bearest not; break forth and cry, thou that travailest not: for the desolate hath many more children than she which hath an husband.
ct: As Scripture says: "Celebrate, you woman who never gave birth! Shout for joy, you who never experienced labor pains! Because the abandoned woman will have far more children than the woman who has a husband."

kjv: Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are the children of promise.
ct: Now you, brothers and sisters, are children of promise — just like Isaac was.

kjv: But as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the Spirit, even so it is now.
ct: But just as the son born by ordinary means persecuted the son born by the Spirit back then, the same thing is happening now.

kjv: Nevertheless what saith the scripture? Cast out the bondwoman and her son: for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman.
ct: But what does Scripture say? "Send the slave woman and her son away, because the slave woman's son will never share the inheritance with the free woman's son."

kjv: So then, brethren, we are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free.
ct: So then, brothers and sisters, we are not children of the slave woman. We are children of the free woman.

kjv: Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage.
ct: So stand firm in the freedom that Christ has given us, and don't let anyone drag you back under the yoke of slavery to the law.

kjv: Behold, I Paul say unto you, that if ye be circumcised, Christ shall profit you nothing.
ct: Listen carefully — I, Paul, am telling you this: if you get circumcised to earn God's approval, then Christ does you no good at all.

kjv: For I testify again to every man that is circumcised, that he is a debtor to do the whole law.
ct: Let me say it again to every man who gets circumcised: you are now obligated to obey every single part of the law.

kjv: Christ is become of no effect unto you, whosoever of you are justified by the law; ye are fallen from grace.
ct: If you're trying to be made right with God through the law, you've cut yourselves off from Christ. You've fallen away from grace.

kjv: For we through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith.
ct: But we, through the Holy Spirit and by faith, eagerly wait for the righteousness we hope for.

kjv: For in Jesus Christ neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision; but faith which worketh by love.
ct: When you belong to Christ Jesus, it doesn't matter whether you're circumcised or not. The only thing that matters is faith expressing itself through love.

kjv: Ye did run well; who did hinder you that ye should not obey the truth?
ct: You were doing so well! Who got in your way and kept you from following the truth?

kjv: This persuasion cometh not of him that calleth you.
ct: Whatever convinced you to turn aside didn't come from the one who called you.

kjv: A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.
ct: A little yeast works its way through the whole batch of dough.

kjv: I have confidence in you through the Lord, that ye will be none otherwise minded: but he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be.
ct: I'm confident in the LORD that you'll come around to seeing things the right way. But whoever is stirring up confusion among you will face God's judgment, no matter who he is.

kjv: And I, brethren, if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? then is the offence of the cross ceased.
ct: Brothers, if I were still preaching that circumcision is necessary, why would anyone still persecute me? If that were my message, the cross wouldn't offend anyone.

kjv: I would they were even cut off which trouble you.
ct: I wish the people who are upsetting you would go all the way and cut themselves off entirely!

kjv: For, brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.
ct: Brothers, you were called to be free. But don't use your freedom as an excuse to give in to your sinful nature. Instead, serve one another through love.

kjv: For all the law is fulfilled in one word, even in this; Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
ct: The entire law can be summed up in one command: "Love your neighbor as yourself."

kjv: But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.
ct: But if you keep tearing each other apart like wild animals, watch out — you'll end up destroying each other completely.

kjv: This I say then, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh.
ct: Here's what I'm saying: let the Holy Spirit guide your life, and you won't give in to the desires of your sinful nature.

kjv: For the flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh: and these are contrary the one to the other: so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.
ct: Your sinful nature craves what the Spirit opposes, and the Spirit desires what your sinful nature fights against. They are constantly at war with each other, so you can't simply do whatever you want.

kjv: But if ye be led of the Spirit, ye are not under the law.
ct: But if the Spirit is leading you, you are not under the law.

kjv: Now the works of the flesh are manifest, which are these; Adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness,
ct: The acts of the sinful nature are obvious: adultery, sexual immorality, impurity, shameless behavior,

kjv: Idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, strife, seditions, heresies,
ct: idolatry, sorcery, hatred, conflict, jealousy, explosive anger, selfish ambition, division, false teaching,

kjv: Envyings, murders, drunkenness, revellings, and such like: of the which I tell you before, as I have also told you in time past, that they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God.
ct: envy, murder, drunkenness, wild partying, and things like these. I'm warning you now, just as I warned you before: people who make a habit of living this way will not inherit God's Kingdom.

kjv: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,
ct: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith,

kjv: Meekness, temperance: against such there is no law.
ct: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against any of these things.

kjv: And they that are Christ’s have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts.
ct: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have nailed their sinful nature to the cross, along with all its passions and desires.

kjv: If we live in the Spirit, let us also walk in the Spirit.
ct: Since we live by the Spirit, let's keep in step with the Spirit.

kjv: Let us not be desirous of vain glory, provoking one another, envying one another.
ct: Let's not become arrogant, provoking each other and being jealous of one another.

kjv: Brethren, if a man be overtaken in a fault, ye which are spiritual, restore such an one in the spirit of meekness; considering thyself, lest thou also be tempted.
ct: Brothers and sisters, if someone among you gets caught up in a sin, those of you who are spiritually mature should gently help that person get back on track. But watch yourself — you could just as easily face the same temptation.

kjv: Bear ye one another’s burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ.
ct: Carry each other's burdens. That's how you fulfill the law of Christ.

kjv: For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself.
ct: If you think you're something special when you're really not, you're only fooling yourself.

kjv: But let every man prove his own work, and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.
ct: Each person should examine their own actions. Then if there's something to be proud of, it will be based on their own work — not on comparing themselves to someone else.

kjv: For every man shall bear his own burden.
ct: After all, each person is responsible for carrying their own load.

kjv: Let him that is taught in the word communicate unto him that teacheth in all good things.
ct: Anyone who is being taught the word should share all good things with the person who teaches them.

kjv: Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.
ct: Don't be fooled — no one makes a mockery of God. Whatever a person plants, that's exactly what they'll harvest.

kjv: For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.
ct: If you plant seeds to satisfy your sinful nature, you'll harvest decay and destruction from it. But if you plant seeds to satisfy the Holy Spirit, you'll harvest eternal life from the Spirit.

kjv: And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.
ct: So let's not get tired of doing what's right. When the time comes, we will reap a harvest — as long as we don't give up.

kjv: As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith.
ct: Whenever we have the chance, then, let's do good to everyone — and especially to those who belong to the family of faith.

kjv: Ye see how large a letter I have written unto you with mine own hand.
ct: Look at how large these letters are — I'm writing this part to you with my own hand!

kjv: As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh, they constrain you to be circumcised; only lest they should suffer persecution for the cross of Christ.
ct: The people pressuring you to be circumcised are only trying to look good outwardly. Their real motive is to avoid being persecuted for the cross of Christ.

kjv: For neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law; but desire to have you circumcised, that they may glory in your flesh.
ct: Even those who are circumcised don't actually keep the law themselves. They just want you circumcised so they can brag about what they've done to your body.

kjv: But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world.
ct: As for me, I will never boast about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Through that cross, the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.

kjv: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature.
ct: In Christ Jesus, it doesn't matter at all whether someone is circumcised or not. What matters is becoming a new creation.

kjv: And as many as walk according to this rule, peace be on them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.
ct: For everyone who lives by this truth — peace and mercy be on them, and on the Israel of God.

kjv: From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.
ct: From now on, let no one cause me trouble. I carry the marks of the Lord Jesus branded on my body.

kjv: Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
ct: Brothers and sisters, may the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

kjv: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, to the saints which are at Ephesus, and to the faithful in Christ Jesus:
ct: This letter is from Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God. I'm writing to God's holy people in Ephesus — those who are faithful in Christ Jesus.

kjv: Grace be to you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
ct: May grace and peace be yours from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

kjv: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who hath blessed us with all spiritual blessings in heavenly places in Christ:
ct: All praise to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! He has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places through our union with Christ.

kjv: According as he hath chosen us in him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and without blame before him in love:
ct: Before the world was even created, God chose us in Christ to be holy and blameless before him in love.

kjv: Having predestinated us unto the adoption of children by Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will,
ct: He decided in advance to adopt us as his own children through Jesus Christ. This was his plan all along, and it gave him great pleasure.

kjv: To the praise of the glory of his grace, wherein he hath made us accepted in the beloved.
ct: So we praise him for the glorious grace he has poured out on us — grace freely given to us through his Beloved Son.

kjv: In whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, according to the riches of his grace;
ct: In Christ, we have redemption through his blood — the forgiveness of our sins — because God's grace is so incredibly rich.

kjv: Wherein he hath abounded toward us in all wisdom and prudence;
ct: He has lavished that grace on us with all wisdom and understanding,

kjv: Having made known unto us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure which he hath purposed in himself:
ct: revealing to us the mystery of his will. This was his good plan, which he set in motion through Christ —

kjv: That in the dispensation of the fulness of times he might gather together in one all things in Christ, both which are in heaven, and which are on earth; even in him:
ct: that when the time was fully right, he would bring everything together under one head, Christ himself — everything in heaven and everything on earth.

kjv: In whom also we have obtained an inheritance, being predestinated according to the purpose of him who worketh all things after the counsel of his own will:
ct: In Christ, we have also received an inheritance. God predestined this according to his purpose, and he accomplishes everything according to the plan of his own will.

kjv: That we should be to the praise of his glory, who first trusted in Christ.
ct: His purpose was that we — the first to put our hope in Christ — would live for the praise of his glory.

kjv: In whom ye also trusted, after that ye heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise,
ct: And you also were included in Christ when you heard the word of truth, the good news of your salvation. When you believed, you were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit,

kjv: Which is the earnest of our inheritance until the redemption of the purchased possession, unto the praise of his glory.
ct: who serves as a down payment guaranteeing our inheritance until God fully redeems those who belong to him — all to the praise of his glory.

kjv: Wherefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus, and love unto all the saints,
ct: That's why, ever since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all of God's people,

kjv: Cease not to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers;
ct: I haven't stopped thanking God for you. I remember you every time I pray,

kjv: That the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him:
ct: asking that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, would give you a spirit of wisdom and revelation so that you can truly know him.

kjv: The eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that ye may know what is the hope of his calling, and what the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints,
ct: I pray that the eyes of your heart would be opened so you can see the hope he's called you to, and how rich and glorious the inheritance is that he's given his people,

kjv: And what is the exceeding greatness of his power to us-ward who believe, according to the working of his mighty power,
ct: and how incredibly great his power is for us who believe. This power works in us with the same mighty strength

kjv: Which he wrought in Christ, when he raised him from the dead, and set him at his own right hand in the heavenly places,
ct: that God demonstrated in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places,

kjv: Far above all principality, and power, and might, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come:
ct: far above every ruler, authority, power, and dominion — above every name that can be named, not only in this age but in the age to come.

kjv: And hath put all things under his feet, and gave him to be the head over all things to the church,
ct: God has placed everything under Christ's feet and made him the head over all things for the church,

kjv: Which is his body, the fulness of him that filleth all in all.
ct: which is his body — the fullness of him who fills everything in every way.

kjv: And you hath he quickened, who were dead in trespasses and sins;
ct: There was a time when you were dead because of your sins and the ways you disobeyed God.

kjv: Wherein in time past ye walked according to the course of this world, according to the prince of the power of the air, the spirit that now worketh in the children of disobedience:
ct: Back then, you lived the way the world lives, following the prince who rules the power of the air — the spirit that is still at work in everyone who refuses to obey God.

kjv: Among whom also we all had our conversation in times past in the lusts of our flesh, fulfilling the desires of the flesh and of the mind; and were by nature the children of wrath, even as others.
ct: We were all right there with them, living to satisfy every craving of our bodies and minds. By our very nature, we deserved God's wrath just like everyone else.

kjv: But God, who is rich in mercy, for his great love wherewith he loved us,
ct: But God is rich in mercy, and his love for us is so great

kjv: Even when we were dead in sins, hath quickened us together with Christ, (by grace ye are saved;)
ct: that even when we were dead in our sins, he made us alive together with Christ. It is by grace that you have been saved.

kjv: And hath raised us up together, and made us sit together in heavenly places in Christ Jesus:
ct: He raised us up with Christ and seated us with him in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus,

kjv: That in the ages to come he might shew the exceeding riches of his grace in his kindness toward us through Christ Jesus.
ct: so that for all the ages to come, he could display the overwhelming richness of his grace through his kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.

kjv: For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God:
ct: For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith — and this didn't come from you. It is the gift of God,

kjv: Not of works, lest any man should boast.
ct: not the result of anything you've done, so no one can boast about it.

kjv: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them.
ct: We are God's masterpiece, created in Christ Jesus to do good works that God planned long ago for us to carry out.

kjv: Wherefore remember, that ye being in time past Gentiles in the flesh, who are called Uncircumcision by that which is called the Circumcision in the flesh made by hands;
ct: So remember this: you used to be Gentiles by birth — called "the uncircumcised" by those who call themselves "the circumcised" because of a procedure done to their bodies by human hands.

kjv: That at that time ye were without Christ, being aliens from the commonwealth of Israel, and strangers from the covenants of promise, having no hope, and without God in the world:
ct: In those days you were cut off from Christ, excluded from the community of Israel, and had no part in the covenants God had promised. You lived in this world without hope and without God.

kjv: But now in Christ Jesus ye who sometimes were far off are made nigh by the blood of Christ.
ct: But now, in Christ Jesus, you who were once so far away have been brought near through the blood of Christ.

kjv: For he is our peace, who hath made both one, and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us;
ct: Christ himself is our peace. He has made Jews and Gentiles into one people and has torn down the wall that divided them.

kjv: Having abolished in his flesh the enmity, even the law of commandments contained in ordinances; for to make in himself of twain one new man, so making peace;
ct: In his own body, he put an end to the hostility between them by setting aside the law with its commandments and regulations. His purpose was to create one new humanity out of the two groups, and so make peace.

kjv: And that he might reconcile both unto God in one body by the cross, having slain the enmity thereby:
ct: Through the cross, he reconciled both groups to God in one body, putting that hostility to death once and for all.

kjv: And came and preached peace to you which were afar off, and to them that were nigh.
ct: He came and proclaimed peace to you who were far away from God and peace to those who were near.

kjv: For through him we both have access by one Spirit unto the Father.
ct: Through him, both groups now have access to the Father by one Spirit.

kjv: Now therefore ye are no more strangers and foreigners, but fellowcitizens with the saints, and of the household of God;
ct: So you are no longer outsiders or foreigners. You are fellow citizens with God's people and members of God's own household,

kjv: And are built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone;
ct: built on the foundation laid by the apostles and prophets, with Christ Jesus himself as the chief cornerstone.

kjv: In whom all the building fitly framed together groweth unto an holy temple in the Lord:
ct: In him, the whole structure is joined together and rises to become a holy temple in the Lord.

kjv: In whom ye also are builded together for an habitation of God through the Spirit.
ct: In him, you too are being built together into a place where God lives by his Spirit.

kjv: For this cause I Paul, the prisoner of Jesus Christ for you Gentiles,
ct: This is why I, Paul, am now a prisoner for the sake of Christ Jesus — because of my work among you Gentiles.

kjv: If ye have heard of the dispensation of the grace of God which is given me to you-ward:
ct: Surely you've heard that God gave me a special assignment to bring his grace to you.

kjv: How that by revelation he made known unto me the mystery; (as I wrote afore in few words,
ct: He revealed his mystery to me directly, as I briefly mentioned earlier in this letter.

kjv: Whereby, when ye read, ye may understand my knowledge in the mystery of Christ)
ct: When you read what I wrote, you'll be able to see how deeply I understand the mystery of Christ.

kjv: Which in other ages was not made known unto the sons of men, as it is now revealed unto his holy apostles and prophets by the Spirit;
ct: In previous generations, this mystery was never revealed to anyone. But now the Holy Spirit has made it known to God's holy apostles and prophets.

kjv: That the Gentiles should be fellowheirs, and of the same body, and partakers of his promise in Christ by the gospel:
ct: Here is the mystery: through the gospel, the Gentiles are now equal heirs with Israel, members of the same body, and sharers in the promise that comes through Christ Jesus.

kjv: Whereof I was made a minister, according to the gift of the grace of God given unto me by the effectual working of his power.
ct: God made me a servant of this gospel as a gift of his grace, working through me with his power.

kjv: Unto me, who am less than the least of all saints, is this grace given, that I should preach among the Gentiles the unsearchable riches of Christ;
ct: Even though I'm the least deserving of all God's people, he gave me this grace — to proclaim to the Gentiles the endless, unsearchable riches of Christ.

kjv: And to make all men see what is the fellowship of the mystery, which from the beginning of the world hath been hid in God, who created all things by Jesus Christ:
ct: My job is to help everyone see how this mystery works — the one that was hidden in God, the Creator of all things through Jesus Christ, since the beginning of the world.

kjv: To the intent that now unto the principalities and powers in heavenly places might be known by the church the manifold wisdom of God,
ct: God's plan was that now, through the church, even the rulers and authorities in the heavenly places would see his wisdom displayed in all its rich variety.

kjv: According to the eternal purpose which he purposed in Christ Jesus our Lord:
ct: This was his eternal purpose, and he carried it out through Christ Jesus our Lord.

kjv: In whom we have boldness and access with confidence by the faith of him.
ct: Because of him, and through our faith in him, we can approach God boldly and with complete confidence.

kjv: Wherefore I desire that ye faint not at my tribulations for you, which is your glory.
ct: So I'm asking you — don't lose heart because of the suffering I'm going through for your sake. My suffering is actually an honor for you.

kjv: For this cause I bow my knees unto the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ,
ct: For this reason, I kneel before the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ,

kjv: Of whom the whole family in heaven and earth is named,
ct: from whom every family in heaven and on earth gets its name.

kjv: That he would grant you, according to the riches of his glory, to be strengthened with might by his Spirit in the inner man;
ct: I pray that out of the riches of his glory, he would strengthen you with power through his Spirit deep in your inner being,

kjv: That Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith; that ye, being rooted and grounded in love,
ct: so that Christ would make his home in your hearts through faith. I pray that you would be deeply rooted and firmly grounded in love,

kjv: May be able to comprehend with all saints what is the breadth, and length, and depth, and height;
ct: so that you, together with all of God's people, would have the strength to grasp how wide, how long, how high, and how deep his love truly is —

kjv: And to know the love of Christ, which passeth knowledge, that ye might be filled with all the fulness of God.
ct: to actually know the love of Christ, even though it goes beyond anything we can fully understand — and to be filled with the complete fullness of God himself.

kjv: Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us,
ct: Now to the one who is able to do an incredible amount more than anything we could ever ask or even imagine, through his power already at work within us —

kjv: Unto him be glory in the church by Christ Jesus throughout all ages, world without end. Amen.
ct: to him be glory in the church and in Christ Jesus through every generation, forever and ever. Amen.

kjv: I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord, beseech you that ye walk worthy of the vocation wherewith ye are called,
ct: So I urge you — and I'm writing this as a prisoner for the Lord — to live in a way that matches the calling God has given you.

kjv: With all lowliness and meekness, with longsuffering, forbearing one another in love;
ct: Be humble and gentle. Be patient with each other, putting up with one another's faults because of love.

kjv: Endeavouring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
ct: Make every effort to stay united in the Spirit, held together by peace.

kjv: There is one body, and one Spirit, even as ye are called in one hope of your calling;
ct: There is one body and one Spirit, just as you were all called to one shared hope.

kjv: One Lord, one faith, one baptism,
ct: One Lord, one faith, one baptism,

kjv: One God and Father of all, who is above all, and through all, and in you all.
ct: and one God and Father of all, who is above all, works through all, and lives in all of you.

kjv: But unto every one of us is given grace according to the measure of the gift of Christ.
ct: But each one of us has been given grace in the specific measure that Christ chose to give it.

kjv: Wherefore he saith, When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men.
ct: That's why Scripture says, "When he ascended to the heights, he led captivity captive and gave gifts to people."

kjv: (Now that he ascended, what is it but that he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth?
ct: Now, when it says "he ascended," that means he must have first descended to the lower parts of the earth.

kjv: He that descended is the same also that ascended up far above all heavens, that he might fill all things.)
ct: The one who descended is the same one who ascended far above all the heavens, so that he might fill all things.

kjv: And he gave some, apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, pastors and teachers;
ct: He gave some people to be apostles, some to be prophets, some to be evangelists, and some to be pastors and teachers.

kjv: For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ:
ct: He did this to equip God's people for the work of serving and to build up the body of Christ.

kjv: Till we all come in the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ:
ct: This will continue until all of us reach unity in our faith and in our knowledge of the Son of God — becoming fully mature, measured by nothing less than the full stature of Christ himself.

kjv: That we henceforth be no more children, tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the sleight of men, and cunning craftiness, whereby they lie in wait to deceive;
ct: Then we won't be like children anymore, tossed around by waves and blown in every direction by whatever new teaching comes along, manipulated by people who use tricks and clever schemes to lead others astray.

kjv: But speaking the truth in love, may grow up into him in all things, which is the head, even Christ:
ct: Instead, by speaking the truth in love, we will grow up in every way into Christ, who is the head.

kjv: From whom the whole body fitly joined together and compacted by that which every joint supplieth, according to the effectual working in the measure of every part, maketh increase of the body unto the edifying of itself in love.
ct: From him, the whole body is fitted and held together by every supporting joint. As each individual part does its work, the body grows and builds itself up in love.

kjv: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that ye henceforth walk not as other Gentiles walk, in the vanity of their mind,
ct: So here's what I'm telling you, and I insist on it in the Lord: stop living the way the other Gentiles live, with their empty, pointless thinking.

kjv: Having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart:
ct: Their understanding is clouded in darkness. They're cut off from the life of God because of the ignorance inside them, caused by the hardness of their hearts.

kjv: Who being past feeling have given themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.
ct: They've lost all sensitivity, so they've thrown themselves into every kind of shameless behavior, greedily practicing every form of impurity.

kjv: But ye have not so learned Christ;
ct: But that's not what you learned when you came to know Christ.

kjv: If so be that ye have heard him, and have been taught by him, as the truth is in Jesus:
ct: Surely you heard his message and were taught the truth that is found in Jesus —

kjv: That ye put off concerning the former conversation the old man, which is corrupt according to the deceitful lusts;
ct: that you must strip off your old self, the way of life you used to follow, which was being destroyed by its own deceptive desires.

kjv: And be renewed in the spirit of your mind;
ct: Instead, let your thinking be completely renewed from the inside out.

kjv: And that ye put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness.
ct: Then put on your new self — the person God created to reflect his own righteousness and true holiness.

kjv: Wherefore putting away lying, speak every man truth with his neighbour: for we are members one of another.
ct: So stop lying to each other. Speak the truth to your neighbor, because we are all parts of the same body.

kjv: Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath:
ct: If you get angry, don't let it lead you into sin. And don't let the sun go down while you're still holding onto that anger.

kjv: Neither give place to the devil.
ct: Don't give the devil an opening.

kjv: Let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him labour, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth.
ct: If you used to steal, stop stealing. Instead, work hard with your own hands at something good, so you'll have something to share with anyone in need.

kjv: Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers.
ct: Don't let any rotten words come out of your mouth. Only say what is helpful for building others up when they need it, so that your words bring grace to everyone who hears them.

kjv: And grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption.
ct: Don't grieve the Holy Spirit of God, who has sealed you for the day of redemption.

kjv: Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:
ct: Get rid of all bitterness, rage, anger, harsh words, and slander, along with every form of malice.

kjv: And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ’s sake hath forgiven you.
ct: Be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving each other — just as God forgave you through Christ.

kjv: Be ye therefore followers of God, as dear children;
ct: So follow God's example, the way beloved children imitate their father.

kjv: And walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour.
ct: Let love guide everything you do, just as Christ loved us and gave himself up for us as an offering and sacrifice to God — a sweet-smelling fragrance.

kjv: But fornication, and all uncleanness, or covetousness, let it not be once named among you, as becometh saints;
ct: There must not be even a hint of sexual immorality, impurity, or greed among you. That's what it means to be set apart as God's people.

kjv: Neither filthiness, nor foolish talking, nor jesting, which are not convenient: but rather giving of thanks.
ct: Stay away from obscene talk, foolish chatter, and crude jokes — none of that is fitting. Instead, let your words be filled with thankfulness.

kjv: For this ye know, that no whoremonger, nor unclean person, nor covetous man, who is an idolater, hath any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God.
ct: You can be sure of this: no sexually immoral person, no one who is impure, and no greedy person — which is the same as being an idolater — has any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God.
