kjv: Let no man deceive you with vain words: for because of these things cometh the wrath of God upon the children of disobedience.
ct: Don't let anyone fool you with empty arguments. These are the very things that bring God's wrath down on those who refuse to obey him.

kjv: Be not ye therefore partakers with them.
ct: So don't participate in what they do.

kjv: For ye were sometimes darkness, but now are ye light in the Lord: walk as children of light:
ct: You used to be nothing but darkness, but now you are light in the Lord. So live as people of light.

kjv: (For the fruit of the Spirit is in all goodness and righteousness and truth;)
ct: The fruit of the Spirit produces every kind of goodness, righteousness, and truth.

kjv: Proving what is acceptable unto the Lord.
ct: Figure out what pleases the Lord, and do that.

kjv: And have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather reprove them.
ct: Don't get involved in the worthless activities that belong to darkness. Instead, expose them for what they are.

kjv: For it is a shame even to speak of those things which are done of them in secret.
ct: The things people do in secret are so shameful it's disgraceful even to talk about them.

kjv: But all things that are reproved are made manifest by the light: for whatsoever doth make manifest is light.
ct: But when anything is exposed by the light, it becomes visible for what it really is — because light reveals everything.

kjv: Wherefore he saith, Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.
ct: That's why it says: "Wake up, you who are sleeping! Rise from the dead, and Christ will shine his light on you."

kjv: See then that ye walk circumspectly, not as fools, but as wise,
ct: So be very careful how you live. Don't act like fools — live wisely.

kjv: Redeeming the time, because the days are evil.
ct: Make the most of every opportunity, because the times we live in are evil.

kjv: Wherefore be ye not unwise, but understanding what the will of the Lord is.
ct: Don't be foolish. Instead, seek to understand what the Lord wants from you.

kjv: And be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the Spirit;
ct: Don't get drunk on wine — that only leads to reckless living. Instead, be filled with the Holy Spirit.

kjv: Speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord;
ct: Encourage each other with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs. Sing and make music in your heart to the Lord.

kjv: Giving thanks always for all things unto God and the Father in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ;
ct: Always give thanks to God the Father for everything, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.

kjv: Submitting yourselves one to another in the fear of God.
ct: Submit to one another out of the fear of God.

kjv: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.
ct: Wives, submit to your own husbands as you do to the Lord.

kjv: For the husband is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the head of the church: and he is the saviour of the body.
ct: The husband is the head of the wife, just as Christ is the head of the church — and Christ himself is the savior of the body.

kjv: Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing.
ct: Just as the church submits to Christ, wives should submit to their husbands in everything.

kjv: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;
ct: Husbands, love your wives the same way Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her.

kjv: That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word,
ct: He did this to set her apart as holy, cleansing her through the washing of water by his word,

kjv: That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.
ct: so that he could present the church to himself in all her glory — without stain or wrinkle or any flaw — completely holy and without blemish.

kjv: So ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife loveth himself.
ct: In the same way, husbands should love their wives as they love their own bodies. A man who loves his wife is really loving himself.

kjv: For no man ever yet hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it, even as the Lord the church:
ct: After all, no one has ever hated his own body. Instead, he feeds it and takes care of it — just as the Lord does for the church.

kjv: For we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.
ct: We are part of his body, of his flesh and of his bones.

kjv: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be one flesh.
ct: "For this reason, a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two will become one flesh."

kjv: This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church.
ct: This is a great mystery — but I'm talking about Christ and the church.

kjv: Nevertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself; and the wife see that she reverence her husband.
ct: Still, the point stands: each husband must love his wife as he loves himself, and each wife must respect her husband.

kjv: Children, obey your parents in the Lord: for this is right.
ct: Children, obey your parents as the Lord wants you to, because this is the right thing to do.

kjv: Honour thy father and mother; which is the first commandment with promise;
ct: "Honor your father and mother" — this is the first commandment that comes with a promise:

kjv: That it may be well with thee, and thou mayest live long on the earth.
ct: "so that things will go well for you, and you will live a long life on the earth."

kjv: And, ye fathers, provoke not your children to wrath: but bring them up in the nurture and admonition of the Lord.
ct: Fathers, don't push your children to the point of anger. Instead, raise them with the discipline and instruction that comes from the Lord.

kjv: Servants, be obedient to them that are your masters according to the flesh, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as unto Christ;
ct: Servants, obey your earthly masters with deep respect and sincerity of heart, as if you were serving Christ himself.

kjv: Not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but as the servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart;
ct: Don't just work hard when they're watching to impress them. Instead, work as servants of Christ, doing God's will from the heart.

kjv: With good will doing service, as to the Lord, and not to men:
ct: Serve with genuine enthusiasm, as though you're working for the Lord and not for people.

kjv: Knowing that whatsoever good thing any man doeth, the same shall he receive of the Lord, whether he be bond or free.
ct: Remember that the Lord will reward every person for whatever good they do, whether they are slave or free.

kjv: And, ye masters, do the same things unto them, forbearing threatening: knowing that your Master also is in heaven; neither is there respect of persons with him.
ct: Masters, treat your servants the same way. Stop threatening them. Remember that both you and they share the same Master in heaven, and he shows no favoritism.

kjv: Finally, my brethren, be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might.
ct: Finally, be strong in the Lord and in his mighty power.

kjv: Put on the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.
ct: Put on the full armor of God so that you can stand firm against the devil's schemes.

kjv: For we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high places.
ct: Our fight is not against other human beings. It is against rulers and authorities, against the powers that govern the darkness of this world, against spiritual forces of wickedness in the heavenly places.

kjv: Wherefore take unto you the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand.
ct: So put on the full armor of God. Then when the day of evil comes, you will be able to hold your ground, and after you have done everything possible, you will still be standing.

kjv: Stand therefore, having your loins girt about with truth, and having on the breastplate of righteousness;
ct: Stand firm, then, with the belt of truth buckled around your waist and the breastplate of righteousness protecting your chest.

kjv: And your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace;
ct: Fit your feet with the readiness that comes from the gospel of peace.

kjv: Above all, taking the shield of faith, wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked.
ct: Above all, take up the shield of faith, which will let you extinguish every flaming arrow the evil one fires at you.

kjv: And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God:
ct: Take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.

kjv: Praying always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, and watching thereunto with all perseverance and supplication for all saints;
ct: Pray at all times in the Spirit with every kind of prayer and request. Stay alert and never give up as you pray for all of God's people.

kjv: And for me, that utterance may be given unto me, that I may open my mouth boldly, to make known the mystery of the gospel,
ct: Pray for me too — that whenever I open my mouth, God will give me the right words to boldly make known the mystery of the gospel.

kjv: For which I am an ambassador in bonds: that therein I may speak boldly, as I ought to speak.
ct: I am an ambassador for this gospel, even though I'm currently in chains. Pray that I will speak it as boldly as I should.

kjv: But that ye also may know my affairs, and how I do, Tychicus, a beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord, shall make known to you all things:
ct: Tychicus, a dear brother and faithful servant in the Lord, will tell you everything about how I'm doing and what's happening with me.

kjv: Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that ye might know our affairs, and that he might comfort your hearts.
ct: I'm sending him to you for this very reason — so you can know how we are and so he can encourage your hearts.

kjv: Peace be to the brethren, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
ct: Peace to the brothers and sisters, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

kjv: Grace be with all them that love our Lord Jesus Christ in sincerity. Amen.
ct: May grace be with everyone who loves our Lord Jesus Christ with an undying love. Amen.
